sign: gemini and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i like to look for the humorous side in everything. i love the outdoors and lead an active life. i'm currently juggling work and pursuing an mba, but still try to get to the gym 5 to 6 days a week. i love hiking, camping, road trips, traveling abroad, trying new cuisines, snowboarding, snorkeling, softball, biking, bbq's, and good comedy/action/horror flicks. above all else, i like the company of good friends. currently going to business school full time, and working part time in the environmental field. i'm interested in learning how companies, big and small, can make their business operations socially just and environmentally sustainable. one day, i'd love to start a green business myself. writing, telling stories, laughing at myself, helping friends out of a tight spot, playing impossible old nes games, being level-headed when shit hits the fan, and remembering something random you said to me years ago. oh yeah, i'm also really flexible for a guy : ) people tell me i'm sarcastic, funny (hilarious after a few drinks), and a hip dresser. they're also always amazed at how much i can eat for a slim guy! movies: office space, fight club - i love situational humor and satire of everyday life thank you for smoking and charlie wilson's war - quick, witty dialogue cracks me up terminator 1 & 2, predator - great action and sci-fi alien, the descent - i'm a sucker for creepy creature flicks  music: classic rock, metal, alternative, and 80's  food: pad thai, unagi, onion rings, curry, and mangoes. i'm always up for trying new, weird foods (snake soup, frog legs, and snails), and i love all kinds of spicy food. if it doesn't hurt a little bit, it's no fun! friends to share inside jokes with, yosemite valley, mangoes, california summer days, traveling to foreign countries, and wikipedia to look up random facts and settle spontaneous bets with friends how there are so many beautiful and strange places in the world that i've only seen pictures of, and how to decide where my next long vacation or weekend getaway should be. packing for a last minute camping trip, bbqing at a friend's house, or clinking glasses with buddies at a pub. i was born in a car. if you're adventurous and enjoy making new friends or if you want to make a new friend regardless of whether i'm the one for you. either way you'll be glad you did.

full_bio: i come to san francisco by way of new york, where i was born, raised and went to school. i think this accounts for my occasionally blunt, no bullshit attitude, although the bay area has been hard at work mellowing me out. (some say it's even been successful)  i've been working in the games industry, in the east bay, as a producer, designer and occasional contributor of polygonal art for the past six years. recent projects involved me working with a bunch of neat folks to make the games where you click buttons on plastic guitar thingies and pretend to play music. it was great fun working with my team of bright, super passionate music loving people, and i learned a lot. but the 14-16 work days eventually took their toll, so now i work saner hours making educational games for 3-8 year-olds in emeryville. it's humbling, really, to see all your design assumptions blown out of the water by a 3 year old.  i'm a big fan of city walking. previous to coming to sf, i worked in new york, a city of walkers (and drivers for that matter, but i didn't own a car, so i was a walker by default), which is probably where it comes from. love to stroll around sf on bright, sunny days, sometimes with my trusty dslr, sniffing out new cafes, and just enjoying the scenery. i've taken up iaido with a fantastic sensei in downtown sf, specifically mus jikiden eishin-ry - if you know what that is without having to look it up, stop reading this and drop me a line right now - and it's been a great addition to my life. the amateur anthropologist in me is fascinated by it, as is the amateur historian, and it makes for a nice workout to boot, but i don't know exactly what it means to me yet. only that i'm taking part in something older and greater than myself, and it's teaching me about balance and humility. also, swinging a sword is fun. observing myself and others. sticking with it to the bitter end. listening. beats me! nobody has ever told me, and i've never asked. books: i've been on a non-fiction/history kick for the past couple years. some highlights include genghis khan and the making of the modern world, gamorrah, cocaine: an unauthorized biography, and many, many books on the current war in iraq and afghanistan, world war i & ii (became grimly unbearable after a while). too much fiction to mention by writers they give you in college. blood meridian. much murakami.  movies: way too many to mention. some highlights off the top of my head would be harakiri, ikiru, taxi driver, chinatown, vertigo, anything by mike leigh. anything by ozu. kurosawa up until dodes'ka-den. anything by wong kar-wai, and most stuff by hou hsiao-hsien. abbas kiarostami and aki kaurismki are two directors i've discovered recently and am greedily watching more of.  i will watch the garbagey action flick or sci fi spectacle and geek out to them in the theater with friends, but they mean nothing to me. (i do have soft spot for a few childhood movies, alien, aliens, blade runner and the empire strikes back being three that just won't let go)  tv: i don't own a tv, but friends insisted that i watch both the wire and the sopranos. so i did, thanks to netflix. i really liked them both, particularly the sopranos. and now that i'm done with them, i don't feel particularly compelled to seek more. unless you happen to have some recommendations?  oh, i really like carl sagan's cosmo. does that count?  music: tom waits, tindersticks, nick cave & the bad seeds, leonard cohen, radiohead, prince, elvis costello, death cab for cutie, rem, the decemberists, the shins, some u2. anything i can play along to on the guitar.  i grew up in a family that listened to a lot of opera, jazz and classical, so that finds its way into my listening patterns, too. falling asleep, then waking up to opera is sublime.  food: i'm not a "foodie", but i do enjoy trying anything and everything i can, at least once. my favorite flavor is probably that of cilantro. and i have an odd obsession with bananas - i went through a period where i put them in everything! i like 'em slightly unripened, so they have a faint citrus flavor to them (not so much a fan of the ripened/browning, sweet ones) recently, i even made some ultra spicy bulgogi... with diced bananas! but don't worry, i don't insist you enjoy them, too. food & water. i pretty much need those to live. family & friends ringing guitars & overdriven amps photography iaido fiction  bonus stuff (if i could have more than six things): travel. i try to get out of the country at least once a year long, hot baths (preferably in japan) fat, lazy cats lounging in the sun the smell of paper in all its many variations warm, sunny days are pretty great, too why people can't be nicer to each other. hanging out with friends, playing guitar at home in my pajamas, practicing iai at home in my pajamas, reading, catching up on my rental queue alone or with friends. prowling around, looking for a new late night cafe. for a while i made it a point to go to at least one concert a month, but work put the kibosh on that; now that i have more time on my hands, i plan to get back into that. large gatherings of people at parties, bars or whatnot burn me out pretty quickly, even if they're friends, and i have to pretend like i'm having fun after a while. (friends, i hope you never read this) you're a sincere, kind and strong person. you appreciate things well and deeply. you're not preoccupied with being trendy or fashionable. you have a nerdy streak (it doesn't have to be pronounced, but it's definitely there). you're passionate about something, even if it doesn't happen to be your trade. you work hard at whatever you do and enjoy a challenge. you like long, aimless walks in the city. you read.  if your nightlife consists of the music/dancing/drinking triumvirate i see so often on profiles here, i don't think we'll have much in common.

offspring: doesn't have kids, but wants them
full_bio: i chose the name julesypbr because i like pbr, and because i had trouble coming up with a screenname that wasn't chosen already and i wanted to hurry up and get to the interesting stuff. pbr doesn't define me, though i often order it.... i'm originally from norcal (santa cruz) and i lived in la for college (usc, fight on!) plus a few years. i moved to sf about 4 years ago for nursing school, which i finished in september. my specialty is geriatrics, and i would eventually love to work for hospice. i like lots of things (flea markets, football, cheap beer, laughing, reading, taking walks, pie, snow, bubble baths, my nieces and nephews); i dislike lots of things (cigarettes, the texture of terra cotta, jerks, coffee, motorcycles, guys with ab photos in their profiles). i have a hard time answering when people ask me what i do "for fun" because i tend to have fun at whatever i am doing. i like to go out on the town with friends and i like to spend time with my family at my parents' house. i enjoy more adventurey things like snowboarding and camping and traveling. but i also have fun doing little things like staying home and reading, or listening to music and organizing my apartment. i'm never bored. i am a nurse, a hospice volunteer, a flea marketer, a word inventress, a unicorn aficionado, a teapot enthusiast, a lover of stories and things ridiculous, an anti-douchebag campaigner, and a slow-eater-tiny-bite-taker. i do many more things, and i'm happiest when i'm with the people i love. being honest. i can't lie. ever. remembering song lyrics and movie quotes. yeah... that'll get me far in life. gift giving. telling stories (true ones). caring about things. empathy, i haz it. roller skating. my hair, my tattoo, my wild hand gestures, my long-winded stories. my sparkling personality? other stuff. books: whichever one i'm currently reading... the glass castle, one hundred years of solitude, the lovely bones, graceling, fire, daughter of smoke and bone, the hunger games, harry potter, one year to an organized life, the ordinary princess.  movies: labyrinth, repo! the genetic opera, the princess bride, i heart huckabees, eternal sunshine of the spotless mind, inception, equilibrium, role models, forgetting sarah marshall, anchorman, the wedding singer, notorious, ishtar, strictly ballroom, who framed roger rabbit?  tv: parks & recreation, the office, glee, modern family, antiques roadshow.  food: sushi, mexican, indian, thai, italian, anything with cheese.  music: how did i forget to put music on here before? let's see, i tend to like particular songs more than artists, but i few i generally enjoy are... david bowie, queen, jay z, kanye, the weathermen, the carpenters, the soundtrack to the last waltz, soundtracks to musicals in general, and the very best show i've ever seen in my life: cher. no joke. she turned back time. i'm going to rewrite this under the assumption that family and friends are understood prerequisites. - dental hygiene (because, obviously) - mechanisms of communication (internet/phone) - my cat (bowie) - glass bottle sodas (num) - my moonstone ring (finger feels naked without it) - *placeholder for awesome thing of the day* do i have my keys? i ask myself this every time i leave the house, because the door automatically locks behind me. did i turn my flat iron off? i'm afraid of burning my house down. i feel like it is justified because my cousin actually did burn her house down.  also, heavier things like the future... where i'm going to get a job, where i might live, what's going to happen to our planet, etc. going out partying or staying in studying or hanging out with friends or visiting my parents.  you're fun, you think i sound fun, you think we might have fun having fun together. you know the difference between your and you're. you're not a douchebag. not negotiable. you're not going to call me a hipster for liking pbr. i liked it before it was... wait. nevermind. you might be able to get my wireless mouse to work. i've already tried yelling at it. *some other clever reason to message me.*

full_bio: i'm originally canadian but am enjoying life in california and not having to worry about frostbite or mismatched mittens. i'm pretty mellow, enjoy connecting with people, reading a good novel, eating spicy food (especially thai or south indian, mmmm). meditation's a pretty big part of my life, and i love hanging out with people who are spiritually turned-on in some way, which i guess is another way to say authentic and self-aware. what else can i tell you about me... i love to swim and sometimes wish i was born an otter or a sea-lion. i think i'm addicted to dried mango.  i am mellow, friendly, and compassionate i work as a teacher and administrator at a school in s.f. and volunteer as a counselor once a week. i also write fiction in my spare time and on slow days at work. :) listening to people, eating spicy food, explaining english grammar, sitting on a cushion the length of my hair, my canadian accent, eh? books? hmm. so many. the liars' club (mary karr), norwegian wood (haruki murakami), pale fire (vladimir nabokov), the mill on the floss (george eliot). movies? i have a thing for australian films, for some reason, i don't know what it is: walkabout, the last wave, muriel's wedding. music -- all over the place: brian eno, jimmy cliff, mazzy star, the smiths. food: thai, south indian, sushi, korean. books, meditation, open-hearted people, the sense of touch, curiosity, honesty the startling pinkness of the flowers on my way to work, a good opening line for my next short story, whether mango would taste good on pizza hanging out with friends, chilling with a good book, eating yummy food and contemplating the dessert menu i love bell peppers and eat them raw, like an apple. you think canada is the best country in the world. just kidding. full_bio: i'm from and currently live in la, but i'm in sf till august, and i'm making the permanent move to sf soon. i did my undergrad at ucsb (too much fun) and now i'm a third-year law student at usc. i love eating out every chance i get, travelling (so far i've been to 26 countries on 5 continents), skiing, scuba diving, swimming, dancing, hiking, and camping. i love meeting new people from all walks of life. my friends mean the world to me and there's pretty much nothing i wouldn't do for them. enjoying my time in sf; exploring the city and the surrounding area. i'm loving the firm i'm currently at. i get to work on really interesting matters and am given the opportunity to do lots of pro bono work. i'm going back to la in august to finish my last year of law school and then i'll be moving up to sf permanently (or at least for a few years) to work at the firm. letting loose, school, skiing, and eating ridiculous amounts of food. i'm really tall. music: edm and rock (disclosure, the presets, justice, fake blood, the 2 bears, the chemical brothers, the arctic monkeys, the xx, the black keys, van morrison, and the doors are examples)  authors: hunter s. thompson, richard dawkins, walt whitman, bret easton ellis, chuck palahniuk, salmon rushdie, ken wilber.  food: sushi, thai, and french.  shows: parks & recreation, 30 rock, archer, arrested development, the office, boardwalk empire, true blood, game of thrones, supernatural, the ricky gervais show, peep show, misfits.  movies: way too many to list, but i like pretty much all genres. sushi, my music library, water to swim in, my friends, air conditioning (at least in la), sunglasses.  out to a late dinner and then either at a dive bar with friends or dancing in a club till the early morning. hmmm. . . i'll have to get back to you on that one :) you're in or near sf and you think we'd get along.

full_bio: why reveal all the good stuff up front? it's much more fun to go with the "less is more" approach. i think learning about one another in person is one of the essential experiences in dating, so why deprive ourselves of that pleasure? however, it's only fair that i tell you something about me. so here goes: even though people regard me as mature and well-accomplished, i'm actually very easygoing and fun. i also enjoy traveling to new places (like everyone else on here, apparently). making friends has never been a problem for me. did i mention i cook, too? working in sf for a bank (not hard to guess which one, apparently ;).  staying close with my wonderful family and friends...they are truly the important things in life.  reading as much as i can...the bart ride into sf makes this easy. in fact, i would say that i easily prefer reading a book (or the economist) as opposed to playing around with my phone during this time.  staying active...i try to make it to the gym at least twice each week and/or go on a 3 mile run. i have to do this early in the morning sometimes, but it's definitely worth it since i always feel better after.  i enjoy learning and doing new things. for example, i recently learned to drive a manual transmission (i don't stall the car anymore!). also, i love trying out new recipes (cooking is a lot of fun to do socially as well). when i can, i try to stay connected to the academic world i left behind either by reading some journal articles or taking advantage of local alumni events. listening, solving puzzles, and projecting a relaxed, laid-back vibe. also, not taking myself seriously (although it is hard to tell from the way i write). either my height or my eyes. this section can easily get out of control, but i'll do my best. also, i'm not sure how helpful lists like these are in deciding whether or not to message/respond to people, but they sure are fun to write!  i tend to not watch much tv but i'm definitely a movie buff and there is ~30 years of useless movie trivial floating through my head. i will say that inception blew my mind (probably one of my favorite films to come out in the past 5 years). however, i have seen some interesting films on netflix, especially documentaries. i would definitely recommend "exit through the gift shop" (about how easily a new movement can be compromised) and "catfish" (the other facebook movie...this movie was really disturbing and, ultimately sad, but i would say it is required viewing, especially for people like us who are looking to meet each other based on what we post).  in terms of books, i just finished "the lost city of z" and "the big short" on a recent trip and i highly recommend both. in terms of fiction, i recommend "the keep" (high-brow lit stuff) and "the half-made world" (not so high brow, but really good). of course, if you are really adventurous, there is always "foucault's pendulum"...  wow, i really didn't keep this section under control. all right, the following sections will be really, really short. i promise :) this changes all the time so feel free to ask me. family and friends will always take up the first two slots, though. where i'm going next (literally and figuratively, of course). either in the city exploring, hanging out with friends, or relaxing at home. i used to play the violin. you want to find out more.

full_bio: ah, i love that giddy feeling of a new social networking / dating site, before the inevitable disappointment and boredom sets in.  i guess i'm a passionate, sometimes inscrutable quiet guy with a romantic streak. fan of contradictions and eclecticism. cynical idealist. listening to bluegrass on the way to the metal show then dancing at mighty after. channel flipping between trash tv and sf government tv. yoga before a dive bar bender. my life is often crazy busy and overextended, but i can lounge away a weekend day doing nothing with the best of them. after 7 years of getting more and more involved (obsessed) with san francisco politics, i'm starting 2011 by pulling back to a more balanced lifestyle. i'm still trying to save the world (or at least our city), but i'm also focused on playing music, riding my bike more, co-hosting an internet radio show, snowboarding, yoga, and dipping my toe back into the internet dating world for the first time in years.  in 2010, i was kind of a focused and practical monk. in 2011, i'm looking for more impractical whimsy and wonder and dancing. my super power is that it's really hard for people to hate me. "oh sorry, i didn't see you there." currently, it's probably my poofy, overgrown hair. books: the bel canto, suttree, sometimes a great notion, don't think of an elephant. authors: harlan ellison, dashiell hammett, hunter thompson, raymond chandler. movies: who's afraid of virginia wolf, charlie verrick, casablanca, what would jesus buy?, pi, helvetica, secretary. music: akron/family, okkervil river, phosphorescent, grateful dead, jessica lea mayfield, sean hayes, bar feeders, neurosis, totimoshi, melvins, zeph and azeem, charles mingus, nas, prefuse 73, bob dylan, steve earle. bike guitar a few close friends sufficient alone time kyanite great radio stations (kalx, kfjc, wfmu, kusf, kpoo, pirate cat, radio valencia, npr, etc.) my other bike how rising rents and property values are continuing to make san francisco a richer, blander, less exciting, less progressive city, and is there anything to be done about it, or is it time to give up and move to oakland. is that too heavy and ardent for okcupid? i'm new around here.  i've also been thinking about who these new enemies are that okcupid keeps telling me about, what i did to offend them, and if i can trust that this uneasy detente will continue or if i should be prepared to defend myself.  oh and doppler radar and satellite imagery. i dig that shit during the rainy season. at pirate cat radio, at least until 8.  any of these sounds good: - you have something that your passionate about. artists make me weak in the knees. activists make me hot. - you like late night bike rides. -you're one of my enemies and you're ready for our climactic showdown. - you're up for spontaneous adventures around the city. i'm looking to take advantage of my flexible work schedule to have some weekday fun. i love being out in the city when everyone else is working. - you're up for following a storm up to the mountains for a midweek powder day of boarding/skiing. - you're interested in a week-long crush and a drink or three in a mellow bar with a good jukebox and seeing what happens.

full_bio: its always hard to write about this sort of stuff...but here's a shot: always up for an adventure on a whim. the worst has been when i drove 5 hours to get lunch...but it was quite worth it. i have eclectic hobbies to say the least. try to spend time as much time as possible outside enjoying what the bay area has to offer. travelling to places that have rich per-biblical history...it is incredibly fascinating. next trip will either be to greece or italy. love animals...growing up my house resembled more a zoo with rabbits, doves, pigeons, baters, cats, dogs and a deer. not too into watching sports...would much rather prefer to get off the couch and do something...though every now and then will watch some f-1. i haven't owned a tv since 2003. not the biggest fan of the east coast winters...they're great to visit...but when driving became more a scene for a roller derby rink, i had to reconsider my choices...hence the move to sf. carpe diem. write code during the week working for a startup. mounted sf sheriff as and when required. riding ponies on the weekends. breaking things....i've managed to break stuff that was considered idiot proof. i have this innate ability....can't really figure out whether its a gift or a curse? my "stolen" green jacket? books: don't end up reading a whole lot of books but more current affairs. truth is stranger than fiction...and more interesting.  food: willing to try anything at least once...but as a staple...burmese would have to be it.  tv: really do enjoy watching a whole lot of old british stuff like yes minister, 'allo 'allo etc family and friends staying in touch with the above good food hmm....may have to think about the other 3 a bit more... problems...all sort of them. from world hunger to how to i take this thing apart. depending on what i have planned for saturday, either going to bed early or hanging out with friends. i really did go to a space camp...it was awesome.  i was once involved in a mexican standoff with cops...no one got hurt...no one got arrested...just a few feathers ruffled. if it strikes your fancy....

full_bio: the best way to self-summarize is with a story.  once upon a time (circa 2007), i was in a wedding party. we were out taking pictures in front of the fancypants hotel where the reception was being held. in the midst of the pictures, i turned to my friend (also in the wedding party) and asked very purposefully, "jacket on or off? which do you think?" my friend, of almost 20 years, replied, "i don't know, whichever one you think is better." i asked again, "i really need to know if i should stay jacket on or off."  the wedding photographer interjected, "why don't you go jacket off?" to which i happily replied, "well that hardly seems like an appropriate thing to do at a wedding. and while you're taking pictures?" every now and then you get a picture where everyone has a genuine smile on their face--that was one of them.  read: i have friends (girls) that are on this site so i know how much bullshit finds its way into your inbox. i refuse to be mixed in with those messages, so my approach is simple: instead of messaging if i'm interested, i'll rate you 4 or 5 stars and you can message me if the interest is mutual. i don't have to be lumped between various crappy opening messages and "i want to have sex with you" and you have one less message filling up your inbox--everyone wins. pursuing a second bachelor's degree in either mechanical engineering or maple syrup tycoonery. winning arguments via jokes. offending the unoffendable (usually on accident). looking like i'm out of place while rock climbing. visiting profiles multiple times because i don't remember which ones i've visited in the past. making breakfast. making sushi. cooking in general. making up words. carrying on conversations while quoting song lyrics without the other person noticing. catching the smallest fish in the ocean. "oh wow! did he just run into that sliding glass door?" there could be a list a mile long here talking about favorites, but i'd be as bored typing it as you would be reading it. plus, it's more fun to find that stuff out as you get to know someone. omg wouldn't it be funny if i were like super literal here and said things like air, food, water, shelter????? no, no it wouldn't.  the greek alphabet. exercise. my dog. your dog. ice cream sandwiches--the kind between two cookies. the internet. whether the bay area is legitimately full of bisexual women or if the selection of guys on okc is so bad that the girls here need to keep their options open. there is no typical friday night for me. here are the possibilities:  out with friends. on a date. home reading something or studying. yes, at home doing some book-learning on a friday night is a possibility. i do what i want. at the gym. i have a morning ritual that i need to share. i call it "the terminator." first, i crouch down in the shower in the classic "naked terminator traveling through time" pose. with my eyes closed, i crouch there for a minute visualizing arnold and i start to hum the terminator theme. i'll then slowly rise to a standing position and open my eyes. it helps me to proceed through my day as an emotionless, cyborg badass. the only problem is if the shower curtain sticks to my terminator leg it ruins the fantasy. yes, this is a joke. you like to have fun. i say this because there aren't enough people with "i like to have fun" or some variant of that in their profile.  you exercise somewhat regularly even if you have an awesome metabolism.  you have a sense of a humor and can make me laugh. laughter makes the world a better place, so let's make it better in tandem.  you are a person of substance. you may be nice to look at, but there should be more to you than what your genes have given you and what cosmetics do for you.  you are educated. this doesn't necessarily mean you have a college degree either. a capacity and desire to learn are far more important than any piece of paper afforded to people who are willing to pay the price and put in the minimum amount of work.  if none of the above, looking like audrey hepburn may be enough.

full_bio: (n.b. i get very few responses on okc, but i trust that if "the one" is here, she will... and i don't buy this "enemy" rating stuff much at all ... :)  that said, if....  you've done the obligatory picture-scan and i've maintained your attention for at least for one sentence and...  you try your best to regard others with kindness first, are open-minded and not judgmental about other's weirdness (i'm within normal weirdness limits), and your appreciate your own unique brand of quirkiness...  you have your own healthy view of the big picture, and how you fit into it...  you approach life with an understanding that everyone's journey is unique, that none of us really knows what will happen next, and can roll with the punches (however imperfectly!) when things didn't quite work out as you had hoped ....  and you're likely to smile and laugh when my hound hailey sneaks up behind you and shakes herself off after she goes swimming at crissy field...  then i have all the info i need to know about you for now :)  now if you can appreciate a man who:  is kind, gentle, gregarious, intelligent, self-aware, trustworty, understanding and forgiving almost to a fault, an exemplary and unique goofball, at times very heart-driven (i make my decisions rather slanted to my heart's instincts, my passionate side is one of my greatest gifts, and when it grabs hold of the accelerator, it can be something to behold, (especially if you're the object of it :), and an attentive and loyal companion and friend...  can fix most anything, is curious, a great problem solver, has the  patience to coach a 7-year-old soccer team (this takes extraordinary skill, mind you :), and versatile enough to have been a firefighter-paramedic and be able to pivot to a career in digital marketing when times got tough...  is aware that he has his own imperfections and is mindful enough to at least make an effort to shift them...  and likes few things more than hosting dinner parties for some of the best friends anyone could ever possibly have...  then i'd say you and i should cut to the chase and have coffee - there's at least a friendly connection going on here!!!!  cheers everyone, hope all of you are doing well!!  john i kicked the new career off less than a year ago and while blazing into a lot of unknowns has its challenges, i wouldn't have it any other way. so much to learn, so much opportunity, and i feel very fortunate to have the blend of skills and that i have and the opportunity to watch this process unfold. as i look back, my path has been a total trip!  i spend tons of time with my close friends, many of whom i've known since i was a little guy. we hike the city, eat, camp, do sunday dim sum, have regular dinner parties, daylong road trips, family gatherings, picnics - you name it.  i recently picked up a very capable camera and am having a ball learning how to use this thing. i definitely hope there someone out there that wants to enjoy a nice marin picnic and hike and doesn't mind being one of my test subjects :)  and of late i'm jonesing to pack up the bike and roll someplace green and chill out for a while. self-teaching on a variety of stuff i know nothing about, talented diy tinkerer (digging my new diy pot/utensil racks) and very experienced home improvement whiz and a fastidious expert at interior detail work (good with interoir colors - exterior colors - meh... :). maintaining a core group of close friends through the decades that are as close as family can get. i have no bro's or sisters, so my close friends fill that role (some since age 5), and i'm considered part of their extended families too, which means i have to eat 2-3 different dinners on thanksgiving day. and since we're talking about things i'm good at, i have distinct talent about getting myself invited over for dinner. truly gifted :) pretty decent cook, too (i can pump out 25 meals for dinner parties with only a bit of stress, although the kitchen is a bloody disaster!)... i've never asked to be honest, so it calls on me to try and crawl into another person's head for their first impression. i think warm, sincere, and gregarious would be in the full_bio: i'm an east coast transplant and i love living in the mission. i'm a lawyer (but in the good way, or at least i think so), a sister, a singer. i love to read. my friends tell me i'm outgoing, and i certainly do wind up talking to a lot of strangers. i also really really like sleeping - naps, nighttime, you name it. i love exploring san francisco, and i just got a bike, so lately i've been exploring on wheels. i can't decide if i'm an optimist or just a cheerful pessimist. i like picnics and i like canning (mostly jam, haven't yet gotten into pickles but i'm pretty sure that's next). farmers markets make me happy. i follow the red sox a little obsessively. i like fall and spring the best, and i miss winter. proper winter, i mean; not four months of rain. during daylight hours, the law. lots of stretching, unrelated to the law. building my community. cultivating my garden, in the classical sense. baking, cooking, reading, singing no clue. maybe i'll ask you that when we meet.  (see what i did there?) i ... hmm. i read a lot, i listen to a lot of music, and i do love me some food. this one's hard.  music: girlyman, the new pornographers, the nields, indigo girls, ok go, fountains of wayne, belle & sebastian, scissor sisters, the apples in stereo  books: laurie colwin, richard powers, chaim potok, gloria naylor, and haruki murakami are my favorite authors. i just have a lot of books, as i rediscovered when i moved last year.  food: mmm, i like food a lot. i'm up for just about any kind of food, except lima beans. i live in the mission, so burritos are high on my list; i also really like sushi and ethiopian food.  what am i going to make for dinner?  where do all these moths keep coming from?  how did that snail get into my house? singing and then eating with friends. i also shot a man in reno, but it wasn't to watch him die. it was for... other issues. you can tell me a good joke.

full_bio: hi! i'm sean. i live in the lake merritt area of oakland in a nice little studio and work in sf. i operate on a level of awesomeness not previously observed in this galaxy. currently, i'm on the lookout for people to share this with, as i feel i'm depriving the world of something terrific.  i have a very sarcastic sense of humor and like to make a lot of wisecracks. i'd like to think i'm somewhat clever and have a quick wit.  i love to camp and get outdoors. can't wait for the warm weather to see what kind of adventures i can get into.  i'm a sf giants/49ers fan.  i like dogs, cats and children. i just don't happen to have any of my own (and not really looking for any at the moment). i work doing it in sf on the regular 9-5. it's a good job and i enjoy it for the most part but have been working on expanding my horizons to other areas of expertise.  i've been getting into this whole fitness craze over the past few months. i go to the gym about 5-6 days a week and at first wasn't sure what kind of fitness goals i wanted to set for myself (other than being generally more healthy and in shape) but recently, i have been thinking about training to climb a mountain. the idea scares the hell out of me, which is exactly why i want to do it!  i also can't wait for the spring as i intend on getting my scuba certification. drawing - i keep an art blog and draw as a hobby (seansdoodles.blogspot.com).  talking - i can be kind of quiet/shy when i first meet people but after a bit, i'll keep on blabbing.  drinking whiskey without turning into a raging asshole. imagine that!  outdoor cooking - i wouldn't say i'm "really good" at it, but getting much better. i love to bbq. i just got a smoker and will be practicing with that, along with my dutch ovens (which i like to cook using the traditional method of using coals).  relaxing - i can make lounging look like a god damn art form. just give me a nice setting and watch the magic happen! :)  paying attention, making eye contact, can carry a conversation on subjects other than entertainment "news."  what i'm not good at - dancing. holy shit, i'm a pretty terrible dancer when you give me a fast beat ... but i'm really good at using my arm as an anchor to make sure the bar doesn't float away. height. i'm a tall guy.  skin tone. i'm fairly pale ... in an awesome, non-albino kind of way (i think the only thing i'm lacking is the pink eyes and pure white hair).  you might also notice that i'll look you in the eye when talking to you. it's amazing how few people do this these days. niel gaiman, sam harris, richard dawkins, heinlein, robert jordan, michael moorcock and others. when it comes to fiction, i'm a fan of sci-fi/fantasy mostly but i enjoy a wide variety of stories. been reading a lot of non-fiction lately. oh and the durt (the story of motley crue). great frickin book! i just started reading game of thrones. i'm already in love with this series.  while i love music, it doesn't define me in ways that i know a lot other people do. i like pretty much anything not pop or country, yet my music collection is almost non-existent. pandora is awesome and i have a number of stations loaded into it and have a number of select songs uploaded on my phone but nothing extensive. i have plenty of those friends who's brains i pick on occasion so i know what to look for. i love 80's music. big hair ftw!  i love movies. i love all kinds (except for musicals and rom-coms, for the most part). i don't make it to the little independent theaters that much but would love to check more out if something caught my eye.  as for tv: mad men, bsg, walking dead, iron chef, the office, the inbetweeners, the tudors, family guy, south park, daily show...  as far as food, i can be a picky eater but have been overcoming this. results vary. i love italian food (along with many other types of cuisine) and love to bbq ... and i make a pretty good homemade pizza. also homemade cinnamon rolls. they are pretty amazing when i get the motivation to make them. family/friends comedy - laughter makes the world go round camping a nice glass of scotch/beer sex you! (aaaaawwwwwwwwwwww) - see, i'm sweet as hell!! :) methods of self improvement.  how to cure boredom.  the universe and all of its wonders.  how people still don't understand the difference between there/their/they're or to/too/two. oh and if you spell lose as "l-o-o-s-e" just stop right now. we won't get along! my grammar is pretty terrible but a line must be drawn somewhere.  i also spend a lot of time thinking about traveling. the places i have been and the places i still need to see. usually at the gym. i occasionally meet up with old friends for a night of poker or just hanging out, but fridays lately have been pretty dead for me. this is where you come in! ;)  i usually save myself for the weekend and try to get out and see family/friends or go to a festival, farmers market, party, hiking. anything really that can get me outside ... though there are still those weekends where all i want to do is veg on the couch. i can touch the tip of my nose with my tongue... and i have a long nose. :d hell i don't know. i'm a pretty rad person, so you can start with that.  don't let these silly percentages go and predetermine whether we would get along or not. if you think you'd like to meet me based off what you see, then let me know. the worst that can happen is we find we disagree with each other on some points and can go our merry way. odds are, i'll still talk to you because well, as it says above: i'm pretty damn rad! i really enjoy meeting new people and would like to meet more people to be able to just hang out with from time to time.  if you want to go hiking in the oakland hills or kayaking on lake merritt (renting them is pretty cheap) or do any other activities, you should drop me a line. i love to get out and do new things.  if you're just looking for someone to hang out with and have some no-strings fun.

full_bio: i'm originally from the redwoods in northern california, and while i love the outdoors, i find the city to be a continually fascinating source of new people and ideas.  i like to read, and especially enjoy playing around in dolores on sunny days, hiking mt. tam, drinking any form of ale, working with my hands and going surfing. i'm just recently free of school and a boss, and happily running my own garden and landscape business, employing my friends and taking little vacations whenever i want.  not complaining, in other words ;) making banana pancakes. that i'm tall, and might possibly have a goofy grin on my face. so many favorite books, a few that stand out are lord of the rings, the things they carried, west of jesus, the sun also rises, ender's game.  top 5 music: red hot chili peppers, beatles, beautiful girls, pink floyd and arcade fire.  ohh food, i do adore thee. japanese & mexican food, crepes, biscuits and gravy, yam fries, buffalo burgers..i could go on. the ocean, my family, good sleep, books, summer fog and caffeine of some sort. shaping my present circumstances to bring about the future life i want to have taking it easy at home after work or having some dinner & beer with friends. i want to build & live in my own hobbit house. you'd like to :)

full_bio: so i realized i spelled my screen name wrong after i answered hella questions. so now i'm sticking with the misspelling. i am not actually a puppeteer.  i love wine (i used to work in the wine industry). i have a wine blog www.exploringwine.blogspot.com. i also enjoy microbrews and other forms of alcohol. i'm really fascinated by fermentations, distilling, and flavors.  i also enjoy camping, hiking, and nature in general. i'm big on traveling and experiencing cultures. i love photography. i appreciate art and enjoy going to museums, but could never be a starving artist type because i love food and being financially semi-stable.  i'm primarily a left brained but i am in touch with the right half. i usually prefer girls who are the opposite.  i'm an avid giants and sharks fan. i also like the 49ers, but its been hard to be an avid fan of them since the 90s. hey look at the niners this year... this is fun again! i'm working in biotech making enzyme therapies. its so nice to have a steady, relaxed job. i miss the wine industry, but stability is so worth it.  i gave up living life in the fast lane a while ago. now i'm off the highway and taking the scenic route. i pull over to enjoy the little moments that life offers up. as ferris said, "life moves pretty fast. if you don't stop and look around once in a while you can miss it."  i'm looking for a girl to hang out with: go to movies, explore the area, restaurants, and have adventures. i'm looking for something relaxed right now. if it develops into an ltr great, but if not that's ok too. i'm really trying to live in the moment and enjoy life. i've spent a lot of my time worrying about the future and my career and such. now i have a good career and i'm focusing on enjoying what i've worked so hard for. i want someone to enjoy it with, but i'm not trying to force anything or rush into things either. i hope that makes sense.  riding my new mountain bike all over marin! heck yeah! going on long hikes that don't cover much ground because i stop to take pictures too often. i'm also good at covering lots of ground quickly because its getting dark and that sunset was too beautiful to not watch from that vantage point (this happened on half dome and more recently on mt. tam.)  i also excel at not getting lost, directions, science, solving problems, random facts, seeing the dirty joke where most do not, and wine tasting.  i'm great at pairing wine, cocktails, or beer with tasty morsels. i'm not a tree. i'm just very tall. books: lamb, afrika zero, snow crash, dharma bums, the alchemist, the hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy, life of pi, ender's game and the ender's shadow series, short stories by steinbeck, ray bradbury, and various other authors.  tv: portlandia (i'm 5 min in and i can't stop laughing) mad men, entourage, dexter, futurama, athf, family guy, south park, its always sunny in philadelphia, big bang theory, boardwalk empire, breaking bad, and walking dead.  movies: super troopers, district 9, pulp fiction, memento, super bad, juno, star wars original episodes, also movies with twists, and various other comedies and epic movies that i can't think of right now.  foods: indian, thai, mexican, italian, bbq, and various others made with high quality ingredients. i also love wine and microbrews. btw don't be intimidated by my love of wine. i also enjoy teaching people about wine so its ok if you don't like it or don't know anything about it. there are so many kinds of wine that there is bound to be something you like (even if i don't).  music: i'm all over the place with music. i mostly listen to rock (a perfect circle, mumford and sons), blues, jazz (ella fitzgerald), hip hop (heiroglyphics, atmosphere), and house music. i also listen to country and various over genres and sub genres depending on my mood. i usually don't prefer pop music. i could do without a lot of things so here is the six things i would prefer to have on a regular basis: great food wine/beer/cocktails a wonderful woman by my side a cat or a really cool dog (no mediocre dogs) hiking a job that i enjoy  i currently have 4/6. no pets in this apartment. cycling, wine writing, and work. on a typical friday night i am getting ready for a short work week. i work sat-mon. in three 12 hour shifts. have 4 days off is pretty excellent it just sucks that they have to be weekdays. i occasionally stutter.  i saw a couple stats on here recently and i'm really surprised that more people on ok cupid have played dungeons and dragons (40%) than like the taste of beer (32%).  i don't understand the trend of exchanging hella messages before you meet someone. sure there are some creeps on here, but i doubt it is the majority. also, do the creeps become apparent from a yahoo chat or ongoing messages? in my friends' experiences you pretty much know from the first message or phone call. you are a chill, positive person. you enjoy laughing and are able to laugh at yourself. you have weekdays off. i work sat-mon. you aren't sure what you are looking for, but you are pretty sure it involves me. you think we'd be a good match regardless of our percentages because really a lot of those questions don't have much bearing on whether or not we could be happy together.

full_bio: hi, i'm jay. i'd like to take this moment and thank you for clicking on me! i'll try to make this a treat for you and give you some of the secrets inside of men on this website..  why are men on here? some are definitely looking to have short term fun. you know those weird guys who send you emails where they have good pics and misspell a little too much.  then there are guys like me who are here looking for that golden nugget in life. you know what i'm talking about ladies.. someone who i have emotional and physical chemistry with. we have all felt it before in life and it can't exactly be explained... but you know when it is there.. have any of you ladies had your profile for more than 6 months? well can i be the first to publicly say that guys who have had their profile a while do notice the other okcupid "lifers" here.  we often wonder why many of these "beautiful women" here with their sweetly written profiles are still single after all this time. i know we all have our stories and reasons but it would be refreshing if people shared a bit with us.  what is my reason? well just try asking me.. =)  i think online dating for men is somewhat of a numbers game..  i probably sign into this website a few times a week and don't really email that many people. i'd say i email less than 3 a month. the "power messenger" guys are usually the new ones hoping for any bite.  i know we all judge each other by our carefully selected pics but looks are "fluid." what really makes a woman beautiful is the way she talks, laughs, walks, wiggles her eyebrows and the way she looks at a man she likes. its hard to get that from a few still photos.  beauty also comes from the inner being. strip away everything and at the heart of it all is a soul that you either have chemistry with or you don't. i know there are many beautiful women on this website who don't get much of a chance because their still pics are not as good. well i'm the type of guy who can see through the smoke and mirrors to see what is important.  let's spend the time to see if its worth it.. i have a job where i am required to be an excellent conversationalist and at times give presentations to hundreds of people.  lets be blunt.. i love tennis.. playing it, teaching it and just being around it. if you are too then message me... =)  i'm also really good at being honest and not wasting people's time. if i go out with a girl and i don't have that chemistry with her i'll let her know. it goes back to that theory of doing the right thing. i've been on the other side of that and although i was a little upset at that time, i more so appreciated it because its better to end a bad lead than chase after one and waste my good energy.  i'm really good at keeping my house clean also. don't you gals love the good feeling of having a clean home? especially the bathroom! =) i strongly believe in manners and class... i've learned in my life that being a good person and being respectful to all people is good for my soul.  is chivalry dead? i don't think so. you hear a lot of guys complain that women go after "jerks" but i have a theory on this..  i don't think women are purposely going after jerks but rather they are going after men with masculine qualities who they subconsciously know that they cannot dominate.  its sad to say but some guys act like a "doormat" in a relationship and that very rarely turns a good woman on. if men don't act like they are masculine a woman will slowly be turned off over time. masculine qualities include giving and deserving respect, being appropriately funny, being chivalrous, adventurous, intelligent and being a good provider. here are some at random..  shawshank redemption dexter house hunters international david guetta how i met your mother the office seinfeld  random piano music (1) - the good feeling i get when i help someone who didn't ask me to. (2) - having a clean home. (3) - my ability to read people's emotions accurately. (4) - weekly chats with family. (5) - trying new restaurants. (6) - my morning coffee. =) where life will take me.  i have a stereotypical "standard life" but the inner side of me wants to take a crazy life turn someday. there is more to life than my beautiful city.. i love it here.. but when its my time someday i'd like to leave the earth with no regrets. sometimes out, or sometimes genuinely enjoying "me time" at home. i took a salsa lesson one time and really liked it. you're fun to be around.  or you would like to have a nice conversation...

full_bio: as usual, someone else has said it better first: "i am large, i contain multitudes." -walt whitman  i'm a multifaceted person (aren't we all?) who's put a lot of time and energy into a lengthy education, but can be surprisingly "basic" as well. i'm finally approaching the end of a seemingly interminable education/training process, and am looking to expand my horizons. listening. for better or worse, quite a few people have told me how easy it is to talk to me, both personally and professionally. -a dry sense of humor (it gets zanier the longer you know me) -a kind disposition (that hides more impatience than i'd care to admit) -my eyes (in part due to a varied appearance; i usually check "hazel" under color options) caveat to all: i have pretty eclectic tastes, so these are just "samples"...  books: -run with the horsemen by ferrol sams (favorite) -the civil war: a narrative by shelby foote -tom clancy (early works he actually wrote) -traffic by tom vanderbilt -the house of god by samuel shem  movies: -the shawshank redemption (favorite) -monty python (mainly holy grail & life of brian) -star wars (the original, untampered trilogy) -dead poets society -notting hill (yeah, i said it. what?!?)  shows: -scrubs (favorite) -law & order -cbs sunday morning -er -duck dynasty  music: here's my main pandora channels: -brad paisley radio -michael buble radio -u2 radio -the black crowes radio -kmdy lives! (stand up comedy)  food: just about anything that's edible, and some things that toe the line. 1)soccer (playing and watching). 2)good books, new & old. 3)laughter. 4)music. 5)my family's support. 6)new challenges (e.g. guitar). everything. in truth, that's not always a good thing, because it can suppress my spontaneous side, but it does mean that i don't make decisions lightly. anything but typical. if i do go to a bar, it's more for games (pool, darts, etc.) than drinking. if there's a new movie out, i'm probably at home watching a dvd. and if it's perfect out and the town is hopping...then i'm probably at work. (looking forward to when the last one is no longer true) that for all i've accomplished to this point, i could have accomplished so much more given my opportunities and abilities. any of the above strikes a chord!

full_bio: hello! where to begin? i am a graduate student at academy of art university in illustration, a major i also graduated from in my undergraduate at mass art in boston, ma. originally i am from the east coast, and my family lineage is mainly european by way of spain :) and other western european countries. i spend almost all of my time on art and expanding my artistic horizons- i love to laugh, and i make jokes often. i want to be a concept artist and graphic novelist after i graduate. also, i make puppets!  i am a passionate and loyal person with a love of all things art and animal related. i love science fiction, fantasy, traveling, theatre/musicals, and going on adventures! i am also incredibly liberal. i am optimistic, but protected by the shell of a cynic.  climb the mountain because it is there :d  i am a vegetarian, i may even go vegan someday.  myers-briggs enfj i have a passion for travel, animals, and the arts, in all their forms. i am currently in the graduate program of illustration at academy of art university, with ambitions in the graphic novel and concept art fields. i would love to explore the planet throughout these ambitions, and to have great stories and adventures therein! art, illustration, sculpture, storytelling, writing, painting, history, language, culture, zoology, exploring, comedy, hiking, cycling, puppetry im a big, tall guy, and i wear ponchos- i get them from spain, which is where my family is from. people tell me my short beard reminds them of wolverine. i love to laugh and make jokes, and i am a gentle giant. i also really like dogs :p too many to list, really, but:  top 5 books: hitchhikers guide, don quixote, ender's game, blacksad, invincible  top 4 music: rem, bare-naked ladies, blue man group, enter the haggis and international bands (mainly rock and indie)  top 5 movies: 1967 rex harrisson dr. dolittle, district 9, star(wars/trek), and my disney representative is the jungle book  top 7 shows: house md, the daily show/colbert report, south park, the office, futurama, robot chicken, game of thrones  discontinued shows: crocodile hunter, the muppet show, babylon 5, firefly, battlestar galactica (and anything put on by the animal planet, discovery channel, bbc or ted)  top 5 foods: vegetarian paella, empanadas, soy(milk, tofu etc.), peanut butter, and fruit art, animals, fruit, underwear, science, sketchbooks humanity, evolution, stories, philosophy, sometimes politics, history, culture, zoology working on artwork. i'm not a party animal. i'm always ready to try new things. you'll have to get to know me ;) you like adventures, science fiction, the arts, animals of all kinds, travel, nature, robots, and jokes/humour  if okc isnt where you prefer to chat, i also have an im account (invadertorc) feel free to chat me up there :)  so i must admit i was nervous to make an okcupid account- a friend of mine talked me into giving it a try. im looking for a relationship, but even despite this im always ready to meet people and make friends. so chat me up :d

sign: scorpio and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: to answer the question at hand, i am just living it i suppose. there are things i am doing with it, like making movies, learning new skills, traveling, meeting new people. i want to do, all the time. i never liked being in one place for too long, that could be for years, or just a nice trip for the weekend, but i always end up in a new place eventually. wanderlust is very comfortable for me.  if you enjoy short-long adventures of camping, road trips (key to my heart, i swear) drives down or up the coast, then we will get along great.  i'm getting back into reading after a long absence, and now i can't remember what distracted me from it...(oh yeah, it was 3 years in nyc) learning how to take apart and put back together my motorcycle. rock climbing and getting into better physical shape (not that it was bad to begin with, just making it better)  i feel like my interests have been on pause for way too long, and the flood gates are open. if you have new things to show me, show me, i am down to explore with you.  update* currently i've gotten involved with maybe one too many projects. so i might be a little preoccupied at the moment  between the fixing up of my first motorcycle, producing my next film, and trying to figure out how to travel with my father to china in the fall...i would love to meet someone who could ground me :) getting people to open up, i have something of a disarming demeanor.  i'm a great listener...or people have told me i am.  making you feel special.  making a fool of myself to entertain others...this includes funny voices, streaking in public, there isn't a lot i won't do to make people laugh.  as a continuation of that, i really love helping people, and being that shoulder to cry on so to speak. i do it almost to a fault as i have been taken advantage of in the past because of this. but it is in my nature, i'm just more cautious now...  untying knots...and tying people in them ;)  and finding beautiful places to stand in awe of...preferably with a glass of really nice, peaty whiskey...poured neat of course ;)  giving movie recommendations...i have see everything and i will find something you like, dammnit :p my eyes...and eye lashes (they're really long) my tattoos my voice my height (tall) my big hands  my general sense of ease... music: the devil makes three, johnny cash, deftones, nin, bob dylan, explosions in the sky, grunge (you know the bands) the gutter twins, jeff buckley, nina simone, kid cudi, tom waits, van morrison, the clash, crosses (+++), david bowie, fiona apple, nick cave and the bad seeds, operation ivy, rancid, ac/dc, rolling stones, beach boys, tvontheradio...just for starters. *i prefer vinyl, but they're really difficult to listen to on the go :p  movies: there are so many...i almost don't even want to start this list...but here goes a little taste: batman. there will be blood. the shawshank redemption. dirty rotten scoundrels. alien. blade runner. unforgiven. the man who shot liberty valance. the proposition. the wild bunch. city of lost children. chinatown. edward scissorhands. all the real girls. the mist. gaslight. north by northwest. jaws. the gold rush. magnolia. the thin red line. old school. animal house. small change. breaking the waves. dancer in the dark...pretty much anything by kubrick, pt anderson, lars von trier. i prefer todd solondz to wes anderson *i love actual 35mm film, but projectors and reels are pretty expensive....i guess bluray will do :/  books: huckleberry finn, american psycho, all nick hornby, the giver, catcher in the rye, edgar allen poe, a song of ice and fire, karouac, jim thompson, hunter s. thompson. the dark tower series. i am starting to get into cormac mccarthy after years of hating "all the pretty horses" since i read it in high school. currently i'm pretty deep into "child of god" which is kinda blowing me away.  i always read an actual book, i can't do the nook, kindle thing (although the kindle fire looked like i could change my mind)  tv: i have mixed feelings on television, but these shows have stuck with me. the shield. twin peaks. deadwood. the wire. dexter. mi:5. friday night lights. luther. blue planet. six feet under. entourage. star trek: the next generation. the simpsons. batman:the animated series 1. the ocean... 2. movies... 3. my best friends 4. family 5. my portable communication device...it has my music whenever i need it, and can contact my friends whenever i need them. 6. sex. yes, cliche i know but seriously it's pretty awesome and really fun to do.  6 runners up cause lets face it, six is a pretty small number when you're dealing with things you "could never do without"  1. good whiskey 2. warm night sunsets....this should probably be in the main 6 choices 3.a really good book, with my lover/partner's head resting in my lap with her own book. (wow, i even cheesed myself out with that one...so now might be a good time to admit i am a bit of a hopeless romantic at heart) 4. a good pair of shoes (yeah thats right...we care about them too :p) 5. laughter 6. *to be decided after i meet you and you show me something new that i could possibly put in this spot. where to travel next, i got the bug after going to south america last year.. what new activities i can find...rock climbing being the latest. what my next film project will be. i write and direct, its my true passion in life. how to get paid for giving my opinion :p (i actually know how, but its hard to put in practice since i can't seem to sit still) whether or whether not to move to la... right now...working :/ once...i picked my nose...and i put it in my mouth...i am not proud of this. i don't like wes anderson films, but i'm willing to try and be convinced otherwise. if you love being near the water.  or just love being outside, for hikes or camping, road trips.  you want to show me the place or thing that really makes you geek out, that thing that just reverts you back to your ten year old self...i love that stuff.  you know who you are, at least in this moment. you're kind, interested in staying in, just as much as going out.  you want to take it slow...hang out as friends and just grow from there. my last relationship left me a little guarded but i'm working on knocking down my walls.  or if you're interested in a fun casual summer fling, which might be better considering mine and i'm sure many others busy schedules. i'm not exactly looking for "the one"...know what i mean.  *i put casual sex in the "looking for" section because i'm open to all kinds of relationships, not because i'm just looking to hook up. i'm also very sexually explorative, including bdsm dynamics.

sign: aries and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i know a lot about little and a little bit about everything. i am a connoisseur of many things, from film to food to art. i am very interested in pop culture, but not the water-cooler kind. i am both honest and a student of euphemism. i make myself laugh and will often share. i am kind, curious, adventurous, subversively irreverent, unusual, both dynamic and subtle. i am a compulsive complimenter, very passionate, and open-minded; even if it isn't my thing i can appreciate it. i am a masculine guy with some refinements (metrosexual not only doesn't quite fit, but is sooo 2003). my musical tastes are varied and particular; lots of electronic music, bjork, radiohead, cibo matto, del tha funkee homosapien, etc, etc. when alone or -after time and trust- with company, i will sing in the car loudly and make no apologies.  i am unusual, intellectual, and hilarious loving my friends -- and still astounded and humbly grateful that they're in my life -- and all the bay area has to offer; conversing and expanding my social circle and intellect; learning how to stride the middle path. i am newly introduced and thriving wildly in polyamory, a community of people open to sharing love and sensuality. though i am in committed relationships with more than one person, none are "primary." i am very open to finding a partner with whom i can share everything life brings. that being said, i have no expectations of this site nor any connections i make; it is what it is, and i want to enjoy whatever comes my way. honest bullshitting. being both analytical and sympathetic. interpreting and fulfilling needs. lately my charming collection of t-shirts... other than that, my smile, my blue eyes (tee-hee), my expression -- sometimes aloof, sometimes warm and inviting. oh, and the goatee's popular. food: yes. honestly, there used to be a list of three foods i don't like to eat... the only one remaining is natto (stricken are durian and tripe - i will give them more chances), and even that i can tolerate (a friend once said "it tastes like beer," and with that thought i can convince myself). i am an absurdly adventurous eater, love to cook and create, love to experience new gustatory delights and libations. if i haven't had it, i'll want to try it.  movies that are important to me: zentropa, buckaroo banzai, the neverending story (potentially ruined by adulthood), city of lost children, delicatessen, willy wonka (the gene wilder one, don't get me started), johnny dangerously, pi, spirited away and princess mononoke, pootie tang, waiting for guffman, time bandits and baron munchausen, casino royale (david niven and woody allen), fight club, kids in the hall: brain candy, the dark crystal, contact, el mariachi, donnie darko, district 9... film is very important to me, in my blood even.  tv shows (i recently dropped cable and am relying on my appletv and hulu... so far, not as crappy as i thought): no reservations, 30 rock, top gear (so pretty), the daily show & colbert, 24, chuck... you know, writng a (longer) list of these really makes me wonder how i get any work done or have a social life. i am an encyclopedia of monty python's tv episodes.  books: honestly i don't read enough, though i come off as very literary... whatever that means nowadays. i love mark leyner, palahniuk, dostoevsky, vonnegut, allende, and sedaris sedaris sedaris!  music: i grew up in new york in the late 70's/early 80's; so the cars, the rolling stones, blondie and kraftwerk are a part of my identity. my musical tastes are varied and particular... they might be giants practically saved my life in middle/high school. i would have given my freedom to be a manservant to bjork on her private island, if she weren't married to that asshole (who i know isn't as much of an asshole as he comes across). i'm newly loving muse and chromeo, and still loving daft punk, cibo matto, aphex twin, del tha funkee homosapien (though i've never seen him live), radiohead... a lot, let's leave it at that. love things well-crafted/meticulous/precious humor humility sensuality indecision. thinking -- as in, techniques to calm my racing intellect. sex. culture. food. ecology. women (which is separate from the sex-thing, but not entirely). art (comes with the job). logistics. people. out for drinks with friends, hopefully meeting new people and potential friends. don't tempt me... so i've found the below, and am just too lazy this saturday morn to revise: i'm looking to be friends first with a humble know-it-all with very few boundaries; someone with a mind as clever and naughty as mine. someone who enjoys non-sequiturs and minutiae, but likes to talk about the grand concepts as well.

religion: atheism but not too serious about it
full_bio: relocated midwesterner, now living in sf. i am lax, facetious, and hating on long nails. pretty big infp, if that means anything. like to think i'm a pretty silly dude with attitude. mostly working, but trying to take in as much as i can of what this city has to offer. recognizing famous people's mugs. people watching. riding it out. movie seeing. time spending. secret judging. thinking about places. my diploma claims i write pretty well. my scots-irish eyes and japanese wit. anything i can put on a list.  i don't read as much i used to/should, but i really enjoy hemingway. currently reading la confidential at a snail's pace.  also haven't been seeing too many movies lately, although that's more a function of my living situation than anything else. just a few of my favorites would be goodfellas, city of god, eternal sunshine, chinatown, discreet charm of the bourgeoisie, annie hall and airplane!. i have a high tolerance for schlock, as well as art-house cinema, and anything that falls in between.  i don't watch a whole lot of tv, but i've been known to dabble. arrested development, the wire and deadwood are probably the three greatest series i've seen.  my musical tastes are varied. just about anything from air to zapp and almost every genre in between. just not pop country, new age or tween pop.  food - sushi, all other seafood, red meat, white meat, street meat, green vegetables, red vegetables, orange, yellow, brown and purple vegetables too, starches, brans, cheeses, mushrooms (and whatever food group they fall into), soup. so long as it isn't rotten, made of bugs or glass, odds are very high i'll eat it. for the sake of adventure, i'd set aside those last three rules should the proper opportunity arise. nail clippers cinema irreverence cleveland-based sports teams fantasy sports (really) arrested development sports. film. music. vinyl. a lot of impossible things. whether or not my car got a ticket for sitting in the street a week. french stuff. in better spirits than on a typical monday night. maybe at a bar, hanging out at home with my roommates/friends, or at a concert. i'm prone to judging people based on their taste in mass media. and i'm a little shallow. first impressions, you know?  i'm a lot more fidgety than i let on. you'd like me to message you back, or want to hang out in the park on a sunny afternoon.

religion: agnosticism and somewhat serious about it
full_bio: making my way as a freelance writer, and currently earning big bucks as a movie and tv critic....ok, the bucks aren't that big, but i do get to go to free movie screenings and spout off about them for some minor bucks, so i'm not complaining.  i was born and raised in san francisco, went to school with some people who are now pretty famous, and love to bitch about how things used to be better, and why'd they change that?, and man, this place *used* to be awesome. which doesn't mean i hate s.f. i love it! i just kinda miss that old mel's that used to be at the corner of van ness and mission, and monkey island at the zoo, and playland at the beach. (ok. they tore playland down when i was about two, but still! i bet it was amazing!)  i like to put on the fancy and go out for cocktails, and will slow dance as long as you can lead well, because otherwise i kind of suck at it.  i also love old cars, vintage clothes, thrift stores, and spontaneous road trips. maintaining an even level of happiness, with occasional highs and lows thrown in to keep it interesting. playing "more than a feeling" on guitar hero. i have no idea, really. but the first time i introduce myself to someone they usually ask me to repeat my name, and then ask me how i spell it. so many...so, so many. i have come to the realization that i would have a lot more room in my apartment if i didn't love books and movies so much. so many bookcases!!!  to name a few...  boogie nights, petulia, out of sight, taxi driver, rushmore, rosemary's baby, star wars, blow-up, cat people, the thing, o brother where art thou, bringing up baby, shriek of the mutilated  lolita, tess of the d'urbervilles, jimmy corrigan the smartest kid on earth, all david sedaris....  mad men, true blood, 30 rock, community, arrested development, lost, breaking bad, louie, and yes, ok, jersey shore.  food-wise, i like just about anything, but will admit to being a bit of a wimp when it comes to spiciness. that means i tend to stay away from indian food... tivo my library card lip gloss a comfortable bed oxygen food what i have to do next... either out with friends for dinner or drinks, or home enjoying some bad tv. i hate to talk on the phone, and avoid it as much as i can... you can make me laugh. make me laugh, and i am putty in your hands.

full_bio: i'm a culture snob who sometimes finds himself watching hours of dexter, appreciates things done well but loves to dance horrendously, and revels in other contradictions. i'm very serious and don't think anything is worthy of being taken seriously except animals, children, emotions and anything that brings joy. etc... i write fiction and was recently working as a mental health counselor, but i need a break from schizophrenics, much as i grew to love them. i swim, do yoga, cook for friends, go to museums and the beach and on hikes and to the bar. listening to people or getting so caught up in what i'm saying that i forget other people exist. i think (hope) more so the former these days. he opens the door for a lady, but what's with that devilish little grin?... i don't know, i've been surprised, somewhat pleasantly, by how off my first impressions can be and i try to make people feel comfortable so we can figure if we get along and on what level. it's got nothing to do with any one person, just chemistry, so we should try to be as relaxed as possible, no? books: louis-ferdinand celine, henry miller, roberto bolano, wg sebald, dante, dostoevsky, nicannor parra and crime novels. other: wittgenstein and the visual arts (and duchamp). movies: snooty films usually of european origin, fellini, cassavettes and romantic comedies are my tub of ice cream when i'm sad. shows: modern dance, for me, is either the greatest or most truly god awful thing ever. i like beckett more than is probably healthy. music: everything from glenn gould doing bach to james brown to fugazi to whatever the kids are listening to these days. and bob dylan except that decade he was on coke and christianity. food: all of it and a lot of it. i like to strictly follow italian recipes or whip together the old random ingredient stir-fry. love, friends, culture, nature, creativity, sex (in varying order depending on the current phase of my life). settling down and having kids or running off to another foreign country. actually, i spend far too much time writing and i really try not to think about writing when i'm not writing, but i fail miserably.  i lived in new york and barcelona and have travelled through asia, the middle east and africa, but things are a bit more low-key and focused now, which i like. my life has always been interesting (to me, that is), and i just don't try so hard anymore. i can come off as a whirlwind of energy and opinion, but i'm much more interested in everything except myself and i pretty much just want to walk through a park or museum, have a conversation, laugh and smile (if i'm not writing or, let's be honest here, having amazing sex). you are open to whatever might happen when two people meet, granted they respect each other.

full_bio: on weekends, you can find me checking out all sorts of live music (i'm a big stern grove fan), sampling new restaurants, hiking or wine tasting. my friends describe me as warm and easy to talk to. when i piss them off, they describe me as fiercely independent and tease me relentlessly for being the only person to laugh at my own jokes. i reference seinfeld at least once a week. i love the type of traveling that brings out my adventurous spirit (riding a camel in the sahara, white water rafting, cruising on the amazon river). between fabulous friends and family, and a growing career, my life is just about complete. my main focus right now is work. i have a new small business that takes up the vast majority of my life.  i spend a lot of time with my friends and family, especially my 2 year old niece. i try to enjoy all the the bay area has to offer.      having pizza and drinks with my closest friends.

full_bio: let's see...i'm from the uk originally, london, and have been in sf for 12+ years. the bay area is a great corner of the world, there's so much nearby. i currently live north of the panhandle, and was in the mission before that. i like to run or swim in the mornings when i can, get some energy going. i love to listen to music, go on long walks, drink coffee, kick back with a good film or book, get a beer or cocktail out, take in a baseball or basketball game. i work in downtown sf as an i.t. ops manager, have been at my current place 5+ years. it's fast-paced and dynamic which i like. i like to keep fit, running and swimming, some weights. walking with music and coffee is great, i try and bike to work when i can. i love to get out of town, marin, the east bay or somewhere at the weekends. further afield is good too, i've been to vancouver, puerto vallarta, and andalucia, spain in the last few years. i have friends in japan and australia i want to visit sometime soon. listening, making drinks, sleeping, writing letters. my blue eyes. books - lord of the flies, raymond carver's short stories, atomized by michel houellebecq, mrs dalloway, one hundred years of solitude, midnight's children, tropic of cancer.  films...i love risky business, there i said it :) harold and maude, the stalker by tarkovsky, the usual suspects, vertigo, the english patient, the towering inferno.  music - lots. kate bush, azure ray, mandalay, american music club, cocteau twins, everything but the girl, florence and the machine, the sundays, house music to run to, classical to relax to sometimes. sun, walks, music, sleep, memories, reading. x's and o's. going for a drink with friends, catching up on the week's news, getting ready for the weekend.  you like what you've read so far.
