full_bio: hi! my name is joshua.  i'm a tall gaysian dude and on any given night, you'll probably find me photographing the amazing people that i know and love, having fun in the castro on weeknights (when drinks are super cheap & it's not crazy crowded), or attending up/downscale events (preferably with free food and open bar).  great hygiene is a+ and a must (especially, stellar oral hygiene).  people think i'm mean when they first meet me, but after a while, realize that i'm always just kidding around, and in reality, i'm just perfecting my standup comedy material on my friends.  i love shallow, "lame", mainstream pop culture stuff, and i am 100% proud of it! i have an 8:30-5 that pays the bills and gives me the allowance to do what i want to do with my life outside of that time period, so i really can't, and don't, complain too much. bringing out the best in people.  and... sometimes the worst in people.  books:  probably whatever ny times bestsellers are current & hot.  domestic, white women magazines (sunset magazine, budget travel, readymade (rip), better homes & gardens, bon appetit, real simple, real simple weddings, rachael ray magazine (don't hate! it has lots of useful info)) and just to balance it out, esquire.  nerdy fantasy stuff (naomi novik, ra salvatore, harry potter/jk rowling, brandon mull, suzanne collins, etc.)  movies:  i'm not a big movie watcher, but i do love: camp, mean girls, saved!, and precious: based on the novel "push" by sapphire.  also, i am super chicken when it comes to horror movies. i can't stand the heart palpitations!  music:  mariah carey (if you dislike her, don't bother)  90s r&b & pop music (i love being constantly reminded of random awesome songs from the 90s via facebook status updates or my mp3 shuffling on my winamp. yes, i still use winamp...)  food:  i will eat any kind of the nuts from trader joe's line of roasted & unsalted.  i put way too much accouterments in my coffee (cocoa, cinnamon, splenda, nonfat milk, sugar free peppermint syrup, etc.).  i grew up with an asian mom who had high blood pressure and crazy migraines, so i am totally used to bland food (everything was very low sodium, and no msg! surprise!!), but i do love a good flavor, but i usually find a lot of things too salty lol  i like to customize my orders at restaurants, much to the chagrin of my friends (what - i want to enjoy my meal!)  i love medium-fancy fruits, such as: pluots, casaba melons, philippine mangoes, papaya, piel-de-sapos, overripe dates, etc.  i can safely say that i am clinically addicted to sugar-free gum.  whether it's entirely too late to learn all the choreography to single ladies?? out and about, unless i'm being lazy, then at home, avoiding texts and calls!

full_bio: i'm a pie-in-the-sky adventurer basking in the big wow of the long now. i'm goal oriented, caring for the collective, and thinking of all the twists and turns that lie in the near future.  i consider myself eco-socially conscious, a people-power progressive. i subscribe to many communities and few ideologies.  i want worms to eat my garbage.  i love being outdoors: hiking, cycling, picnicking, bouldering, backpacking. i find rainy bicycle rides and ice cold rivers to be great for one's circulation, as long as there's hot springs or hot showers. some places in nature are made of tasty salad or medicinal tea. i want to learn more about the plants in these places.  i've been gently exploring ethical non-monogamy over the last couple years, finding that i best view relationships as held by connections, rather than held in containers. this forces me to examine the assumptions i have gained about relationships from parents / culture / etc and helps to achieve clarity and honesty within myself and with others.  i cuddle, stretch, and meditate, not at the same time.  i play guitar, mandolin, hand drums... i can't dance to canned music, but if its live and funky? i'm down. i am self-employed and loving it as a freelance graphic designer, illustrator, musicmaker, permaculturist... i live in a wacky house in the sunset. i am beginning to work on illustration arts a lot right now. i'm spending a lot of time playing with self-sufficiency skills + creative arts right now: gardening, carpentry, sewing, herbalism, music, poetry. california geography. we'll never get lost together, just lost in each other. "strange outfit. definitely your style." my favorite film is a scene where we're at my house finishing a bottle of red wine. you're singing hafiz to the home-brew fermenting in the closet while i accompany you on a steel-string guitar i rescued from the streets of east oakland. my housemate is cooking dinner in the other room: a huge raw vegan salad with a small cut of fish and organic brown rice on the side. nothing makes sense.  --  i'm currently reading buckminister fuller and listening to the bluegrass show on kfjc. big ups to leo kottke, joni, mason jennings, gillian, devendra, animal collective. i'm glad i read ishmael, the prophet, e.e. cummings, gandhi's memoir, be here now, and hope for the flowers.  films: la belle verte (http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c5cmmm_srpm); william kentridge (http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=szivwktmzz0); waking life. clean food, water, and air are the big three. then comes clothing at number four, but that may become less important as the climate gets wackier.  actually, i'm pretty sure that if you piled enough of us together, you'd solve the warmth part of the clothing issue, and probably the modesty piece of it too. i don't think i could ever do without togetherness, or a good blanket. maybe that puts clothing at #6.  on a more serious note, i am hope to never be without a sketchbook, a guitar, a cooking fire, good friends, high hopes and dreams, and an expansive wilderness at our doorstep. sustainability. urban planning and design. how we can reorganize society to increase ecological health and human happiness. how i can be more productive at the things i am good at and love to do most. that dream from two nights ago. getting a solid jam crew together to play music with. either i'm exhausted because i've been doing beautiful things all week and i'm going hiking with some folks in the morning, or i'm enjoying my favorite books, movies, shows, music, and food (see above). i lived in a psychedelic circus warehouse. i would do it again. you know who bread and puppet is. you listen to radio lab, but you'd never live in nyc. you are an infrequent participant in consumer/media culture. you need out of the city to get nature in your life, for days at a time. you could spend weeks there. you need to dig dirt, dance, sweat, sing, and breathe. you are car(e)-free. we'd bike there. you'd hitchhike again. even when you're back home, you're still in adventure mode. life is art, and art is cheap. hurrah!

full_bio: i have difficulty with internal dialogue so when i think i'm out of ear shot of another person i will talk out loud to myself because it helps me think. but your never as alone as you think you are and i have been caught talking to myself on many occasions. some times the subject matter is more embarrassing then others but there is always slight shame and curiosity to how much the other person heard.  i'm the worst speller you will ever meet. i'm dyslexic so i spent the majority of my public school education in learning disabled classes but they were nothing more then regular classes with extremely dumb down curriculum. this trained me at a young age that i wasn't very smart and to this day i feel the need to prove my intelligence in the eyes of others.  i was a bed-wetter in till my early twenties. i don't know if it is because i have a small blander or weak blander muscles but it provided me with many embarrassing moments. it was especially a problem after getting drunk.  i was sexually abused as a child and it seriously effected my sex life. i was a virgin in till my mid twenties because i had trouble getting or maintaining an erection when i was with a woman which i attribute to me being abused as a child. because of this i have had to live some what of a lie with all my friends to avoid questions and feel normal, a lie which still slightly remains today.  this is all true and i'm putting all this in my profile because i had an epiphany where i realized that the secrets that i have kept have not really protected me but rather kept me imprisoned to them. also i think it is pretty funny to tell complete internet strangers things about myself that not even my closest friends know. so enjoy it, i guarantee you will never read a profile like this.

full_bio: i love to be outside. i love to be active. i like to have fresh flowers on the kitchen table. i love love love to travel. i think humor is essential. i think sarah silverman makes the world better because she forces us to stop being so serious all the time. i believe in moderation. i think extremism (even the righteous kind) usually leads to intolerance. despite being gay and eating organic vegetables, i still think of myself as an old-fashioned midwesterner. i am not afraid to admit that spending a sunday afternoon tracking investments and contemplating passive income excites me. i am very good at seeing both sides to something. kindness is something i value greatly. i believe in trying hard. traveling whenever possible, deepening my yoga practice, being really good at my job, learning new obscure hobbies, teaching, eating my way through san francisco, etc, etc. multi-tasking empathy holding my breath underwater scrabble taking ridiculously long bubble baths making kids smile taking charge in a crisis laughing until it hurts logic puzzles making greeting cards with glitter pens ignoring most grammar rules you know, i have no idea. i've never asked. movies: the big blue, strangers with candy, serial mom, debs, romey and michelle's high school reunion, little miss sunshine, zoolander  food: fresh fresh seafood, peaches that are so juicy they burst all over your chin when you bite in and become impossibly sticky.  books: oh my, this could take ages. lets go with: 1st book i ever read: watership down. 1st book i underlined so much it glowed in the dark: written on the body. best scifi book: the golden compass my passport my computer feeling the ocean my parents open sky all my limbs how to make healthy food taste as good as unhealthy food wondering if i'll ever be able to meditate for more then 10 minutes where i'll go scuba diving next sipping wine, listening to music, subjecting friends to new recipes, talking about anything and everything. i don't like sushi. i may be the only person in san francisco who feels this way. the discovery of fire was essential to our human development. we should cook our fish in tribute. you think we would have interesting conversations. you think we could make each other laugh. you've just got the hankering to.

full_bio: likes: bacon; stars; compassion; thunderstorms; jews.  dislikes: automatic public toilets that flush before you finish; meter maids; tsa workers; phlebotomy. i just finished my mfa in creative writing. i'm still working on publishing my thesis. when i'm not working, i'm usually nesting, reading, watching movies, etc. sometimes, i drive around. every now and then, i'll rescue a dying bird and nurse it back to health. not really. reading between the lines; standing around at parties, looking cool while holding a cup; chess; spotting the short lines for the check-out at target; using semicolons when serial commas would clearly suffice.  books: candide; naked lunch; the stranger; winter of our discontent; white noise; blue of noon.  movies: annie hall; the royal tenenbaums; trainspotting; the big lebowski; there will be blood; magnolia; eternal sunshine of the spotless mind; the squid & the whale.  tv shows: curb your enthusiasm; flight of the conchords; game of thrones; the adventures of pete & pete; freaks & geeks.  music: the caretaker; sonic youth; the dandy warhols; joy division; the kinks; wham!  food: carpaccio; tacos; cookie crisp; bacon. nasal spray; glasses; lens wipe; book; sleep; something to write with. my hilarious childhood, the constant war in my head between passion and practicality. sneaking up behind evil henchmen, snapping their necks, and catching them as they fall. when there's one slice of pizza left, i never take it, but i always want to. you got legs, and you know how to use them.

(curse you, formally polite hoosier upbringing!) i'll get over it eventually. but even though i don't wear it on my sleeve, i do in fact pay a lot of attention to physical chemistry. ...you know me in real life (or you ought to!). also, if you don't mind that i'll take a while to get back to you. also, if you noticed that i'm seeing someone: i've got the romance base covered, thanks. just out for new friends!

full_bio: when i get homesick, i listen to classical music because i miss my cello. right now, i'm obsessed with idyll mvt 7 by leon janacek. it has a really passionate middle section (with a bitchin' divided cello part at said passionate part).  let's see, i'm a uc berkeley student and proud of it. i'm an avid atheist, but i'm (or i like to think that i'm) not one of those rude ones. everyone's entitled to his or her beliefs, and i definitely have mine too.  i'm a huge nerd in nearly every way, i'm an aspiring computer science major, i absolutely love web designing (the intricacies of css and html, oh! so beautiful!), i play a cello and, if i live for anything, it is to write. oh, and i love 420 to pieces! i'm a computer nerd!  i'm doing national novel writing month for the fourth year, and i have a good feeling that this is the year that i am going to complete a novel that i actually like...or, it was until midterms killed me. and the fact that the first four chapters of part two, which is just scene setting after some shit goes down, is 13000+words. this is going to be a long, long book. i am excite. writing: this is all i do with my life besides school. i live, breathe, bleed words. seriously, if you cut my arm open, words would come out instead of blood. there isn't much that i genuinely care for--something that my life would be nothing without. writing is that one thing that keeps me living even when i want to give up. it is my soul, my single lover, always there for me, always waiting for me to open my third eye once again and let her in. needless to say, i'm a bit obsessed with writing. it's the only thing that i care about more than myself, my friends, my whole entire world.  coding: oh god, i love coding. there's just something about opening up a blank .txt document and seeing what happens in the course of a couple hours. of course, i don't get to code until i spend a few hours with photoshop, making some (hopefully) pretty design. and that feeling when i finally change the ending from .txt to .php and open it up. oh, it's the best! it's indescribable, because i made that, even if nobody else cares for it. uh, i don't know. probably my chest. front by erich maria remarque, harry potter series <3  tv: star trek (tos and tng are definitely my favorites, but i'm one of those geeks that's seen them all), doctor who (tennant ftw), torchwood, dexter, firefly, dexter, queer as folk, skins, game of thrones, fullmetal alchemist: brotherhood, cowboy bebop, samurai champloo, lie to me, arrested development, community, modern family, the guild, house, battlestar galactica  movies: little miss sunshine, star trek ii: the wrath of khan, star trek (2009), sherlock holmes, deathproof, pulp fiction, dazed and confused, fear and loathing in las vegas, rolling kansas, mulan, forgetting sarah marshall, harry potter, inception, star wars iv, curse of the golden flower  music: muse, the flaming lips, cake, portishead, portugal. the man, arctic monkeys, the strokes, interpol, gorillaz, mgmt, spoon, tv on the radio, sondre lerche, stamps, the format, fun., imogen heap, johnny cash, yoko kanno, daft punk, beirut, the bird and the bee, two door future operating system, and the crazy characters in my novel. they never leave my mind to just my thoughts. either cooped up in my room playing video games, watching tv or writing  or  i'm getting my party on. sometimes i get the party on with myself too (...not in a weird way!). i have pi tatooed on my left wrist, and i plan to get phi on my right for my birthday.  most of my family thinks that i brain and my mind two different entities: my brain is me, my mind is everything that my writing and feelings encompass. you want some 420 friends! you like to write and want to bounce ideas off of me. full_bio: if i can be out-witted, you win. i am the type of person who thinks outside the "buns". if i know what i want i will go after it with my heart- i tend to be too sarcastic at times, but why be so serious all the time? i love challenges - like running the lyon street stairs on a sunday afternoon. i crave spontaneity -- like dressing up randomly with my friends for santa con and am constantly planning theme parties on a wim. but despite my magnetism towards energy and excitement, there's a part of me that is completely happy with sitting in an sf park all day, reading the alchemist, enjoying some wine, and maybe even wearing a wig.    the avett brothers, edward sharp and the magnetic zeros, albert hammond jr, the xx, alexander, regina spektor, brett dennen, mumford & sons, tuesdays with morrie, celestine prophecy, curb your enthusiasm, seinfeld, californiacation, always sunny in philadelphia

sign: virgo and it matters a lot
full_bio: i am comforted by sorting things into boxes, both real and imaginary; dishes composed largely of seafood and rice; and nesting in bed in the middle of the day.  things that make me happy without fail include brilliantly colored abstract paintings by the likes of mark rothko, morris louis, and wassily kandinsky; foraging for wild edible mushrooms and plants with my two boxers at my side; and making kids laugh. oh, and anything gratuitously gory like zombie flicks or mma.  when i have money i blow it on bizarre menagerie, local art, photography monographs, franco-belgian comics , cookbooks, field guides of all kinds and all-day, themed food forays in the city.  i am deep-fried, tempurpedic, and undead. if you have any idea what i'm doing with my life, i would love to hear about it. changing my mind. my prehensile tail. books: schott's miscellany, ever since darwin, fun home, way of seeing, immediate family, stalking the blue-eyed scallop, ragtime, catcher in the rye, house of the spirits, one flew over the cuckoo's nest, the great outdoor fight, oishinbo, botany of desire, all the pretty horses.  movies: brad bird-pixar collaborations, a fish called wanda, groundhog day, lilo and stitch, shaun of the dead, the hurt locker.  shows: deadwood!!! arrested development, breaking bad, community, parks and rec, no reservations, this old house, sesame street.  music: tribe called quest, pixies, andrew bird, billie holiday, beatles, no doubt, dj shadow, snoop, t-rex, yeah yeah yeahs, busta rhymes, pharcyde, aimee mann, etta james, elastica, salt n pepa, tlc, mariah, jay reatard.  food: fried chicken, collards, beans and rice; shrimp, snake beans, and kabocha squash in coconut milk; kakuni (japanese braised pork belly) with a soft-boiled egg and menma; albacore or salmon nigiri and a california hand roll; pistachio macarons from miette; kumamoto oysters; brownie sundae with salty caramel ice cream; la farine morning bun and a pretty little cup of coffee. 1)color vision, 2)my sister, 3)my boxers 4)pilot gtech pens and vellum notebooks, 5)kids, 6)animal fat and the maillard process. making lists. smoking a fatty and trying to figure out what else i can top with black forest ham. my other ride was assimilated by the borg. you know what i'm saying.

full_bio: thus spake ok cupid: i am experienced in love, sex-driven, independent, experienced in life, artsy, experienced in sex, creative, sloppy, kind, introverted, adventurous, spiritual, thrifty, confident and geeky. i am not into exercise, wealthy or old-fashioned.  i, however, might describe myself differently. i'm a clipper card-carrying sf resident, i prefer boots to stilettos, i like to dance and go watch real live people perform on a stage in front of me. i'll agree with okc and say i'm both introverted and kind. i have friends who love me exactly the way i am, which is to say that i don't have a huge circle of friends, but a small group of really good ones. i love to cook and especially bake, and feel a calling to make homemade birthday cakes for people i know.  i drive a scooter and love riding around sf, stopping to look at the gorgeous views which are everywhere in this city. i have 318 photographs of the sunset from my front window, and about 147 photos of city lights at night from various vista points. traveling is essential for me. i love sitting still while the world moves past me. in one of my favorite travel moments this past winter, i was riding a train in england, bought a cuppa tea, triangular white bread tuna sandwich and nummy biscuits from the rolling snack cart, and watched the green undulating hills slide by.  i have been single by choice for a while now, and just recently decided that i'm ready for love. i'm not putting any parameters on what that may look like: at 50 and as a part-time parent, it can be complex. i genuinely like and respect men and a find a wide variety of you fascinating and entertaining.  i am a riddle, wrapped in a soft blankie. feeling extremely lucky. i have a job i like and i'm good at, colleagues i love, friends who love me, the world's greatest daughter, a great apartment, and i live in one of the most beautiful cities in the world. not bad, eh? i love to cook and almost always have homemade desserts lying around my kitchen. i can remember at least one line from most pop songs from 1970 through today. i'm a good writer and am especially talented at editing personal essays for school applications. i own a glue gun and i use it. i can fake my way around most latin-based languages. my smile, my slightly loud and a bit raucous laugh. i read novels, in style magazine, scientific articles, my horoscope, cookbooks, personal ads, the chronicle. although i read a lot of novels, i can never remember the title or the author, but i could tell you in excruciatingly boring detail about my favorite scene. i also don't like to keep books i've read because, well, why does anyone?  i don't own or watch tv, but thanks to the aforementioned in style subscription i can follow most pop culture conversations at the water cooler. i don't really like to see movies at a movie theater because most of the time i end up wacking my forehead with my hand and saying "why did i just spend $12 on that? ($15 if it's in 3-d)"  i like food, especially delicious food. yesterday i made petit fours and today i made kale chips. i eat pretty much everything else in between.  i'm not the kind of person who sizes someone up by the music they listen to, so it doesn't particularly matter to me, but here's a few cds i bought recently: kt tunstall, rogue wave, freelance whales, cole porter, lenka, cake, death cab for cutie. nothing? how much longer can i hold out without getting a cell phone? do i have enough money to retire in the next two years? oops, i just got a cell phone, so now i think about how in the world to use it. monday is the new friday. i read my horoscope every day. you lke to tell stories. you can make me laugh until i hiccup. you're interested in meeting in person and leaving the electronic world. i'm not much of an online chatter. for that reason i prefer men who live in sf.

full_bio: i'm a bay area transplant via new york city via san diego. summaries are tough. biking, climbing, stretching, eating, and sleeping my way through time. trying to make a life in the urban planning/public policy world. trying to figure out how best to be. talking excitedly, eating with extreme pleasure, laying in the sun, cooking (i excel in the under-appreciated realm of drunk-food cuisine), dancing with a fair degree of abandon, hiking, singing loud and sometimes beautiful nonsense. i got big eyebrows, and sometimes my cheeks get really red. books: walt whitman, david foster wallace, rushdie, richard brautigan, tom robbins, james baldwin, mike davis, rene daumal, rumi.  music: so much, but to try to be brief... tune-yards, animal collective, fela kuti, akron family, francois virot, tallest man on earth, clipse, ratatat, why?, karl blau, krautrock, tropicalia, post-rock, folk, new orleans brass bands, all music that forces you to dance, songs that make you cry, songs that fill you with pure, hot joy.  food: oh man, everything. 1. the sun 2. ice cream 3. shoulder-stands 4. my friends 5. loud speakers 6. sleep vernacular architecture, urban landscape history, california, psychoactive experience, cute girls, what the hell i should be doing in life.  i am fairly earnest, and sometimes find it scary and disheartening to be thus. you like good loud living and aren't too jaded.

full_bio: to begin, i'd like to pose a question... wouldn't it be wonderful if men and women could come to the realization that we're truly different and that we don't have to have everything in common in order to make a relationship work? what happened to the concept or idea that what you "bring to the table" can be unique and different than your partner... and that's what makes the feast so much more exciting and delicious!  that being said...  plain and simple, i'm a guitarist... in the mornings, i sell guitars at an amazing local music shop, here on the peninsula... in the afternoons, i have a thriving teaching business instructing the youth of america how to rock! *wink*... and then on the occasional weekend, i back it up by playing live music in a local cover-band as well, where *i* do the rocking... lol! no, i'm not the wealthiest man on the planet, but i wouldn't trade what i'm doing for any other kind of work... i'm my own boss, i make my own hours, and i have a very enriching, if not fulfilling, experience passing on what i know to others about playing guitar, making music, and sharing the joy of it all with others... especially kids!!! (that's because i haven't forgotten what it's like to be one!) :o)  i've been described by some of my friends and family as one of the nicest people they know (man, have i got them fooled... *insert sinister laugh*... lol!) my sister has referred to me as being "terminally polite"... don't get me wrong, i definitely have a dark side... i think that we all do, right? meaning, anybody who declares that they don't have "baggage" is basically in denial, right?! like it or not, we're all carrying something along from our past relationships... romantic, platonic, familiar (meaning, pertaining to family), you name it... it's made us who we are, no?!  who am i looking for? i'll know when i see her smile... pardon the clich, but she might be "the girl next door"... i know, i know... *yawn*, right?! lol! but it's the truth... someone who finds pleasure and satisfaction from the simple things in life... good food, good drink, enjoying friends, going to san francisco giants games, taking in a movie... hopefully likes seeing her man play some live music every one in a while... maybe likes to dance?! ;o) i'll know when i see her smile... nothing wrong with her being stylish or enjoying the finer things in life, it's just that i would prefer to be with someone who isn't so necessarily high strung or high maintenance... don't get me wrong, i may not be "metro-sexual" or super-stylish, but i certainly "clean up" well and enjoy dressing up and having a good time!  beyond that? chemistry... plain and simple... either we have it or we don't, right? are you willing to find out? i am... trying to get through it the best and only way i know how... adjusting. adapting. accepting. wouldn't you like to know?! *blush* (oh, and i play a pretty mean guitar...) physically? you'd have to ask them... although i have been told nice things about my smile, voice and hands! hmmm... otherwise, i've been told that people feel quite at ease or quite comfortable soon after meeting me... oh man... where to begin?  books: not a huge reader, but hoping to change that soon... last reads were both sports themed... moneyball and seabuiscuit.... self help, too... there's nothing wrong with you had a huge impact on helping me understand my approaches and how i've dealt with things since childhood... and how i can improve that!  movies: pretty much an appreciator of everything... from "chick flicks", to "action", to "indy"... will always be a star trek and star wars geek, no i don't discriminate... lol! anything that's fun, thought-provoking, heck... even love it if it makes me cry!  tv: daily show, colbert report, big bang theory, almost anything on the palladia channel, shameless, modern family, 30 rock...  music: once again, i don't discriminate... rock, blues, jazz, classical, funk, i like it all... yes, even *some* country music... just has to be good, have quality and integrity!  food: yes, please... ;-) seriously, i've been lucky enough to have had many wonderful experiences enjoying *incredible* meals over the course of my life... from cyrus up in healdsburg, navio at the ritz in half moon bay, redd up in yountville... to simply enjoying a killer burger... heck, even a double-double "animal style" from in 'n' out will make me smile! music guitars playing live music orange juice all kinds of yummy cuisine you what?! lol... too much!!! either at a gig, enjoying the company of good friends, or crashing with a good movie... hopefully with you! being a musician, that i really like trashy pop-music... *blush* :o) ... you think you should!

full_bio: i enjoy spending time with family & friends, sneaking off to tahoe to go snowboarding for the weekend, hiking in marin, mountain biking, & playing soccer. i try to strike the right balance of work & play, so i take my job seriously but i won't let it get in the way of enjoying the things that are important to me (having fun). i think honesty is always the best policy; family always comes first, and i consider my closest friends my family.  my weekends are usually spent with a group of friends in the golden gate or dolores park, bbq's & camping, long dinners with family & friends, playing with my friend's three-year old son, or enjoying a couple of well crafted beers in spots like elixer or monk's kettle. a really great weekend would start with a hike in marin or a long bike ride to fairfax and back through tiburon, followed by brunch, an outdoor concert or street festival, having dinner at that new restaurant i've been hearing about, or staying in and cooking dinner with friends and a few bottles of wine.  i am adventurous, cute, and cuddly living it one day at a time and enjoying every moment of it. i work as a commercial insurance broker for a small insurance agency here in the city. it may sound boring to some, but it's actually very rewarding and it keeps me busy. knowing what i'm not good at: flamenco dancing. i found that out the hard way! either my smile or my boyish charm. movies: lock, stock & two smoking barrels; slumdog millionaire; pan's labyrinth; the godfather trilogy; braveheart; gladiator; away from her; man on wire; hott fuzz; no country for old men; there will be blood; the orpahange; trainspotting  books: omnivore's dilemma, lush life; slam; the geography of bliss; kick ass; the kite runner; in the hand of dante; crazy for the storm  music: reggae, devotchka, radiohead, miike snow, black keys, talib kweli  food: mom & dad's lasagna, burrito's from cancun or papalote, ceviche from limon, little star pizza, the burger at mission beach cafe, the falafel at herbivore, pliny the elder beer music, my bike, toothpaste, cold water filled canteen, papalote burritos, sunglasses. friends & family are a given, but i'll keep going...local's, good coffee, green tea, books, iphone, sun block, good beer, sitting in dolores park on a sunny day playing washers with my friends. what i'm going to be doing this weekend, my next meal, current events, work, my family, how to be a better human. drinks at elixer then maybe dinner at monk's kettle. driving to sacramento to visit friends. heading up to tahoe. or at home watching the netflix movie that's been waiting for me all week. my sock and underwear drawer is color coordinated and i own the movie love, actually. you are adventurous, cute, fun & smart.

you are crazy and doesn't like fun!

i am a-okay with it. you should too. i live: in the mission district. very fortunate to share my life with two really amazing friends. we have a wonderful energy and bond. we are more like a family. i am: spiritual. meaning i believe in something greater than just humanity itself. i was raised catholic but, dont let that scare you. embracing optimistism is my new faith/outlook. let's face it, life is too short. living in the moment is important to me now. i constantly strive to recognize the positive and appreciating all that is around me. i was: a suburban misfit, who grew up feeling misunderstood. i pushed my way threw school and then at 18, graduated and moved to sf; i've never looked back i do: want to learn more about life. maybe you would like to share in that with me. making people laugh at themselves and, then at me. i am 6'3. that is pretty tall. i like every book by david sedaris. any movie starring meryl streep or julia roberts. what? shows that include music or cartoons are a plus. i do not watch or actually have cable. so... not sure if i have good or bad taste in music. but i like what i like. ie. 70s disco, 80s pop, latin ballads, torch songs, love songs, dance songs, blah blah blah.. eggplant is my enemy. all foods otherwise are welcome! family friends triscuts and cheese almond milk laughter jameson and or bulliet bourbon what to think about.. this question is lame. who goes out on friday night? you only get one lifetime, embrace it.

books), consuming vast quantities of pizza, reading extremely quickly, eating bagels with lots of vegetables on them, and being generally nerdy. that my hair is extremely long, and then my eyes. books: neverwhere/american gods (neil gaiman), snow crash (neal stephenson), neuromancer (william gibson), the matador series (steve perry), peace (gene wolfe), the harry potter series (j.k. rowling), computer or on my ds.  you're looking for someone to talk to who knows proper english spelling, punctuation, and grammar, and isn't afraid to use them. you like nerdiness, and aren't afraid of someone full_bio: i'm a grad student from the midwest, transplanted to the bay area. (note: to avoid confusion: i go to grad school in california, but i'm in michigan for three months doing research. i consider my home state indiana...)  i am highly attracted to complex, if not contradictory, systems...i'm an ex-math nerd, love lacanian psychoanalysis, am fascinated by political thought and theory...  i have the mouth of a sailor (sailorette?), but don't that fool you. i have an obscenely moral side, and i tend to expect the same from others.  i think i have a lot to offer - but i also humbly know that there's *so much* out there i have to learn, and i'm quite open about admitting shortcomings, mistakes, trying new things, and so on...  i'm totally apathetic to religious debates, but at the end of the day, being raised catholic means that i am extremely loyal and honest.  i love throwing myself into new things...roller derby, motorcycling, lacanian psychoanalysis, love...i'm very passionate and believe that life is best lived by 'falling for it'...whatever 'it' might be. try your best and give your all, and at least at the end of the day, you will suffer from neither regret nor guilt.  i am brutally honest, hard-loving, and a little bad-ass i'm a graduate student studying gun-rights organizing. i also am an editor. while i definitely spend too much of my time reading esoteric social theory, i also spend a lot of time thinking about how to make my work interesting, accessible and relevant to people outside the academy...probably like most graduate students! ...writing theoretically beautiful but not necessarily meaningful prose ;).  ...laughing about the stupidities of life.  ...accepting my own contradictions and the contradictions of others.  ...balancing my life as a cosmopolitan redneck. wouldn't want to know... books: i tend to nerd out when it comes to books. surprised? here's the current list: hermeunitics of the subject by foucault (still getting through this one), gender trouble by butler, any of lacan's seminars (again, moving slowly through this one), anything by zizek, kojeve's introduction to hegel, laclau and mouffe...  movies: vertigo, solaris (tarkovsky...not the george clooney version), zizek's pervert's guide cinema, weird documentary/social analysis-type movies about early capitalism and psychoanalysis  music: favorite artist of all time: eminem. and unfortunately for almost everyone i've dated, i have a real soft spot for the top 40. but don't worry, i can totally rescue any jay-z or miley cyrus song through prudent sociological analysis, if you'll give me the chance! i'm also mad for classic rock and 80's hair bands...yup, guns n roses, bon jovi...oh my. i think i'll stop this list right now.  food: yes. in no specific order...  1. my wonderful, wonderful friends... 2. www.peopleofwalmart.com 3. clever hyperbole 4. vodka 5. my two kitties (i know, most girls only have one!) 6. my past experiences - mistakes, successes, and everything in between.  if i could remember, i'd be happy to tell you. everytime i hear "heaven is a place on earth" by belinda carlisle, i pretend that she's singing about the impending revolution... you think we both might have a few tricks up our sleeves to show each other!...tell me what our ideal first date would be!  (and i wish this went without saying...but unfortunately i have to say it. please do not contact me if you have any history of being disrespectful or dishonest to women...as much as i am open-minded about different viewpoints and ideas, sexism has no place in my life! nor should it have one in yours. xo!)

full_bio: i moved to san francisco from colorado last august. i love living and working downtown! i was born in san jose, but grew up in canada as my entire family is canadian. i can tell you the rest of the story in person, if you like what you read below.  since being in sf, i've done the following: visited golden gate park 8 times, went to bluegrass in the park, the polk st blues festival, took a 3 hour class in blues dancing, danced salsa to a live band, became a season ticket holder for a local theater company, attended many improv comedy nights, played tennis at alice marble (what a view!), sang karaoke all over the city, had the best cocktails in the coolest pubs, attended a lecture on drug cartels, visited moma, attended a 1912 theme new years party and many more things.  maybe you can show me something new and exciting! also, malbecs, cabs, and merlots in winter and mescato and sangria in summer. stouts and vodka all year long! found the best punch house in haight, you have to try it! npr: listen to it all the time! you like what you've read, enjoy intellectual conversations, have an open mind, love to travel, not afraid of silence, confident in yourself, kind to strangers, interested in politics, like history, can latin or swing dance or willing to learn, you love food, embrace life, you're funny, and you smile and laugh a lot.

full_bio: i work in the web technologies domain, it is a rich blend of art, science and engineering; i find building stuff that connects people and enriches their lives daily very satisfying.  outside of work i enjoy food, cars, travel, wine and being fit. over time, i want to learn sailing and flying; play the piano, read more classics and start a non-profit focused on disrupting the education space. i have a very good memory, particularly remembering faces, names, lyrics to songs, cologne and then some. i love learning languages and i can pick them up pretty quickly. also, i enjoy cooking and trying out new recipes.  i enjoy artsy, quirky and independent films like those by wes anderson, martin scorsese and woody allen. i have watched all of their major works. a selection; midnight in paris, moonrise kingdom, bullets over broadway, rushmore, last days of disco. then, some others like pina, girl on the bridge, sliding doors, before sunrise, before sunset, la dolce vita.  my musical inclination is equally eclectic. my morning starts with coffeehouse favorites like feist, iron and wine, regina spektor, jack johnson into the afternoon with the black keys, florence + machine, awolnation, and unwinds into the evening with fleet foxes, mazzy star, diana krall, norah jones and lise de la salle.  i read a fascinating article on how practice trumps talent in almost all disciplines and i am following it up with geoff convin's, "talent is overrated". my last fiction read was chuck palahniuk's, "survivor". i am working through gdel, escher, bach.  i also indulge in the opera, i think it is the epitome of art for where can you find an exhibit of music, theatre, costume and lights, all in perfect harmony. i enjoyed carmen, don giovanni and the ring cycle the most. i am still waiting for sf opera to bring barbiere di siviglia, lakme and phantom of the opera to the town.  there are certain kinds of cuisine that always make my evening no matter what. sushi, ceviche and steak top that list. on the other days i prefer comfort food like pho, paella or a warm bowl of jambalaya. my family and friends top the list. i love music to the point that i built a central music system that plays music in all my rooms. being a voracious reader, i have to be connected to the internets for my daily fix and my macbook tops off the list.  at a happy hour with friends in fidi or unwinding at a lounge. every friday is different. i take frequent micro vacations or road trips to point reyes, big sur and random places in marin.  you are confident, smile, not afraid of awkward moments and willing to meet. i invite you to connect, it could be the beginning of something wonderful.

full_bio: what a remarkable age, the one we live in. here i sit at a keyboard, facing a computer screen, looking for someone. who? maybe you.  and who am i? i shall attempt to describe myself by describing who i am looking for.  i am looking for someone with whom to explore life in all its glorious manifestations: joy and pain, creation and destruction.  someone with whom to dance lifes dance--upon concrete streets at midnight--cracking the pavement.  someone with whom to awaken--beneath canopies of millennial redwoods to the sweet whispers of symphonic spring streams.  i am looking for someone who embraces the ebb and flow life- love and inspiration, suffering and sadness; someone who delights in the mystery of this ephemeral existence; someone who naturally elicits conversations that change the way i think and deepens my understanding of myself and the world.  i am looking for someone who challenges me to inhabit my truest self and to love as outrageously as we are all ment to love. i have been working in the solar industry as a mechanical engineer for the past 4-5 years, and was in construction doing green building before that.  however, i am in the midst of a transition towards working with people as a therapist/counselor- helping clients to open and express who they are  -some background-  as a youth i became interested in environmental issues, and, believing the solutions were technical began my engineering studies. this lead me through the career path that has brought me to my current work.  however, while in college and with the beginning of my meditation practice, i started to see that the issues were much more complex, and that the source of a whole lot of turmoil on this planet was rooted in mistaken perspectives and understandings within our human hearts and minds. this inspired my current quest and passion.  -the present-  i feel super-inspired to help people find what they most deeply seek. over the past decade of going deeper into my own process, and also with the coaching and support of comrades and teachers, i feel like i have become more fully alive, free, open, and loving. the journey continues, growth continues, challenge continues, and simultaneously i feel compelled to share some of the tools i have been given along the way.  a year ago i transitioned to working part-time as an engineer so that i could devote more to my study of the process by which humans transform themselves, to spiritual practice (meditation,qigong), and opening and enjoying life- dancing, exersizing, spending time outside, hanging out with friends etc. its been an amazing year.  i have recently completed a 2 year hakomi training (experiential, somatic, mindfulness based therapy) and am now starting to pour energy into building a coaching practice based on hakomi.  it is my first shot at a career working with people. it scares me to be slowly stepping away from engineering, a job that has supported me so very well over the past years.  simultaneously, by feeling the fear and moving directly into it, i feel more alive, connected, and excited about life than ever. designing stuff, building stuff, laughing really loud, loving people, loving life, supporting friends, challenging friends, following my heart, being present, fixing bicycles. my smile, my laugh. books: i am deeply inspired by most of the books of david deida- blue truth and the way of the superior man are both favorites that i can say have changed my life.  i like fiction but over the past few years i havn't been reading much for pleasure. i would like to change that. the latest work of fiction that i really enjoyed was ender's game.  movies: i wept a few tears when i saw the kings speech- shawshank redemption is a standby, as well as i heart huckabees.  shows: when dj's lay down tracks that are strong and deep my body moves and i feel good. however, i don't go to many shows- most of my exposure to dj's is through the ecstatic dance and dance jam scenes.  music: i love great artists in all genres of music. some favorites: john prine, tom waits, the postal service, blackalicious, jurassic 5, amon tobin, rufus wainwright, antony, leonard cohen, bjork, greg brown, michael franti, radiohead.  food:  i love the food of asia- indian, thai, and chinese are all standbys with ethiopian and italian thrown in occasionally for continental variety.  i love making mexican food and if i had to live on one thing for the rest of my life it would be rice n' beans.  most of the time i like to eat simple, delicious, fresh, homemade meals that include local veggies and fruit (organic preferred though i am no purist). connectedness and love of friends and family hot sauce meditation bicycles the wild blue yonder how humans change for the better. dancing with friends. i have an inner perfectionist that is a major pain in the ass and that i would just as soon let go of. the dude has less and less power over me but still causes me a fare share of grief. i am happy to say he doesn't measure others by the same standards he applies to me.  however, related to this pattern my easy-going intention for the year is to be less serious, have more fun, and to make lots of mistakes. you think we may be a good match.

therapist counselor helping clients to open and express who they are some background as a youth i became interested in environmental issues and believing the solutions were technical began my engineering studies this lead me through the career path that has brought me to my current work however while in college and with the beginning of my meditation practice i started to see that the issues were much more complex and that the source of a whole lot of turmoil on this planet was rooted in mistaken perspectives and understandings within our human hearts and minds this inspired my current quest and passion the present i feel super inspired to help people find what they most deeply seek over the past decade of going deeper into my own process and also with the coaching and support of comrades and teachers i feel like i have become more fully alive free open and loving the journey continues growth continues challenge continues and simultaneously i feel compelled to share full_bio: i am enjoying life these days--in an open marriage to a wonderful, sexy man whom i adore. newlyweds!  i am an open-minded, thoughtful aesthete. i love food and cooking. art. books. music. fancy shoes. goofy humor.  i don't exercise as much as i should but i look alright anyway.  i don't know how to ride a bike.  for me life is about balance, about being comfortable in my skin and in a variety of different situations. i adore travel and learning about other cultures, especially through food.  a glass of red wine and a good book are heaven. a table loaded with great food, and surrounded by good friends and great conversation is probably where i am happiest. working a lot--i love what i do, and i work for myself so i sometimes find it hard to limit my hours.  fixing up an old house. no kitchen at the moment.  learning about myself and my partner, questioning monogamy, finding my best path. making long to-do lists and then ignoring them. cooking. sleeping. matching colors. artfully arranging things on my mantle. throwing my clothes on the floor. my glasses, my smile, my beeple. i love reading, i am a fiction gal. eg. his dark materials (pullman), a visit from the goon squad (egan), cavalier & clay (chabon) anything by jean giono, especially joy of man's desiring. anais nin, henry miller. the lord of the rings. for semi-mindless entertainment i adore the simenon novels about inspector maigret.  also i dig cooking memoirs, like blood bones & butter by gabrielle hamilton. and books about sex and relationships, like bonk by mary roach, or sex at dawn (amazing read!), or opening up by tristan taormino.  not a big tv watcher, but i do love mad men and the occasional niners game, and while on jet blue i can watch an endless amount of hgtv. ouch.  i like movies in theory but never seem to carve out time to go. some faves are eternal sunshine of the spotless mind, room with a view (the one with helena bonham carter), singing in the rain. oh, and old eddie murphy.  music: love love love music. no opera, no country (though bluegrass i like). wide range in music...right now lots of glenn gould playing bach (especially at work), andrew bird, the xx, erlend oye, chris joss, ursula 1000, local natives. old rem, kate bush, david sylvian of any era. i love jazz, too, from sonny clark to john zorn. add in a bit of bossa nova, a dose of yacht rock, and 3 days worth of funk, and you will have an idea of the 12000 songs in my itunes file.  and food? papardelle bolognese. fried artichokes. pulled pork. tea leaf salad. carnitas. fennel. fennel pollen. steak tartare. tuna tartare. peaches. english toffee. collards. lobster with drawn butter. spicy green papaya salad. vindaloo. bacon (mmm bacon candy). ramen, rice noodles, buccatini...did i mention i like food? prolly in reverse order: good sheets and down pillows books noodles (any genre will do) a glass of something (like wine) or a cup of something (like coffee) my loved ones how i can get everything done that i am supposed to get done. love, sex & relationships what i am going to eat next. how we are going to finish our kitchen so i can start cooking again. cutting holes in plywood with my fein multi-tool. washing drywall dust out of my hair so i can go get a yummy dinner. or maybe working late. or maybe at a dinner party. or losing at pool at a dive bar. or seeing a live show in a small place. i'm a little shy and a lot picky. you are a dashing, handsome man...  or a gorgeous, slightly butch woman...  and  you are respectful of my relationship and interested in a friends or friends with benefits situation...  (or you love food and wine and cocktails as much as i do.  or you want to recommend a great book on relationships, especially open relationships or polyamory.)  please remember that i am really particular and also too busy to be devoting much time to meeting new people. but you never know!

full_bio: your ideal date is:  a. a night out on the town, dining and dancing b. a quiet evening at home, reading or watching a movie c. a road trip to the country, destination unknown d. a visit to the local comic book store, browsing and kibbitzing. e. it really kinda depends.  if you're like me, then you're an "e" sort of person. i always have a hard time with the multiple choice questions. i would like the trip to paris and a quiet day at the beach. i would enjoy that fun evening of music and dancing or the one spent with bad reality tv and popcorn. why choose between a day at dicken's fair or the edwardian ball and a drive-in? i like them all.  to some that might sound a tad indecisive but rest assured my friend, it is not. rather, i am complex by nature and eclectic in my interests. sometimes i am quiet and introspective, seeking balance. other times i say to heck with balance and follow a path passionately.  i was born on the right coast but moved to the left coast many moons ago. i retain the ny state of sarcasm and bluntness, but am learning to temper that with acceptance and tolerance.  i don't want a twin, but i get along best with folks who have a wide range of interests, a fondness for reading, are honest with themselves and others, and have a passion for something in their life. like me, you want a friend and a partner, someone to share new things, discuss the world or sit in silence with. i was busy being self-employed for 15 years, when i had the brilliant idea to go back to school. i have now graduated and with paper in hand i am working on an internship (and still am self-employed). i have been told (by them folks what should know) that i am a good friend (and apparently those same attributes would make me a good girl scout). i can be a good listener and i'm good at recognizing when i'm not being a good listener. i'm a pretty darn good communicator, creative, and honest. i am a very loyal person, and value the people i've had in my life for 30 years and more very highly. i try hard to understand another viewpoint, even when i really don't (or, especially when i really don't). depends on who they are and where they are when they're noticing. some things to watch: true blood, mad men, rescue me. some things to eat: italian, indian, and sugary things that are probably not good for me. some things to hear: neil finn, rem, wilco, silly wizard. some things to move to: cajun, swing, lady gaga. some things to read: mysteries, graphic novels. some things to ponder: how much longer these lists could be once we know each other. the people who love me, my cats, a way to express myself visually, music, silence, and .... my new career, the silly (yet wonderful) kids i work with, the possibility of travel, the nature of humans, the state of the universe, and how it all fits together. you know, the simple stuff. working on an art project, reading, doing paperwork, vegging out, hanging out, attempting sleep, working, any combination of the above or none of the above. i take a terrible picture - been told i'm much better looking in person. otherwise, you'll have to ask and i'll most likely answer. you're interested in getting more in-depth answers to all those 'match' questions.

full_bio: from a friend- amidst the unknown beautiful wisdom, shared integrity, and strength, shown within tales of the lost & the found. framed in our fears we free each other; deep breaths of truth our gift. not seeking, but embracing the moments shared. then was now, captured with you. sensual energy emits mind, body, and soul so pure that only unconditional love exists, reveals acceptance, breaking of the shackles only ourselves have locked, new light shattering the world of created prisons, and freedom is within all this. seeing no conditions, seeking no reward, enjoying space and time in existence, holding on for awhile, free within each others tales of hearts broken,and mending, having selfless intention. a muse in the matters of the heart, giving light, strength, and purity, no judgement withholding power between the tears. great healer of natural sorts, knowledge beyond the years. your openness brings gifts to many they will cherish for lifetimes, as they transcend into higher states, within these realms of reality. i am blessed to have shared these moments of freedom, with the presence of your pure soul. until next time, my new dear friend. my solitude begins opening my spirit to shine life with positive intentions hip hop, mc'ing, dj'ing, breakers, graff artists, beatboxers, snowboarding, surfing, skateboarding, tattoos, piercings,yoga, dance, capoeira, jewelry, fashion/clothing, multicultural-lifestyles, nature, body/heart/soul/mind, news, reading, meditation, dance, live music, holistic medicine, sustainable living, consciousness expansion, good cinema/film:festivals, chillin with good people :mary jane, so many other things. i have a very wide spectrum of interests. i could go on forever about things that i am interested in/fascinated by/try to pay attention to. im learning living and hopefully loving a beautiful, intelligent, dynamic, woman of virtue who deserves my love, and reciprocates this in an amazing cycle of energy exchange! one that is both mystical and grounded, and everything in between. some of the above, most things i put my mind into, dancing, being a complicated human... my presence... i think i don't know i'll have to ask some people or you can meet me and then tell me... i could go on forever with a list of music all kinds of stuff. i love music. music is life. an art form i could not go on living without. i've seen thousands of shows, now it's time for my contribution...  movies... lots you know good ones like city of god...  not much t.v. but obviously there is entertainment, laughter, and even knowledge to be had from t.v. but it sucks you in, even if it is something you loathe to watch. way too much other stuff, going on/to be doing...so yeah, but of course simpsons!!! the family our generation grew up with!(though strangely those cartoon characters never did age?)  i love almost all food!!!  and i used to read voraciously, i still do but just not books so much. that too is about to change. i am a very adept reader. a list will replace this someday soon. forget six things...  my mom is my biggest hero, she made me into the person i am today, and that person, believes in love, truth, and integrity, above all else. she made it through loss and betrayal, and she always took the highest path. other than that, there are so many inspirations... hasta la victoria para siempre... r.i.p. and respect to my brother... adriano aleixo nascimento p. - life is about change. sometimes its painful. sometimes its beautiful. most of the time, its both. i am a complex but kind (most of the time) individual. i love music, art, culture, people, and living life to the fullest. i seek the highest potential of each individual, and of the collective mass of humanity on this planet. i believe that love is our source, and the foundation of our existence. we must learn to cultivate it, spread it and receive it. to love, and be loved deeply; that is worth more than anything. but it can be difficult to find. you almost have to wait and let love find you. i am now 31 years old. time flys by whether you are having fun or not. so sometimes, i just like to get up and get down and party like an animal. relax and forget about the pain, and the trials and tribulations of the world. yet, other times i feel guilty being me/middle class/american and what not. most americans and many others elsewhere in the world are living a life of materialism with a lot of hedonism mixed in.(myself definitely included) we are stuck in this world of capitalism, we are a product of that system. is this a good thing? it is a complicated situation. people are starving, people are dying. there is disease, and there is war. our planet and nearly all its inhabitants are hurting. but, do we care? should we care? care more than about getting the new iphone or ipad, or missing the concert/party. these thoughts can be bothersome. especially if you carry the weight of it all. if you personally empathize with these situations. the plight of humanity, plus all species, and our planet earth. if you question the values of yourself within this system. all of that coupled with the ups and downs of my personal life. hectic. i try to find a balance, i'm trying to build a balance. so there you have it. you've tapped my brain. well, i guess i let some of it drain out for you. before i leave you if you made it this far, here is a simple and fair warning. i deal in reality, truth, honesty and integrity; no childish head games is what i am striving to find with friends and with romantic partners...  may i be blessed enough to find this that i am looking for. the answer to my question no typical night for me... i'll admit anything to the right people but nothing to all of you that i haven't already... if you think we would get along. if you feel like it.

full_bio: i've been described as an "industrious young man" by a neighbor. who happens to be an older esl gentleman  i take a lot of pride in that. what i do for work is what's called being a pacs administrator; a sys admin for diagnostic imaging informatics systems. we manage the workflow/systems that take a picture of your bones, organs, boobs, babies inside of bellies, skin heebie geebies, butthole cameras, and other diagnostic imaging tools for doctors to do their thing and help fix you.  i like what i do. i get to look at cool images all day created machines that use different spectrum of light and/or sound...and i get to play with the machines.  at one point of my life, art school was an option. then i decided that i should have a more grounded career. i started considering what parent's generation went thru to do their best to assimilate an american lifestyle in their late 20s (when they first came to america from the philippines) and as i got older i understood more and more of what they went through. so i pretty much take it upon myself to make sure their efforts weren't in vain...i mean they want me to be happy in whatever i do, but also don't want me to be a broke ass in my endeavors to finding happiness in what i do for a living. i guess to sum it up, work pays for what i love to do and i don't love what i do...but like i said i do like what i do. this is the part where i should be bragging about myself. talkin' bout i'm this successful knuckle dragging, yet refined, alpha male. and bringing shit up from my past that probably doesnt have relevance anymore.  but the more believable things im good at would be...  riding my bicycle, eating, having fun, and im pretty good at drinking. i've been also told that i am a "good ear" and a self aware individual. the handsome face that still has a charming school boy quality to it.  or  i'm really not that handsome, and my face definitely does not have a school boy charm quality to it at all. im constantly in the process of reading at least 4 books at a time and me being a slow reader, i try to read at least 4 books a year. books in rotation:nothing to envy, digital imagining and communication in medicine (the real page turner of the group), a people history of the united states (don't judge me cus i havent read it yet :) boxing lists : bert sugar and teddy atlas  i am a total outerspace future sci-fi movie person; blade runner (i can never decide if i want sean young or daryl hanna to be my lady) alien, moon, sunshine, fifth element, 12 monkeys...prometheus! no, i'm not on ridley scotts nuts... but i will admit this, i think the 1990's bruce willis is the last greatest american action hero. lately, i've found myself watching a lot of movies from the late 60s. spaghetti westerns, midnight cowboy, harold and maude, the graduate. i like the style in those movies and the awkward acting and fab story lines  not into concerts, but i love watching bands in small intimate venues. i learned that i don't like specific genres of music more than i like certain/specific things when it comes to the music i like listening to. such as, i like the voices of ruben blades; the lead singer of kings of leon (i forgot his name, actually i don't think i've ever knew it in the first place); lastly, tell me who has a smoother voice than michael mcdonald?? all of them have this brassy deep tenor quality to thier voice that massages my ear drums. i also love the way astrud gilberto sings like a lazy person; the clarinet-like voice of fiest. and bjork...who doesn't love bjork? i love love love the sound of the vibraphones and dislike the sound of the saxophone. love love love the music curtis mayfield, roy ayers, herbie hancock and erykah badu. recently, for some reason i can't stop listening to sheila e's a glamorous life. if someone could actually get into a conversation with me on how many levels this song is so good, please pm me and i will buy you a drink  i love food. i can eat the same things everyday, or try new things everyday. but i am picky and eat really healthy, and i believe eating pork is healthy along with drinking alcohol. it's all about moderation and savoring the occasional indulgence -regularly. people  time to center and relax  pens  two wheeled transportaion (motorcycles and bicycles over driving a car whenever possible)  touch  things that physically make me feel better which includes my yoga; acupuncture; physical therapy; making sure i can do more pull ups than the average joe and long walks/hikes.  (see how i snuck in the six pack as one item in the 10 items or less line?) how to manage the boundary where i should just let life happen vs where it's time to hustle and do shit about things.  what should i eat later.  old people getting older.  will i ever i feel like an adult, like the ones i grew up around?  particle smashers  why the fuzzy pictures??? relaxing... drinking wine and a eating roast beef sandwich while watching espn friday night fights. a-typical friday nights usually ruin saturday mornings with hang overs. i'm known to be an "open book" if you like no pressure respectful people who like enjoy themselves...  and sharing food

full_bio: hi. my name is kc and to get on this ride you must be exceedingly clever. i am less of a lady, more of a creature.  i'm interested in anarchism, social justice, philosophy, technology, sex positivity, and subverting social institutions. i am often the one woman with weird hair and tattoos at a party full of corporate shills. this happens more often than you might expect.  i am deeply in love with an amazing person, and our relationship is hardly monogamous. that said, casual sex for the sake of sex isn't a high priority for me right now. i think it is lines like this that led a friend to describe my profile as "stand offish." i think that's a perfectly fair assessment. to my fellow contrarians: feel free to continue reading! tons.  right now i am studying journalism and philosophy. i've also spent the last couple of years in professional kitchens. chances are, if you eat out a lot in san francisco/bay area i have cooked a meal for you. if you often frequent a certain bdsm themed coffee shop in town, i have likely made you a latte.  i occasionally show up at conferences and meetups of the hacker/security flavor, so if you recognize me feel free to say hello. i'm planning on being at defcon and hope this year. i'm less interested in telling you what i'm good at, and more interested in sharing and showing you. i am a snarky motherfucker and my tolerance for dumb is low. i try to be nice, but if you aren't entirely confident about your ability to be clever on the spot i might scare you away. but never fear, i am easily diffused- if you can make me laugh.  oh, the tattoos. people probably notice those too.  what people usually don't notice is my age. it's safe to say that i'm more savvy than a lot of people under 25 i've met. i actually *really* hate filling out sections like these because they end up sounding like pissing contests. all i'll say is that i grew up in the bay area punk and hardcore scenes and it shows in my tastes. if you're really interested, you can go check out my last.fm profile! http://www.last.fm/user/urb4n  i love my collection of books; from my old equestrian riding manuals of the 70s to more recent anarchist literature. i don't own a tv. as i mentioned, i've cut my teeth in the world of restaurant kitchens. i dislike the term "foodie," but food is a very important part of my life. in no particular order...  - my pocketknife - one tube of the perfect blood-red lipstick - mischievous smiles - a couple dollars in my pocket - one good pair of boots - a partner in crime truly understanding and impacting the world, and why i spend so much time obsessing over what i recognize as a feat which is impossible. flirting with your heroes.  on mondays i can usually be found in my natural environment propping up one of dna's bars at deathguild. i used to bartend topless at an honest-to-god speakeasy. if you don't feel threatened by a strong, passionate woman under 25 years old.  if you are not turned off by scars.  if you own your sexuality. though i'm not super eager to pursue casual flings, i am always appreciative of those who have a grasp on what they like and can communicate it.

full_bio: "if men knew what women were thinking life would be boring, if women knew what men were thinking life would be lonely!"  a lot of people here are incredibly immature. all they focus on is how someone looks. but i learned, from an early age (it's a long story, i'll tell you some other time), that beauty is common. sure, its work to maintain, but so what if you're beautiful? there are so many beautiful people in the world. look around. big deal! beauty is an accident  so you got lucky in the gene pool- but what have you made of yourself. that's what i am interested in. so for me when i meet someone that is attractive, it does nothing for me. and in fact i often wonder if they've gotten lazy cultivating the other parts of their personality, the ones that don't fade.  now i could sit here and write a paragraph about myself telling you all about my great qualities that may or may not interest you. the only things i think you really want to know is i am not under any kind of court ordered supervision nor do i have to see a professional on a regular basis to get medication to regulate my mental state. i don't live with mom and monday through friday from 9-5 i am at an office earning a paycheck.  i'm more than the usual amount of interesting and funny, so you'd better like to laugh. think you have a personality that can keep me interested? if you do i would love to meet you and see what kind of friends we would make. i mean that's where it all starts right?  i am a sucker for a good conversation so i hope you can keep up! trying to be the best attorney ever. everything. how tall i am.      you're smart, witty, attractive, adventurous, and open minded.

full_bio: i love to make people laugh. im a really big dork, and participate in all things dorky. this includes - d&d, mtg, a few board game nights, graphic novels, collecting vhs tapes and vintage children's books, movie marathons, cult films, collecting old 8mm prints and watching them on my old bell and howell! listening to old live radio dramas (the cinnamon bear is a favorite of mine) i have an okay collection of 1930's style jazz records (which is my favorite period of music) i play video games - but very sparingly because there is too much to do outside. i love to cook and im currently learning how to pickle. i have future goals in wanting to learn how to make jam from old fruit.  i enjoy life and consider each breath a gift. my strengths are in my wildly active imagination, which i apply to film making and skateboarding. my weakness is dyslexia, which effects my spelling and grammar in a significant way. if you can look past this, you will actually find and down to earth, intelligent, and loving man who (at times) suffers from a hero complex. with that knowledge, its important to tell you i skateboard on a regular basis. so i get @#$&ed up a lot with cuts, bumps, and bruises. writing two webisode series - i write and direct films cheering people up and having a wild imagination i have a crooked nose or curly hair or my glasses. i really dont know what people notice im too distracted with trying to make them laugh to notice what they see in me. i've covered most of the classic through my english lit. degree, but since i was a wee little boy i loved reading shakespeare! i enjoyed reading: zen and the art of motorcycle maintenance, dark tower, comics, and any vintage sci-fi.  movies: way too many here, but i will list three for fun 5th element, citizen kane, and wet hot american summer.  music: pinback, the cars, (here is a quick story about the cars in parenthesis. when i was 5 years old - and when i was still living with my natural mother... (another story to be heard) i would listen to the cars everyday with an 8track player. i would listen with huge headphones looking out into wild scape of the apartment complex i lived in. everyday i would come home and listen and see the world unfold in front of me. i think these moments were the most shaping moments of my life. and im very grateful),  food: bacon cheese burgers, pho, sushi, anything fish. family food cuddles/sex gaming of all sorts hiking/camping skateboarding new stories playing boardgames with friends then out dancing or have a drink with good people. i wet the bed till i was 6. i would always dream about peeing and just go while sleeping. im not embarrassed.  now its just periodic wet dreams. i've been in all types of relationships (self shoulder punch) - and most have been amazing! i've learn and grown from every encounter! i kind of looking for someone with very similar interest. i've never dated a geek kin spirit and would love to enjoy our similar interest! plus lots of sex! if you don't fit this mold then lets be friends - because i love meeting new people!  see you around!

full_bio: i've worked mostly in creative industries: fashion show production/styling, fashion design, and photography. i've played lots of competitive sports. i read about a book a week, i dunno if that means i'm a bit book-ish or simply inquisitive.  i tend towards being generous, thoughtful, and per-maybe-haps intelligent(?). maybe a bit snarky or sarcastic at times.  attraction to me is based on little things; like how kind someone is, or the ability to make someone laugh, sharing simple moments, mannerisms/gestures... the grace, intelligence, and the strength a woman carries herself with.  beauty alone doesn't make a lady attractive to me. there, i've said it. this year has been crazy... right now, my lil' doggy has been not-so-well, so i spend a lot of time lqqkin' out for her. that means going out less than i might otherwise. generally i think i work harder to treat people with respect, consideration, and kindness. i like to ponder abstract things sometimes (not drug-induced!)  i like to solve problems creatively. i also pretend to speak languages i don't know. many have said i'm a pretty good cook. my dog? i dunno, she's a cutie.  me? i dunno. the next first person i see i'll ask what they usually notice about me. or the first usual person i see i'll ask what they notice. or the next person serving me a notice, i'll ask about them first. books: gosh, maybe nabakov's "lolita." lee child for his pacing. orson scott card for his storytelling. tom robbins for his metaphors and escapism language. the curious incident of the dog in the night-time by mark haddon for its brilliance. soft! by rupert thomson for its premise and his unique storytelling. dave eggers... just for being cool like dave eggers is. the books "consilience" and "fabric of the cosmos" for content and learning. bertrand russell for all his philosophical views.  films: gosh... baraka, brazil, il conformiste, metropolis dark/noir: dark city, jacob's ladder, pi funny: le diner de cons (not the remake: dinner for schmucks) and snatch by guy ritchie sweet: funny face w/ audrey hepburn & frank sinatra. all the rogers/sinatra films all coen brothers films  shows: the short-lived sci-fi-future-shakespearean-cowboy-western tv show by joss wheadon, firefly. i also have to admit an interest in america's next top model for its legitimate representation of the fashion industry, the modeling world, and the craft of photography.  music: right now, i dunno. mostly older music: lots of u2, sting, sade, moby, massive attack, classic jazz/swing/big band (cannonball adderley, herbie hancock, diana krall, cassandra wilson, ella, louis, chet, count basie, tommy dorsey, etc). newer-ish: future sound of london, some indie stuff, ambient stuff like aes dana and old cocteau twins tracks. good company. love. kindness. comfort. creativity. beauty. after three cups of coffee, does it count as a meal?  how the word eye' is made of three vowels and not one is the letter i'.  anesthesiologists practice the study of anesthesia, right? so why aren't proctologists called analysts?  if i could chose a new word for an orange, what would it be?  whoever named the 'warthog' certainly didn't give it any sympathy.  if we use the term making love', are we better off always making love' with strangers since we wouldn't need to with those we already love.  is there any fruit/food sexier than mango? sorry ladies, i don't think chocolate is sexier than a luscious, ripe, slippery, sweet mango. but hey that's just me. i mean, if you said bananas are sexier than mangoes, well chillin' with a few friends and/or my sweet lil' dog. i've done night clubs, bars. i've done a-list-ing in nyc.  lose the pretension- be real... for me, now, it's about the people more than the event. dang, i already admitted i've watched antm. ain't that enough already?! lol.  at least two of my long-term girlfriends thought i was gay when we first met. lol. i've heard the whole: "dress nice, clean, sympathetic/kind, nice furnishings," etc... and the worst of all: "nice guy". lol.  i'm not gay. i'm secure. i don't have strong biases nor prejudices. i can talk circles around most guys when it comes to sports. i can more than keep up with most women when talking fashion. you want to talk, see if we have more in common, are open-minded beyond just hooking-up (i've never had a one-night stand in my life). maybe something good happens- even meeting a new friend. can't regret trying, right?  i, personally, think this scene typifies so much of what i love. yeah, it's silly, fun, sweet, and sentimental... aubrey hepburn & fred astaire  in the darkroom scene of funny face. (i'm not sure i can embed here. it keeps dropping out on page refresh?). huh.  cheers.
