full_bio: lemme see...i was born and raised in the northeast, got a couple ivy league degrees, and then moved out west to seattle. recently arrived in san francisco, which is like seattle if it were more urban and had better looking people. and more compact - i love being able to bike to work every day.  i'm a full-time geek by day and a part-time actor by night; film, theater, television, writing, performing, whatever, i love it. a lot - i love doing new things, maybe to an excessive degree.  i like get out and go biking or hiking or climbing or skiing or kayaking or ... i love that the city is perfectly placed for all this. exploration and discovery are great, especially in the city; there's a lot of cool hidden stuff - art, food, scenic vistas. and of course, travel; i just got back from 3 weeks in japan, and quit my last job to spend 4 months tooling about europe.  i cook reasonably well - i make a killer guacamole. expanding to baking. i'm learning spanish and french at an exceedingly slow pace, and have enough german to tell people to please stop asking me for directions at the train station, i don't know, i only look german, es tut mir leid. i play a lot of musical instruments...poorly. useless trivia and board games. if you enjoy recreational mental sparring, we will probably get on fabulously.  i love smart television - i'll watch pretty much anything hbo or bbc (as long as netflix has it).  i also enjoy movies, books, music, and food, and perhaps will come back here in the future to write in examples of those things.   out with friends - hopefully in a car or on a plane to somewhere interesting.

sign: capricorn and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i'm a freelance writer and a world traveler. i specialize in exploring areas that tourists can't find. this applies not only to physical destinations, but emotional and intellectual explorations as well.  after settling down in the east bay for about a year, i've explored and absorbed a lot of the vibes here, regarding the cultures, societies and attitudes, and i must admit, after seeing a good chunk of this country and some of the world at large, this place has some really interesting games to play and some very nice views to offer. better than most in our western culture. but it's not the only game and it's not the only picture, and it's nice to meet up with people who can see beyond the illusion of the bay area bubble, as nice as it can be, and expand horizons to a lived life, rather than a stylishly consumed one.  and dig this, i not only have radical theories about how this can be done (in so many diverse ways) but i've also (just as radically) proved it by living it, working and playing it.  it just so happens that currently i'm playing the hunker down and finish writing some books (fiction and non) to not only make my mark and dent in this world, but also a to help inspire others and co-create the next series of adventures.  being creative, it's a job. it's constantly fighting the forces of un-creation and habit.  so along with the novelty of writing a novel, i'm looking to meet like-minded people and females especially who can shake things up when even being creative becomes too habitual for comfort. a lot of occupy: cal and the oakland varieties. giving guest lectures at colleges around the bay on the subject.  the diversity of my writing comes from many angles; social justice, green jobs through clean energy/organic food, travel, making tomorrow better than yesterday by living today non-cliche...  doing whatever i can (right now it's writing, acting, investigating) to smash patriarchy and the dominator society/culture, to a partnership society can flourish ... circle of life and evolution through revolution.  work is work, but i'm also a fun guy to be around. well, i like to think i'm a skilled and innovative writer with a niche market. though i don't know how much of that skill i'm actually applying here on okc... being an entheogenic activist who rarely partakes. too rarely, really. it seems i'd rather have pizza and coffee these days. wtf?! i must be getting old... not being completely full of myself (only partially). casual heroics. sometimes. being comfortable with my level of control in extreme situations. my unconventional look, long hair, goatee, dressed in mostly black, but not goth, a smile that's ready to spread across my face.  oh, and lately, my outrageous sideburns. i don't know where they are going, but it's an interesting journey... too much to list, i'll add more later  the once and future king, food of the gods, the emperor wears no clothes, the island, even cowgirls get the blues, lady chatterley's lover, count of monte cristo, the war with mr. wizzle, steal this book, you can't go home again ...  animatrix, matrix, v for vendetta, adaptation, seven samurai, waking life, a scanner darkly, meet john doe, ninja bachelor party, yojimbo, excalibur ...  samurai champloo, cowboy bebop  tool, ratm, hybrid, some electronic progressive trance, dethklok. if you can dance to it, or headbang to it (whatever mood you're in), appreciate artistically, but not passively consume it. also: gruvis malt *optimism, even through the worst. (not the namby-pamby kind, the hard fought kind.) *earned enthusiasm, as jaded and cynical just aren't as fun. *a strong sense of justice. (and smaller awareness of imperfection) *a healthy libido *a creative mind that is up to having fun and exploring new ideas *sense of adventure thinking.  my books, and creative works, which focus on how life is lived, and how in theory it could be better... what a culture would look like if they actively engaged in experience enrichment fulfillment instead of monotony. how to co-create that culture. love, in many, many forms. how to honor and worship that love and have that love reciprocate. especially in female form.  how platonic friends are excellent too.  a for instance and an example of how my mind works: i spend a lot of time thinking about organic food. not just the superficial "yay, i'm going to pay higher prices for produce to be healthier and save the earth... somehow." as many whole foods consumers might as they pass through a check-out stand with a cashier who has no health care or union. but more along the lines of examining how there are 5 million less farms in the usa than 80 years ago (!). about industrial agriculture, coolie labor and wages for back-breaking work and horrible conditions (with no benefits). how being an organic farmer has become this aristocratic pursuit for only those who can afford it.  what a major land reform movement might look like if we really valued clean sustainable food justice. having your own land, being your own boss... instead of being a wage serf to the system.  gotta feed the revolutionary mind, and the revolutionary stomach.  oh, and huey freeman's series opening rant from the boondocks. you know the line. "doesn't that offend you?!" if not actively in an adventure or quest (or date, which can be both), brooding in my fortress of solitude. shocker: i have never owned a cell phone. i'm not anti-luddite; but i don't see the necessity of being on-call 24/7. somehow, i manage. you should message me if i message you. honestly. i don't write blanket form letters or crude come ons that are justly ignored, but i take the time to write individualized messages. so even if it's a "no thanks, not interested." great! good luck with your searches and i'll continue mine. but just because we're using computers doesn't mean we have to be robots about this; human courtesy counts!  you should message me if you are looking for a person who is challengingly (the good kind) unique, but thoughtful enough to catch the normals up to speed, if they want to take the journey. life is an adventure, dullness is the enemy.

offspring: doesn't have kids, and doesn't want any
full_bio: ugh, you know? i'm little and sweet and kind of an asshole. i ride my bike here or there. i'm a constant headache, a tooth out of line.  if you see me roaming around the mission, awkwardly wave at me so i can awkwardly wave back then question my life later. i'm a barista and a book-keeper at a tattoo shop and i cuss for a living. i don't have days off.  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2gkl_zojqjc  ugh making fun of people without them noticing. narrating dogs. television. that i'm not 5'7". we all got it coming; forgetting sarah marshall; workaholics, the new girl; top 40, punk/ hardcore; burritos and sushi because sushi is just little burritos. burritos, burritos, sangria, burritos, burritos, beer. beer. let's. get. weird. uuuggggggghhhhhhhhhhh i've watched the "to friend a predator" episode of workaholics about 15 times. multiple times in one day. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfvxvhb0qjo  always have a scheme or a poorly thought out plan. wake after nine but before noon. have roommates regardless of your age. be a success sexually but remember your failures. understand the world wasn't made for you. understand your parents are just people and be nice unless they unmarry. understand sexual partners know you better than your friends and treat them warmly. have definite opinions but understand the world does not care. judge people on how they will feature in d&d campaign. love animals. do not treat retarded people like lepers. really don't treat lepers like lepers. be kind to people. listen to renee and angela. do not be afraid of other people's opinions. understand art has a context and don't dismiss things outright. don't resent people with money. don't be married. do not have children. avoid hard drugs, they make you talk too loud and that's annoying. understand people have the right to be tasteless. approach doors with confidence and not fear. attach yourself to people who are funny, distance yourself from uptight squares. embrace the media. try new things.

full_bio: i am a born and raised new yorker and spent my whole life living in the heart of manhattan. i moved out to the bay area in early september to start culinary school and i'm in love with the area already. i was raised by theater people and thus have been one for most of my life, (you might pick up on this from my fairly healthy knowledge of musical theater). i even went to high school for technical theater but then decided to go a completely different direction for college. the last two years of my life have been spent bouncing around the departments at ccny and working in a scene shop in midtown manhattan, but now i've come out to the bay area to start over and try something new.  pretty much, i'm the tomboy that grew up. i was never very girly and even had a hard time with being feminine as a kid, but as i got older i slowly became more comfortable with myself and sort of fell in to my girly side. leather jackets, motorcycles and fast cars will always be a guilty pleasure of mine and i am not afraid of getting dirty. i can be a bit crude and foul mouthed, but it's just part of growing up in a scene shop with a bunch of burly carpenters as my surrogate uncles. if you can't deal with the weird crap that comes out of my mouth, you're shit out of luck. humor is extremely important to me; i just wouldn't be me with out it. i love a good laugh and i can take a good joke too. not a whole lot is intensely sacred in my book. i like to think that if you can't laugh at yourself and what life throws your way, then you're taking things way to seriously.  i love cheesey sci-fi flicks and really bad b movies. watching really bad horror movies had to be one of my most favorite past times as a kid and even now. mystery science theater 3000 was the shit when i was growing up. i also love a lot of cult films and was a member of the new york city rocky horror shadow cast for the last two years. being a part of a shadow cast has helped me open up to become a more outgoing person, and it definitely gave me a place to be as ridiculous as i want. i am also very open with my sexuality and i am not afraid to talk about it. you have to be pretty open minded with these things when you perform along to a movie about transvestites, so i think its intensely important to have an open view on life.  but over all i like to think i'm a pretty chill person, very little bothers me and i just like to have a good time in general. sure, i can be a bit quirky and strange at times but no one's completely sane right? besides, normal people tend to be boring anyway.  i am dolly dagger, ziggy stardust and glycerine queen. i moved to california to start school at the california culinary academy in san francisco. so by day, i'm slicing and dicing in the city but i hope to fill the rest of my time up with new friends and adventures around town. cooking, wandering, doodling, writing, styling people's clothes (costumer habit), acting foolish, solitaire, cutting up shirts, thumb puzzles, singing along to elvis or joan jett, fixing things, playing with special effects make up.  i'm no mastermind but i'm quite handy with a hammer or a sewing needle. i'm the blonde (now red head), semi-delicate looking chick who wears flannels and torn jeans and never takes her headphones off on the train. but once you meet me, you'll see that i ain't no flower. i've also been called cute for pretty much my entire life. i'll admit, i hate it but i've grown to deal and accept it. i will always look kinda young, and supposedly adorable.  i may be a little trashy, but i think its classy.  people also seem to love my hair color but personally, i don't care so much for it. its boring being a blonde. i like dying it various shades of red, pink and orange so it tends to change when the mood hits me. also, i never wear matching earrings. i loose too many of them so i just stopped caring. historical fictions and fantasy are definitely my favorite genres but i love trippy stories over all. my top favorites include alice in wonderland, through the looking glass, the queen's fool, automated alice, the other boleyn girl, wise woman, macbeth, behind the mask, i claudius, and the hollow's series.  i like almost anything, except for extremely cheesey romance movies. those i can pretty much do without. the faves are rocky horror, shock treatment, synecdoche new york, pulp fiction, elvira mistress of the dark, the pawn broker, fight club, nightmare before christmas, beetle juice, tank girl, the hangover, hedwig and the angry inch, one flew over the cookoo's nest, ghostbusters, fear and loathing in las vegas, slc punk, cabaret, spirited away, castle of cagliostro, chicago, alien, star wars, the shining, mall rats, dracula, i was a teenage werewolf, singing in the rain, hairspray (1988), blade runner, indiana jones, clerks 1 & 2, blues brothers, the producers, jay and silent bob strikes back, young frankenstein, blazing saddles, marx brothers anything, anything monty python, many cheesey sci-fi channel movies, robin hood men in tights, plan 9 from outerspace, the day the earth stood still, anything from wallace and gromit, and most zombie flicks. i have a growing list of movies i need to go watch including franken-hooker, let the right one in, the orphanage, inception (i know i know, leave me be) and things like that. i just was introduced to a movie called forbidden zone; oh my fucking god that was horribly funny, and just plain confusing for that matter. i love those kinds of movies.  i don't really watch tv all that much anymore but i do watch dexter from time to time. a good friend showed me the first season and now i'm kinda trapped. i used to watch a lot of black adder and fawlty towers as a kid. you might also catch me watching reruns of top chef, iron chef america, house, whose line, mst3k, futurama or next generation online.  i have a wide palette when it comes to music. i was raised on classic rock, country, r&b and jazz, but i have a growing love of ska, hair metal and punk rock. i could always have more music in my life. i listen to ac/dc, the beatles, iron maiden, the who, the animals, poison, motley crue, joan jett and the blackhearts, billy idol, the clash, carbon leaf, cream, daft punk, sublime, streetlight manifesto, mustard plug, reel big fish, the dandy warhols, basshunter, devo, david bowie, the doors, distillers, elton john, dropkick murphys, electric light orchestra, elvis, james brown, john lee hooker, jimi hendrix, janis joplin, the donnas, the grateful dead, ok go, pogo, pink floyd, chacko, the sounds, red hot chill peppers, radio head, queen, the postal service, the ramones, the specials, aquabats, sex pistols, the runaways, status quo, suzi quatro, vanilla fudge, wolfmother, world inferno friendship society, yardbirds, yngwie malmsteen, cream, velvet underground, blind faith, and plenty others.  food is slowly eating up my life and i really can't complain too much. though i am not italian in the slightest, i grew up cooking it and of course eating it! i make one killer risotto. i also make a really tasty peasant dish from france called tian (i really just enjoy spiffed-up peasant food over all). i absolutely adore spanish food though i kick myself for not knowing more recipes. i also love german food because it always goes well with beer. i don't know a lot of cajun recipes but they really interest me, plus i'm on a mission for the perfect gumbo recipe. i also like thai, japanese, indian, chinese, mexican, southern comfort, and good diner food. i also adore good brunches on the weekends. mimosas are a girly vice of mine. but a greasy cheese burger with bacon and avocado is always welcome. california has corrupted me in the ways of the avocado; i kinda want it on everything. and believe it or not i really don't like wine, or at least i've yet to find one i really enjoy. i'm more of a beer and bourbon sort of gal. good music, leather jackets, laughter, good food, good sex, and good company.  roller coasters and combat boots are a close second to leather jackets. life and all its oddities.  i never really stay on the same thought for very long. the only that might pop up repeatedly these days is what i have to do for school and what am i gonna cook/get for dinner. everything else is up to the universe to decide. looking for adventure, more often than not involving booze, hanging out in the city with foodies or hanging out with my room mates and friends. ask, i'll probably tell.  i'm a sucker for a man/woman with nice shoulders. it's just something i always notice.  fun fact: i sometimes slip up and get a bit of a southern twang when i'm not paying attention. it's just something my accent does unwillingly. i'm not from the south at all but a lot of my family is and hanging around them only makes it worse. you want to take me on adventures around the bay area in search of strange sights and sounds, great food or ridiculous fun. i'm down for it all. also if you're willing to teach me how to spit fire, weld or drive a motorcycle, by all means send a message my way!  i am not one for instant messaging so don't expect me to answer those too often. and i will not answer messages that just say "hi" or "hey, you're cute". sorry if that's a bit bitchy, but you're giving me nothing to go off of here.

full_bio: april 2012: my profile is turning into a long (boring) blog. final update! i now own my own little piece of downtown oakland and am calling the east bay home again. this is going to be an amazing summer.  december 2011 update: breaking news! i suck at dating! well, that's not totally accurate - i just feel like i'm supposed to be playing a game where nobody will take the time to explain the rules. having said that, the more time i spend with women (and the more okcupid horror stories i'm told) the more convinced i am of my own status as a mega-lotto jackpot of potential partners.  i was a bit discouraged recently about what i was (or wasn't) getting out of dating, but someone made a very interesting point to me - that the returns from dating aren't regular or incremental. the proper approach is to keep at it, because you never know how close you are to actually hitting the jackpot - and all those mediocre dates you went on until you did hit the jackpot won't be relevant anymore.  so, ladies, here i am. let's meet up and get to know each other a bit. it is a gamble, and of course i can't promise you'll win, but the prize is worth the risk.  =========================================  life moves pretty fast. some people reinvent themselves all the time out of their own volition. i reinvent myself because everything around me changes so much that the old me doesn't seem to fit anymore.  i've lived all over the bay area, and in a few spots in la (but i was born in new mexico, and grew up in new york).  i've gone off to college 4 or 5 times, and started majors in an equal number of disciplines (computer science, portuguese, piano, philosophy). somehow i never managed to wrap it up and get a degree.  i've worked a lot of computer nerd gigs. i've been a temp for a retail chain, a back-room jerk-dungeon-dwelling computer mechanic in a mom & pop shop, a tech support peon for a nationwide isp, a corporate trainer paid to ooze bullshit for 8 hours a day, and now i'm an ubernerd sysadmin at a gaming company. for a while, i was sure i'd be a dj, but it turns out my talent for self promotion isn't as strong as my talent for mixing.  i've been fortunate enough to visit 20 or so countries in my time on this planet, and have spent a few years overseas if you add it all up.  i'm a connoisseur and a hedonist, but that doesn't mean i lack gratitude or compassion. i drink deep, of wine and of life.  i love to fall in love. femininity makes my pulse race. when a woman has caught my interest, all it takes is a peek at the hairs on the nape of her neck or the curve of her collarbone to set my heart aflutter.  i'm also old enough to have some perspective. i've been around the block enough times that i'm able to experience an emotion without being controlled by it. maybe that sounds dispassionate, but i think it just means i leave myself open to feel things others shy away from.  i'm on this site because i want to start a new love affair. if you don't have any passion left in you, don't bother with me. i've had a shameful number of opportunities handed to me on a silver platter. shameful because i let them pass by.  the one i've got now is something special. my career, and this job in particular, has allowed me to grow into the man i aspired to be. i appreciate the responsibilities i carry, and feel fulfilled by my self sufficiency. i work very hard, but work gives me purpose. i look forward to my future, because the dues i've paid are finally coming to fruition.  right now, i'm grabbing onto this opportunity and wringing everything i can from it. appreciating beauty. that might mean the sparkle in your eyes, or it might mean the aroma of thai holy basil when it is first added to pad ka prow.  i'm pretty good at a few other things:  i was taught to cook thai cuisine by a fabulously talented chef. i'm pretty good at that.  i've spent quite a few hours honing my video game skills. i love the craft and the art of video games. i work at a gaming company. i don't have the time to play the games that i want to, but the games are a big part of me. i'm pretty good at them.  one room in my apartment is full of vinyl. i've collected a ton of records, and i mean that as a measure of mass, not a figure of speech. my taste in music is broad but deep, and i'm pretty good at knowing the right song to play for any mood or any time.  there are probably a few other things that i'm pretty good at too. you'll have to ask me about them when we meet.  i'm pretty bad at a few things that are relevant to dating. i'm kind of neurotic and stuck in my head, and am also very bad at figuring out what is going on the heads of other. i totally miss cues in body language, subtext in speech, and all sorts of other parts of communication that other people grasp intuitively. that means i have no idea when it is appropriate to go in for a kiss, and end up ruining plenty of romantic moments by trying to talk it through. my approach to deal with this shortcoming is to over-communicate... some people deal with it gracefully, others are put off when i say something along the lines of "i like you a lot and think you're very pretty, i want to kiss you right now". hopefully you find this kind of quirk endearing. when i'm concentrating on something i get a look on my face that is halfway between pain and annoyance. apparently, this is a little intimidating to those who don't know me.  i swear, behind what might look like a grumpy exterior, i'm feeling fantastic. music: i think people pay attention to this answer more than any of the others, so i'm going to break it down by genre and list a few more artists.  soul: al green, donnie hathaway, marvin gaye, curtis mayfield, the isley brothers, roy ayers, stevie wonder, rick james, prince, the meters, dwele, raphael saadiq, the gap band, ike and tina  hip hop: a tribe called quest, de la soul, the pharcyde, organized konfusion, j-live, common sense (before he was just common), edan, shinsight trio, wee bee foolish, souls of mischief  currently in high rotation in my dj sets: bugz in the attic, seiji, mj cole, zed bias, wookie, xxxy, george fitzgerald, ma1, julio bashmore, floating points, martyn, hackman, doc daneekah, osunlade  drum and bass: photek, adam f, high contrast, nu:tone, makoto, london elektricity, omar, roni size, dj patife, dj hype  other electronic oddities: jazzanova, truby trio, koop, most stuff on compost and sonar kollectiv  rock, or something like that: beck, steely dan, nellie mckay, the plantlife, talking heads, the youngbloods  jazz: john coltrane, milt jackson, charles earland, grant green, nina simone, mccoy tyner, orange pekoe, soil and pimp sessions  disco: tom moulton, larry levan, walter gibbons, shep pettibone  reggae/afro beat/whatever: jimmy cliff, toots and the maytals, fela kuti, cymande, gilles peterson, tony allen  i could keep going with this list for a long time, but that should be sufficient to give you a taste of what i'm into. i'm kind of obsessed with this stuff... can you tell?  movies: richard linklater is my favorite director, waking life and before sunset are my two favorite flicks that he made. charlie kaufman writes movies that flip my lid, and adaptation is my all time favorite movie. judd apatow hits the spot when i want to hear some dick jokes. stephen chow is the best when i'm in the mood for chinese. steve mcqueen is always good, and that 30 seconds of footage from the jazz club in bullitt blows my mind every time. wes anderson is occasionally brilliant, but nothing he does will ever compare to rushmore. i could watch the big lebowski every day unti i die. apocalypse now, city of god, annie hall, network, animal house, tron (the first one), american splendor, moon.  tv: the wire is the best tv show ever. it is the true epic of our time. there are plenty of other shows i dig too: mad men, firefly, strangers with candy, peep show, sherlock, six feet under, arrested development, louie, freaks & geeks, futurama, breaking bad, archer  books: i got a kindle and i've been devouring the reading material recently. my favorite book of the past few months is john dies at the end. some other recent highlights: less than zero, in defense of food, world war z, abraham lincoln: vampire hunter, women (bukowski), anathem, freakanomics, flatland, american gods, the rampaging fuckers of everything on the crazy shitting planet of the vomit atmosphere (yes, really).  comedy: bill hicks, louis ck, jerry seinfeld, richard pryor, george carlin, norm mcdonald, mitch hedberg, david cross. i love the craft of comedy. let's go out and see some stand up.  video games: chrono trigger, final fantasy 2/3, doom 2, quake, team fortress 2, battlefield 2, bionic commando, super metroid, castlevania, uncharted, portal, warcraft 3, batman: arkham asylum, darksiders, fallout 2, dead space, diablo 2, world of goo, dance central, rock band, metal gear, heavy rain i could really go on for hours with this one.  food: southeast asian is my cuisine of choice. thai, in particular. i also love vietnamese, burmese, japanese, chinese, korean, malaysia, indonesian, etc. mexican is right up there, but i love good food from anywhere. i'll try anything, and i'm only picky in that i demand that my food is made with love. when i cook, i full_bio: what i am into hiking biking swimming dirtbiking wakeboarding cooking weight lifting gardening  i am fun, organized, and adventurous. and most people think i am pretty funny. in a dry smart-ass sort of way.  well, i don't like trying to describe myself, but i'll give this my best shot. i have been told i act like someone a lot older than me. i'm up for just about anything fun. i've already listed most of my interests. life is short, and i'd like to squeeze as much out of it as i can. we will all leave this earth, some of us will have helped people along the way and had a good, fun life, and some of us won't. the two usually go hand in hand. sometimes at night when peets coffee shop closes i take the pastries and stuff they are throwing away and give it to the guys at the shelter nearby. i like to do little stuff like that sometimes. i would prefer someone else who is happy and enjoys life. i don't like lots of drama. i like to enjoy myself, and i'd like to share some of it with someone else. i enjoy anything from rock climbing, to weightlifting, to good food, to walking around downtown. lately i have been spending a lot of time wake boarding, dirt biking, swimming, bicycle riding, and been walking a lot more lately. i am usually in the gym at least 3x a week on top of that too. if you don't, that's fine. i'm not necessarily looking for someone just like me. i'd like to start out on a casual date or coffee (no pressure) and see where it goes from there. as far as who i am looking for, i am not sure. if we hit it off, great. if not, that's fine too. ideally i would like to have someone to spend time with and enjoy each other's company, but it all starts with dating. if it goes somewhere, great. if not, that's fine too. if i make a new friend that is good too. life is all about the journey. it would be fun to meet someone who is into the same things as i am, but i would be just as happy with someone who can introduce me to new things. it might be better to meet someone into different things than me, so we can teach each other new things. anything else you want to know, just ask! i exercise a lot and am very career oriented. i usually work hard during the week, and blow off steam on the weekends. on top of the gym, i usually swim a little over half a mile once a week in the pool. i eat healthy, live healthy, sleep well, drink lots of water, etc. if you don't that's fine, just because i do something doesn't mean everyone else needs to. i spend a lot of time trying to help people. i like to ride my dirt bike, tend to my garden and am doing my best (despite the economy) to push my career forward. gardening. eh, i'm ok actually.  i've got a bunch of fun stories from traveling around the world, scuba diving, survivalism, dirtbiking, etc. i think some of them are pretty funny.  learning from smart people. trying to ignore the knuckle-heads, lol.  i also seem to pretty good at pulling my motorcycle out of wherever i have crashed it in to. except for that one time it fell on top of me in a ravine. for some reason people think i am either some sort of law enforcement or military. never been either, i think it's the short hair, and because i work out. sometimes i'll use it to play jokes on people, lol. book, don't know. i read lots for work, and usually leave it at that. movie; the matrix. i like nine inch nails, amon tobin, portishead, tool, little people, odd nosdam, etc., but am open to lots of stuff. i have been getting into dubstep lately. i love pasta. i have rediscovered and fallen in love with pesto. mmmm... ...pesto. i am very simple. i spent a long time backpacking in the mountains so i don't really feel like i need much of anything beyond food and water. seriously. i am not very needy or materialist. though, getting off coffee would be a toughie. the weekend and what adventure i have planned for it :) depends. might be getting ready for a trip. i don't do facebook. i don't do myspace. but i do crash motorcycles :) oh yeah, i'm also a certified reiki healer. you want to talk. you aren't superficial.

sign: virgo and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i walk two french bulldogs 3 or 4 times a day. i swim alot. i'm pursuing an 'athletic' body type. i love to read. i'm looking for someone both smart and happy -a good head on your shoulders with a positive attitude- to enjoy life with. i love music. all of it.  i have two projects i'm working on: 1)a booklist ('1001 books you must read before you die') and 2) my blog (a fun hobby). i have a disability, the upside of which is i don't have to work.  a quote i like is "i have always thought heaven to be a kind of library." i'm omnivorous: i read everything. if only there were time for it all (i should learn to speedread, eh?). wouldn't it be cool if you could freeze time, and teleport, and time travel, and be immortal? i would totally read everything (and listen to all the music, too).  anyway, i was an international relations major at ucd (third world studies emphasis, minor in spanish). i've been to china, and would love to travel more. mostly, i love nature. i spent many childhood summers in the redwoods by the russian river. i haven't been hiking or camping in awhile, and would like to resume. among the things i like are documentaries, animation, nature shows, taiko drums, cirque du soleil, and of course good food. i bicycle everywhere. i grew up in san francisco. i'm a boar (which they say is most compatible with sheep or rabbits), but hopefully not boring. exercise (swimming, walking, bicycling), reading (i'm currently into buddhism, short stories, and the hunger games), staying informed (i read the nytimes daily, and try to read the economist and christian science monitor weeklies). musically, i like college radio alot, like harvard's whrb, cal's kalx, or gdradio.net (the grateful dead). my blog at http://jesseteshara.blogspot.com (please visit! & tell me what you think) . reading. and reading some more. did i mention i like to read? yup, reading.  i hope to live til i'm at least 120. that gives me another 80 years. oh, yeah! i tested 140 iq once. i won a journalism award in high school. i got a scholarship to cal. staying busy. i'm told i'm the kind of personality that keeps on goin', no matter what trials and tribulations life throws at me. i was voted most kind in grammar school, lol. i can't think of anything else. giving messages. spelling. ha. my aura? i'm not sure to be honest. i have blue eyes. probably the dogs. they're a lot of fun. but they get screamy whenever they see a cat, a squirrel, another dog... -douglas adams, isabel allende, dostoevsky, jonathan franzen, gabriel garcia marques, anne rice, michael moorcock, kurt vonnegut, mark twain. tent of miracles (jorge amado). the lost steps. the sand county almanac. -fantastic planet, grave of the fireflies. -i don't watch much tv. when i had cable (once upon a time), i enjoyed comedy, mostly (daily show, dr. katz, standup). i really miss beevis and butthead, heh heh. -i like to listen to music while i'm on the computer. -thai, sushi, chinese, mexican....  and sweets, a bit too much, probably: i.e. kerns, swedish fish, riesen, it's its, ben n jerrys, horchata, baklava, see's, mrs. field's... but i'm eating less sugar, on my health kick/ for daily maintenance. 1)basic needs (food, shelter, clothing, clean water, sanitation) 2)others, people, pets, friends, family, community 3)happiness, humor, smiles, laughter, joy, bliss, fun 4)stimulus (i.e. books, magazines, music, movies, the internet, the library, my blog)...reading and writing. 5) love (god? truth? reality?), or liberty. either one. 6)as david bowie sings, love is not loving. so loving, too. which is another way of saying sex, which maybe i should call intimacy.  of course, the buddhists say these are cravings or 'attachments', so maybe i should just stick to life and health, if even that.  ack. i should replace loving with life, health, and strength. i can do without sex. religion and spirituality health i just moved, and i don't have a routine yet. hopefully, dating! will tell over coffee if you like what i wrote. if you want to roll the dice! (or know more about me) and, preferably: -you don't smoke -you are not a tv addict -you are a pisces, taurus, or capricorn (they say), or virgo like me. -somewhere else says cancer and scorpio are good for me, too.

science, what i really value is success based on one's own creativity and life experience. navigating, cooking, budget travelling, making up words that sound german, untangling headphones, peeing full_bio: haven't had much luck with dating sites, but decided to update my profile in case my dating mojo changes. i don't watch much tv, therefore i'm easily entertained and can have fun doing just about anything, especially with the right company. i love to play sports but watching them bores the ____ out of me, although going to a live event can be fun. when i'm not sitting in front of the computer trying to convince you to date me, i'm probably on the computer doing something else, or enjoying the outdoors playing tennis, hiking, swimming, riding my bike or motorcycle if the weather's nice, skateboarding, ice skating, jet skiing, water skiing, wake boarding, playing at one of many snowboard parks in tahoe, hitting the gym, people watching, dancing, making music, poetry slams, emcee battles, going to shows, playing with fuzzy critters, spending time with friends and family, tech conferences and anything else that sounds interesting.  i'm honest and to the point, but at least try to express my honesty in an entertaining way. i don't like to play head games, though it's not so bad without the games. i think i'm addicted to traveling, but i haven't been doing enough of it lately and i'm about to relapse. i've been to hawaii and mexico several times, spain, amsterdam, italy, canada, beijing, and all around the states, just to name a few places off the top of my head. i've flown, gone on mobile home adventures, took cruises, climbed, rode different four legged creatures, snorkled, parasailed, free fell, held a tarantula, an iguana, a ferret, ...  i'm spiritual, but not religious. i believe that if we take bits and pieces from every religion, then maybe we can form something credible, but many religions have way too much hypocrisy, look down on free thought, persecute anyone who shows proof of something that contradicts the church such as astronomers who proved that the world isn't flat and that the earth revolves around the sun, when religion taught otherwise. plus, way too much bloodshed has gone on throughout history over who's got the coolest god(s), when it's still the same creator(s). i'm pretty sure that all the ignorance and violence over religion isn't god's will, just people's misinterpretation of it as an excuse to conquer and pillage other nations. i believe in common sense over hocus pocus, and try to keep the gears moving without loosing too many screws.  i'm trying to find somebody with serious long term potential, to share my life with and grow together as a family while leaving any unnecessary drama behind us. i work in the hi-tech field setting up, securing, and scaling online infrastructure that can host millions of daily visitors, and easily automate tasks across hundreds and even thousands of servers in minutes from a central location. i currently work for a video game company based in sf, and with offices in other regions like brazil, russia, uk, and china. my job is fun, and i love what i do, therefore going to work can actually be exciting, although technically since i have remote access to everything, i can get my work done from pretty much anywhere. i even hacked my android phone to access any piece of equipment with it, so i don't even need to carry a laptop around. thinking outside the box. figuring things out. i'm pretty handy too. i don't know, perhaps you can tell me. books: i mostly read computer books and stuff about network security and hacking, but i've also enjoyed reading books by authors similar to george orwell.  movies: office space, grandmas boy, the ex, just friends, super troopers, slumdog millionaire, the salton sea, the illusionist, surrogates, matrix, ...  music: i like pretty much anything with a good beat and positive, clever, and/or entertaining lyrics. i mostly listen to reggae, underground hip hop, acid jaz, trip hop, dubstep, drum & bass, jungle, ... some artists that i like are living legends, amp live, zion i, sage frances, mos def, talib kweli, hi tek, blackalicious, deltron, slug, atmosphere, k-os, bob marley, black uhuru, barrington levy, dub nation, dj vadim/oneself, othello, just to name a few off the top of my head.  food: i love spicy food. some of my favorites are mexican, indian, japanese, thai, middle eastern, greek, and i'm open to pretty much anything else. i was a vegetarian for a couple years, but now a days i eat certain meats, but just choose not to eat smart animals. i'm all about the seafood though. 1). loved ones 2). good hygiene 3). food/water n shelter would probably be important 4). the internet 5). music/dancing/exercise 6). mental stimulation world events. how so much ignorance can be continuously pumped through the airwaves. miseducated and unparented youth. why so many people put on a fake front to try and fit into a certain group? why some of the most religious people i have ever met have also been the most racist, cruel, and violent? making music. going out. visiting friends and family. i like to wear woman's underwear........ on my head!!! i can wear yours, you think you're my soul mate, you're bored and need somebody to talk to, you share some common interests and want an activity partner, or if you just feel like it.

full_bio: in any society, the artist has a responsibility. his effectiveness is certainly limited and a painter or writer cannot change the world. but they can keep an essential margin of non-conformity alive. thanks to them the powerful can never affirm that everyone agrees with their acts. that small difference is important.  it ought to make us feel ashamed when we talk like we know what we're talking about when we talk about love.  "i see a rhinoceros."  i recently started a blog about the place i live. check it out http://hardwoodstudios.wordpress.com/  also i shaved my head, original skinhead style. i miss my hawk. look up lupin the third, that's what i look like now. starting a revolution in the olive oil aisle of your local organic food chain.  inciting a riot at the dmv.  getting a prime college degree in table talk (and accumulating debt for it). laughing at my misfortunes.  holding my liquor.  political banter.  hiding the fact that i take myself seriously. i got a name you've probably never heard before. i love the smell of gasoline. problems. art. guitar. cigarettes. whiskey. sex. ronald reagan. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k4sxiesjlru i confessed my first love through a poem. you wanna start a band.  you wanna talk about art and stuff.  you're the cat's pajamas, and the toad's eyebrows, and the bee's knees.  you make no distinction between washing the dog and breaking the law but you can explain the difference between "good" and "well."  you need a toyboy.

full_bio: not sure how i wound up here, but in a nutshell, i spent a significant amount of my free time the last couple years working on a book (fiction, but substantively about mathematical philosophy, religion, and linguistics) and in so doing put certain aspects of my life on the backburner a bit - so i guess this is part of my effort to stave off social atrophication and maybe meet someone interesting. and frankly i'm curious about this whole world-wide-web thing ...  other than that, i'm not going to write too much about myself or ask it of you either. i'm not big on facticity as a rule - i'm more intrigued by who people are than what they are. and anyway, i'd rather save all that for a real conversation at some point ... daylighting as an attorney (ip lit) as i finish up said book. wouldn't mind writing full-time at some point. the great meta-goal is to raise society out of postmodernity - wish me luck with that. not being fool enough to think i'm "really good at" anything. yes, that's tautological - but well, hell. i don't know - not getting caught up in the day-to-day and week-to-week?  i will tell you what i'm terrible at though: playing the drums. i just started learning so cut me some slack. empirically, my sense of humor. though depending on the context, sometimes it's simply the sense that i care.  and i guess some people like my big eyes. but i suspect that's just because they see the above therein.  then there's the chance that maybe all of my feedback is biased and most people actually just see a big goofball. i'm pretty much a classics junkie all around, but generally anything with artistic integrity (or an absolute lack thereof) can captivate me. awful movies. great books. bad jokes. good times. old friends. new projects.  [my real answer is probably: "nothing." i honestly have few attachments. "the six things i could never do within" would be a more interesting question ...] life and language. paradoxes and the infinite. lazy freewheelin' road trips into the setting sun ... likely doing something derivative of my six favorite things. otherwise maybe playing hoops, fine-dining on beer & pizza, or possibly jamming with my slapdash beginner band wherein nobody knows how to play their instrument whatsoever (it's refreshing to have the bar set really really low sometimes). that reminds me: i'm also soliciting band names if you've got a good one. this is my second emprise into online dating. the first was in the 90s. there were only like 43 of us dweebs in the country "on-the-web" at the time, telnetting and usenetting along when ... anyway, it's a funny story i'm willing to tell sometime, even if i'm shaking my head right now as i think of it. you have a good heart and a good mind. those are axiomatic - i figure everything else i care about pretty much follows naturally from those. (though i suppose a good liver never hurt anyone.)

full_bio: i'm a nice guy. to a fault, perhaps. i can come off kind of quiet and shy, but it's all a deceptive ruse. once i feel comfortable enough, i'll open up and then just keep on opening. i value humor in others and a good personality; although, mine tends to run a little on the dry side. i think that's how i can sometimes get away with saying truly horrible things.  i am pretty open-minded and i'm always looking to try something new. one of my absolute joys is sharing the things i love with the people in my life, and vice-versa. to me, that's what an ideal relationship is all about. oh, and butt sex- of course. i'm currently working at a library and i am pretty happy about it. i find great joy being around books and the people who love them. i've also been doing some bartending on the side. trying to get my side hustle on, holla! having an opinion. also, i can cook pretty well. and i sing some mean karaoke. first, people usually notice my height. i think my tallness must be deceptive because everyone forgets about it until they see me again and they're like, "when did you get so tall?" mmm, since always? next, probably my premature grey hair. i like it, it's distinctive or whatever. lastly, that i look nothing like a "typical" juan. ah well, that's people's preconceptions for ya. oh boy, here we go...  books! i love interesting fiction that messes with your head. some of my favorite authors include: david foster wallace, haruki murakami, thomas pynchon, katherine dunn, raymond carver, patrick sskind, jg ballard, phillip k. dick and so on.  movies! i love dark comedies, psychological thrilllers, sadistic foreign films, japanese insanity, and blissfully unaware camp. also, a lot of the stuff that everyone likes is good too.  shows! i have a real weakness for modern british comedy. i mean a buying region 2 dvds off amazon.co.uk kind of weakness. basically, they produce the funniest, most twisted shit. anything involving chris morris or the league of gentleman is tops in my book. i also like us stuff like curb, parks and recreation, arrested development, mad men, and breaking bad. also archer, cause it's amazing.  music! various forms of pop in descending order: electro, euro, synth, dance, teen, urban, indie... and coming in the bottom two country and then punk- the lowest of all pops.  food! i love food! if you forced me to pick a favorite i would probably say thai, but i also love sushi and pretty much anything you can preface as ethnic. glasses, phone, ipod, laptop, books, and... slippers? my feet get so cold! the past, the future, and especially the present. i have one of those brains that is near-impossible to shut off. usually with friends, either having a casual night in watching some kind of madness or out and about at a bar or club. i would be perfectly fine dating my twin; even an evil one. i just haven't found him yet. you're down for whatever. oh, and if you find smart and funny oddballs appealing.

full_bio: for some reason, i can't come across nearly as funny on this thing as i am in real life. ah, fuggit.  i am an outgoing, but still quite laid back gal.i always have a smile ready for anyone. i was raised in the south so i've got southern sweetness, but still a very open minded and idealistic personality. i am very respectful and get along much better with people who are equally so. i'm very motivated, but can also just chill and do nothing..but only for a little while!  i am outgoing, mellow, and friendly i'm a cardiac telemetry nurse. i've been working as a nurse for a little over three years... and am now doing traveler nursing. for those confused, it means i travel around the country to hospitals with nursing needs. i work there for about 3 months at a time (unless i extend the contract) and then off to the next place. in other words, a bit of a gypsy. in the future i plan on getting my masters and becoming a nurse practitioner. cooking (mostly without recipes!), making people laugh, sewing, my job as a nurse, yoga! staying motivated, spontaneity, listening, trying new things, making friends, guitar hero! being a tourist haha is my friendliness, i suppose. i'll always smile and introduce myself like any polite person would do! if this means a physical feature, then i'd probably say my curves :) all of those in one category? wow...books-any ones i particularly like the storyline of. i honestly don't read as much as i'd like. hitchhiker's guide is one of my favorites, as well as old classics like matilda, wrinkle in time, etc. i'd love any suggestions...and maybe borrow here and there :-)  movies- i can enjoy so many kinds of movies..comedy, drama, documentaries, fantasy, some horror, a bit of romance if the storyline doesn't suck! some of my favs:wedding singer, close encounters of the 3rd kind, willy wonka and the chocolate factory, slc punk!, pirates of the caribbean, amelie, labrinth, dark crystal, almost famous, 50 first dates, romeo and juliet, cars, the notorious bettie page, shawn of the dead, grindhouse, kill bill, .  music-mostly rock...including classic rock, modern rock, alternative rock, punk rock, and some dance rock. my favorite band is pink floyd, and i also love kings of leon and many, many others. pandora is my favorite thing in the world...my radio stations on there are white stripes radio, thievery corporation radio, and a few others depending on my mood.  food-i'd have to say thai, though i love so many different kinds of ethnic foods. i just love food. food is so versatile, fun, and tasty!! 1. my cats (only 2!) 2. my car-i don't know what i would do if i couldn't get around whenever i wanted. 3. my chill time. without it i would go crazy 4. my good friends 5. variety-its the spice of life, man. 6. food-all my good eatins'!  i try not to hold possessions in too high regard...because they do not possess us, we possess them...and i'm also bad at losing things :p a smaller list would be what i don't think about, haha. i think about my family a lot, my friends, where i want to go with work, attempting to understand politics and government, what i watched on history channel that day, what i'm going to cook that night (or 3 am, whatever haha) i am definitely not one to party every friday night. if it just so happens i'm not at work, i may go out to the local watering hole. (the real gypsy, as our options go around here) i'm also just as inclined to stay in with a good friend, a j and a movie. i am not going to label myself a party girl or hermit..just happily in between. a girl never gets tired of sweet nothings in her ear...'just sayin :) you're laid back, not too shy, ready for adventures, not wanting to be too serious,and also accepting of alternative smoking habits!

full_bio: energetic, intelligent, outgoing, "got it together" gal out to do it all and do it well. obsessed with achieving my best and living a balanced and fulfilling life. my natural curiosity often motivates the spontaneous moments in my life and fuels hours of interesting (and often humorous) stories. i love learning new things, traveling to new places and meeting new people. i moved to the bay area 8 years ago and absolutely fell in love with all it has to offer. i enjoy live music (most types), salsa, tango, and swing dancing, snowboarding (at least before the ankle surgery- see, a story), riding on the back of a motorcycle, hiking, eating good foods paired with equally impressive wines, hours at the beach, and drives along the coast.  i am often searching, sometimes finding, and always loving life in short- i help companies make new friends through innovative products/services and delightful designs. i run a research consultancy that specializes in translating the consumers' story into winning strategic product and business solutions- and i love it! full_bio: i'm away for until mid july, 2012 traveling in europe. i'll respond to any messages when i get back. have a good summer, enjoy yourself.  describing someone with words is difficult. i would say i'm a nerd, but that means different things to different people. i'm somewhat introverted, but in situations i'm comfortable in i'm funny, interesting, and easy to talk to. paradoxically i don't have any problem going up to a stranger and saying hello. i've had wonderful conversations with people i randomly talked to on the street while traveling. in rome, for instance, i spent a nice hour struggling to talk to a local through the english they learned in school. meeting people, above all else, is what makes travel memorable.  for anyone who knows the myers-briggs personality profiles i am an intj and intp. here are two links if you want to read up on them: http://typelogic.com/intj.html and http://www.personalitypage.com/intp.html both describe me partially, though not fully. sometimes i'm more like one, sometimes more like the other.  though i am an interesting person and have tons of stories to tell (from travels and school) i don't always open up to people immediately. i need to build a connection first. specifically, trust is the most important thing to me. i don't lie - obviously i keep things to myself if i know they'll do nothing but hurt people, but in general i'll say exactly what's on my mind. having spent my entire life in school i figure why stop while i'm ahead, so i'm continuing with a phd at uc berkeley. :) having spent last year (2010/2011) studying in the middle east (it was an interesting year to be in that region), i'm very happy to be in a nice, normal (ha ha) city with things to do and people to meet.  i usually dance at least once a week. typically it'll be a combination of lindy, west coast swing, blues, ballroom, and recently contra. it's a great way to meet people and be social now and then. i enjoy dancing with beginners and especially teaching. while i was studying in the middle east i actually brought friends together and taught ballroom lessons.  i also go to plays regularly. i really enjoy theatre. it's very good at making you think, and i can really feel the artistic interpretation by the director and actors that i don't experience as much in television.  oh, and with the nerdiness comes the occasional movie night or better yet board games. board games are a wonderful social tool. they're fun, they let you interact with people, and you don't need to constantly stress about having something to say. the best party i've been to involved 4 tables, 25 people, 7 hours of board games followed by hours dancing.   just about every day while making dinner with my roomate we watch the daily show. as for other tv shows, i like psych, house, white collar, pinky and the brain, home improvement, firefly, dr who, big bang theory, how i met your mother, and dr. horrible's singalong blog. oh, i'm also a star wars fan - though i take my fandom lightly.  food... my favorites are italian food and my native german food. 1. internet - remembering all the things you need when programming up a strobe light for that party that just suddenly started is tough, a little cheating really helps. 2. dancing - i'm sure you've figured that out by now. 3. good friends - i already gave one thing that makes traveling awesome. visiting old friends is another. 4. batteries - it's a bit sad, i know. 5. blue sky with clouds - my definition of an awesome day involves a clear blue sky with a few scattered clouds. ideally with a clear sunset that illuminates the clouds in spectacular colors. 6. six?  well... usually dancing. sometimes i'll go to a play, though. i usually manage to have several too many things i want to do on friday - definitely the best day of the week!  message me if you want to chat or do something awesome or go dancing or play some board games or teach me something or you made it all the way to the end of this page or have a funny joke to tell me. seriously though, i'm looking for someone who can help me to expand my interests or wants to join in on mine.  oh, and in case you're wondering why i have such a stupid name: when you sign up and pick a name, okcupid suggests appending -tron or -o-matic to the end of whatever unavailable name you wanted to choose. i think both are ridiculous, so of course it's amusing to take both.

full_bio: ok ok... i guess i'll summarize myself... re-located to marin a year ago after 25 years in the bay area (rwc, menlo, palo alto) raising my two daughters (now full grown and (mostly) fledged. i'm devoted to my career in early childhood and moved for my current job, as well as to begin again in a new place, but close enough to keep my ties to the bay area. i love exploring marin- the hills, the towns, the marshes, on my new fold up bike as well as on my vespa 250. i work more than i should, and yet, it energizes me. i love most music, have a good ear, and can play most anything on the piano if it's in the key of c. staying present while growing older. learning how to lead with respect and integrity- and pause before i speak. climbing the 60 stairs to my lair, listening, riding horses and laughing it off. not sure- direct gaze? blue eyes? age spots??? definitely my direct manner. sushi, mexican, ethnic foods, movies/books that make you think, berkeley rep plays, singer/songwriter music, bluegrass/newgrass, indie rock, jazz, stanford women's basketball... nature, animals, my vespa, a good book, open communication, loving my work living in the moment with integrity and grace winding down from a long work week, catching a movie, filling up my weekend with fun stuff. what a stupid question...

full_bio: more than anything else, i like learning, and i spend most of my time doing just that (which is how i justify going back to grad school, reading finance blogs, and taking golf lessons). for example, one thing i learned the other day: did you know that the world record for holding breath under water (unassisted) is 11 minutes and 35 seconds? that's absurd. people are amazing.  i'm also one of those people that enjoys working and frequently is excited to go to work. i've been lucky to have enjoyed everywhere i've worked. though, this may not be the most flattering thing as my first job was a bob from office space and my second job was in finance. back in grad school for my last year  i have lived in a bunch of places - brazil; israel; denver; ann arbor; new york city; sf; and palo alto.  one thing i love about different cities is the first question people ask you:  new york - what do you do? sf - what do you do on the weekends? haifa- where did you learn hebrew? it's not very good. sao paolo - how the hell do you function down here without speaking portuguese? palo alto - are you a grad student? you look old i have a sick bird call. i haven't tested it actually hunting birds, but it's a pretty sweet bar trick. i wear really loud ties i grew up next to the first chipotle and have been hooked since i was 14. without exaggeration, i have eaten over 1000 burritos google ipad chipotle golf central park espn  obvious disclaimer: family, friends, health, and all that are clearly the top of this list, but given that everyone says that, i thought i'd be more interesting.   in both new york and sf, i never once turned on my stove. if it can't be made in the microwave, i don't know how to cook it. if there's a good chance we share a sense of humor. if you're passionate about something. oh, and bonus points if you're under 5'5"

full_bio: south american man, well educated, professiona, this is my homepage, so i have to say something about myself. sometimes it is hard to introduce yourself because you know yourself so well that you do not know where to start with. let me give a try to see what kind of image you have about me through my self-description. i hope that my impression about myself and your impression about me are not so different. here it goes.  i am a person who is positive about every aspect of life. there are many things i like to do, to see, and to experience. i like to read, i like to write; i like to think, i like to dream; i like to talk, i like to listen. i like to see the sunrise in the morning, i like to see the moonlight at night; i like to feel the music flowing on my face, i like to smell the wind coming from the ocean. i like to look at the clouds in the sky with a blank mind, i like to do thought experiment when i cannot sleep in the middle of the night. i like flowers in spring, rain in summer, leaves in autumn, and snow in winter. i like to sleep late, i like to get up early; i like to be alone, i like to be surrounded by people. i like country's peace, i like metropolis' noise, i like the flat cornfield in champaign. i like delicious food, wine and comfortable shoes; i like good books and romantic movies. i like the land and the nature, i like people. and, i like to laugh.  i always wanted to be a great writer, like victor hugo who wrote "les miserable", or like roman roland who wrote "john christopher". they have influenced millions of people through their books. i also wanted to be a great psychologist, like william james or sigmund freud, who could read people's mind. of course, i am nowhere close to these people, yet. i am just someone who does some teaching, some research, and some writing. but my dream is still alive.  this is a brief introduction of myself. if you are interested in knowing more, read my articles or take a look at my pictures. do not expect too much, and keep your sense of humor. i'm working in a hotel management area.

full_bio: i love traveling. i've been to more than 30 countries in the last 5 years. some of my favorite memories include witnessing the great migration in kenya, climbing machu picchu, and spending almost two months eating and beaching my way across vietnam, cambodia, and thailand. let's swap travel stories... (if you have any good ones, i might even share how i got deported from russia!)  traveling really embodies my personality: curious, open to new things, global, and a bit unexpected.  i am witty, ambitious, and loyal. i was born and raised in texas (hence the name), though sadly i don't have that cute southern drawl. i moved to chicago for college and spent five years there after for work, with a few stints abroad in johannesburg, moscow, london, and zurich. after grad school out east, i made the big move to sf about a year ago for work. i'm still exploring the city and welcome any advice! ... dreaming. ok fine, more like sleeping. you tell me... books: time traveler's wife, 1984, davinci code, devil in the white city movies: shawshank redemption, joy luck club, out of africa music: david guetta, euro remixes, adele, seed, the fray, sarah mclachlan, britney, garth brooks, carrie underwood, lady antebellum food: i love to cook and have a thing for ethnic food... thai, vietnamese, indian. and i'm a vegetarian. 1. my close friends. i'm an only child, so i value my best friends as family. 2. spinning. i used to teach an indoor cycling class and enjoy staying fit. 3. blue bell homemade vanilla ice cream. it's amazing and reminds me of home. 4. travel. i love seeing new things. it seems the list of places i want to visit keeps getting longer with each trip i take. 5. late nights. i am totally a night owl. 6. tbd ... how it's not good to overthink things. ... cooking for friends and sharing good conversation and a nice bottle of wine.  ... you're smart, sincere, and sane.

full_bio: i consider myself highly committed in my relationships and my work. i am an ambitious person, work had consumed me for a while when i first came to the valley back in '98, but i have taken my life back, it can be quite a challenge here, working for a fortune 5 company. i look forward to spending time with my friends who i am very true to and family. i have one sister and the most entertaining nephew.  i love biking, snowboarding, trips up the coast, or going to tahoe, this is what california is all about. i take my mountain & road bike out regularly on every trail up and down the skyline, marin and the east bay hills, also venture to other great spots on group rides or with friends.  i eat frequently and don't skimp (but stay very slim), my favs, are pizza, sushi, or just a good 'ol burger but i love many types of food and coffee ice cream is like candy to me. although i do like to eat healthy if it's an option. bars and clubs are not my regular thing, but when i go i seem to still get carded?! oh, and i take care of my body so i'm looking for the same.  i have discovered many things in life that i enjoy and remove stress from my life, but i try to learn and move forward. i run regularly and bike. enjoy ceramics for several years and dig away in the garden. i have a sarcastic/dry sense of humor. i think i have high integrity, genuine, sensitive and loyal...are these traits that the right woman is looking for? i'm a little reserved to start, but will get comfotable and show my colors before you know it. i hold a large amount of respect for women and i still will open the door for you, years later. i have learned a lot from my past relationships and i'm ready to build a healthy relationship. in transition after 13 years in south bay. doing the things i enjoy foremost and building from there. have made a few friends in the few months here so far. i have a good foundation and things are stable but i do recognize this is a new chapter or volume in my life's story. trying new things, listening, kissing, navigation, fixing stuff, details and being prepared. i've been told many times that i look like a certain popular comedian/actor or two...will let you guess that one. as well as the intensity of my eyes. that i have a butt, apparently not that common for guys?? mostly a live 105 listener; coldplay, rush, snow patrol, the postal service, oasis, days, chemical bros, green day, dmb, bnl, david gray, ray lamontagne, deadmau5...  into a wide variety of movies, pretty much open to every genre.  most of the books i read are non fiction. i do like some fiction from po bronson and audrey niffenegger. coffee...sleep...my bike...outdoor activities...igear...sex

full_bio: a crackling fire under a starry night: i am ancient, warm hearted, bright, passionate, enveloping, feisty, consuming, never ending love. i burn fast or slow, never fading in the darkest of times.  there's so much more to be said, and it's better said in person without the technology we've all become so addicted to. this profile is vague for sure.... also, i know exactly what i am looking for in my partner in crime.... clear intentions and authenticity stands out like a shining star! i'm a full time chiropractic student with about one year left... i am learning to help people heal their own body through releasing interference in the nerve system and with that comes a lot of self reflection of how i can serve many communities and myself through this unique art. i'm learning all the time... in particular how to have a blast in all that i do. life is a joy and a blessing, and i am well aware that there are only 2 things i control in it: what i focus on and where i put my energy. that being said, i am putting my energy and focus into my future, hoping someone wants to join me on the ride.... cuz like i said, it's gonna be fun! i am casually looking for the one (yes i do believe this... tho i don't believe being monogamous comes easily to humans, it takes work and i am the product of living proof that it does work)... and while i'm definitely looking to settle down, it's not until after we've had the most possible fun and exploration of the world and each other! loving. all kinds. cooking. all kinds art. all kinds i have an excellent sense of direction, like my internal compass is always right on. my eyes and that i'm pretty much always smiling. these things are better discussed in person... but i can say i'm into the good creative stuff. food, music/shows, dancing, art, movies, books in about that order. not really into sports or tv... but i do like live basketball! family and my people. goooooood food: cooking, eating, laughing over it... trees and sunshine... exploring places where trees hang out, or don't, but always sunshine. anything with ginger in it. going for the super chick thing: shoes, yes, a plethora to choose from! chaotic organization. music and movement. love and affection.. (the real stuff and points if you know who sings the song). finishing school so i can have my life back... which means time for bike riding, art shows, music, traveling, spontaneity and getting my business started! personal and spiritual growth and how i can help the world learn to love more. on a more immediate note... my next meal and getting things on my never ending lists crossed off! laying on the couch decompressing from the week or out with friends... dinner, drinks, dancing, shows, art murmur... which leads to the beginning of the list. let me get back to you on this.... you're intrigued and ready to get offline. you like to laugh and find real connection more important than dating for dating's sake.... you can float between worlds well and want to have fun!

full_bio: wildly curious. present. optimistic.  i moved out to the bay last year from ny, and it's been a great ride. i love the sense of possibility in the air, that i can go running on the beach in feb, and that balance isn't just an aspiration here. i like creating things, and understanding how people work. startup life has been great for this. listening / talking to people. asking questions. empathy. helping people learn things in intuitive ways.  capturing the essence of a place through photography. (i love taking pictures of people as a way to tell the story of a place).  having adventures in the kitchen. (i make a mean truffled scrambled eggs on weekends.)  being comfortable in new places. (i've lived abroad in india, china and europe, and so adapt easily.) my infectious laugh. positive / high energy. i love luscious prose (jhumpa lahiri), nonfiction with a strong human element (the new yorker, immortal life of henrietta lacks), and food books (michael pollan, alton brown). i recently bought a kindle and it's amazing how it's changed my reading patterns.  best film i've seen recently was "jiro dreams of sushi". it's about the best sushi chef in tokyo who is 85 and still passionate about his craft. it was a great meditation on what it takes to really master something. it also made me hungry. :)  i love exploring new restaurants, and picking up new things at farmer's markets (trumpet mushrooms, happy hen eggs). my favorite spots right now include -- kiji sushi, lers ros thai, una pizza napoletana and cheese board pizza. i'm always on the hunt for the best japanese, pizza, soup noodle and vietnamese sandwich places in the bay. let me know if you have any recommendations!  some of my favorite pandora stations are coldplay/u2, diana krall, dave brubeck, rhianna, and acid jazz. 1. travel / new experiences 2. great food prepared with love 3. supportive community of friends / family 4. a great book on my nightstand that i can't wait to finish 5. sunny outdoors 6. yoga / meditation how i can be better than the person i was yesterday. my next trips / how i can make the most of my weekends. (right now i'm looking into dusting off my camera to go to yosemite when the dogwood trees are blooming.) out at dinner / drinks with friends, or driving out of town for a weekend trip a minor claim to fame is that i've managed to have never eaten a mcdonald's burger. you're curious about the world... and want to grab a cup of coffee

full_bio: i'm just gonna come right out and say it: i'm geeky. i like video games, i studied computer science, and i have an embarrassingly large collection of fantasy novels and movies.  ok, now that i haven't scared you off, here are the important things. i've lived here in the bay area my whole life. i love california and can't imagine leaving it (well... unless i were to retire to the south of france... a man can dream).  i don't know when i realized i was a francophile. i experimented with cooking crme brle in my late teens. before i knew it, i was drinking red wine and taking french classes. it really hit rock bottom when i was huddled outside of sacre coeur begging for crpe scraps. but i went to american rehab, and i've been on the wagon ever since.  i did some acting in high school and college, until i found my true passion in improv. i miss it so much after graduating that i'm actually taking some classes in the city to get back into it. i think it's more fun to be spontaneous and accepting, and it certainly makes life an adventure!  i can be pretty sarcastic, but i tend to think of it as making fun of bad situations. black comedies and dark humor are always funny to me. and i respect wordplay and a quick wit. clichd silicon valley answer: i started my own company. but my company makes video games! so we have fun!  i'm left handed. i know, how many minorities can i be in at once? only other southpaws know my pain. books: tough call. i read pretty voraciously. right now i'm reading the pillars of the earth. i've been thought the usual: harry potter, lotr, hunger games, dracula, something wicked this way comes.. this is starting to not be the usual. sorry, i get carried away like that. oh, and i'm really big on sherlock holmes.  movies: zombieland, death at a funeral (if you say which one, you're out), indiana jones, back to the future, star wars (esp. the empire strikes back), classic disney, and modern pixar.  shows: arrested development. hands down. i also watched dead like me, firefly, and scrubs. right now i'm watching it's always sunny in philadelphia, community, the daily show, and the colbert report. law and order: svu is good too.  music: anything. i'm just as likely to rock out to the beatles, queen, lmfao, or rhcp.  food: i love food. i love cooking food. i love eating food. i love zagat rated restaurants, a good steak, and a glass of red wine. i've made everything from lasagna to french onion soup to pizza. 1) love 2) computers 3) books 4) laughter 5) red wine 6) video games the duality of good vs evil, light and dark, work and play. how much we impact the world and what we can do to make things better. whether turtles know they are slow, or if they just think the world moves by too quickly. i like going out to the movies, or to the bars with my friends. i'm no stranger to the city or santa cruz, especially when dancing or karaoke are involved.  although, there is something to be said for just curling up on the couch with some hot cocoa and a blanket. oh, i'm a singer. in the car, in the shower, walking down the street, even in the middle of conversation. i break out into the song on the radio or in my heart. rockband says i'm pretty good, so i'm gonna stand by that.  sometimes, though, it gets lonely singing solo. i'd be nice to have some duets. you are intelligent, witty, and attractive!  and if you don't think you are.... message me anyway!

full_bio: i am a fancy-pants hairdresser that plays with chemicals all day. i like to live on the wild side, so i don't wear gloves. when i'm not risking life and limb for the sake of beauty you can find me at the giant's game.  in addition: i am a san francisco native i have an insatiable appetite for karaoke i can drink you under the table  i am cheeky, unpredictable, and full of shit single handedly bringing back the perm, one head of hair at a time. uno, balancing hot plates on my forearms, the color wheel, dancing badly, awkward silences, making inappropriate interjections to end said awkward silences, recommending the appropriate shampoo for your hair type dr. jang tells me i have really nice teeth. books} catcher in the rye, anything garcia-marquez, still life with woodpecker, geek love, the bell jar, twisted shit you shouldn't read at night, like knockemstiff. just finished the family fang, which has nothing to do with vampires.  movies}troop beverly hills  music} gypsy brass bands  foods} tacos, green papaya salad, avocados, alfajores, and those corn on the cob shaped fried custard filled dough things that you can only get in chinatown  i once spent hours roaming chinatown in search of these corn on the cob custard filled doughnut things. i thought i found them once, by following the smell of fried sweet dough, but alas, it was only the fortune cookie company. 1) boys 2) jameson 3) clean underwear 4) tabasco 5) mayonnaise 6) karaoke the balance of good and evil, global warming, peace in the middle east, and why it isn't socially acceptable to drink before 5pm ignoring the fact that i work saturday mornings i haven't completely grasped the art of flirting. whenever i think i'm doing a good job, shortly thereafter i'm asked why i'm being such a jerk. you are original, you are funny, you live locally, and you enjoy a drink (or seven). vegetarians, vegans, ex-cons, pot heads, the unemployed, picky eaters, light weights, sober people, the french, and men who do yoga need not apply.
