full_bio: i grew up in the bay area and currently live in the city. i'm a true italian. i have a big fun-loving family-- we cook together, party together and know how to have a good time. i grew up playing sports and like being active. i love food, the sf giants, movies, lake tahoe, trying new things & traveling the world.  i'm as real as they come and hate drama. most circumstances can be solved very simply! in short, i'm very honest & straightforward.  i believe that everything happens for a reason and that karma can bite you in the ass.  i will try anything once (except skydiving or spicy foods) and am a big fan of variety- whether it be something as small as a meal to my everyday work routine.  i'm just as comfortable in a tshirt and jeans as a dress and heels. i currently work in sales for a printing company. i embrace the "work hard, play hard" mentality.  usually on weekends i am trying a new restaurant, going on a hike around the city, out for a night of drinking or playing sports. * cooking * randomly spouting off movie lines * playing sports * making people laugh my smile..personality wise, probably how friendly and sarcastic i am. movies: the usual suspects ace ventura pet detective bridesmaids la confidential silence of the lambs no country for old men memento as good as it gets the green mile my cousin vinny minority report the godfather elf any pixar movie documentaries that make you think .. i could go on forever  shows: dexter nurse jackie sons of anarchy walking dead seinfeld the league 60 minutes  music: i have a very eclectic taste: david gray, red hot chili peppers, florence and the machine, janet jackson, grace potter & the nocturnals, journey, lady antebellum, sugarland, cee-lo green, lupe fiasco, coldplay, chris brown, drake, frank sinatra, michael buble, adele, ne-yo, phoenix, 80's music, r&b, techno....  food: italian, greek, mexican, japanese, thai, indian...pretty much anything. pretty healthy overall but everything in moderation. * sleep * friends & family * a sense of humor * music * being active * good food what i want to accomplish next..what my next meal is..if only i got 30 more minutes of sleep last night. usually at happy hour unwinding from a busy week or getting dinner with friends. there's a decent possibility of the netflix queue calling my name. despite being athletic, i'm also a spaz.. always hurting myself! you are down to earth, can laugh at yourself (and others) and like to explore new things.  if you think humans are better at determining common interests than a computer is.  don't bother if you take or post photos of your body parts.

yup. green? yup. yellow, even? you bet, just put me in the sun. books: the bell jar, a prayer for owen meany, extremely loud and incredibly close, cat's cradle, ender's game, the fountainhead, and principles of modern chemistry.  movies: eternal sunshine of the spotless mind, wedding crashers, the big lebowski, swingers, dr. horrible's sing-along blog, forgetting sarah marshall, a beautiful mind, gattaca, fight club, i heart huckabees, being john malkovich, batman begins, the boondock saints, and office space (according to my netflix rankings).  music: sorry, i'm terrible at remembering band/song names. lately i've been listening to a pandora station based on starfucker and i like it. please feel free to judge me.  tv: arrested development, modern family, the office, 24. though i don't believe i've ever watched any of them on a television.  food: i'm open to most anything - though i love a good burger & guinness (pour house texas burger, anyone?).   out in sf or berkeley. or watching netflix with a glass of wine. or sometimes in lab.  you're awesome.

full_bio: hi. i'm bret. i am from ohio and moved to the bay area back in december. after graduating i lived in cincinnati, oh for one year as a broker and got sick of it rather quickly. i then moved to washington, dc and absolutely loved it! but now i am here in the bay area and don't know why it took me so long to move here. i love my family and friends. i like to think i was raised well and that i am a well rounded person. i love sports, all sports, not including nascar. one of my favorite things in the world is going to the ball park, doesnt matter which one but i am a cleveland fan all around and more then proud of it but am slowly becoming a giants fan as well(different leagues so they never play eachother). i'm pretty much up for doing anything. i love going to the movies, playing sports outside, going to the ballpark, roadtrips, concerts(rock, alternative, indie rock, and i also love country music),and a huge fan of stand up comedy. i go to a lot of shows, concerts, and athletic events if you're down to go?!? just living life as it comes really. i run my own company with one of my best friends from high school that we started back in august and am loving every second of running a start-up and being in cali. sports, history, business, cheering people up, random knowledge, being random, singing every song that comes on, disney trivia, people watching etc.  books: tuesday's with maury. how to win friends and influence people. dante's inferno. spending time in the loo, politics within the simpsons, anything that has to do with history, lincoln's bio, in the footsteps of the band of brothers, and currently reading the short stories of edgar allen poe(i know right?!? but they're actually really good).  movies: wedding crashers, forgetting sarah marshall, anchorman, dodgeball, goldmember, super bad, grandma's boy, liar liar, all shrek movies, kung foo panda, bruce almighty, eternal sunshine, inception, and every disney movie(mostly the classics), and i pretty much laugh at anything that comes out of will ferrell's mouth. braveheart, last samari,cinderella man, for love of the game, truman show, i like to watch planet earth, life, america: the story of us, and steven hawking every once in awhile as well.  tv shows: modern family, simpsons, family guy, american dad, shameless, californiacation, breaking bad, justified, community, parks and rec, workaholics, east bound and down, the killing, the walking dead, game of thrones and a few more  music: rock, indie rock, rock alternative, and alternative including cartel, red jumpsuit, 30 seconds to mars, oar, jack's mann, mumford and sons, three days grace, and many many many more. and i also love country music as well. blake, brad, jason a, zac brown etc. but toby keith is annoying!  food: anything!! i love trying new food..mexican and sushi are awesome. midwest values root beer floats music cereal...every kind! friends and family the beach history, business, how and why people can do certain things, and trying to wrap my mind around how big the universe is and how small we all are.  what cereal am i in the mood to get next. in the city with my friends, hanging out at home watching a movie, going to the movies, at a concert, ballpark, or shinanigans on the beach.

full_bio: grew up in southern california. moved to northern california for undergrad. ventured across the bay bridge last october (moved from berkeley to sf).  i believe in hard work, good coffee, and having a good breakfast.  i think a sense of humor is one of the most attractive qualities a person can have.  i love to cook -- love it even more when i'm cooking for those dear to me.  morning is my favorite time of day. i'm happiest in/near water. i'm a beach girl at heart and desperately missing the sun. i would live in a sundress and flip flops every day if i could.  part bookish. part adventurer. part girly-girl. part goofball. part super serious. part homebody with wanderlust tendencies. part mover and shaker. graduated from uc berkeley in may 2011. rhetoric major (and commencement speaker), creative writing minor. spent the last nine months working as a writer for google.  on the list of fun to-dos: trying out new coffee shops and restaurants, strolling through any and all parts of sf, seeking out live music, visiting museums i haven't been to. oh, and finally watching mad men. haven't been this excited about a show since lost. * also: constantly trying to rally a group for pub trivia. have a group you play with or a favorite spot? need a new teammate?  i write for pleasure as well, and consider myself so fortunate to have studied with some truly phenomenal poets at uc berkeley and umass amherst. grateful to have won a handful of prizes/awards. stoked on recently being published. finding humor in the everyday, doing things for the people i love, smiling at strangers, picking out gifts for people, doing crossword puzzles in pen, word games, coming up with new recipes, and having superhuman strength for someone my size... i also play a decent game of ping pong for a girl and make a seriously mean breakfast in bed. you tell me. augusten burroughs, vonnegut, proust, wilde, carroll... poetry: whitman, wallace stevens, john ashbery, ben lerner, geoffrey g. o'brien, graham foust, timothy donnelly...  movies: roman holiday, la femme nikita (the film, not to be confused with the beyond-awful television show), wet hot american summer (what? you haven't seen it??!!), royal tenenbaums, good will hunting, gladiator, batman begins, v for vendetta, singin' in the rain, some like it hot. *more recent movies that i really enjoyed: the town, the fighter, zombieland, an education, the hurt locker.  shows: arrested development, the league, workaholics, top chef. mad men and lost warrant restating.  music: old, new, and lots in between. here's a sampling: dylan, cash, neil young, ray lamontagne, josh ritter, lumineers, yeah yeah yeahs, andrew bird, broken social scene, phantogram, buena vista social club, the ditty bops, elbow, elliot smith, jose gonzalez, joao gilberto, m. ward, thao, soul position, ryan adams (yes, i'll admit this).  food: i never get sick of tacos or ceviche or the larger categories of mexican and peruvian. also love japanese, tapas, seafood, and mediterranean. family and friends laptop cold-pressed coffee books the ocean flip flops shouldn't it be "go, bears!"... how does one "go bears!" anyway? no, seriously -- all things language and words, some more productive than others. i'm often writing things in my head as i go about my day (a proclivity that i sometimes wish i could stop). also: places i've traveled to and want to travel to. notable highlights of this last bit include bruges, the baths at virgin gorda, and someday sailing throughout the greek isles. possibly out with friends or in with a movie. i've been known to rock out to oldies in the privacy of my own home... i clean when i'm stressed. you're easy going, down-to-earth, honest, and generally happy/positive... someone who works hard but doesn't stress a whole lot about things, knows the value of getting out and enjoying life, and sometimes likes to just kick back and take it easy.  i'm a sucker for a warm smile and great sense of humor.  if you're someone who likes to hit the bars every night, we probably aren't the best match. also not into super high-maintenance guys (read: you're really fussy and/or overly into your appearance).  i think common interests/hobbies are important, but identical personalities are not. i think ideally, two people should complement each other.

full_bio: i was born and raised in los angeles, but i've lived in sf on and off for many years. i went to pittsburgh for undergrad, amsterdam for grad school, and studied abroad in australia. if it's not obvious, i really like to move around and experience all the world has to offer, but i'm certain ca will always be my home. i support wildlife and ocean conservation causes and do my share to protect the environment. i work in breast cancer research in palo alto and find my job to be rewarding in so many ways.  i like: wine, movies, stand-up comedy, animals, shoes, whiskey, going to brunch, home decorating, rocking out in my car on a summer night, sleeping, road trips, really spicy food, the beach, shopping, sandwiches, geography, traveling, coffee, and running. i'm a caring, down-to-earth, genuinely good person who treats people well and in a way i expect to be treated.  i'm not interested in going on 1,000 dates; i just want to find someone who's so great i can leave this site for good. i would like to find someone who is career driven, but also has the ability to balance their life and make time for a relationship. also someone who's confident, smart, has a dry sense of humor and wit, and enjoys the finer things in life. someone who enjoys weekend trips or just staying home to watch a movie, drink wine and cook dinner together. lastly, i want someone who spoils me, but also likes to be spoiled in return.  reading people people usually tell me i smell good and that i have nice eyes. richard dawkins, sam harris, christopher hitchens, bertrand russell, david cross. comedies, 80's ficks, burn notice, the big bang theory, cougar town. the boss is my favorite musician, but i like most music with the exception of "head banging" and "elevator" music. family, friends, hot sauce, coffee, laughter, and sunshine  watching a movie at home or going to happy hour with friends. i'm obsessed with lifetime movies. you're honestly interested in a relationship, you're not on the rebound or too busy to date, and you like my profile and think we have things in common!

full_bio: development of new abilities and the advancement of existing ones is my strongest drive in life, which defines a very large part of who i am. my passions are numerous and through their natural ebb and flow of motivation i find myself almost constantly enthralled by some wonderful skill to pursue, some carefully planned object(s) to create, or endlessly enjoyable research to assist either cause.  because of this, finding fun exciting people to share things, do things, teach things, learn things, and enjoy things with is rather high on my priorities list.  as for some things i'm into:  i've done parkour for 5+ years, and would have to credit the discipline as being possibly the most important and enjoyable in my life. if you ever feel the interest, i'd be more than happy to give you a 10 minute lesson that could very well change the way you look at and interact with the world  beyond parkour, i like to explore the physical world in every way i possibly can. i rock climb (quite a bit), skimboard, surf, play paintball,rock climb, do partner acrobatics, bike, ride a unicycle (trials), climb trees, hike, backpack, ski, and dance like a fiend to drum&bass.  when i've satisfied my physical spirit or it's too dark out for the above activities i sew(a lot), juggle, do photography, model, play backgammon, tie knots, and dabble in endless underdeveloped skills required for creating the many different things i love to create. i also love playing magic and board games (ricochet robots and jungle speed are my two favorite games ever, small world is quite good as well) an engineer by schooling with a background in consumer electronics, i am currently working in hardware r&d at sony playstation. the jury is still out on how passionate i am about engineering, but for now it seems to make the most sense. i work pretty hard to stay active in my other interests, though, and excitedly search/think/dream for my true "life calling," if it ever does come my way.  my most active searching in the that direction at the moment is my attempts to design and manufacture a line of backpacks for skimboarding. currently, progress is good =) exploring the world pushing myself enjoying myself creating things learning things thinking about things jumping onto, under, around, and all over things my hair (long), my clothes (colorful), my energy (exuberant), and maybe my shirtlessness (frequent)  you should of course let me know your observations, though. music is a very, very great passion of mine. while i listen to many different things, i most frequently gravitate towards electronic music - mainly drum&bass, breakbeat, trance, and downtempo/ambient. if you would like to start a somewhat inexhaustible conversion, then mention music!  as much as i would like to, i have not recently figured out how to integrate reading into my regular life. irregular life has resulted in quite a few books of notable interest, however: the ashley book of knots, atlas shrugged, the virtue of selfishness, zen and the art of motorcycle maintenance, and anything calvin and hobbes, to name a few.  my favorite food is home made food. i make a very mean waffle. tranquility challenges exertion inspiration exploration music "there are no evil thoughts except one: the refusal to think" -francisco d'anconia  i think about the concept of "fun" vs "progress", about motivation, and whatever it is that makes life worth living.  i think about all the awesome things i'd be off doing if i wasn't doing the awesome things i was already doing.  i think about what's currently on my workbench and what's next in line.  i am hopelessly in love with doing things if you have a dog you'll let me play with if you like mornings, and would like to go adventuring at 6am if you would like to do more than just eat lunch on your lunch break if you like to go skinny-dipping in the ocean under a full moon if you want a project/sewing partner to make cool things with if you would like to learn about one of my hobbies if i would like to learn about one of your hobbies if you would like me to model for you if you would like to model for me if you tire of the easy going types, and want a bit more excitement

full_bio: i'm a 24 year old graduate from uc davis. i love to live and laugh and most appreciate those that can do the same. i also love to get out and do almost anything, be it sports, watching movies, going to the bars, or whatever. i'm most looking for new friends around the area as i just moved back from davis and don't know many people outside my work. i'm not opposed to looking for a significant other but most looking for a friend. that being said, i'm happiest when i'm active, talking to people, helping others, or working with my hands. i'm a person who most loves honesty and i really like to pride myself on being honest with others. i'm always trying to better myself in any ways possible. i'm a real romantic at heart and to me, it's not necessarily about always going out to dinner or drinking at the bars but being in the company of those you care most about.  i am kind-hearted, happy, and friendly i was working at genentech, but i really couldn't stand the way they treated their interns. so... right now i'm just trying to "keep on swimming" as dory might say. when i'm not working i'm usually relaxing at home helping my little sister in classes (because as an older brother there is no greater means of torture then making her read and do math) and also going out in the company of friends to find random adventures, or just settle at drinking at the bars. i love drawing. a long time ago i used to draw portraits of people but a lack of time has prevented me from doing it lately. i also love cooking. i wouldn't say i'm a professional but i think i do a pretty good job making some pretty weird dishes haha. i'm also really good at making people laugh and cheering people up when there feeling down, but that's the goofy side of me. my funny eyebrow (you may understand if you met me in person haha), my laugh and i guess my optimistic personality. i also have been told i also have a very warm smile. especially my laugh. i have been told that when people hear my laugh it makes them want to laugh. catcher in the rye has always been one of my favorite books. i don't know why but it just moved me in some weird way. in terms of movies i have so many favorites but if i had to give a number one i would probably say pulp fiction. some others that i love include superbad, reservoir dogs, taxi driver, goodfellows, the big lebowski, finding nemo, full metal jacket, iornman, and many more. i really don't have a particular taste or preference in music i guess there is the proper time for almost any music. and i like all types of food really. 1. music, especially from the 80's 2. my family, as they are always here for me. 3. laughter, because without it i don't know what i would do 4. faith in myself and those around me 5. my appreciation of the little things in life, like a sunset or a scenic drive when your stressed. 6. and finally, koolaid.... yes koolaid just because it is awesome.  i used to have love on my list but as i live longer without it i realize love isn't a necessity in life, love is just a wonderful addition to it. the future but in all honesty my mind drifts a lot of places. do to the nature of my work, no friday is typical. some fridays i work others i do not. it really just depends. but, when i do have what i would consider a "typical friday", it's usually spent with good people in good company. although lately my friends have become lazy and docile :-\ i guess that i'm a closet nerd? i actually have a zelda tattoo, but you'll never see it unless your lucky. lol i like to play video games in my down time as well as work out. i know i'm very weird in that way haha you want to hang out, get to know me, or even just talk? i guess there would be other reasons but those were the first that really came to mind.

full_bio: i'm having fun. lots of it. striving for a righteous existence. spending time outside, as much as possible. listening to music, as much as possible. learning and learning and learning.  what i'm really doing with my life is science. i love science! and taking care of kids. though these days, it's mostly science that i do. stuff.  making my friends guffaw. dancing. reading. getting bruises. drinking coffee.  i'm really bad at moderation. but i'm working on it.  i like those things very much.  this morning i was totally engrossed in the book endurance, shackleton's incredible voyage. he makes me want to go outside and discover new lands. without, you know, getting lost in antarctica for a year.  i do love to read -- contemporary american fiction, early 20th century stuff. faulkner and orwell and fitzgerald. i like nonfiction a lot as well. apparently, i am about to get into some serious adventure stories after i'm done with the shackleton one. i like the new york times. i like the new yorker. i like the new york review of books.  apparently, i like literary things from new york.  and really, i love to be surrounded by books. they are my comfort.  few things make me happier than music. music is the best. i was raised on classical music, but these days, i listen to independent rock and roll, like everyone else in this city. the past few weeks, my favorite artists have been mount eerie, polica, the dodos, horse feathers, au, sharon van etten, youth lagoon, beach house, gayngs. this will change in the next few weeks. my favorite concerts of the last few months were the alabama shakes, sharon van etten, wild flag, tune-yards, tanlines. but really the best was a few months ago in new orleans, and my buddies and i stumbled upon a trio at the maple leaf which blew my mind. best show i've ever been to.  music!  as for shows, i first think of rock and roll. and then i think of npr and radiolab, and this american life, and sound opinons. those shows are wonderful. tv, i can do without. though i do enjoy a few hulu-provided tv shows. 30 rock, parks and rec. and, there has never been a better tv show than arrested development. as for movies, sadly i fall asleep while watching movies. often.  food -- i love. most kinds. i try to eat healthfully and conscientiously, but, man!, i love me some bacon.

offspring: doesn't have kids, but wants them
full_bio: i'm a polish leprechaun, working as a self-employed beastmaster in the bay area. i currently share a roof with my rescued critters ~ a dog & a parrot. though city slicker by current geographical coordinates, i will always be a hillbilly at heart, born & raised somewhere in the foothills of appalachia... i will flee for the mountains again one day to make a wounded utopian nest for myself, my loved ones, & any other dispossessed critters and madfolk out there who wanna join me when the deal goes down... but for now the ocean is my home & i adore it here. animals are my life & sanity. within the next 5 years, i intend to buy some land & start my own sanctuary / biodynamic farm / hillbilly haven somewhere in the pacific northwest. the more folks i can network with along the way who share my vision & have anything valid to contribute to my great work, the happier & more content a soul i will be. i believe in the power of true community & enjoy the company of others who do as well. relating to & speaking with animals, drinking whiskey like the hillbilly i am, listening, giving good advice, hugs, reading people like dogs, finding happiness in the simplest of things old geezers & homeless dudes tend to dig my collection of ugly old man hats first & foremost... & little girls often notice the big hairy meathead canine so perpetually by my side before they run away squealing in terror... but beyond that, i have no idea too many, so i'll stick to favorite books ~ fiction: watership down, house of leaves, the little prince, geek love, starmaker, zen & the art of motorcycle maintenance... anything by kurt vonnegut, david mitchell, china mieville, jrr tolkien, barbara kingsolver, alan moore, ursula le guin, hp lovecraft... poets: ts eliot, octavio paz, ee cummings, sylvia plath... non-fiction: the world without us (it's like post-apocalyptic porn for science dorks. love it!), cosmic serpent, end of time, of water & the spirit, history of phi, tibetan book of the dead, secret life of plants, other end of the leash... & i'm currently madly in love with all things rudolf steiner 1. my critters ~ the whole animal kingdom in fact. they've been my greatest deepest love since the age of 4. 2. nature ~ too much concrete, machinery, & technology can be crippling to the soul, so i need to detox in the natural world often. 3. my banjo ~ my girl beulah is the best medicine 4. passport ~ i live in a constant state of migratory restlessness & do my best to leave the country at least once a year 5. books ~ i need them like oxygen; they are constant sources of inspiration & personal evolution 6. moonshine ~ the poetic cosmic kind as well as the drinkable rotgut kind. both kinds give me wings & sing my soul. critters mostly, theoretical physics & time theory & other science-geek stuff, my banjo, genetic mutation & evolution, animal cognition, crazy dreams, words words words, cryptozoology, my imaginary friends in faraway lands, ghost subculture, non-ordinary realities, parasitism in all its myriad forms, fractals, elementals, the anima mundi, fungus & bioremediation, cosmic winks & cosmic kicks in the tookus, death & rebirth probably bustin' banjo beats for birdies & beasts... or going on whiskey walkabout through the hills & secret stairwells of san fran with my dog... i often work 7 days a week, so there really is no 'typical friday' for me... i learned the secret of life from the belly of a happy snoring grunting pig & have been altered for the better ever since.  also? i believe in faeries. no, *really*. i'm a celtic pagan by blood, yo. the elementals are my homies.  i have a mullet. ...your heart is bigger than your ego. ...your integrity runs deeper than your wallet. ...your intellect & wit are sharper than your tongue. ...your soul is more beautiful than the skin you're in.  ...also, we'll get along well if you enjoy the company of critters & kids, & if they love you back. they are far better judges of character than most adult humans & i take their intuition to heart often.

full_bio: ok time for a new self-summary im chriss (2 s' on my birth certificate im not being weird) im 20 im in a relationship... that is kinda... idk. so im not on here looking to hook up. its like my release from facebook i guess lol i used to be happy and carefree, but recently i just feel like im getting less happy and more stressy. mostly down to my relationship :[ im nice to everybody since i dont see the point in being mean i am a practicing christian (i dont like that term) which some people dont get since im gay but whatever -__- hopefully in a couple months this will be a lot more positive :/ working on an art history degree. trying to keep my relationship together -_- blowing huuuuge bubbles with gum ^.^ finishing massive jaw breakers i can only think of stuff i can do with my mouth... which makes me feel like a fattie im good at giving advice! even though im lame at taking it, hense being where i am now :s nothing really... im average my hair? people always comment on my hair if they have something to say my voice too... apparently i sound 13 ahhhhh, im lame. :( i dont know any cool books, but ive read the davinci code and all of the harry potter books <.< sue me not really a movie buff, i just like them long with a story line and in colour! black and white is too cultured for me i have the music taste of a 14 year old emo girl, so im not name dropping bands im a vegeterian and not because of some pretentious white middle class reason! i just saw a video of what they do to animals on farms and it grossed me out, but i have no problem with people that eat meat. affection, i dont think i need love, but i do need people who care about me and i can care for :s id die without a support network cell phone, im a text addict internet, im lazy, why go out when you can talk on aim :] now im struggling.... im gonna say hair straighteners and fructis shampoo & conditioner _ t-shirts! i horde t-shirts, i love the smell of a store on new clothes, mmm my mom, i thought shed come under affection, but then i changed my mind. shes so much more than that. i dont get people who say they love their boyfriends/girlfriends/husbands/wives whatever as much as they love their moms my boyfriend if you can answer this, then you need to get out more, seriously planning out your schedule is just weird i played with barbies as a child i used to dress up in my moms clothes i wanted to be a girl i dont now... but i did xd you live near mill valley and arent a douchebag.

sign: aries and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: first, and foremost? i'm a nerdlette . this means i'm geeky, weird, quirky, and i love intelligent/sardonic humor. i play video games, i like to talk about bizarre and otherworldly things, i like pressing buttons, things that light up, weird gizmos/gadgets, foreign languages, and cheese. i do not like shopping or makeup. my style of dress is a mix between casual jean/hoodie/tshirt nerd, a mock vintage/victorian stylist (50s swing dresses are awesome), and brightly coloured socks. i love obnoxiously cute things like cupcake merchandise and little sushi plushies that have eyeballs in them. i like to be included in witty banter, though will probably lose every time because i'll start laughing about it.  i enjoy crazy, random happenstances. the range of things that could possibly fall into this category are essentially endless, but it thrills me when pieces of a puzzle just fall out of nowhere and are placed together effortlessly. a more common example is the excitement i feel when i find an abundant amount of corresponding interests in a person. to share is one of the most important aspects of my life; i find it uncomfortable and awkward if i am unable to express the things that i love.  i put my best effort into everything that i do. some would call me a perfectionist, but i would simply call myself thorough. i don't want to live my life with regrets, and because of this, i am much more satisfied if i put my heart into all that i try and achieve. there are times when i fall short (i'm supposed to be human, after all), but find that i am extremely resilient and do not let the feeling of failure taint my impending outlook.  i enjoy disney, reading, writing, cooking, singing, drawing, video games, star-gazing, shooting zombies, laughing, bubble-wrap, chocolate, baking, sharing, massages, the feeling of silk, music, christmas,splashing in puddles, hugging, rainstorms, dreaming, and loving. ----------------------------..:|:..-------------------------------  i studied japanese for about 4 years, and visited tokyo/aomori for 2weeks back in 2005. my proficiency level tends to rollercoaster up and down at random times, and i can understand a lot more than i can speak.  i have weird eating habits. i like eating cheeseburgers upside down, ketchup on potato chips, and for some reason, my brain always registers ice cream as being hot, so i have to blow on it before consuming.  i have 53 pairs of socks, and you will probably see every single pair.  i have a weak spot for guys who will wear a fedora. (purr). also, musicians. specifically: guitar and/or violin.  i love puns, riddles, and play on words.  i enjoy having my hair brushed!  the secret to my heart is through my stomach. if you feed me, i have no other option than to show you my unwavering affection.  intelligence makes my heart flutter. i also love deep, meaningful conversations about anything, and short, fun conversations about nothing.  i speak fluent dinosaur.  trust/honesty is very important to me.  i was once tricked into eating a tentacle. it had suckers on it. and was purple. i'm an artist and a writer. about a year ago, i wrote a short story that i would like to have as a segment in a much larger collection, and published (of course). i haven't touched the idea in some time, but would very much love to cross paths with it again and see where my imagination takes me.  as for art, i graduated from college with a bachelor of science degree for media arts & animation. i moved out to san francisco to seek my fortune after being laid off from an animation studio (2 years), and struggled to find a place in the games industry. about a month ago (april 2012), i landed a 2d art position at a company that makes games for the iphone! i couldn't be happier and feel that i am absolutely living the dream that i set out for.  when i'm not doing art for work, i'm spending my time illustrating for the stories that are in my head. i enjoy both traditional and digital, but find that digital tends to better fit into my lifestyle. i also do clay figurines when i want to be more hands on. i like to sculpt and would very much like to learn how to make ooak dolls. keeping in touch. being positive. listening to others. multitasking. smiling! doing horrible impressions of things. quoting favourite lines from movies/tv shows. daydreaming. being open about my thoughts and feelings. ~my personality ~my eyes ~my smile ~my socks (i love crazy, loud socks)  ----------------------------..:|:..-------------------------------  i've been told by many that i have a positive outlook on life, or that being around me is like being around a living, breathing light that carries with it a sense of warmth, kindness, and happiness. i'm not sure what it is about me that defines these traits to another person, but i do try what i can to make others smile, to be there when they need someone, and to be as understanding as possible. ----------------------------..:|:..-------------------------------  books~  the darkangel, a gathering of gargoyles, the pearl of the soul of the world, the thief, queen of attolia, king of attolia, blood and chocolate, the silver kiss, look for me by moonlight, the thief of always, flowers in the attic, sabriel, lirael, abhorsen, in the night garden, fairy tales, game of thrones  ----------------------------..:|:..-------------------------------  movies~  butterfly efflect, phantom of the opera, nightmare before christmas, sweeney todd, classic disney, wuthering heights, the invisible, may, eternal sunshine of the spotless mind, the 10th kingdom, legend, the dark crystal, labyrinth, pans labyrinth, lost and delirious, day after tomorrow, the last unicorn, the prestige, moulin rouge, the proposal, never-ending story, tangled, the devil's backbone, city of the lost children, the avengers, blue valentine  tv~  game of thrones, rome, downton abbey, doctor who, supernatural, the office, lost, my name is earl, ramsay's kitchen nightmares, master chef, family guy, american dad, how it's made, gargoyles, gummi bears, ducktales, vampire diaries.  anime~  blood+, escaflowne, fma, fma brotherhood, air tv, canon, clannad, elfen lied, kimi ga nozomu eien ----------------------------..:|:..-------------------------------  food~  japanese, mexican, thai, american, italian, peruvian. i just love food! i'm willing to try almost anything. if it tastes good, i'll eat it. i'm particularly fond of cheese, chocolate, and shellfish. ~family ~friends ~internet ~chocolate chip muffins ~art ~happiness stories, dreams, goals, emotions and people.  also, in between relationships, i like to evaluate what it was about my previous ones that i liked, and didn't like. i think a lot about that information to help guide me towards someone that has the traits and qualities that i'm looking for.  i want something that feels very warm and genuine. i want to slip into someone's life as if i had been there the entire time. i don't want distance; i want to share my life and have someone be a part of it just as much as i'm a part of theirs. i seek a relationship where we can be playful and silly, but also deep, dark, and serious. i need both to be truly happy. i need to be with someone who is able to express their emotions, and accept each other for our flaws. after all, loving the imperfect allows one to love and accept their own imperfections. being typical is one of the most boring things a person can be. i don't ever like to have a typical day; i will try to find something new or different that will keep me from falling into patterns. the last thing i want to do is to become a robot and let my life take control of me.  so, the best answer that i can give here is "being creative". i will do what it takes to entertain myself. if i'm in a relationship, i will most likely want to be with my partner. i have an okc profile. an entire story exists about my username.  ...  i watched an entire season of project runway. and enjoyed it. i interest you. you are intelligent, funny, giving, and open. you will watch disney with me. you like to cook/bake! you will do random and weird things with me just because. you like to laugh. you will let me tackle you. you like to play video games, and shoot zombies. you get my reference when i shout out: "exterminate!" you know what colours to mix to make violet. you believe that the great dm in the sky actually exists. (i'm curious to know what my character sheet would look like).  don't be afraid to message me. i promise i'm very nice :)

full_bio: i'm a heart felt thinker and a hard, honest worker. i believe i come off as simple minded, but i'm not really, i just have my own "secret" thought process. i always wonder how i could have done things different in the past and appease the ones i inadvertently hurt. most of the time people think i'm being hard on myself but i think it's just a way of trying to be a better person, since i do have a habit of crossing lines others have drawn. on the flip side i can be goofy and random and am more likely to put up with bull-shit than blame other people for it. i believe we're better than we think and meeting our potential is always a true bitch in life. i like to take my time doing things. except for driving to and from work. i'll admit that not acting with road rage doesn't exempt me from having it. otherwise at home or on days off i'm prone to doing whatever i do without hurry. going to barnes & noble, watching a movie, taking a road trip and the like. all in all i have my vices like i expect most people do, but i'm not into bickering or philosophizing about faults as much as i'm into gossiping about human predicaments and amazing occurrences. on that note, as for gossip, i'm not really that much into talking incessantly and am happiest occupying my butt with modestly opinionated women of the like. and one at a time too. a tango takes two, not more not less. i'm an electrician, in the third year of my career. which is not such a sly way of saying, i don't know what i'm doing with my life. i mean i'm going through a state certified program, but that's just politics and paperwork.  daily, i work. i cook as much as i eat out, and i love good food.  i watch netflix, or do my best to score game in walnut creek and fail utterly because i'm willing to give up that information publicly to women i'd prefer wouldn't be turned off by me.  reading a game of thrones and not watching the shows.  google sketchup-ing my dream house.  backpacking and camping and outdoors (you must love these).  i have ideas, it's just which ones will i follow and which others will i be leaving behind. after all one can't simply do 'just enough' and be satisfied.  yep. that sums it up. i have a career and i'm trying to save money and build credit so i can inherit the lands of my elders. then, only then, will i be satisfied hahahahahahhahaha!  in the mean time i kick (the shit out of) it. whatever i stick my guns to. shove it okcupid, i'm not to be judged that simply. i'm a drifter. for the time being i've been listening to radio, old school r&b disco shit, while helping with my father's bookkeeping.  here's just some of the media i like.  movies...  v for vendetta hancock superman (1+2) star wars (episodes iii, iv, v) the fifth element 7 pounds legends of the fall blade runner the departed pan's labyrinth labyrinth indiana jones series terminator 1 and 2 time traveler's wife the departed and lots lots more!  ... documentaries...  history channel tlc hgtv discovery national geographic and lots lots more!  ...shows...  metalocalypse family guy robot chicken and lots lots more!  ...books...  george r r martin's game of thrones philip pullman's his dark materials series steven king's the dark tower series dean koontz, tons of... john grisham's the pelican brief, the associate, the client.. james hilton's lost horizon mark mother fuckin' twain (samuel l clemens) and many more!  ...music...  system of a down garbage muse brad paisley kenny chesney gaga red hot chili peppers jimi hendrix the beatles sheryl crow sarah mclachlan fiona apple maroon 5 justin timberlake timbaland 2 pac x-ray dog immediate music duran duran the police billy currington ok go deirks bentley michael jackson michel sardou sara evans george moustaki francis cabrel dixie chicks queen acdc timbaland oh land and much more!!  ...food...  stir fry pot roast bbq soups pastas costco (yeah, me too) and many others! i project my hatred on this section. apparently i can't do without that. oh but yet, i can actually do without that. then apparently i can't do without hypocritical bi-polar bull shit.  so the question is, is that two things i can't do without or just one? lets just say i'm a dreamer and the less i dream the more focused i become. i'm like a dried out bean with a worm in it (jumping beans).. you simply can't know which way i'm going to roll. either that or i'm like a football... if you can't keep your eye on me i might just hit you in the face. the most private thing i'm willing to admit (on okc) is that the last time i filled out this section it was suggested to me more than once that i don't admit the most private thing i was willing to admit. but alas, don't fear! if you take any interest in me so far do not be discouraged by my out-righteous appearance! for these are just words, and love speaks more truly than any such nonsense.  but on a serious note; poker face.

sign: leo and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i live in pursuit of knowledge and experience. i am a hopeful romantic. i love witticism and am turned on by intelligence.  i am a extrovert who spends a lot of time in her head.  giving a public speech on ethics one second, dancing at the bar with a drink in my hand the next. lets see if you can keep up.  i prefer ideas to beliefs, winter to summer, intensity to complacency, and prince to michael jackson.  i love the feel of old classic muscle cars and the power and excitement in quad, atv, and dirt bike racing.  don't promise me tomorrow, give me one hell of a day, one day at a time, and tomorrow will take care of itself. i am currently working on finishing up my undergraduate degree. i am majoring in english, with a concentration in literature (sub concentration in african american literature), and philosophy, with a concentration in bioethics. i've spent the last 4 years doing work in child development and working with elementary aged children.  i work at a private children's school and as a senior lab manager. i also volunteer with the breast cancer society and different social justice organizations within the bay. i plan on going to law school in the near future. randomly breaking out into a dance. helping other people reason through difficult problems. cooking, baking, and cleaning. playing the devil's advocate. convincing people to do things they normally wouldn't do/ give into their desires. making others laugh and feel good. my laughter books: tuck everlasting, brave new world, white noise, catch-22, wuthering heights, house of the scorpion, silence of the lambs, killing me softly, the surgeon, choke, fledgling, a visitation of spirits, beowulf, the awakening, the giver, inferno, as i lay dying, hamlet, a streetcar named desire, a million little pieces, the crucible, the republic, candide, the unbearable lightness of being, lolita.  movies: fight club, meet joe black, the libertine, purple rain, saw, hamlet, the man in the iron mask, ever after, the bone collector, the picture of dorian gray, gladiator, original sin, and once upon a time in mexico.  favorite food: avocado, jalapeos, pad thai, mango fried rice.  music: i love all genres of music.  shows: i don't watch much tv. great literature laughter autonomy (my mind) music witticism knowledge i am concerned about the "good life" and what it means to live a good life.  i often find myself thinking about the construction of masculinity, gender performance, and sexuality. i am extremely interested in issues concerning end of life treatment and debilitating disease, advances in science, sexual deviance and taboos, physiological disorders, and the development of ability challenged children. doing research, enjoying a cold beer, and writing. when not doing these things i am out on the town with friends, usually dancing. why don't you ask me? 1) you are witty and/or love to laugh or 2) you can properly cook/make a double baked potato or 3) you might be interested in partnering with me on my quest for world domination or 4) you're bold enough to take what you want...

full_bio: first the important stuff: i am an entrepreneur at heart which is another way of saying that i believe that there is always a way to get there from here. i've just moved to san francisco (south beach) after spending the past three years in miami (south beach). i have been described as funny, laid back, intelligent, brazen and innocent. (that last one is way off the mark, mind you). i like to discover new parts of cities, laugh, make people laugh, chat about psychology, movies and philosophy, drink hot coffee and eat cold yogurt, travel to other countries, look at pretty things, and wonder lots. this doesn't have to happen every day -- though it's something to shoot for.  stuff your parents will like: i am the ceo of an internet company with two rounds of funding (yay). so, basically i've built my dream job and need to be pinched every so often. (we probably won't mention to your parents that things are pretty demanding at the moment). but, i think it's important to have balance in life. i feel like i am at the stage where i have my priorities in life, and am looking for a serious relationship with the right woman. also, your parents will be pleased to know that i have degrees from brown and yale  although the first was in political science  so potentially some questionable judgment. - started a business and moved to sf 6 months ago. - exploring psychological motivations in advertising - making shit happen - finding clever solutions for sticky problems - getting lost - basketball  - tv: mad men, 30 rock, the wire, breaking bad, downton abbey (don't judge0=) - movies: crouching tiger hidden dragon, mulholland drive, district 9 - books: guns germs steel, zen and the art of motorcycle maintenance - music: outkast, gnarles barkley, lana del rey, deadmaus, gorillaz, black keys 1. family 2. questions 3. watermelon 4. waffles 5. tbd what's for dinner oot n' aboot

religion: christianity but not too serious about it
full_bio: it's the little things in life that make us truly happy  watching the sun set over the golden gate; that feeling of exhausted exhilaration after biking up that last hill; finding a lost remote control in your recliner (it was crazy, i'd looked there 3 times, and it magically appeared on the 4th search!). yeah, those are the things that sustain us, i think, and i like to hope to find at least one every day :)  i'm a little silly, a little geeky, but not too over-the-top with either. yes, i've attended star trek conventions; no; i've never asked the actors what life is like in the q continuum(yep, i actually saw that happen). yes, i enjoy british comedy; no, i've never taunted anyone in french from a castle parapet (yet). i have a lifelong abiding love of science; i focused on almost nothing else in college until i switched gears and ended up with a degree in theatre (naturally). i'll easily lose myself in a book, but especially in an engaging conversation, and i'm a sucker for someone with a sharp intellect who's not afraid to use it. i'm always down for a word game, whether it's a round of scrabble, a session of boggle, or a crossword puzzle. (deep dark secret: i'm terrible at bananagrams. i don't know what it is about that game, but it and i are not friends.) actually, when it comes to board games in general, i almost never say no  i'm one of the few people i know who will actually want to play an entire game of monopoly.  when i gotta get outside, i enjoy hiking, but biking (both road and mountain) is by far my favorite way to enjoy some fresh air. (i've tried running a few times, but there's just way too much running involved for my taste). when it comes to spectator sports, i'm at the ballpark to support my oakland a's every summer; in the fall, it's all about cal football. i've been known to schedule major events around the cal-stanford game so i don't miss it; i know, i have a problem but i've come to terms with it :) i'm an actor by trade - being able to bring stories to life, to work doing something i love, and hopefully teach people about themselves and the world around them, is pretty awesome. my other job lets me help people get outdoors, which is not only rewarding, but reminds me to get out there too! i'm also searching for the perfect blend of art and science to help kids get excited about both. haven't found it yet but i can't wait until i do :) baking cheesecake. seriously, it's pretty good. hmmm i've never asked! i'm told i have a nice smile, so maybe it's that. the last few books i read were a biography of queen elizabeth i, a short treatise on performance theory, a dinosaur detective novel, and a spider-man novel, so yeah, i'm all over the place :) some of my favorites, though, are: the odyssey, one hundred years of solitude, the phantom tollbooth, the metamorphosis, the harry potter series, jurassic park, galapagos, watership down, solaris, haroun and the sea of stories.  my favorite movies include blazing saddles, the artist, life is beautiful, the usual suspects, cube, robin hood (the animated disney version), bound, anchorman, anything by miyazaki, the great dictator, the fifth element, clue, what lies beneath, spy game, memento, throne of blood, the departed. i also like jason bourne/jason statham type-movies, those are always a good time. guilty-pleasure movies include rock star and the remake of planet of the apes.  tv faves: game of thrones, psych, how it's made, colbert report, old cartoons (looney tunes, tom & jerry, etc), star trek, jeopardy, cooking shows in general (but good eats in particular!), any and every nfl game, mythbusters  i'm always listening to music and lots of it, from all over the map, so trying to whittle it all down is a challenge. my bread-and-butter is probably 90's rock(stp/smashing pumpkins/pearl jam/soundgarden), but i'm always finding old standards and new classics to love. what i've had in circulation lately: james taylor, carolina chocolate drops, pizzicato five, bread, a tribe called quest, vince guaraldi, metallica, lady gaga, lupe fiasco, static-x, anita baker, the smashing pumpkins, sam cooke ok, i could be here all day, so i'll just stop there.  as for food? i really enjoy it all, i can't even pick out favorites. but hey, the great thing about living in the bay area is that you don't have to choose! i love the fact that so many different kinds of food are right here at our fingertips. - the smell of being backstage (fresh sawdust and wet paint) - my family - music! - quiet moments to myself - my friends - opportunities to help and serve others ...anything and everything, really. there's not much that doesn't interest me in some way, shape or form; i have a deep thirst for knowledge on any topic i encounter, so i soak it up wherever i can - from books to articles to websites to people. never mind that my mind pretty much has a mind of its own, so i just go where it takes me! probably in rehearsal :) but when i'm free, there's a bunch on the menu. things like oakland art murmur and chabot space museum (after dark) are always fun. during the spring/summer, friday nights are a great time to catch an a's game. there's always lots theatre to see, concerts to attend but then, there's a whole lot to be said for dinner and a movie with friends, or even just staying in and cuddling up after a long week. well, i just admitted that i like really bad mark wahlberg movies, so i think that's enough shame-sharing for now. you've actually made it this far and you like what you've read!

full_bio: "what ethnicity is he?" - your inner voice, about 3 seconds ago.  you're not alone. in argentina, vendors thought i was italian; in australia, hostelmates thought i was argentine; and in beijing, cabbies thought i was one of the "minority peoples" from west china.  won't tell you just yet.  but i will tell you that i grew up in 4 different states: nj -> oh -> ca -> fl. hoping never to see another pair of jorts again, i went to college about as far away as possible. been here ever since, with stints in spain, china, australia/new zealand, argentina, scandinavia and other places with "forners".  that should be enough for now. please read what's in parentheses below. reading judicial opinions. mostly to see how the characters turn out.  otherwise: exercising/taking advantage of rare sunny weather.  and --- i spend a whole lot of time singing; males in my family have done this since russia was czarist, so i come by it naturally accents/impersonations  (including the "jive" speakers from airplane!, a character named pico from my middle school spanish textbook, etc.) salt n' peppa. and i'm not talking about shoop, shoopy doop. like a zoo animal, i need to consume a certain amount of food in a given day. if i don't, i'm hungry at night and can't fall asleep. as for the type of food, i'm pretty flexible.  gotta mention robin hood: men in tights on here. it's been on every resume i've made, and more importantly, it's in the script.  also: snl dramas like happy gilmore, best in show, coming to america, desperado, bats!, cool runnings, good will hunting, the jungle book (i looked strikingly similar to mowgli as a kid), rookie of the year (also looked like this kid, during the less goofy american pie years, i swear), avalon, and the sandlot.  tv: modern family, always sunny, stewart/colbert, and espn "the ocho". older tv: wonder years, seinfeld, and west wing.  sam cooke's not so current; i also dig zac brown band and bruno mars, and even likes me some gaga.  if you're not sick of this section yet, other music i like:  sublime (favorite band), dire straits (favorite guitarist), metallica, jodeci, the eagles, gary clark jr, mayer hawthorne, fitz and the tantrums, blackstreet, tupac, the darkness, pearl jam, collective soul, hootie (not putting this in the category below), me first and the gimme gimmes, jarabe de palo, bush (first cd i bought), oasis, offspring, ac/dc, ccr, ray charles, billy joel, chris brown (just kidding, ladies), 80's power ballads, steely dan... my family and friends "the man and his music" sun satire cleveland indians (even though studies have shown that rooting for cleveland is one of the most painful experiences that a grown man endures in life) arithmetic whether "kim jong ill" could work as a rap name.  too soon?  this might result in big fat zero dates for me: i used to be pretty good at slinging yo-yos.  if it helps any, i taught little kids how to yo-yo in a high school community service project, and one of them is reigning heavyweight yoyo champion you prefer a guy who excels in a dinner party scene more than in a bar scene

full_bio: i'm a free-spirited, fly-by-the-seat-of-her-pants accountant, which could be seen as a contradiction of terms, but... it's true! i am generally super outgoing, but i really value my alone time as well. i grew up in idaho, but after living in sf for nine years, i don't see myself ever straying too far from the city. i love skiing, but i am also super content on the beach watching the waves. i consider eating one of my hobbies, but i mostly try to stay in good shape and take are of my health. lots of contradictions, but that makes for lots to talk about. i work so that i can play! i cherish weekends and vacation time when i get to hang out with friends, and i also travel a fair amount. during the week i also hit the gym or do yoga. i head up to tahoe a lot in the winter to ski or snowboard, and during the summer i like to host grill-outs in my backyard and catch giants games. i'm also a very amateur vegetable gardener. i have to do *something* until football season starts! we lived in pittsburgh when i was really little, and being as how idaho has no nfl teams, i remained a steelers fan -- i almost never miss a game.  what else? i once was the singer in what we called "fake band" -- we only played covers, and we mostly just rehearsed for our own enjoyment, although we did have two performances where we got paid in beer! trying to learn a little guitar now, so that i can sing some more. getting a party started, singing karaoke, being silly, quoting anchorman, showing affection, listening to what you have to say, doing math in my head (like restaurant/bar split tabs. i'm always put in charge of this, ugh!) either the color of my eyes or the volume of my voice (it goes to eleven on occasion). recent reads that i liked: the corrections, a visit from the goon squad, the stieg larsson trilogy, and mindy kaling's funny book called is everyone hanging out without me? (and other concerns)  movies: i love going to movies, and i like so many! comedies such as anchorman and bridesmaids; action moves like sherlock holmes, all the batmans (batmen? haha), and the girl with the dragon tattoo; and i also like girly movies like crazy stupid love and dirty dancing, but i would never expect you to like them. if you ever watched them with me, though, major extra credit points for you!  shows: mad men, sons of anarchy, justified, homeland, the amazing race, parks and recreation, community, modern family, happy endings, the league, oh and jersey shore and the bachelorette but we will skip over those embarrassments.  music: soooo varied. seriously, from pearl jam (my all-time fave) to miranda lambert and a lot in between. i went to the recent gotye show, and i have tickets for the upcoming avett brothers show. not sure what that says, but there you have it.  food: yes, please. i love eating at new restaurants, especially if they are secret finds, or amazing cheap(er) eats. there is a time and place for gourmet as well, of course! i love a good piece of red meat whether it's a filet mignon or a hamburger, i never turn down carnitas, and i can eat a huge bowl of pasta or a good pizza all by myself. i try to eat clean foods during the week so that i can let loose when i go out to eat on the weekends. occasional sunshine coastal air fresh california produce good manners in others my passport tunes what i want to be when i grow up. :) usually at happy hour, followed by whatever/wherever the night takes me. if i don't have plans, i like how empty the gym usually is on fridays -- that means i get my own bag in boxing class! i'm a hugger! you know what movie my handle is from, or if you're curious enough to find out.

full_bio: as a soft-spoken optimist, this easy-going, light-hearted gentleman enjoys his residence in san francisco. the beauty of the bay is worth my daily commute back after a long day from santa clara.  grateful for life's opportunities and the joy they bring, i am a realist who looks ahead. leading a simple life, i feel that expectations can intrude on the surprise that our existence holds.  born and raised in new york city, i moved several years ago to the bay area, and four years ago to san francisco. food seems to be big in my life; enjoying eating out as much as dining in. currently working in the corporate, i look forward to enjoying life with the right companion. a great sense of family, an entrepreneurial spirit, and a passion for all things positive comprise my world.  wow questions like that are always hard to answer about myself.. i usually get noticed for my long eyelashes..  six things i could never do without would be the gifts of laughter, family and friends, my integrity, good food, more food, and shazam..  on a typical friday evening, we might enjoy a bottle of wine, a delicious meal, and a netflix movie; all of which could be a perfect evening. among other favorite nightly activities, i do enjoy a night out at a bar or lounge, perhaps also with friends.  just really depends! hmm.. for now i guess; i put my pants on one leg at a time..

full_bio: i'm a busy sf activist doing all i can to make life tolerable for the 99%. i am passionate about human rights and empowering the disenfranchised. i have a four-year-old daughter, who is the love of my life (but i do have as yet unrealized snuggling potential.) my life is really hectic and nontraditional. hopefully you keep yourself busy, too. oh yeah, and i like to dress up in black leather and go dancing at goth clubs when i have the energy. update: i am no longer working myself into the grave for employers who don't particularly appreciate it (woohoo!) i am really enjoying doing what the hell i feel like doing...and i stay surprisingly busy doing it :) researching the healing properties of essential oils (and sourcing raw materials) is my latest hobby, and i'm developing the website for drugusersunion.org...slowly.  but this was me last year...i work in non-profits...i'm the editor of street sheet and the program coordinator for the sf drug users' union. i know, total conversation stopper, but i love my work even though it can be very stressful and demanding.  i was a web developer (for the second-largest intellectual property law firm in the us) in a past life. i was a fetish art model in the life before that. my first college major was linguistics. then i moved toward computer science, and finally settled on technical writing. writing, editing, listening, public speaking, community organizing, sarcasm/irony/wit, and freak-wrangling. yes, i said freak wrangling. that skill is somewhat imperative to both of my jobs, and also comes in handy at parties. i'm also a decent swing dancer. i have green hair and a sarcastic tongue. i think people probably notice those things first. people also comment about my enormous rack when i'm not wearing a jacket (i usually wear a jacket these days), and my expressive eyes. i don't really watch tv much, but when i do, it's generally a documentary. not really into movies that much (although i am writing a screenplay bit by bit), either, but fight club, ironman, and donnie darko are my favorites. i have better things to do than sit at home watching the idiot box, although it can be nice to just snuggle up with a movie or true blood once in awhile. the walking dead has given me quite a zombie movie habit though. i have been reading the lorax by dr. suess to my daughter a lot lately. man, dr. suess really knew what was up. the last grown-up book i read was the permanent revolution by trotsky (someone pointed out that this book actually has a lot in common with the lorax...and *no*, i am *not* a trotskyist.)  one of my all-time favorite books was freakonomics. i love all kinds of food, although indian food is my favorite. i eat healthy, organic, raw, and meat-free as much as i can muster, but i have an insidious addiction to bacon. goth and industrial music are what i usually listen to, but i like lots of kinds of music. my favorite bands are oingo boingo and they might be giants. squirrel nut zippers and reverend horton heat are pretty awesome, too. my friends? my family? what a strange question. change is good, so i like to do without the things i'm usually attached to, at least once in awhile.  1. my daughter 2. laughter/silly stuff 3. sarcasm/wit 4. words/communication 5. rocking out to loud music 6. my friends 7. breaking the rules :)  things i could do without: 1. math 2. gratuitous limitation my plans for world domination. peaceful world domination, of course ;) which includes putting an end to the war on drugs.  i also spend a fair amount of time thinking about how i can turn random orphaned electrical parts into fire-breathing robot monsters. i like repurposing things to give them a new life.  and lately i keep wondering which profiles pop up as "similar" to mine when you check out my profile. be a doll and clue me in. dancing with my girlfriends at cat club (if i manage to get that disco nap after work.) my daughter's dad lives with us, full_bio: is below! centers around being in a city that's new in one sense, but an old friend in another -- it's the first time i've lived here, but work has been bringing me here for years.  right now i'm exploring the bay area through a combination of long-distance runs and longer-distance pubcrawls. if there's a good coffee, wine, beer, sushi joint, or steakhouse, i want to try it -- and if i can do so outdoors, all the better. i work down on the peninsula and i love what i do, but at the same time i'm a "work to live" as opposed to a "live to work" kind of guy. getting lost. i'm seriously gifted at it. the navigational talents definitely got split in the family -- you could get my sister drunk, beat her up, shove in the trunk of a car and drive 500 miles into the desert, ask her which way is home and she'd point straight at it. me? i get lost backing out of my driveway. are that i smile a lot, and my shoulders shake when i laugh. which i do often. are good things to have. i've always found the best way to describe them is to give a general description of my tastes, and then provide examples. so with that,  books: you can implement it in any genre you want, but what i like is astute observations on human social behavior. favorites include "the picture of dorian grey", "dune", and "crime and punishment"  movies: i like psychological thrillers, and clever dramas/mysteries/comedies. "the thomas crown affair" (the remake), "back to the future", and "gattaca" come to mind  shows: same general tastes as in movies, though the construct of a tv season lends itself towards dramas and comedies and away from mysteries and thrillers. "freaks and geeks" was great, as was "scrubs" for a while. nowadays "modern family" is the only thing that really holds my interest.  music: i don't have very strong tastes in music. i don't like rap, and i don't like heavy metal. outside of that i believe music should match with the environment and mood at hand. so i like jazz at a jazz club, pop/rock at a bar, and easy listening at vineyards in napa. outside of that general matchup, i don't have a terribly strong opinion.  food: anything ethnic, and then good steak and great burgers. low-end i want chipotle and in'n'out, mid-end i want andalu and burma superstar, high end i want ozuma and the best steakhouse you can find are...  good friends great outdoors intelligent conversation goals new restaurants/bars/cities/countries to explore favorite restaurants/bars/cities/countries to go to again the algorithms of human social interaction. and whether i'll be able to run boston now that they've sped up the qualifying times. unplugging from the work week. i sort of object to the premise here. i don't like the idea that i would ever have a "typical" anything and in a sense it would bother me if i thought that anyone identified me as being predictable. but loosely, on a typical friday night i'm looking to explore something new with good friends. every person who knows me will tell you i'm an extrovert. they're right in the sense that that's how i behave, but it's mostly an act. i like the benefits of being an extrovert (friends, a good social network, professional opportunities, etc.) but it's definitely forced. deep down inside i'm more of a "bond with you 1 on 1" kind of guy. you like the way this reads.

full_bio: i fantasize about having 40-hour days so i can have time to make lots of cool shit and then sleep a long time, every single day. i want to make my own backpacks and climbing clothes, and i definitely want my garage to be a woodworking shop when i get old.  i love rock climbing, music, and graphs. i also like riding my bike everywhere (both mountain biking and road biking). in the winters i like to snowboard, and this summer and fall i hope to beef up my surfing skills. i love travelling, new experiences, and spending time with close friends.  i grew up in austin, tx, and have been in the bay area since i was 17, with the exception of two years in boston. when i first moved to palo alto i thought i hated it, but i've really grown to love this area and everything we are so lucky to do here. i'm in the city just about every weekend, and berkeley at least once a month.    music: i grew up listening to pavement, modest mouse, sonic youth, and weezer. when i was really young i liked kenny g and phil collins. now i listen to and enjoy lots of music; my mega favorites include wilco, the kinks, ween, four tet, the flaming lips, fleet foxes, spoon, and michael jackson.  food: i love food. trying out new foods, making foods, eating fresh bread with butter. my staples are fruit, cereal, meat, and yogurt. i have a special place in my belly for flan and custards of all kinds.  movies: amelie, a serious man, after life, blade runner, there will be blood. and i like michel gondry a lot. and wes anderson.  tv: arrested development, breaking bad, futurama, and the first seasons of flight of the conchords and third rock from the sun. right now i am watching the wire and wonderfalls.  books: hard-boiled wonderland and the end of the world. edward tufte's books. i enjoy comics (like nausicaa and v for vendetta), and i like reading non-fiction so i can learn how things work. on my side table right now: wild fermentation, the savage garden (about carnivorous plants), the stories of john cheever, and the cyberiad.  i'm a dave eggers fan. i was very excited the first time i saw mcsweeney's lists. i get the new yorker and read some every week, but am constantly behind.  other things i like: bikes, people who love to make stuff. terry gross. and i have a soft spot for justin bieber. (really!) that's-what-she-said jokes (x6) - visualizing data - where my next trip is going to be - climbing - how we learn ghostriding the whip i still like phil collins. - you are crazy awesome - you want to go climbing or bike-riding - you like to put yourself outside of your comfort zone in order to learn new things

full_bio: i'm an energetic passionate man who hasn't quite figured out he isn't still 40. i play tennis three times a week, and still run up stairs two steps at a time. although a stable long-term relationship would be nice, for starters, i would like to find an attractive woman with whom i can hang out, have stimulating conversations, spend romantic evenings, travel and have fun. this past year i have spent five weeks in holland and brazil; a partner who loves to travel would be ideal. i'm a successful professional, not thinking about retirement, although i like to take frequent vacations. i am a mature man, who respects and appreciates women, has done a healthy share of introspection, is low maintenance, and would like a similarly mature woman. i'm a psychotherapist with an active and successful career treating clients and providing training to other therapists. i do a fair amount of traveling for my training work. i work hard, play hard and also like to hang out and people-watch. i am handy with tools and am just finishing remodeling my house. i enjoy fixing just about anything except dinner. i'm an ace at washing dishes. i am also an excellent judge of people. people tell me i look much younger than my age. also that i am a good-looking guy, but my posture needs work. i like just about all mildly spicy foods, but keep to a healthy diet. i rarely eat any kind of meat, but do eat fish and eggs. i love sixties music, the beatles, early bob dylan, the stones, mammas and the papas, and peter paul and mary. i don't care for movies with gratuitoius violence. a good slapstick movie can still get me going. friends, cozy clothes, passion, sunlight, diversity, intellectual stimulation i've been single for 10 years. enough is enough! full_bio: i've never really been good at these things. i mean, i've never really felt like someone who knows himself all that well, and so trying to describe me to others has always seemed like a disingenuous activity.  still, what i can say: i'm a pretty okay guy, i think. i guess?  man, i am really awful at this. what do people normally talk about? when i try to really push my brain to come up with things to say about me, i can think of all these wacky kind-of-uncomplimentary things. i'm overweight. i'm a voracious reader, but i can't stand anything actually useful, like newspapers or science. i'm often indecisive. i tend to over-think everything and live inside my own head. but these aren't the things i want to be stressing on a personal summary for a dating slash social networking site, right?  because at the end of the day, these are true things, right, but not the whole story about me. i am also kind (i like to think) and clever (sometimes) and often charismatic (when i can be bothered), and i over-think things because i want to find meaning in everything in life, because i'm sure that there is meaning in most things, or a lesson, even if that lesson is that one shouldn't depend too much on the world to teach them lessons.  i try too hard to be funny because i like people to like me, because i like most people and it's always nice when the people you like like you back. i'm good at seeing multiple sides of an issue - sometimes to a fault - which is where a lot of my indecisiveness comes in, but i also think that makes me more sympathetic and understanding than i might otherwise be. i'm awful with numbers but good with words. except when i'm not.  i want to be a writer one day. i was a literature and writing major in college, and though i'm often discouraged by the fact that everyone and their mother wants to be a writer - and a fair majority of that demographic is better at it than i am - my dream has not quite died. sometimes i think it's because i've read so much over the years, and somewhere in my heart of hearts i want to make the transition from sidelines to participant. sometime i think it's because it's the only skill-set i left college with, and i don't know what else to do with my life. and sometimes i don't know why, sometimes i'm confused myself, and know only that a life not centered around creation and expression and fantasy and words would be hollow and stagnant and a little bit like dying.  i believe my good qualities outweigh my bad ones (although doesn't everyone?), and i mostly hope that my good qualities are more good than my bad qualities are bad, if you see what i mean.  so yeah. i'm probably pretty okay, i think. although not very concise. or very good at staying on topic. almost nothing, to be honest, although i'm trying to change that. reading books. sometimes. if they're not boring.  my genres of choice tend towards magical realism and science fiction / fantasy. the yiddish policeman's union has been my favorite book for a couple of years now, although others include lamb, anansi boys, bridge of birds, and pretty much anything by terry pratchett (although, interestingly, my favorite of his isn't a discworld book, it's nation, the sort of gently humanist novel he wrote a couple of years ago).  for movies, eternal sunshine of the spotless mind tops the list, with shakespeare in love, inception, let the right one in, and bill and ted's excellent adventure filling in a few more slots on the top ten list, although i would be hard pressed to rank them in order of preference.  my musical tastes are kind of all over the place. i avoid rap and r&b and i'm not a huge fan of popular country (although i do like the really country country stuff, the kind that's all full of banjos and fiddles and mandolins), but i like most everything else. ben folds, barenaked ladies, james taylor, sara bareilles, and the forecast are all pretty high on my list. there's a lot more, but i'm kind of awful at lists. books, conversation, uh, water, food, oxygen, uh, gravity? the passage of time. i don't know why this isn't as mindblowing to everyone else as it is to me. every single moment is entirely unique from the moments that follow and precede it, and we all grow older and older and don't notice it as it happens even though we can remember it when it's occurred. it's crazy! things change! at every point in time and space, things are different than they used to be. i think that's amazing. at a casual hangout with friends. board games, a movie, or frisbee golf if it's nice outside.  ...you are awesome. or even if you are just pretty great.
