full_bio: i am happy with myself, but...life is meant for 2! i am looking for a man who is honest, warm, passionate, accepts the diversity in the world, family-oriented, hard-working, great sense of humor, enjoys the outdoors and working out. your winks and emails are welcome! having chemistry is really important but your personality will count a lot.  about myself: i am a woman full of enthusiasm for life. i am a bit reserved when you meet me for the first time but i will open up little by little. i am easy going, fun, energetic, an optimistic, diplomatic, polite, romantic, honest and smart.  anyway, if you want to know more about me, send me an email. maybe we can have a conversation over coffee or a glass of wine. let's become friends and see what happens. i work full time at a school on weekdays and i teach a fitness class twice a week. learning languages, swimming, doing the hula hoop, roller-skating, being diplomatic, resourceful, and silly. my smile, my laughter, my eyes. books i am not much into reading. magazines are okay  movies 21 jump street, the avengers, matrix, scary movie, 21, the king's speech, forrest gump, too fast too furious, bossa nova  shows seinfeld, the office, jay leno, the big bang theory saturday night live  music i love pop music!  food i love many types of food from brazilian to vietnamese. my family, my friends, the sun, my phone, my coffee, my music, my workout how i can become a better person in many aspects of my life! it really depends but sometimes i want to go out with my friends for dinner, a movie or drinks or simply stay at home and enjoy some quiet time i sometimes listen to justin bieber you want to get to know me better.

full_bio: been up in the bay area about a year now, originally from orange county. i like to go on road trips, bike about the city, go to shows, bum around museums, hang out in parks, write/draw in the morning and i've been known to spontaneously participate in spanish karaoke. trying to get back in to more creative projects outside of software development. enjoying company and going with the flow of things. feeling like shit after eating at the cheesecake factory. on the subject of cf: i went because i like to indulge in touristy activities on occasion and it's on a roof. cool.  and making blended margaritas. my glasses and the hat i wear most days everyday. favorite comics: dmz, local, northlanders, y: the last man, the walking dead. just finished the hunger games series; pretty legit.  all time favorite shows: community, firefly, parks and rec, doctor who. i am currently digging: the walking dead, peep show and portlandia.  always enjoy kevin smith's flicks and older comedies like groundhogs day. wes anderson. i think my favorite story types tend to be ones that take place in a single day or night like dazed and confused or nick and norah's infinite playlist. dark knight, duh. the happening.  #1 decemberists. lately: wild flag, yellow ostrich, starfucker, metronomy, brite futures, tycho, alabama shakes and the shins.  mexican > indian > sandwiches > salads.  i don't really like the happening. seeing - i like design and working on visual projects. hearing - life would be a lot less enjoyable w/o music. hat - had it for a while and doing my hair in the morning is at the top of my "yeah, no." list. vans - every time i try switching to something else i always come back. macbook - main tool i use for most of my projects. friends/family - because you feel like a real asshole if you don't include them in this kind of list. teaching kids programming. teaching girls programming. teaching anyone programming. teaching.  if there will ever be a salute your shorts reunion. either seeing a movie, going out to dinner/bars in the mission or exploring other parts of san francisco i usually don't visit.  sometimes i read a bit and am in bed before 10:30  grandpa status.  this last friday: listening to steadier footing covers. i miss hanging out w/ my nephew and little brother back home.  also i have to wear braces for a few more months, so we should hang out soon while i am still saying "green grapeshhh" instead of "green grapes". you're down for hanging out, laughing, grabbing a drink, seeing moonrise kingdom or want to show me something cool about the city i haven't seen before.

full_bio: i am originally from moscow, russia and came to the us when i was 14. i am thankful and love all parts of my life whether it is my work, hobbies, my friends and family and any new experiences that i find along the way. i love traveling, active lifestyle, learning, singing, poetry, books and movies that make me think and so much more. i think i have a strong core with a passionate, curious, excitable and goofy topping :). i am easily amused and love smiling. i am hoping to find a partner who will share my interests, passion for life and learning and the wonderful time we'll have together.  i am looking for someone who will never cease to inspire me. a person with a strong core who combines intelligence, a kind heart, passion for learning, perseverance and a good sense of humor :). someone who is not afraid to open their heart, explore new things and share new experiences. a person who loves kids and animals (you can tell a lot about someone by just these 2 simple factors). a person who loves their work, hobbies, their friends and their life as much as i do! and finally, a person who lives their life not for just pure self-enjoyment, but strives to make the lives of others better as well. i am an executive in a large consulting company. my focus is internet strategies for fortune 1,000 companies across industries. i am absolutely passionate about my job and don't really consider it work most of the time :). singing, analyzing, exploring, outdoor activities, learning, languages, fighting my fears, creating a meaningful life :) openness, smile, accent, passion for life prose/books: mikhail bulgakov (master i margarita, belaya gvardiya), george orwell (1984, animal farm), fedor dostoevskiy (idiot), milan kundera (the unbearable lightness of being), ayn rand (the fountainhead - her writing style not the theories).  poetry: shakespeare, robert frost and a multitude of russian poets (zvetaeva, pasternak, akhmatova, brodskiy, zabolzkiy, etc)  movies: one flew over coockoos nest, habla con ella, shawshank redemption, lars and the real girl, four rooms, deconstructing harry, matchpoint, midnight in paris, le doble vie de veronique, goodbye lenin, red, white and blue, pianist, the brothers bloom, kinsey, sophie's choice and many many others (mostly non-mainstream)  food: sushi, salads, russian food, greek/mideterranean, love trying new stuff! family & friends, singing and music, exercising, my cat, thinking, new experiences self development/actualization, new information, books i read, movies i watch, conversations shared with interesting people catching up with a small group of friends over dinner or drinks, visiting my family, listening to live music or traveling to a new place. when someone tells me that i am not able to do something, i can get stubborn :) if you share my interests, respect women and looking for a deep connection and new shared experiences

full_bio: have you ever experienced this scenario: meet a random stranger, introduce yourself, strike a conversation, keep in touch, meet up over a meal or drinks - fast forward a year or two - this person is now a close and dear friend? it may not be everyone's cup of tea, but i can gladly say that i've experienced this scenario quite often, and in the process have met a lot of cool people and made many a close and enduring friendships.  i sincerely cherish the people in my life: my wonderful family; my amazing and growing circle of friends. they make life's journey sweeter, and they continue to teach me invaluable lessons, inspire me to improve, to give, to learn, and to embrace the vicissitudes of life.  i also cherish the city that i live in. although i've been fortunate enough to travel quite a lot, both domestically and internationally, there is no other place i would rather call home than san francisco. it truly is a charming city: its people - diverse, intellectual, benevolent; the colorful and eclectic neighborhoods which i love to trek across and explore. it never ceases to amaze me that, having lived here for four years now, i continue to stumble across awesome new discoveries: a delectable restaurant, a hole-in-the-wall record shop, or a cool new lounge blaring live music. and then there is the sublime scenery that pervades each corner of the city, from the bustling district during a typical workday, to the breathtaking views of the bay and gg bridge at crissy fields, to awesome city views at mission dolores and twin peaks, and the breakers at ocean beach. i hope to call this city my home, and its people my neighbors, for a lifetime. i work in sales and business development for a publicly traded software company, where i've worked at the past four years. i love my job, although like any other job it can be a bear at times. the people with whom i work are more than just colleagues. even if i wasn't working with them, we'd still be kickin it as friends. another cool perk is getting to travel across the country and at times overseas. i'd say that the job suites my personality. i'm not the type that could sit behind a desk from 9-5. if i'm not meeting & greeting people, i am not a happy camper.  in addition to the day job, i volunteer my time at a couple of non-profit organizations. my goal is to eventually dedicate a larger portion of my time to such organizations. in my opinion, when it's all said and done, life comes down to how much of a positive impact you've had on other's, not the wealth or material you've amassed. - being a dependable and trustworthy friend. - laughing. isn't it amazing how much better life is with a healthy sense of humor? - staying active. running has become a passion. snowboarding, boxing, hiking, climbing, and football also make the list. - traveling. i've traveled across europe, south america, the caribbean, and middle east. next on the itinerary: asia-pacific and africa. i'm good at coordinating trips, being a tour guide for my traveling companions, and dealing with the occasional surprises and emergencies on the trail. the big ol' smile on my face. music runs the gamut from rock (classic, grunge, hard, spanish), jazz (particularly nola jazz), blues, rap, electronic, classic (european and middle eastern are particular favorites). currently hooked on band of horses and arcade fire.  books: just finished conversations with power, by brian michael till. big fan of the economist, the onion, and wsj. other favorite reads include a new earth (eckhart tolle), the grapes of wrath (steinbeck). really enjoy malcolm gladwell's writing style.  food: japanese, thai, persian, indian, iberian, mediterranean, southern (luv me some shrimp & grits).  movies: an assortment of guy movies and comedies - the big lebowski, braveheart, gladiator, shawshank redemption, full metal jacket, revolver, dumb & dumber. recently saw and enjoyed un prophete. - family - friends - humor - sports (participating, not spectating) - coffee (peet's & four barrel in particular) - traveling how not to think so much and just enjoy the moment. hitting the gym, then hanging out in the city with friends. is that i won't admit to anything private on this site...would be happy to spill some beans in person though. ...you want to, of course :)

full_bio: whats up - im 21 and living in san francisco, working on a degree from sfsu. i love to meet new people and make legitimate relationships around substantial things we have in common. i love music a bit too much haha, going to music shows, people who have manners and good energy, being active, snowboarding, and really just enjoying life in an optimistic manner. if you're vibin with this let me know -- working and getting through school at the moment, would love to travel after to get to know more people and myself more. im about to graduate from university and plan to see india in the very near future! trying to see things positively my optimism about life and experiences, this is our existence and we are responsible for how we choose to look at it. i love food a tad too much, hahah - and love to discover more ways to enjoy all this world has to offer in that sense. having turned vegan from vegetarian recently, i'd love to meet some people who share this a similar passion.  i try to stay away from tv and too many movies, and love to spend time doing things outside and seeing the city.  music is a huge passion of mine, i love a lot of different things but mostly stay around underground hip-hop and dubstep/electronic! music, friends, food, water, life, perspective. who i am, want to be, and how the world works. kickin it with some good people, making some dank food, possibly going to a music show, and just enjoying the gift of living! i cant decide if i love or hate humanity hahah you like what you're reading, feel we have something in common, and are down to earth and a real person.

religion: atheism but not too serious about it
full_bio: originally from tahoe, recently moved to san francisco after spending the last two years in oakland. i went to college at ucsc, where i studied russia and eastern europe -- it was interesting and great for travel, but it has nothing to do with what i'm doing professionally right now.  i'm into books (used to work in bookstores, i get excited talking about reading), bicycles, photography, trying new food, good beer, etc. but all of those things are more fun to experience with good company, n'est-ce pas?  i work in berkeley and have a lot of friends in oakland, so i'm not snobby about going out in the east bay (which is pig latin for "beast", by the way). i'm pretty fresh to sf though, so i'd love to have adventures around the city with someone fun too! i work for a company in berkeley that builds research websites for university libraries. it's interesting for now, but i'm considering grad school/moving abroad again in the next few years. we'll see. right now i'm just enjoying being stable and living like an adult. most of the time, you know? grilling cheeses russian accents book recommendations i get asked if i'm jewish all the time. i'm not sure why this is.  i have some distinctive glasses these days, although they're not featured in my pictures here. i'll try to work on that. books: i haven't been able to travel for a while, so i get my kicks vicariously through books. john le carr and paul theroux are great at bringing vivid atmosphere. prague, by arthur phillips. it's about budapest. i'm also big on steinbeck and hemingway and assorted nonfiction and travelogues. the best things i read this year include: norwegian wood (murakami), the tiger's wife, the yiddish policeman's union, state of wonder, freedom (it was worth the hype). and the scott pilgrim books -- i'm not usually into graphic novels, but they were hilarious!  if you're on goodreads, say hi! yeah, i'm a book nerd.  music: i saw girls at the gamh a while back and i love that this beachy california guitar thing is trending these days. other bands i'm into lately are tennis, best coast, the radio dept, yuck, dr. dog, camera obscura, wooden shjips, cults, surfer blood, rilo kiley, the black keys, real estate, etc. less new, but other favs are john prine, the grateful dead, the allman brothers,  movies: casablanca and the life aquatic duke it out for number one. fantastic mr. fox, charade, the departed, lock stock & two smoking barrels, lots more.  tv: arrested development, harvey birdman, lucy the daughter of the devil, and father ted make me laugh a lot. scrubs is a guilty pleasure. and i have recently gotten into mad men. bless you, netflix.  food:: yes please! sushi, mexican, vegan, zachary's, ici, i'm down to explore. someone please help me understand korean food, though. the flavors baffle me. 1. good music 2. independent bookstores and mcsweeney's internet tendency 3. an ocean nearby 4. my bicycle 5. a camera of some kind 6. my friends, who have really come through for me in the last year intentionality and what makes people act the way they do; how sex and sexuality and relationships work for society and for me (kind of revising/exploring my views lately along open/momogamish lines); books that i want to read; how to finish that story i started writing; how to be more creative...  ...and how appropriate it would be to pull out an arrested development quote in any given situation ...potentially out for drinks and burritos in the mission, or drinking wine at a friend's house, or reading at home... or at oakland art murmur. it really depends. that i'm a liiiiiittle addicted to instagram  that i have a terrible memory -- if i show up multiple times in your visitors, it's not due to creepy intentions, it's because i didn't remember that i saw your profile last week until i read your paragraph and was like, "oh yeah." my bad. you want to get a drink and talk about books or movies or music or life or whatever :)  you're low-key and have a good idea for a fun date  you want to be shown around my neighborhood in ocean beach (by the n line!)  you're okay at least starting off casual -- i'm open to where things might go, but i don't like putting a lot of pressure on seeing someone right away

full_bio: i am a 33 year old male living in berkeley. i work with computers on a daily basis for an startup company and just moved to the area. i frequently confuse terms that most people usually get right - like boot reer. no, it's not a bad thing, and is much more amusing than it sounds. in general, i enjoy movies, reading, and the company of funny & intelligent people over a pint. working out to the tune of mike tyson's punch out creating art, and generating confused responses seeing symbolism in everyday experiences being excited over silly things and trying not to burn my brain out in the process of learning new things. i'm really good at being a listening ear, fighting off hordes of demons, and doodling on bar napkins in order to amuse myself and others.  the first things that people usually notice about me are: my tattoos, the tshirt i'm wearing, and my wicked sense of awkward humor.  my tattoos represent my secret powers that only the very lucky find out. my tshirts are both clever and nerdy, which you will quickly find is how i would describe myself, my awkward humor is less inclusive of inane jokes but more of a charming shy comeback that perhaps i will need to explain to those less awesome than i (which wouldn't be you, of course). books: fiction. i read about a book a week, i also consume quite a bit of comic books. recently i've purchased a nook, so i'm curious to see how that affects my novel consumption.  movies: i love movies and watch many various types. my favorites are normally sci-fi, horror, and mockumentaries. if you get a chance you should watch "never been thawed" or "darkon" both are hilarious.  music: cosmic varieties  food: healthy mainly. i tend to not cook real meat at home, and mainly eat morning star farms or quorn meals. i also make home made beef jerky as well as fruit leather. - my two cats - chai and/or coffee in the mornings because it is delicious or can be soothing after a good fight to the death - smoothies as they give me the energy for the epic battles i wage - sketchbook because i enjoy bar napkins, but at times, you need something a bit more sturdy - good friends. i've heard they are awesome. - dorks/nerds/geeks    you should message me if you enjoy discussing fantasy, movies, or your own personal endeavors. i enjoy a good conversation, a good laugh, and some flirting.  ...and you would be interested in celebrating national peanut butter cookie day.

full_bio: i moved out of france when i was 10, to montreal. then followed a job offer 4 years ago in san francisco and got to love this place as yet another home. work-wise, i never intended to work that much and that seriously for the man. i'd want to do my part to make the world a better place but i find ingenious ways to market video games at the moment. i find okcupid really awkward, cold but meeting people in bars/clubs is getting old and i'm willing to give this a try. becoming a better person day after day. professionally, i'm doing something very challenging and fun. i like working on innovative projects and my coworkers are pretty awesome. i'd like to work on something bigger though, using games to teach would be my ideal. since a little over a year, this city became really good to me. i've met a few very nice people which make me feel right at home. i'm in here to find a partner for urban and not so urban explorations and adventures, discussions and party. procrastinating, having fun, having a serious chat, being awkward when nervous, nervous when awkward, remembering to take deep breaths, enjoying the little things, fixing the world issues in a discussion. i look younger than i am. french accent. i have big eyes too! people tend to notice when i try to give a not-so-discreet look their way. books: michel foucault, schrodinger cat, 7 habits of highly effective people, the mismeasure of man, delusions of gender, technomad talks: ted, rsanimate, gamepocalypse food: healthy local and organic food as much as possible music: minimal tech, deep house, reggae, rocksteady and a lot more tv: breaking bad, fringe, south park, daily show/colbert report movies: zombieland, idiocracy, inglorious bastards, la haine, i am love, water, laugh, dance, chat, sun quantum physics, nature of consciousness, time perception, grassroots politics, the universe. where am i going next, why am i worrying about tiny things. and of course, i'm spending time wondering why i receive plenty of visits but no messages.  you'll have to ask. it's most probably about me being an attention whore. i'm mostly looking to meet new people with a mix of silliness and deep thoughts. if you feel like giving me a tip or 2 about online dating, i'd take it too :)

full_bio: i'm a big believer in the simple pleasures: reading a book, building a bonfire in the garden, hiking, or going out dancing will always trump screen-time. i can even be vaguely hostile toward devices with screens, having once shackled a tv to my ankle to urge fellow college students to observe national tv-turn-off week.  when i am not engaged in public tomfoolery, i'm either learning something new, committing random acts of kindness, or searching for enlightenment.  i recently took a blacksmithing class at the crucible. woodworking might be next. i used to be a yoga teacher, and have learned many dances over the years-- salsa, tango, swing, contra dancing. if i'm in the middle of a good book, i can forget to eat lunch. when i do remember to eat lunch, i have usually made myself something amazing. right now for example, i'm eating a sweet potato gratin with smoked swiss cheese, lamb sausage, and rosemary.  ridiculousness is important: i like dressing up in costumes and will do so at every opportunity. dogs and little kids appeal to me in their capacity for silliness and unbridled joy.  i pursue a pretty active spiritual life, which is not to be confused with a "religious" life. it does mean that i meditate, and that the deeper questions of this human existence have intrigued me since i was a kid. it means i buy dinner for homeless people in my neighborhood a couple times a week, and that i can get pretty furious about injustice and greed. i value introspection and compassion above all else-- both in myself and others. intellect without compassion is dry.  so there's a lot more, which i now compress into this transparently obvious keyword paragraph: hiking, gardening, foreign films, travel in central america, sustainable development, anthropology, radical pedagogy, occupy, organizing, farmers' market, bike parties, theme parties, bluegrass, yoga, taoism.  regardless of whether you are a taoist bluegrass banjo virtuoso anthropologist with a prize winning community garden plot, i will get along well with you if you are...  a) kind to waitstaff, tollbooth operators, and other strangers b) are committed to something larger than yourself c) find humor in the absurdities of daily life d) are wickedly smart, as you define it  let's squeeze the juice out of the day together, and drink up. i'm a high school english teacher--a pretty radical, hilarious one, i might add. possibly headed into community organizing or back to school for a phd in anthropology in the next few years. and fantasizing about moving somewhere greener. listening without judging. finding the exact right words to help people solve their own problems. telling stories. dissecting philosophical arguments. singing. i walk fast and use fancy words. this year, screen-time that was worth it: the artist, the fighter, the fall, sweet land, as it is in heaven, downton abbey, the wire. books: the immortal life of henrietta lacks, the warmth of other suns, blood and thunder, empire of the summer moon (i'm a sucker for narrative historical nonfiction). my sister my parents good walking shoes cast iron skillet journal ginger whether homesteading is a realistic possibility, and whether i'd get too bored. making some kind of elaborate dinner, mercilessly crushing the competition in a game of balderdash, or dancing. i take between 3-6 nutritional supplements every day you like what you have read. or you can finish this limerick: there once was a ski bum from brussels  if it says i don't reply frequently, it's because i get a fair number of messages that say "hey, i like yer pics lets hang out." if you put some effort in, i'll say hi :)

full_bio: i'm a stranger to myself. oddly quixotic, i like to chase dreams and clouds. some may say i'm ungrounded. some may say i'm flighty. i just say i'm usually in several different places at once, so i guess its just a weird conundrum of sorts. come see for yourself. currently picking up the pieces... one step at a time, i'm making sure my life is going the way that i need it to go. whether or not it seems exciting or just plain mundane, at least i'm taking time to ensure a life thats worth living. being epically strange. like, really strange.  writing poetry, speaking before i think, smiling, dancing, laughing, making vegan foodstuffs, obsessing over random things, obtaining knowledge and an open worldview. i'm small, but spritely. i forget that i'm not very tall. i tend to feel like everybody's just tall, and i'm not very short. well, thoughts can be deceiving. i've got short hair that changes color pretty frequently, and i'm vegan. otherwise? i dunno... i wear dark eye makeup, or thick rimmed glasses. i've got a pretty big russian jew nose... and i tend to stutter on occasion. books/authors... chuck pahlaniuk [pygmy, fight club, rant, haunted], plato [euthyprho, crito], kurt vonnegut [timequake, cat's cradle], michael crichton [jurassic park, lost world, sphere, congo], dermaphoria, brian greene [an elegant universe], nietzsche [thus spake zarathustra]... even though he's a raving syphillitic lunatic, the zombie survival handbook, the ethical slut.  movies... the matrix, y tu mama tambien, lucia y el sexo, inception, the fifth element, fight club.  shows... psych, house.  music... the glitch mob, excision, deadmau5, datsik, moby, tomfoolery, infected mushroom, mindless self indulgence, flogging molly, the red elvises.  food... quickly. just a note: i have a severe quckly problem. i can't go for a day without at least one bubble tea and a fried tofu. i swear, that place is something else. philz coffee. granted, i work there, so i guess i'm biased, but that coffee is really better than any coffee i've ever had. it actually tastes like coffee. not just coffee, but complex, delicious flavors, like coffee is supposed to have. red berry, tobacco aromas, sweet, nutmeg, chocolate, buttery, more and more than one would ever get from starfucks or piteets. mmmm... caffeeine... sushi, namely cha-ya, since they have freaking amazing vegan sushi, with brown rice. like brown on rice, yo. vegan junk food- aka anything that i can find inside or out of a drugstore or supermarket that you wouldn't expect to be vegan. raw foods, macro-veganism, anything freegan (aka out of a dumpster or acquired for free), food not bombs, most anything ethnic... like indian food, mexican food (/drool... boorriiitoooos *zombiewalks*), mediterranean (falafel!), matzo farfel (don't ask), mochi, red bean anything, hemp juice, booze, anything hemp.  if i could live off of just a few things in life, it would be coffee, chocolate and... ah... yeah. that would be about it. oh, and dubstep. 1. michael... terralthra17 2. dance 3. music 4. art 5. good food 6. flow arts existential complications of the umpteenth degree. i tend to make lots of connections in my brain between various forms of consciousness, mantras, philosophies, and physical realities. i enjoy re-creating myself simply by utilizing the incredible tool of the mind. we say that the mind-body connection is imperative, like in yogic studies and whatnot. even if i do try to take things with a grain of salt, i can definitely say that my mind is usually my best tool and favorite thing to tinker with.  you can never change who you are... but you can always change how you are.  i'm honestly afraid of stupid people.  for that? you'll have to ask me in person. you've got an open mind, an open heart, and a willingness to explore new things in life.

full_bio: hmmm.....how to summarize.... competent, self sufficient, friendly, social, parent, traveler, scuba diver, intelligent, funny, caring, compassionate, volunteer, friend - not necessarily in that order.  paraphrasing from facebook - i don't have an amazing figure, although i have some nice curves. i am, in my opinion, far from being considered a model. but i am me. i enjoy good food and wine. i have scars (some physical, some emotional) because i have a history. most people love me, some just like me and a few, i am sure, hate me. i have done a lot of good but some bad too. i love my pj's, and sometimes love to get dressed up. i go without make up most of the time. i can be random and crazy but i can also be serious and task driven. i do not pretend to be someone, or something, i am not. i am who i am and at this age it's unlikely i will change. when i love, i love with all my heart and with loyalty the strength of a lion. i do not make apologies for the way i am, i just am. living one day to the next and enjoying the journey. have two great kids i have the honor of parenting and being a part of their lives. working on maintaining the wonderful relationships i have with family and friends. trying to be a responsible member of the world. looking for a companion to help me enjoy the ride. i'm good at a lot of things, not sure what i am really good at. would like to think i am really good at being a parent, a friend, partner, sibling, lover, contributor to society, probably my hair...but not really sure... books - pretty eclectic taste in books so no real favorite. love a good mystery, a quick beach book, chick books, ...  movies - michael (john travolta), thelma and louise, just saw 21 jump street and thought it was hilarious.  music - again - pretty eclectic in my taste - southern guitar rock, jazz, classical, r&b, r&r,  so - don't know if it's an inability to decide or just that i like a lot of different things - and nothing as a favorite - my children, my siblings, good wine, my friends, chocolate, sense of humor, someone to enjoy all of the above - not always in this order.. my children, my family, my friends, work, the prospect of a partner - what i want to do next. no such thing as typical - ranges from hanging out with kids, reading a good book, traveling somewhere, out with friends, sunsets at muir beach.... that i am online trying to meet new people enjoy a smart woman with a good sense of humor who is comfortable with who she is. you are looking forward to enjoying life's little and big adventures and a someone at your side.

sign: leo and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i have no stomach for "conventional wisdom." if it's conventional, how can it be wise?  i'm an artist and and activist. scattered but single-minded. i'm happy when my pessimistic predictions are wrong. i enjoy nature and coffeehouse. i have jeans and a cashmere sports coat; they look great together. don't like store-bought shirts, though; prefer colorful batik and white cuban guayaberas and the hand-dyed stuff you find at arts festivals.  i'm a history buff but think more about the future. i like quiet romantic evenings and loud concerts. candelight and sunlight. i'm bookish but also physically active: cuban salsa dancing, yoga, and lately i've taken up belly dancing. yes, you read that correctly.  the physical and spiritual are not opposites; the physical is the path and gateway to the spiritual. creativity unites them, for me. the purpose of my life is to make beautiful things, whether it's a poem or story, a dance, a video, my weekly newsletter, whatever.  i like the neighborhood where i live because it sounds like something from the i ching: wind over lake.  you? sort of like me but complementary, not opposite. a relationship needs to respect dual citizenship: the life that each partner has outside of it, not just inside. we may not do everything together, but we're still "in it" together.  a woman should be kissed well and often. i know you've got hands and can open a door by yourself, but it would give me pleasure to do it for you. unless i'm holding the grocery bags. if you start a pillow fight on sunday morning, you'll get the trouble you're looking for. i enjoy long weekend getaways, but that can wait.  we're both that person friends say are great and it's just a matter of time until we find "the right one." it's that time. don't you agree? creative and politically active work for a labor union that represents public service workers all over northern california. i'm the "new media guy" and edit the weekly e-newsletter. starting to spend more time with my own creative work, especially video dance. creating. guessing what time it is by looking at the sun's height above the horizon; though now that i've jinxed it by telling you, i won't be able to do it when we meet, especially if we meet at night. i don't think it's always the first thing, but i'm told i seem like i'm from new york, even though i'm not. native new yorkers swear they can tell which neighborhood i'm from by my accent. i find that hilarious. i like new york. i'm just not from there. philip k. dick, eduardo galleano, pablo neruda, thomas pynchon, mary oliver, and anything about quantum physics or cosmology. poetry. "landscapes painted with tea" and "london fields." battlestar galactica, rome, weeds, dexter. i've been without things, so i can't honestly say there's anything i "could never do without." not that i liked it, of course, but as a friend reminded me, "it's all just stuff." what i really can't do without are: passion, commitment, connectedness, artistry, yoga and dance, literature. books are a "thing," but it's the story that matters, not the tangible medium (i like e-books). i do have to admit, however, that my life and job would be problematic (more likely, impossible) without an iphone and other technologies for doing creative work. the state of the world. the state of my body and spirit. the state of my life. what i can do to improve all of them. creating beautiful things. rehearsing with my salsa performance group. then we go out dancing at a club or concert. that's not to say you and i could never go on a friday night date, but the group is a huge part of my life and i'm very committed to it. if i admit it, it won't be private any more. you're done waiting and want a real relationship.

full_bio: my friends would best describe me as full of life and humor. im originally from the bay area, but spent the last 9 years on the east coast. i am a definitely a silly girl, that loves to laugh. and who spends far too much time thinking of ways to make other people laugh.  i think i have a nice curiosity for life. im always looking for that next challenge/adventure, but also enjoy (sometimes maybe too much) a night in watching bad tv and chilling on the couch. i love live music, movies, great food, and watching football (yes, i said football!!)  my friends and family are my number one fans (and vice versa). so my ideal match would fit right in with my crazy group of friends. the right person for me is open to meeting new folks and accepting of all kinds.  i think overall im just looking to date someone who i would call my friend. someone with a sense of humor, a kind heart, consideration for others, someone who's non-judgemental, and above all honest. boom, there it is!!! i've run a couple marathons (slowly), sky-dived, done a little improv comedy, whatever i think will be cool for my bucket list. when im most scared to try something new, is usually when i end up having the best experiences. point being, i love to fill my life with memories, and spontaneity, and to push myself whenever possible. being spontaneous trying new recipes laughing when no one else thinks its funny dancing to my own beat (literally, its embarrassing) organizing other people (not so much my closet) did i mention laughing? on first sight, i guess it would be my smile. books: the power of now, the artists way, the brief wondrous life of oscar wao, the 4 hour work week, and a few sappy classic love stories to round out the collection  tv: im all over the map. sons of anarchy, archer, family guy, martin re-runs, greys anatomy, modern family and a sprinkling of random reality shows (yeah, i know)  movies: i can do mainstream, or indie. and i can a watch a movie over and over again if its great.  music: take your pick....r&b, hip hop, alternative, some jazz....whatever moves me  food: ill try anything once. i love all types of cuisine. indian, japanese, thai, american, italian, whatever. - family - friends - sunshine - creative outlets - quality time - football sundays my next move...  i cry during commercials sometimes. dont judge me, just hand me a tissue and walk away...lol. - you consider yourself a weekend warrior 60% of the time. (i wont judge you for the other 40%, we all need a few lazy days to recoup)  or  - you love life enough to try new foods, see new places, and to jump in a car on a whim because we decided we want to see this cool (???) and why not now!!  or  - you can take a joke, and maybe throw one back my way on occasion

full_bio: i grew up on the east coast and finally realized that life is too short to always be cold. unfortunately 2.5 years in san francisco taught me you can be cold even in california. i moved to oakland about 2 years ago and other than the ease of public transportation and missing walking everywhere, i am pretty happy with where i am (and quite a bit warmer).  my personality is still pretty east coast, i say what i think and like a heated discussion. i enjoy conversations with people who make me think, even if i don't agree. i prefer the company of people who can keep me on my toes and keep me thinking. i also tend to be on time (or early) so chronically significantly late people drive me somewhat insane.  i am funny, talkative, and opinionated the current economy and job situation has had impacts on my life but oddly enough in some good ways. i've had the chance to do more traveling and the free days have allowed me to spend more time with friends. working only part time has let me step back from the intense schedule that has been my life since college and spend more time smelling the roses. baking which i will foist upon you because i do not want the completed food at my house.  cross stitch (amazing how many people you meet when you do this in public, kids are fascinated).  beginning levels of languages, fluency however escapes me to my great dismay. how outgoing i am, i also apparently somehow always look ridiculously happy, books: i am a voracious reader and generally do not go to sleep at night without reading for a while. a recent 2 week trip found me packing 18 books, most of which were completed before i returned home. i will read almost anything other than romance and have a preference for hard science fiction and medical thrillers.  food: yes please and lots of it. i love food, generally the spicier the better. i'll eat almost anything and can actually count the foods i don't like on one hand. i've probably tried any weird food you can think of and am always up for something new. preferences usually run to asian food particularly thai, but i'm currently pretty into a peruvian restaurant i just discovered. my iphone (yes i am one of those), the gym, sarcasm, good friends, the ocean, travel my next adventure. i'm pondering taking off the month of march and traveling but the location is still up in the air. out with friends, a few drinks, a few laughs. i have a 2nd toe that is longer than my big toe, but only on one foot. you are fun, dont take life too seriously but have an actual plan in life. also, if you don't live in the bay area, i won't respond. to be honest what is the point?

thinking i also tend to be on time or early so chronically significantly late people drive me somewhat insane i am funny talkative and opinionated the current economy and job situation has had impacts on my life but oddly enough in some good ways i've had the chance to do more traveling and the free days have allowed me to spend more time with friends working only part time has let me step back from the intense schedule that has been my life since college and spend more time smelling the roses baking which i will foist upon you because i do not want the completed food at my house cross stitch amazing how many people you meet when you do this in public kids are fascinated beginning levels of languages fluency however escapes me to my great dismay how outgoing i am i also apparently somehow always look ridiculously happy books i am a voracious reader and generally do not go to sleep at night without reading for a while a recent 2 week trip found me packing 18 books most of which were completed before i returned home i will read almost anything other than romance and have a preference for hard science fiction and medical thrillers food yes please and lots of it i love food generally the spicier the better i'll eat almost anything and can actually count the foods i don't like on one hand i've probably tried any weird food you can think of and am always up for something new preferences usually run to asian food particularly thai but i'm currently pretty into a peruvian restaurant i just discovered my iphone yes i am one of those the gym sarcasm good friends the ocean full_bio: maker of things. passionate communicator, careful observer. assertive, demanding, intense. fiery, sharp-tongued, usually in charge. overeducated, understimulated, casually supersonic.  european ex-nomad with a tinge of an accent and a fistful of old world charm. i run a witness protection program for electrons. they do as i say and no one gets hurt. it's a pretty spectacular setup. when not at work, i spend a lot of time with my puppy, who has a lifelong vendetta against sprinklers but really likes secluded beaches and lonely hikes. i passed a quarter million miles of travel a few years ago; these days i travel far less but enjoy it more. in short: work hard, sleep little, eat well, drive fast. catastrophic puns and creative distractions: i've written a book, raced cars, and given lectures around the world. i can also ride my bike with no handlebars, keep a rhythm with no metronome, and see the strings that control the systems. i cannot yet tie a knot in a cherry stem, split the atoms of a molecule, or lead a nation with a microphone. these are areas of ongoing self-improvement.  my favorite piece of non-fiction is richard feynman's qed. i watch castle, community, and still haven't finished mourning the end of house, but otherwise infrequently operate a tv. bbc's new sherlock was just excellent. i have a soft spot for masterfully crafted science fiction like firefly and battlestar galactica.  i love pitch-perfect satire; dr. strangelove is an all-time favorite, and i quite enjoyed in the loop. dr. horrible's sing-along blog is too long to be called a short and too short to be called a feature film, but it was spectacular. and i recently saw indie game: the movie, which was just delightful.  despite a long-lasting love affair with electronic music, my actual playlists tend to be all over the genre map. my food tends to be simple and unprocessed. i have mastered the art of hard-searing steak rare; if this were not so, i could be a vegetarian. if i like you, i might tell you about the time i found myself sitting on a cape in the middle of a desert, my worldly possessions hundreds of miles away, and all i missed was the puppy.  but a few things i'd rather not do without? minimalist design and elegant typography. dark chocolate, dark beer, and darkly roasted coffee beans, sometimes all at once.   as a card-carrying member of the godless liberal egalitarian elite, i donated to planned parenthood after the komen disaster, love me my npr, and get anxiety attacks when i'm more than an issue behind on the economist. what if president obama calls and asks me a current events question? i'm a sucker for crazy-smart, kind, independent, opinionated, driven women. who are excited about their life. which is roughly in order. controlled chaos counts.  being passionate about things is hot. the world is a fascinating, complicated, messy place; my coping strategy is a fucked up sense of humor, which you can ideally at least appreciate from a distance. intellectual chemistry is important. this means, among other things, that you're well-read and not religious, and do not shy away from examining your beliefs. physical attraction is just as important, which means you're active, fit, and take excellent care of yourself. (i love staying in and having a lazy sunday, but the occasional 15-hour hike with seven thousand feet of elevation change is also a bloody good time.) it helps a lot if vanilla isn't your favorite flavor.  points if you love to figure out how things work, or you make things, or you haven't lost your sense of childlike wonder, or you have probing questions about people, the universe, and the sheer magnitude of it all.

full_bio: i'm a norcal native - grew up in the countryside of napa, which as a kid seemed incredibly boring, but now, seems awesome. after a brief stint in new york for college, i realized california owns my heart and moved back to the bay area for law school. i've been bouncing back and forth between napa and the sf area since.  i have a penchant for loud music, foreign cities, the california countryside, people with kind eyes and easy smiles, whisky by the fireplace in the winter and gin & tonics by the pool in the summer, champagne and pinot - always and anywhere, high heels, boots, sundresses, the sound of ocean and rain while i'm sleeping, other people's dogs, being awake when the rest of the world is asleep, clean sheets, long showers, being lazy in the sun, skies that make me feel like i'm living inside a painting, summertime.  i don't feel like a house is my home unless it is filled with music and flowers.  i run, lots.  i appreciate honesty, intelligence, an open mind, a kind heart, and a good sense of humor.  i make a mean mixtape.  and i'm really excited about the future. during the day, practicing law with a small firm in sf. happy to have a career that challenges me on a daily basis.  outside working hours: chasing the sun; spending as much time outdoors as possible. exploring. traveling. hiking. backpacking. going to concerts. gardening. seeking out self-aware people. seeking out new music. improving the beauty of the spaces around me. learning. striving to be a better person, for myself and those around me.  living fast. improvising. most of all, kindness. also: listening problem-solving finding the light in the darkness compassion accepting life's truths horticulture lounging poolside hijacking the soundtrack finding the good in people making you dinner seeing the beauty in the everyday. smile. i like to pretend i care about the rest of this but really i'm just obsessed with music: emancipator, pretty lights, health, jj, arcade fire, m83, yeasayer, brenton duvall, fever ray, emily wells, paul basic, the knife, pictureplane, cslsx, adventure club, deadmau5, kanye, lil wayne, keep shelly in athens, wale, leonard cohen, ratatat, washed out, crystal castles, weeknd, glass candy, austra, golden filter, anything sampling biggie or tupac.  i keep a lot of novels around the house, but most of the time i just read the nyt and call it a day.  tv & movies: in moderation. i get restless if i sit still for too long.  food: yes please. anything and everything. and i love to cook. second chances a fridge well stocked with champagne sunshine hope/possibilities music the high of a long run self improvement. how the past shapes us. relationships. people, and why they do the things they do. how much there is still to learn. meeting friends out for dinner or drinks. hosting or attending a dinner party. going to a concert. or resting up for a weekend adventure.  you want more. i like people who take chances. victory to the bold.  or, you want me to make you a mixtape.  bonus if you know how to build/fix things, are tall, have style, and/or have pet(s), because i love them but don't have any, only houseplants. but none of these are essential (except maybe the style part; i can be particular). what is essential is that you are intelligent and funnier than most, but also, at your core, kind. and excited about the possibilities in life.  full profile disclosure: i am presently living in napa, but keep my profile at sf because i work here, so meeting up in the city is never difficult for me, and i prefer it. and should we become friends, i find that most people do not object to the idea of the occasional visit to wine country - it's a beautiful place with much to offer (not least of all: reliable sunshine). however, if you're only interested in meeting people who live down the street from you, or you're scared of crossing bridges, then we probably are not meant to be. best of luck in your search!

religion: atheism and somewhat serious about it
full_bio: 'ello one and all. personally, i am pretty laid back and casual. my best friend's wife calls me a "free spirit," which i like. i am a huge film geek and i tend to occupy a good amount of my time in that arena. i am not one that goes out to bars and/or clubs a lot unless someone i know is performing. i just enjoy spending time with my friends, going to movies, and making the occasional road trip to various areas.  my friends are the most important thing to me and i am proud to be called "uncle" to my best friends' kids. though i like kids, i am not interested in having any of my own. sorry!  professionally, i have worked as an insurance investigator for over 8 years. though the home office is in sf, i am rarely there as my job takes me everywhere in the bay area, and at times, beyond that. my job can be stressful from time to time, and contrary to popular belief, it is really not too interesting or exciting though you may find it to be. i can explain my work to you in further detail if need be.  also, in my spare time, i like to read and write a lot.  in addition, i am back performing in the rocky horror picture show as an eddie, dr. scott, and criminologist, and perform once or twice a month (i was first involved performing in it from 1995 to 2000). if you would like additional information as to when, where, etc., just ask.  i am just looking to find someone with similar interests and kind of go from there. thanks for reading. :o) what i'm doing with my life is finding a girlfriend! at least on here. that is why we are here, right? :-) seriously, aside from work, i am working on various hobbies and projects to include film, writing, reading, spending time with my friends, and working on my place among other things.  for example, there are times i will drive all the way to the stanford theatre in palo alto to just see a classic film because i can. i will go to the castro theatre in san francisco to see a great film. i will go to the paramount theatre in oakland to see a classic film they are showing. it would be nice to bring someone special with me to share in that fun. :o) finding things that others can't, remembering things others can't, relating to someone no matter who they are, conversing, and being there for someone. i'm not really sure. i guess it would be my eyes or my smile.  aside from that, i am very friendly and interested in what the person i am conversing with is all about. also, i am quite opinionated about things especially movies. books- the great gatsby, 1984, a clockwork orange, tropic of cancer, many types of film books, and biographies. anything by james ellroy, david thomson, warren ellis, and joe queenan.  movies- as i am a film geek, i have a large list that consists of over 200! due to this fact, i will provide 30 of them to give you an idea: all about eve, bringing up baby, bonnie and clyde, chinatown, dogfight, duck soup, exotica, gas, food lodging, grand illusion, heathers, henry fool, intolerance, kiss me deadly, lone star, longtime companion, milk, mulholland drive, my life as a dog, my own private idaho, the player, raise the red lantern, the rules of the game, the searchers, sweet smell of success, the third man, totally f***ed up, the unbearable lightness of being, waking life, wings of desire, and y tu mama tambien.  you will note my major love for film noir. the other genre i enjoy a lot are german expressionism movies. i love all film, but those genres are the ones that i really get into. essentially, my church is going out to see a great film.  music- sonic youth, the cure, concrete blonde, oingo boingo, danny elfman, ani difranco, dresden dolls, daryl hall and john oates, curve, the animals, velvet underground, bauhaus, juliana hatfield, p.j. harvey, devo, joy division, nick cave and the bad seeds, neko case, the new pornographers, tom waits, garbage, 80's, and so much more. as far as i am concerned, any new music from the past 12 years or so has sucked. any good music from that time period is very few and far between for me though there are a few newer artists listed.  television- i have not had cable or rabbit ears for well over 13 years, and it has made me a better person! it is a rarity that i watch any t.v., and overall, i really haven't missed too much! be that as it may, here are some past and somewhat current when i see a bit of the telly: mystery science theater 3000, south park, hogan's heroes, miami vice, tanner '88 (mini-series), l.a. law, futurama, and tales from the crypt.  food- pasta in all of its form, pizza, stir fry, any kind of rice, a good burger, and chicken caesar salad among others. more on the treat/junk side, i love snickers, reese's peanut butter cups, haagen dazs' coffee ice cream and ben and jerry's cinnamon buns ice cream. 1. my friends. 2. my nephews and nieces. 3. my movies. 4. my television (i need it to see my movies). 5. my mystery science theater 3000 collection. 6. my vcr and/or dvd player(s). why michael bay is still allowed to make movies and why he hasn't been killed of some torturous death! ...either going out for a movie, at a cafe or restaurant reading and/or writing, at home relaxing and watching a movie, or spending quality time with one of my friends. i am not one that really goes out to the clubs or anything. as i wrote before, i do that on occasion, but it is usually with friends or if a friend of mine is performing. not so much private, but more like private weaknesses. despite yourself, everyone has weaknesses towards things that are opposite of you and/or just plain embarrassing so to speak. here are some of mine:  wrestling- i am a huge fan of professional wrestling. in fact, i went to wrestlemania xix in seattle back in 2003. yes, i know it is fake, but i have always enjoyed it and followed it for the most part since i was a kid. i still follow it, but not to the magnitude that i use to. however, it is still kind of fun as my nephew ty is a big fan and we get to talk and relate to it a lot. :o) if you are curious, my favorite wrestler of all time is the great muta.  richard marx- don't ask me why, but i have always enjoyed his pop rock a lot, especially his first two albums. i can't pinpoint it, but it is just something about his music i like. "don't mean nothing" is probably my favorite song due to his lyrics. you would be surprised how some of the popiest songs ever have some of the best lyrics.  melrose place- i started watching the show after the infamous "seinfeld" episode. it is cheesy, dumb, and stupid, but there was something about it that made me like it. perhaps it is kind of like wrestling as you root for your favorite characters. my favorite was kimberly (marcia cross) as she was just so psycho! my second favorite was peter (jack wagner), primarily because he had some of the best snappy comebacks ever and jack wagner delivered it so well. i am still waiting for him to be casted in a supporting role in a movie as the typical friend with the smart-ass/sarcastic remarks as he is perfect for that type of role.  if i think of any others, i will reveal my horrible secrets here. ;-) if you can use the word "you're" properly in a sentence. since when did proper grammar and good spelling take a back seat?

full_bio: i am a happy person and aim to live every day to the fullest.  i graduated from college with double major industrial engineering and soft systems (mexico - italy). later on, i went and started my own it consulting company in mexico. four years later i sold it and moved to texas since i was due for a new adventure and i was also awarded a scholarship for a masters.  staying active and healthy goes a long way in my book. i am up for anything outdoors...tennis (4.0 usta) - kayaking - inline skating - acro yoga, anti-gravity yoga...very active i know- i have two little guys (yello labs) which i run 5k every saturday (otherwise they would rip my place apart).  i rarely do anything half-way, i am creative, working hard, and getting inspired. staying @t home and playing video games and geting a check from my familly! {just kidding}  i work as a project manager (pmp) and a instructor ( friends thought i was teaching people how to be a pimp, go figure) i enjoy cooking italian and mexican food from scratch and staying busy.  lately going more green and experimenting with new recipes (stuffed peppers are currently at the top of my list).  music: classic rock, oldies and contemporary jazz  food: mexican, italian and any spicy foods  movies: italian, british, spanish & french productions 1:doggies 2:fam & friends 3:dancing 4:coffee 5:tea 6:netbook my family and friends are scattered across texas, mexico and europe, so i try to visit them when i can. i've spent some time living and traveling internationally (buenos aires, mexico, italy, switserland and germany), but i suppose i'm about due for the next big trip or what is the next adventure i can go on... whether it be big or small. cooking and enjoying time hosting dinners for friends or getting ready for the weekend trip :-) i am on this site

full_bio: i'm pretty introverted or similar, but when something is exciting i can't help showing it, and i am just an energetic person in general. i also have a very mediocre sense of humor and never use sarcasm at all.  so, things that are exciting include: sunshine going out to eat eating delicious, fresh fruits cheesecake anything with almond extract beauty and the beast, or any fairy tales and books traveling anywhere with friends singing along to songs with friends taking naps with kitty on sunday afternoons new used clothes game nights happy hours that include food soccer games and perhaps more! i am in art school. it's fun and stimulating. painting is exhilarating and the other students are all so very clever.  lots of people have asked and will ask me, "what do you want to do after you graduate from art school?"  apart from all that, i have an office job off and on, work with kids on sundays, and i like to hang with friends for galleries, movies, trivia nights, gaming, and food/baking.  i'm on a soccer team or two. i have my moments.  additionally, i'm good with kids and a good drawer, getting better at transcribing from my imagination. i know many card games. thrift shopping. i always have the biggest pupils in the room. and it's not from drugs, okay. i like reading books: jane eyre, any sci-fi/fantasy (e.g. orson scott card, neal stephenson), anne rice, other dark romance. thrifted novels.  i like the dark knight, inglourious basterds, and many from the coen brothers ("you're not wrong walter. you're just an asshole."). favorite tv series is rome, and i also enjoy watching true blood, the wire, and the beginning seasons of snl, you know, with gilda <3 recent: i'm watching twin peaks for the first time and also just saw the graduate.  indie hippity-hoppity... and sometimes just oldies.  i love ripe fruit such as peaches, grapes, oranges, yum (apple pears when in season)! gruyere cheese is a childhood favorite. chicken thighs are delicious. mild curries or kormas are adult favorites. escapes from reality (ex: see next item) sleeping a lot fruit! my frands exercise arts what do i want to paint next?! display of craft vs. concepts. taste vs. quality. the one liner is the new black. (see: reddit, twitter.) probably making bad jokes somewhere.  you like artists you are passionate about something

full_bio: let's see...  i graduated in 2010 from uc berkeley with a degree in history, with a focus on latin america. i wrote my undergraduate thesis on political ideology in chile during the 1960's.  it's my goal to be a man-of-letters :). also a bit of a travel junkie... enjoying life.  navigating my way back to academia...i'm going to get my phd in political science. stepping out of my comfort zone.  pablo neruda.  jorge luis borges.  por supuesto, hay mas... i work out while listening to npr.    you can bake chocolate chip cookies.

full_bio: i'm irreverent. a people-watcher. a time traveler. dancing and singing to old music. intellectual arguments. catching myself by surprise. laughing as much as possible. picking out peculiarities. just moved to the bay. (if you have any leads on work, send a message in this direction.)  i'm really into perception and memory. right now i'm doing this year long art project. basically, i'm taking pictures every day and writing poetry, to map out where and who i am and locate it within a certain time in my life. i did a project similar to this three years ago and it still is an amazing reference point for me.  i'm interested in anarchy, consent, the middle east, and contradiction. parallax thinking. my mind moves in metaphors and analogies.  socializing, meeting new people.  i can also play a chopin waltz (d flat, op.64). i'm tall. ok, not "b-ball tall." but i've always been tall. it's a psychological condition. a really great psychological condition. at first i'm either really reserved or really out there. (don't you love vague horoscopic statements?)  also, i currently sport a mustache. i like it. i don't know how long he's here to stay but i've welcomed him on. conversations with history  slavoj zizek (although i don't necessarily agree with him), chomsky, my favorite novel is mrs. dalloway with gravity's rainbow a close second. period pieces are so fucking weird and important, even better are things from the actual period.  david graeber, david lynch, ulysses, 8 1/2, akira, the bible (esp. judges and jonah, they're pretty funny (and i'm not religious btw (i meditate sometimes but i don't even think of that as spirituality, really (lately i've been thinking that the perception of mind, body, and spirit as separate entities is fucked to begin with))), mf doom, the clash, johnny cash, godard, finnegan's wake (but seriously though), sleep (dopesmoker), minor threat, lightning bolt, afrirampo, akb48, roland barthes, fatal cradle (my hibernating band), almost doesn't count by brandy but only the first 2/3 of the song before you don't really care about the repeating chorus and change it, ravel and schumann solo piano pieces,   , earth, wind, and fire, chinatown,  , sinatra, bossa nova (the genre), social psychology, direct action  pho, sushi, chicken parm, shwarma, rice, sauted vegetables ok, here are six things that i really like in other people:  honesty, curiosity, conviction (but definitely not blind conviction), self-awareness, good conversationalist/arguer, and realistic optimism or at least optimistic pragmatism  i could do without people who often say things like "that's just the way it is." i don't like cynicism or biological determinism either. my death. it puts the rest of my life in perspective.  hypnosis and the sheer power of existence. cognitive psyche.  i am constantly defining and redefining myself for job applications and cover letters. it's extremely exhausting and i want it to stop. with friends!  you like horror movies, have traveled around another country alone, and think it would be fun to leave it all and go live on a sailboat. (the sailboat thing is the least important, the traveling is the most.)  also, i think sex-positive feminists are really rad.

full_bio: andrew_william is my primary partner in life and various trouble making. i tell him everything.  i like to get personal. i have no problem disclosing my personal history. warning* it is a bit much for some to swallow, but if you cant handle my past you most certainly cannot handle who i have become because of it. i am not afraid to admit my faults. i have learned valuable lessons from the mistakes i have made and i am more than willing to share them with you if it helps you to learn something about yourself. i am very comfortable with myself inside and out.  with all that being said, i find laughter comes easy. i would like to think i have a great sense of humor. i love to laugh. i love to make others laugh. however, i don't appreciate racist, sexist or jokes about lgbtqi people. oddly enough dead baby jokes are a riot. i have many quarks like that, that will surely keep you on your toes ;) under construction. painting my nails, video games, snuggling etc.. my hair? books: the contortionists handbook by craig clevenger; moll flanders by daniel defoe; do androids dream of electric sheep by philip k. dick; and frankenstein by mary shelley.  movies: ikiru by akira kurasawa, ponyo by miyazaki, star wars, serenity, and dreams by akira kurasawa as well.  music: nick cave, yeah yeah yeahs, mgmt, feist, the beatles, cotton jones, vetiver, johny cash, wu tang; lots of different stuff ;)  food: healthy.  art: banksy, basquiat, warhol, duchamp sunsets, dramatic weather, kisses, laughter, cuddles, and salads people. why do we do the things we do? petting my kitties and thinking of an adventure for caturday ;) weird. under construction.

full_bio: laughing and smiling are at the center of my worlds. i enjoy working out, hiking, golfing and touring wine country. this thing keeps yelling at me that i have to put 500 words or more on here so.....  i think i'm funny. i like people who are also funny (major bonus points if after meeting me you determine that i am indeed funny). i believe sarcasm is the ultimate mental foreplay and should be deployed at all times and all costs. this of course sometimes proves problematic with those in the population who don't "get it".  i own a tuxedo and a couple of very nice suits and can clean up well when the situation requires - wedding, funeral, ballet opening, etc. however, i much prefer to be in shorts and flip flops with the top down heading to either a friend's bbque or a music event in wine country.  being physically active and fit is very important to me. i find my mental state to be so much better when my body feels energized.  i'm told that i look and act (in a good way) much younger than my birth certificate would indicate. looking for someone who can keep up regardless of their birthdate.  "it may be the devil or it may be the lord, but you're gonna have to serve somebody" bob dylan i'm assuming he likes his food spicy and would it be asking too much to get a pair of hot mitts? (i'm an atheist btw) ;-)  and as my good friend ricardo montablan was fond of saying..... " smiles everyone, smiles ! " hanging onto the tail of the tiger!  i am a partner in a 401k management company. making a killer meal and figuring out the perfect wine to go with it.  hiking in the hills around my house.  planning an awesome bbque party.  kissing (or so i have been told).  asking questions that help me to understand whether we have the possibility of an intellectually engaging conversation.  knowing that i need to have spell check turned on so that when i type "intellectual" i don't misspell it and look like a complete tool.  making people around me laugh.  ostrich lassoing (it's not as easy as you'd think)  partying like a band manager (had to give up the rock star lifestyle on doctor's orders).  looking at your profiles and wondering why you chose to include certain pictures that really don't make you look attractive.  knowing that some of you have just read the last line and are thinking that my profile needs help too. ;-)  clearing the cache on my ipad.  holding the door open confession: yes i know that this helps me to look like the gentleman i am in public, and hopefully makes you feel taken care of and a small bit special, but it also allows me to check out your butt. ;-) is my height, my smile and my laugh. i'm 6'5" and wear size 13 shoes - so i suppose it depends on whether you're looking up or down - if you are focused somewhere in between i guess we can just eliminate the small talk huh? ;-) i listen to almost all kinds of music. not too keen on f-bomb rap or acid rock, but most everything else goes. i have a very ecclectic pandora playlist.  arrested development, blazing saddles, hbo comedy, entourage a wine glass flip flops a glass of fresh oj to start my day good friends being outside pandora what i'm going to do next for fun - where and with whom and how much trouble can we get into....  some of the questions on this site and a) who thought of them and b) who would actually answer some of them....  does the fact that i've never been to burning man (and probably never will) mean that i'm not cool....?  what contest do you have to win to get one of the cool "awards" on this site? having a couple of cocktails to start the weekend off right. i'm not nearly as vanilla as this profile might indicate - oh and i sometimes skip to the "sex questions" when reviewing your answers! you're attracted to tall, dark, and funny - and there's a part of you that most people don't really know about.

full_bio: i love to travel, explore, and hit up yummy hole-in-the-wall places.  my favorite neighborhood in sf is the castro with the mission being a close second. probably because there's a bookstore and a cafe on every corner.  i complain about taking the muni like everyone else but i secretly love people-watching on the ride. even if there are a lot of crazies that like to ride back and forth. mostly grad school and all its minutiae peppered with adventures and good times with friends whenever i get a chance. baking cookies and bread right before exams.. as stress relief!  exploring the city on (rare) sunny days :)  knowing the difference between "their", "they're", and "there".  butchering an english accent quite enthusiastically.  books: cloud atlas, watership down, black swan green, a tree grows in brooklyn, game of thrones, his dark materials, harry potter, city of thieves, shantaram, stiff, and memoirs + anything about india/north korea, medical science, psychology/behavior - chick lit/twilight/most "tween" fiction movies: fight club, almost anything pixar, the dark knight, the prestige, iron man/x-men/geeky comic book movies shows: the walking dead, community, fringe, revenge, downton abbey various other oldies but goodies: scrubs, frasier, boston legal food: chicken and waffles make up my last meal request. oh, and peanut butter. my phone books green tea my endless collection of scarves music/youtube/spotify facebook... sadly :x food, and where my next yelp destination will be :)  i like to hog the mike during karaoke. hehehe... you want to finish the 7x7 100 eats list :)

full_bio: where to start? i'm a 26 year old bartender who works in san francisco out on the piers. i just quit that job recently and am now working in sonoma, california, smack dab right in wine country and i'm learning so much about the wine industry because of it! i'm a late bloomer but i'm making up for lost time in a big way- and loving every second of it! after i turned 21 my life really started to pick up speed. i love to go out and dance, and i recently got my motorcycle license. i'm smart, funny, have an infectious laugh and am extremely loyal. i'm dependable, a hard worker and a good friend.  i love to travel and plan to do more of it (this past summer i got a chance to go on two separate cruises- if you haven't been before run, don't walk!). next up on my list to visit are germany and korea. so far i've trekked to vietnam, china, indonesia, france, italy and spain. travelling is really one of the best things a person can do, and i'm 100% in favor of it. just got back from new orleans for the first time, amazing city, bourbon street is crazy and there is amazing food there, will definitely be planning on another trip next year!  i am humourous, nice, and fun working as much as i can, i am pretty driven and dedicated to what i do, and i love my job so it's hard to complain about anything right now, making and saving as much money as i can :) brushing up on my motorcycling skills whenever i get the chance and reading and learning what i can in life.  planning the next trip and figuring out where i want to travel next. games, dancing (i try!) reading, doing nerdy/geeky things sometimes. eating sushi and other goodies. my sweet dance moves or my smile hah :) favorite books: brave new world, atonement, on chesil beach, amsterdam, various fantasy novels. currently working on the prison nation. just finished up the confession by john grisham, and i read the girl with the dragon tattoo, the girl who played with fire, and the girl who kicked the hornet's nest in about 2 weeks time, hunger games trilogy, song of ice and fire series by george rr martin.  favorite movies: dark knight, district 9, braveheart, iron man, transformers, old school, the professional, aliens, the hangover.  music: pop, hip-hop, rock, michael jackson (r.i.p) pretty open to listening to anything, i usually end up enjoying just about every genre of music. currently re-enjoying some older music like garbage, muse, katy perry, rihanna, taio cruz, red hot chili peppers.  food: love pretty much anything and willing to try new things all the time, currently on a sushi craze. thai, chinese, vietnamese, french, italian, mexican/spanish food are all awesome. my friends, books, my computer, family, cars, my motorcycles, sleep! full_bio: i live on a cliff at the end the world. i keep watch for mermaids and the occasional pirate ship but mostly talk to my dogs, read, take beautiful images, or plan what i'll eat next.  when that isn't enough i explore the coast or condescend to drive into the city where there are always bars and good friends waiting.  i travel a lot. i love bad tv, good music, think frank sinatra had a lot of class -- and really great hats. i have a great job and i love what i do. work is high-stress. it is also high-fun. some friends and i run a business where we do what we love and take shit from no one. they are the editors, i'm the publisher.  i am established at this point and having that part of the equation settled leaves me time to do things i enjoy rather than chase or improve my career. i do what i love, its more than enough.  at the top of the list are always photography, playing with my dogs, being on or near the ocean, seeing what new trouble my daughter can cook up and always learning new things.  i'm as happy standing in a river, tying flies on to my leader after a big tangle as i am catching a fish; i generally put them back. i can get just as lost in a jigsaw puzzle or lego set as i can a museum, so staying home happens a lot.  i do look at cities as if they are museums and am far more interested in finding the history than the boutique. i also enjoy establishing a sense of familiarity with cities i fall for and will go back a number of times in short order and then every few years; to watch them change.  i love bars (i used to own one) but they need to be dark and have a juke box or a piano. taking pictures, getting into trouble and then back out of it.  i'm really good at the trouble part -- makes for fantastic stories.  catch 22, casablanca, tom waits, mash, the wild wild west, herbie hancock, the beatles, the way you wear your hat, h.r. pufnstuf, the magnificent seven, sinatra, martin, dropkick murphys, ray la montagne, diana krall, elvis costello, rosencrantz and guildenstern are dead.  well didn't that feel like a stream of consciousness exercise?  the best play i have ever seen was 'engima variations' by eric-emmanuel schmitt.  i'm happy with any style of food if we're talking restaurant. at home i cook, well and frequently. mostly french, indian, german and good ol' american. mexican and guatemalan have been creeping in, mostly because my daughter is interested. my daughter my 3 dogs good books good music a camera velvia 50  when i start having typical anything, i'll let you know. an anachronism is me.  also... the dogs do talk back.
