full_bio: not a theme for a buddhist (the no-self, everything is empty of permanent being thing) but soto zen's founder eihei dogen wrote, "to study the way is to study the self." the path to buddhism has been long, winding and enriching - it feels like home right now. so, my self: hard-working self-employed landscaper. puerto rican descent (mom, three brothers and a sister live in pr). born, raised and educated in southern new jersey. five years in manhattan after college teaching english at a prep school in nj. moved to cali in '82 for a change of scene. compassionate, art lover, bird watcher, people watcher, tree-hugger, left-leaner, fun-lover. like the art galleries and music halls. a big fan of s.f. shakespeare festival, hardly strictly bluegrass festival, and dia de los muertos mission procession. spending a lot of time on the gardening business and meditating on the next phase of my life. visiting my daughter and her fiance in brooklyn as often as i can. showing family and friends around san francisco. helping friends prepare for a party or a big meal. creating a flower arrangement. giving a massage. listening. enjoying the moment. the housemate who started me on okcupid wrote,"my really easy-going persona." i'll take his word on that. beyond that i don't know. favorite novels? pride and prejudice, light in august, the sun also rises, dracula, moby dick, 100 years of solitude, to kill a mockingbird, henderson the rain king, women in love, the mists of avalon, 2666 (roberto bolano), out (natsuo kirino), lord of the rings, her fearful symmetry (audrey niffenegger).  favorite poets? walt whitman, rainer maria rilke, john keats, gary snyder, edna st. vincent millay.  favorite non-fiction? the masks of god (joseph campbell), the gospel according to jesus (stephen mitchell), the fate of the earth (jonathan schell), zen mind, beginner's mind (shunryu suzuki)  contemporary writers i like: chuck palahnuik, natsuo kirino, herta muller, stephen king, david sedaris.  what about films? let's see...all time favorites: cabaret, wings of desire, manhattan, maltese falcon, women in love, mccabe and mrs. miller, 2001: space odyssey, last tango in paris, pinocchio (disney), the american friend, tootsie, blade runner, sundays and cybele.  i'll see anything by clint eastwood, almavador, hayao miyazaki. luv the sf film festival, foreign films, offbeat indie stuff and sci fi.  musically i have my favorites: bjork, neil young, the dead, the stones, juana molina, gillian welch, mia doi todd, sigur ros, cat power, neko case, shelby lynne, joni mitchell, rufus wainwright. i've been listening to arcade fire's suburbs a lot. i like a good salsa band, a good blues band, anything i can dance to. and i like to check out new music and venues. not averse to classical music or operas, but not enough time to see/listen to much of that. luuuvv the garden of memory summer solstice concerts at the chapel of the chimes in piedmont. my daughter s.f. zen center my hearing a garden a guitar love whatever is going on. recouping from the week and preparing for the weekend activities of which there always seem to be more than i can attend. i'm shy. ha, ha! you like doing the things i like (you don't have to like all of them) and are willing to invite me to explore the things you like. hopefully you have a spiritual nature, a musical nature, a humorous nature (i can be a humorous fellow - runs in the blood), a poetic nature, an artsy nature - again, no need to be all those people.

full_bio: important things you should know about me: i enjoy salsa dancing, crossword puzzles, and philosophical conversations over red wine. i can't stand most reality tv shows. i love playing pranks on people.  i grew up and went to college in california, but journeyed over to switzerland for grad school. in 2008, i got swept up by the obama campaign, and then moved out to dc to help save the world (which turns out to be harder to do than i thought). i recently moved back to san francisco, and am still working on doing that here. i work at a progressive political startup based in san francisco. it's a lot of work, but also very rewarding. i pride myself on having many secret talents. you'll have to get to know me to learn what they are. my beard. or my sarcastic sense of humor. books: anything by salman rushdie, the foundation series, ender's game, harry potter, the hunger games, a song of ice and fire  movies: the usual suspects, up, fight club, slumdog millionaire, kill bill, black swan, sin city  music: simon and garfunkel, green day, les miserables, good songs from every genre  food: indian, thai, or mexican (although i'm also a big fan of a really good burger)  what life will be like in the future and how i can help make it better usually at a game night or house party with friends, though sometimes at home watching tv. i grew up in california, but am exceptionally clueless about the geography in this state (chico is next to fresno, right?)

full_bio: i am a passionate and driven person deeply in love with metaphor for the story..  i am an athlete and an artist.  i want to dream the future into being in collaboration with community. and so i think about how my choices impact others, and how to walk the talk.  i would rather bike than drive, but i love traveling and don't mind flying or long road trips. i love the smell of the ocean and sand between my toes. i would rather be hot than cold; and therefore love the sun, hottubs, and saunas.  i love my 15 year old dog and consider her my kid.  i like to dance to old-skool hip hop and deep house (no techno please). long bike rides on the weekend make me smile and are pretty much a necessity (30-70 miles). although lately i have been doing insanity and made it to month 2 - the max workouts and love it - even if i am sore all over again.  i must have my coffee in the morning, but need it to be fair trade.  i am interested in folks who want to connect on many levels in person, appreciate and adore independent strong women, laugh a lot, self-reflect, play, and communicate. i want to meet people who love their body and mind and are perceptive lovers.  i am also interested in a deeper connection and don't have a lot of time to divide between many people, so i want to build something deep with one person right now and in 2012 i want to be "wooed."  i love meeting new people and dinner parties.  for fun: read (mostly fiction), work-out, bike ride along the coast preferably, walk my dog, theater, comedy, live music, dance, performance.  i am queer and care more about who you are than "what" you are. update: just finished my first century ride! oakland to sacramento fundraiser for public education in oakland - ride for a reason. 109 miles in 93 degree weather was no joke, but i loved it anyhoo.  teaching photography & spoken word in a public high school and loving it most days, except when i have to deal with unsupportive and/or helicopter parents. writing my art manifesto. update: just finished v 2 and printed it with lulu, pretty excited about this edition. collaborating with other artists making and performing art riding my bike cuddling with my dog loving shaun t's insanity engaging and collaborating with youth and artists of all ages speaking my mind living my life listening & asking questions being perceptive critical thinking & self-reflection photography i'm not sure. my lip piercing? food: homemade fried chicken, sushi, ethiopian, indian, dark chocolate, red wine, coffee, mushrooms, dinosaur kale, bacon, homemade soup, grass-fed free range hamburgers, belgium waffles, anything spicy, i love to eat.  music: mos def, the roots, jill scott, erykah badu, gil scott heron, nina simone, j5, etta james, al green, talib kweli, meshell ndegeocello, chali 2na, angie stone, blackalicious....  books: in the city of shy hunters, the shadow of the wind, written on the body, gut symmetries, the man who fell in love with the moon, the alchemist, zahir, 11 minutes, the 5th sacred thing, fall on your knees, art & lies, stone butch blues, a queer time & place, zami, push, autobiography of red, tipping the velvet, run, water for elephants, a thousand splendid suns, in the time of the butterflies, elegance of the hedgehog...  movies: fire, water, & earth (all by deepa mehta), anything by hayao miyazaki, esp. spirited away, antonia's line, high art, when night is falling, set it off, city of god, slumdog millionare, smoke signals, coraline, amelie, enemy of the state, matrix, whale rider, what the bleep do we know?, la vie en rose, frida... coffee, books, analog photography, underground hip hop/music, slam poetry, long bike rides, my dog- not in that order, and yes, i know it's 7 and that my dog will not be around forever. but what is "never" or "forever?" sweat and water need to be on the list and 9 is my favorite number so it must makes more sense. social justice, words, images, education, sex, politics, training for my rides, light, love. having dinner and/or a drink or at the say word! monthly poetry slam plays, comedy, dinner parties, dancing, live music reading, working out, or sleeping after a long week this entire profile, experience ...you love life, know yourself, are expressive, want to connect on many levels, are serious and don't mind making the first contact, are actively romantic rather than hallmark status, understand that binary gender is harmful and boring, are sexy and strong and want something deeper and longer.  if you want to ride for long distances. ;0)  warning: any institutions or individuals using this site or any of its associated sites for studies or projects-you do not have permission to use any of my profile or pictures in any form or forum both current and future. if you have or do, it will be considered a violation of my privacy and will be subject to legal ramifications. (it is recommended that other members post a similar notice.)

full_bio: lakers, football, and gym, the 3 greatest entertainments in the world. i recently graduated from grad school with my mba. i currently work in the technology services industry. as i continue to grow, i want to transition towards a more business oriented role.  on the side, i try to help (even if it's little) combat poverty and environmental degradation. the institutions i support are kiva and project keisei. talking smack to fans of opposing teams  books - end of poverty by jeffrey sachs, bad samaratin by ha joon chang, winning in emerging markets by tarun khanna  movies - avengers, dark knight, patriot, a few good men, body of lies, spider man 2, saving private ryan  shows - big bang theory, scrubs, lost  music - whatever is on the radio  food - anything and everything! chomp, chomp... 1. family/friends 2. gym 3. laptop/phone 4. food! 5. football 6. movies my future and how i will achieve my goals. in between my future goals, i often think about my next meal. either out with friends or hanging back watching a movie.

full_bio: thanks in advance for wading through the following mishmash of personal attributes/experiences. send me a note and i'll gladly return the favor . . .  i do a lot of different things in my free time, like going to plays, camping, and hosting sunday dinners. i have taken hip hop dance classes off and on for 7 years, i once shot a pheasant, and i have been paid to perform stand-up comedy in four states (i no longer perform, but i can still bring the funny).  travel-wise, i lived in japan and brazil for nearly a year each (a while ago, now). most of my trips these days are to visit family in such exotic locales as grand island, ne, but i did go to baja this fall. when i travel i prefer to couchsurf or stay in communal living situations and meet weird people. this is what happened in baja, and it was delightful.  i like whale watching, drinking coffee on my porch, hummingbird feeders, and falling asleep next to the ocean. i don't know anything about wine except that i like the red kind, and if some new, cool band becomes popular i am generally the last to know.  i used to be mostly vegetarian, but no more. given time next to a flat body of water, i will skip rocks until my arm is sore. plotting an escape from my day job at a bank while enjoying the many wonders of san francisco and elsewhere. so getting outdoors, dancing, snowboarding, making people laugh, etc. and then studying for the gre. making blackened catfish and omelets. but not for the same meal. also, giving toasts at weddings. i am blessed with the ability to reach your highest shelves and change your most unreachable lightbulbs. (but not dunk your basketballs, sadly.) written word - catch-22, the entire calvin and hobbes catalog, any book or article by john irving or michael lewis podcasts - radiolab, risk, wtf stage - any one man show at the marsh. music - like everyone, i listen to a variety of stuff, but i have come to realize my preference is cheesy, danceable, lowbrow fluff. big fan of the scissor sisters and paid full price for madonna tix 3 years ago (awesome concert, btw), but i've also seen the sf symphony 6 times in the last 2 years. i also very much enjoy 80's night at the cat club. make of that what you will. movies - where to start? a fish called wanda, pan's labrynth, murderball, 50/50, the south park movie, fog of war, dr. strangelove, the bourne trilogy, casablanca, almost famous, shawshank redemption, fight club, cronicas, the lost boys of sudan, defending your life. 1) high thread-count sheets. they do make a difference. 2) butter. 3) thick, warm, over-the-calf socks. 4) library card. 5) a comfy couch. 6) seriously, butter. how to own less stuff. lately, i find myself getting jazzed when i find something cluttering my apartment that i can sell, donate, or throw away. it's great. sitting around with a couple of friends sipping a tasty beverage and shootin' the breeze. on an ideal friday night i am dancing. i recently started weightlifting with regularity/purpose for the first time in my life and i am a little embarrassed at how much i enjoy it. i am still skinny, but i enjoy making crazy, grunty faces into the mirrors at the ymca weight room. does that make me hard core? i'm guessing not.  also, i went to running of the bulls once and totally chickened out the moment i saw bulls running at me.

full_bio: i like to try new things. i love going to new plays, movies, festivals, late night semi-illegal fireworks shows in golden gate park... anything really. i'm interested in meeting people who have fun hobbies or are willing to introduce me to new activities (and who are willing to indulge when i want to go to something random).  recently i've become interested in wine (hence the name), and i've been going to a bunch of tastings across the city. my favorites are sweeter wines (sauternes, rieslings), though i'm still trying to explore all the regions of the world. medical school. i give good advice. my smile. tv!: the west wing (best show ever), 30 rock, arrested development, skins (love british tv!), pushing daisies, modern family  movies: the shawshank redemption, donnie darko, american beauty, triplets of belleville, almost famous, once, ratatouille, exit through the gift shop, district b13  books: whatever i'm reading now...  food: basically anything and everything. family friends dried fruit my iphone/the internet sorbet my mini cooper. parking anywhere is a dream. i recently got into archery at golden gate park, so i think about that a bit. i'm also constantly searching for new wine tastings in the city. probably at a movie with some friends. ... you want to.

full_bio: i really like to grab life by the balls and make the most of it. i've lived in germany (that's where i'm from), england, france, new york city, rhode island, scotland, and san francisco. i like good absurdist theatre and try to make it when i can. different perspectives are important to me and i usually get along best with people who speak a couple of languages and are on a somewhat circuitous path through life. myself, i've worked in a business consulting firm (meh...), studied method acting, ran a theatre company, worked in plenty of bars and restaurants, got a degree in linguistics and taught philosophy. it's made me realise there's no one thing that i'm destined to do, and that i'm probably gonna be exploring different kinds of lives for a while to come. in fact, i may start my own scuba school... right now i'm getting a phd in philosophy. but i also dabble in journalism, work on my aerial acrobatic skills, and produce, direct, write and perform theatre. rhyming; figuring things out: broken toilet flushes, indecipherable handwriting, gadgets without instruction manuals; playing musical instruments mediocrely; running up stairs this may be a toss-up between my brains and my green eyes. books: kurt vonnegut, joseph heller, douglas adams, victor hugo, herman melville, fyodr dostoyevsky, leo tolstoy, boris vian, heinrich bll, margret atwood, pg wodehouse, thomas pynchon, pierre boullle, douglas coupland, michel houllebecq, jose-luis borges, friedrich drrenmatt, charles mee, tom stoppard, sam beckett, jean-paul sarte, friedrich nietzsche  movies: 'once', 'papillion', 'lock stock and two smoking barrels', 'the saddest music in the world'  music: tom waits, gogol bordello, georges brassens, die toten hosen, pixies, einstrzende neubauten  food: southern french (lots of seafood...), sicilian (swordfish!), thai (curries)... -rewarding relationships -the written word -adventure -change -some form of expression -my wits because of my work - the mind, the meaning of meaning, metaphysics, evolution... in general - where i'd like to travel to next, how to get out of paying parking tickets, whether it's too late to become an astronaut after all, random stuff like the name-letter effect or the introspective illusion, how there could be a canton in switzerland that didn't give suffrage to women until 1990, whether it makes any sense to learn esperanto... there is no typical friday night - sometimes i'm watching a play (sometimes i'm in a play), sometimes i'm out dancing, sometimes i'm having a dinner party, sometimes i'm in new york, sometimes i'm home watching a movie, ideally i'm out at a bar having a wicked conversation with a kick-ass person i usually look at the photos first when i check out a new profile...

full_bio: i'm a relatively new transplant to the left coast...just like everyone else i've met so far. is anyone actually from san francisco? i've lived here for about 2 years and absolutely love it. aside from my frequent cravings for a slice of pizza or a bagel (did i mention i'm originally from new york?) i think that this city is perfect for me and i am looking for someone to enjoy it with.  i will warn you that i am very sarcastic and have a dry sense of humor. also, i absolutely love to travel and am very spontaneous so if you want to hang with me an up to date passport is a must :) i work in marketing at a start-up in silicon valley. i played volleyball for 3 years in college so still find time to play pick up games now and then. i am also a fairly good cook. i am the world's pickiest eater so i literally had to learn how to cook to survive. i'm also really good with numbers. i can use logic to argue my way out of a paper bag. but please, don't make me prove it...i'll win. my height, for sure. but i guess if i'm sitting it would be my eyes. if you ask my dvr, i'm big into reality tv and embarrassing teen dramas. hmm, maybe that should have gone in the "the most private thing i'm willing to admit section."  i read when i travel and it's typically business related books like freakonomics or great by choice.  i listen to a variety of music but mostly enjoy rap, acapella, and acoustic music.  when i should get my mba. why people put both ketchup and mustard on their hamburgers. how many more airline miles i'll need to get status. don't judge me until you've been upgraded to first class...you'll never go back. having a glass of wine with my roommates and heading out for a night on the town.  you think you can keep up ;)

full_bio: as you might guess from my username, i make my living working with technology. and i've read _snow crash_ (though i'm not a huge scifi fan).  i'm bright, playful, forthright, affectionate, trustworthy, genuine, low maintenance, crunchy. i'm seeking a committed relationship, but i'm also happy to grow my social circle.  i enjoy the outdoors and love to go on day hikes. i'm strong and capable and care about fitness and eating healthy ... most of the time. i enjoy walking to my errands and biking to work.  the arts are always woven into my life - i enjoy participating as well as just appreciating. i've pursued classical music seriously; i took some painting classes a couple of years ago and especially loved the life drawing class. i've done social dancing: i've learned to swing out, to sugar push, to gypsy, to ocho. i like to read.  i believe the foundations of relationships are deep mutual respect and good communication. i want people in my life whom i can trust. laughter, listening and honesty are cornerstones. i don't expect you to meet my every need, but it would be great to be comfortable together through most of our many faces (silly, compassionate, focused, relaxed ...). i'd like to learn from you, to tap your acquired wisdom; i'd hope to supply similar offerings to you. enjoying the journey. seeking to become a better person. helping out, cracking wise, laughing, listening, focusing, showering affection ... i've been told i have a great smile; others have remarked on my heartfelt laugh. movies: * action: casino royale (the new one), the italian job (both), indiana jones ... * comedies: groundhog day, coen brothers, saving grace, best in show, clockwise, take the money and run, strictly ballroom, wallace & gromit ... * drama: eternal sunshine of the spotless mind, eat drink man woman, blood diamond, michael clayton, hotel rwanda, american beauty, good will hunting, chasing amy, water ... * documentaries: the corporation, super size me, king corn, buena vista social club, david attenborough about animals, a great day in harlem ...  books: * memoirs that read like fiction, esp. about people overcoming circumstances and/or doing extraordinary things. some favorites: three cups of tea, the long walk, coming out of the ice, rocket boys, an unquiet mind ... * fiction favorites: animal dreams, one true thing, ordinary people, all the king's men ...  music: classical, jazz, celtic, bluegrass, latin, folk, and even some plain old rock and roll.  food: i appreciate "foodie" choices; i love most "ethnic" food, and hate using "ethnic" to describe non-mainstream-american cuisine. i can cook tasty food, but am rarely motivated to do so. i am a fantastic sous-chef. really. hope laughter joy friends beauty cuddling how to contribute more to the world; how to solve the programming puzzle that currently is tripping me up. going to a movie. playing "euro" style board games (have you heard of settlers?) or just hanging out with friends. going to the symphony or occasionally the opera. relaxing at home. on the internet?? you're reading it. you're interested, intrigued, curious, or had any other reaction.

full_bio: i'm a california native who ventured south for a while, but i'm happy to be back and have been settling in for the past year. because i am a nurse, i have a little bit of a different schedule than most but i enjoy the flexibility it affords me. i spend a lot of time caring for sick babies in the nicu, spending quality with my son, and relaxing with friends. since i moved back i have been able to reconnect with my bay area and friends and love to explore new places to eat, hang out and dance. what else? i really enjoy meeting new people and checking out spots i haven't been to before. i like to be active and outside when i can. recently back in the bay area and loving it again. working full time, and being a mom. trying to have fun out with friends, and meet new people. oh yeah and get back in shape!! gym time and yoga when i can. multi-tasking!! also cooking and baking, so i'm told... my curly hair? or maybe smile... music i love, all kinds. the roots, john legend, erykah badu, talib kwele, mos def, mary j blige, willie hutch, adele, coldplay, muse, bright eyes, the killers, elliott smith.... food, anything yummy although i am not a big meat eater. i enjoy cooking/baking as well. books, anything by david sedaris, the lovely bones, unaccustomed earth by jhumpa lahiri, the zahir by paulo coelho. movies, comedies are usually cool with me, the departed, dreamgirls, 21 grams. i like indie stuff as well like the motorcycle diaries and amelie. good food, good coffee, good music, my friends, my son and my ipad! self-improvement and awareness to get where i want to be in my life.

full_bio: i'm the type of person that always tends to see the cup as half full. not one to linger on the negative things that get thrown on my lap; i was given life to enjoy it. love to laugh, show affection to those that i care about... i'm not one that would ever hurt a person in any way whatsoever. unless they asked. then maybe. okay, so some people need to learn some lessons... but it's all love!  someone who can laugh at even the most mundane things. preferably a guy that likes to go out and dance... don't really know what to write here since searching for a fantasy tends to leave a great mess in reality. soooooo am just looking for a nice guy that can give me hugs when i need 'em  i'm new to this ok cupid site... a friend suggested that i give it a try, so here i am! i'm mainly looking to expand my circle of friends... i've been single for a couple of years now and am open to dating if/when that time presents itself. but not rushing out with the hopes that every single person i come across will be "the one". i figure that all that will fall into place on its own...  i am definitely open to meeting people to hang out with. i'm also really trying to fill in the 500 words on this profile to make me "45% complete"! so...  i really like going out to check out live shows... all types of shows. whether they be in new york on broadway, or on a small local community theatre - it is all great to me. i'm also very kick back/relaxed... maybe too relaxed for some. i like to take things easy and do not mind being low key on the weekends spending time with friends and family.  i also really enjoy traveling... there are several places that i would really like to go to - they include australia, chicago, boston, toronto, italy, france... guess all the usual places.  have i reached the 500 words yet? no! aaahh!  okay, are you still with me? let's see what else i can mention on here... hhhmmm - oh, after many many years of wanting one, i finally got a mac! yay! does that count as something interesting to write about? well, although i was always the class nerd in elementary (loving computers back in the 80's was not a cool thing to do like it is now!) i haven't totally kept up with all the new technology as i thought i would. i mean, i do not even have a digital camera!!!! the only cam i have is on my phone, and it's 2 mega pixels.  okay - i think i have almost reached the 500 characters... i am sooo curious to see what the next steps are going to be on here! whoo hoo!  i am dorky, curious, and relaxed smiling being silly i'm tall (well... taller than most... 6 feet) i love going out to the movies, seeing everything from silent films down to the most recent release - love it all!  love tons of different styles of music... i am a latino but also born and raised here in the bay area so i've been exposed to everything from motown to cumbias... jazz, blues, rock... patsy cline, nat king cole, elvis (i'm a huge fan), all the way to lola beltran, selena (i think i'm the ultimate fan haha), lady gaga, frank sinatra, stevie wonder... there are just too many to list (and they're all so random)     you'd like to hang out -- doesn't have to be anything more than friendship... i love meeting new people

religion: atheism and somewhat serious about it
full_bio: hey everyone! after a few years of roaming around like a vagabond through japan, seattle, portland, and most of europe, i've been settling in to san francisco for the time past year. i'm slowly making more friends in town, but i'm up for making more. i'm married to a wonderful woman who has lived here for over 7 years and knows tons of people, but none of them want to bike from nob hill to tiburon or go camping with me. i'm usually very laid back and a homebody, but i do get very passionate about politics, art, music, and beer. only one of those is really worth getting worked up about, though. try to guess which one.  for better or worse, i also tend to practice a kind of radical honesty, which stands out, but sometimes in the wrong ways. want to know if your ass really does look fat in those jeans? please, for the love of all that is holy, don't ask me!  i have a number of hobbies, and listing them all would bore you absolutely to death, but chances are good if you're not into death sports or cutting, we'll probably be able to share something. i'm also willing to be introduced to just about anything!  i am the punkest nerd or the geekiest punk you'll ever meet.      diy working for the japanese government in sf. also, trying to get settled in sf and meet people, which is proving uncommonly difficult given how friendly everyone seems to be.  i've also been spending a lot of time lately testing the limits of my cooking abilities. i bought the huge williams-sonoma and essential ny times cookbooks and i'm making one large meal from them every sunday. some recent highlights include pad thai, orange chicken, tomato and bacon meatloaf, and spanakopita. speaking japanese cooking making no-bake cookies computers and technology buildin' and fixin' harboring the illusion that i am a polymath answering jeopardy questions spelling deadpanning sarcasm choosing fresh fruit puzzles researching new music living without a car this is not a good way to judge me, since i tend to make terrible first impressions (see "radical honesty"). tenth impression, though, and i'm a catch.  i have an incredibly strong sense of responsibility.  i look kind of scowl-y at first glance, but i'm really very open and friendly, though not very outgoing. books: vonnegut, heller, mailer, orwell, london, kerouac, greene, akutagawa, shakespeare, dostoyevsky, twain, plato, non-fiction (politics, history, and science) and textbooks. i am originally from portland, and every time i go home i end up with half a suitcase full of books from the largest independent seller of new and used books on the planet. powell's represent! i currently have a to-read stack on my desk that includes revolutionary road, a book about string theory, the collected essays of christopher hitchens, and the lonely planet guide to turkey and greece.  music (whew, here goes...): against me!, anamanaguchi, andrew bird, art blakey, art tatum, belle and sebastian, black keys, bonnie "prince" billy, bob dylan, camera obscura, cat power, cocoon, elliott smith, elvis costello, etta james, frank turner, heatmiser, hot snakes, jackson c franck, jsbx, justin townes earle, kay kay and his weathered underground, kings of convenience, led zeppelin, m ward, man or astroman, metric, matt pond pa, mountain goats, murder by death, niel young, old 97's, oscar peterson, parliament, quasi, queen, ray charles, robbers on high street, rocky votolato, sam cooke, sharon jones, simon & garfunkel, sondre lerche, spoon, stephen malkmus, stevie wonder, sufjan stevens, ted leo, the beatles, the clash, gaslight anthem, hold steady, the jam, the minutemen, mynabirds, rolling stones, shaky hands, tallest man on earth, weakerthans, the who, the thermals, tower of power, van morrison, violent femmes, wilco, and the zombies.  i also love these guys: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f8yyrzu_0w0  food: sushi, gyuudon, okonomiyaki, sukiyaki, nabe, ja-ja men, pad see ew, brats and (micro)brew, korean barbecue, pae jun, kimchi jigae, mcmenamin's (bonus points if you know what that is), roti, curry, cheese naan, chicken tikka masala, turkey, gin & tonic, smoked salmon pizza, quesadillas, vanilla yogurt with strawberries and bananas, hermiston watermelons, grilled cheese, any kind of breakfast food (update: i just found a place nearby that makes croque madames. my secret plan is to go there often and get very fat). sushi, reading material, the intertubes, my guitar (with replacements strings), a good tool kit, my mom and sister whether i should shave my head and go meditate in front of a rock garden in japan, why frogs and bees and chimps are disappearing, whether or not my grammar is correct, why there are 50% more men than women in the 20-34 cohort in sf county, what the hell happened to public support for high speed rail in california, and ways to make some new friends. out at a concert, playing guitar, having a belgian beer, refusing yet another shot of tequila being thrust at me by my coworkers, preparing to cook a big meal on saturday, or just sleeping off a week's worth of overtime. if i could have any superpower i'd have matter manipulation. with it i'd be able to rearrange the protons, neutrons, and electrons in anything and form it into something else. for example, i'd be able to take a pound of lead and change it into a pound of gold, or a pound of pickles, or a tiny one-pound monkey, or anything! i could use this power to turn all the toxic detritus from our materialistic and superficial consumer culture and turn it back into something useful (garbage in --> garbage out --> garbage in). i could change pollutants in rivers and oceans back into pure h20. also, according to relativity and quantum physics, matter is just huge collections of energy, so it should be possible to break down matter into clean, abundant energy. i'd use cockroaches as my energy source. i hate cockroaches.  i also plan to start running for president as soon as i become eligible in 2022. if you feel like your academic factor is lacking, or, i suppose, if you need help on your homework.  or if you think i'm neat.  or if you want to learn chess/shogi/cribbage with me.  or if you think you'd make an awesome video game/pizza/microbrew buddy. basically i'm looking for friends who will challenge me creatively and intellectually.  or if you want to help me use my restaurant groupons.  or if you want to take over the world. knowledge of exotic poisons or being able to kill a man with a house key is a huge plus.

full_bio: i'm a musician turned engineer turned manager guy turned entrepreneur. i'm pretty well traveled and try to do adventurous activities as much as possible. if i could work three months and take one off i would do that forever. i love to ski, bike, climb, play and listen to music, and be social.  i am smart, athletic, and a gentleman i was a computer engineer, then executive, and then entered the startup world where i do consulting, advising, and work on my own ideas. i'm happiest when i get a good dose of sports, friends, and alone/productive/creative time. analytical thought, laughing, and being open minded. i know, that also seems paradoxical to me now that i've written it. i'm smiley, warm, and usually either well or interestingly dressed. and after short conversation, if i'm not really drunk, you can tell there is a brain in head.  family, my health, friends, music, adventure, heros, food, and water. creative ideas, how to solve problems, and how to have fun! either at happy hour/dinner or the climbing gym. the later i've grown to prefer. i'm generally very clean and organized and sometimes eat food that is so healthy people make fun of me. i also have four nipples. just kidding.

full_bio: i am a newly separated, fun-loving bbw living in oakland, ca. i love to write, travel and share funny, embarrassing, frustrating, joyous moments with people i love. ultimately, my goals in life are to see as much of the world as i can, make meaningful connections with people who bring joy to my life and to some day have a wonderful family that tries my patience, compassion and capacity to love. right now as i am going through the process of a divorce, i'm just looking to date and see what happens from there. trying to keep an open mind and open heart. i'm in a job that affords me a life i love and gives me enough creative outlets to keep me engaged. it may not be what i want to do for the rest of my life, but it's good for now. i have dreams of being the neighborhood mom someday and or running my own event design business. taking care of people, cooking great dinners, creating beautiful spaces, and adjusting to life's ups and downs while trying to make the most of the time here. i'm an avid explorer and am really good at finding unexpected treasures in unknown places. hmmmm...i would guess my laugh or my smile. books: song of solomon, bastard out of carolina, a fine balance, grapes of wrath, wuthering heights, and just about anything with a strong southern woman protagonist. these days i read more non-fiction that fiction. i love books about people who have overcome incredible odds in life.  movies: any of the john hughes 80's movies and that era (16 candles, some kind of wonderful, pretty in pink, st. elmo's fire), et, the godfather (all of them. that's right...all of them. "leave the gun. grab the canolis.") pulp fiction, amelie, tie me up tie me down, high heels, framingham eight, steel magnolias, private parts, fried green tomatoes, bridesmaids, howard and kumar go to whitecastle, what about bob, election. a sense of humor, friendship, love, laughter, adventure...all the typical stuff. plus, great meals with friends. cheesy reality shows. toni morrison novels. trips to point reyes. my dog. and the values and sense of the absurd my dad instilled in me. diving into a swimming pool full of cupcakes and eating my way out. traveling to remote places. why boy george still hasn't asked me to marry him. what an evening with molly ivins, sarah vowel, anne richards, dolly parton, and dorothy allison might be like. all the things i should be doing but am not. rescuing more pitbulls. point reyes, ireland, morocco, scotland, france, australia...you name it. tired from the work week but looking forward to catching up with friends over dinner, working in the garden over the weekend, and planning a long walk or hike with my dog. if i weren't a woman, i would be a drag queen. if you're open to or have openly dated a big woman. it's not a fetish, but you enjoy the pleasure of a soft body. message me if you can make me laugh, your actions and words are the same, you are kind, and have established a life of your own. if you like to travel, try new restaurants, spend the day driving to a new place, sit on the couch and cuddle on a rainy night, and can kiss me until my toes curl, all the better.

full_bio: he who lives the most interesting life wins.  this is the philosophy i have adopted for my life. it's all about living an interesting life. for me what is interesting is knowledge gained by new people, places, ideas and experiences. what makes these things really interesting though is how you apply this knowledge. in order for your life to be truly interesting you have to be doing something with it that inspires you.  i am inspired by community building, both on the micro and macro scales and everywhere in between. for a micro example, while i am in san francisco i spend my time at a warehouse with 22 excellent people living in it. this community really cares about each other on a very personal level. on the macro scale, i spend much of my time working on stability in afghanistan which i believe has an impact on the global community. if peace on earth simply means the end of war, that's what i intend to happen in my life time. some people call it naive, but it's an interesting challenge. for the in between i work directly for burningman helping make the event happen. the burningman community is unique and powerful in many ways and it is my belief that it's affecting a very positive change on our world.  so i am here looking for interesting people to have interesting interactions with or perhaps to even live an interesting life with. i'm a computer geek who travels. i spend most of my time in san francisco. about a quarter of my time is spent in afghanistan and the rest is split between the various places my family is and gerlach/black rock city. i find travel to be very interesting... sarcasm and finding out what she said. i'm a fairly sharp cookie so that engineering stuff also comes pretty easy to me. traveling is kind of my way of life so i suppose i'm good at that as well. that all goes hand in hand with communication. after all, you can't have community with out it. my height is probably it. i am nearly two meters tall for those of you who speak metric. i do have a very deep voice so people on the phone comment on that a lot. appearance and the like aside, i am often very outgoing and am not afraid to speak my mind. in fact i'm not afraid of much, which is probably why i am an adventure junkie. authors: neil stephenson, joel rosenberg, c.s. friedman, edward abby, robin hobb, gregory david roberts, heinlein and many, many more. my favorite all time book is shantaram.  movies: princess mononoke (anything miyazaki) and garden state to name two.  music: electronic, except dub-step. i've been djing for almost 10 years now and i can get into just about any electronic music, but dub-step makes me want to be elsewhere. i also like live jazz when i can find it  foods: i'm a sushi nut, love the stuff. i even make it on occasion. i can say from experience, the best food in the world is a tie between japanese and indian. when i'm cooking it's often something with my wok these days  comics: xkcd is amazing! i also am a fan of sinfest, diesel sweeties and cyanide and happiness. community books music water (not just for drinking) adventure sexy fun time  most if these improve when combined ;) people, what makes them tick, why do they do what they do and what makes them happy. i love people watching in bars, human mating rituals are hilarious and you get a great vantage point from the dj booth.  a mental journey i have been on for a while is thinking about what makes a community. i heard someone say something a long the lines of "fifty thousand people in five square miles does not a community make" and he was right. it's shared hardship that bring people together. it kind of sheds a new light for me on people who are going through hard times and their life. the people in laos are the fifth poorest in the world and from everything i could see, the happiest. life does not have to be easy to be good, in fact in some ways the opposite is true. so is the path of finding ways to make life as easy as possible the right one? not for me! scoffing at the idea of a typical friday night. i do not believe in such things, at least not in my life. there is adventure to be had i am going to have it! i am not bothered by nudity. i really like to talk to people who are in action working on projects that inspire them. to me doing something that inspires you is just about the most important thing in life. so if you are up to something with your life, i want to hear about it.  if you are not doing something that inspires you but want to be, that's an interesting conversation too.

full_bio: here's the deal...we all hate these parts of the profile where we describe ourselves. we sometimes read them on others profiles but usually only when our interest is already aroused. i have yet to find the perfectly crafted description on any profile, but i do know that using this area as a platform to rant about things you don't like about your ex-boyfriends is tacky and cliche and we should all agree to knock it off. so i'll make you a deal...lets try chatting...test it out...if after a lengthy conversation of substance you're not completely satisfied then at least we agree we gave it a shot  athletic workaholic often finding myself in trouble due to a desperate need for a sarcasm font. i have a wicked quick wit. harry potter, shut up i like it!  things that irritate me, then i write them down. i'm writing a book about common sense. for example, when getting on an elevator, wait for everyone to get off before you try and get on. and if you're the guy whose going to stand in front of the buttons, offer to push them for everyone else, otherwise i have to reach in front of your crotch and i'm not even going to look at you...and that's awkward.  i have a perfectly rational fear of clowns, and i'd rather be robin than batman (read into that what you will). if forced to make a choice i would always choose goofy/funny over hot...but if there is a god i won't be making that choice. i'm looking for a relationship full of fun, adventure, passion and blindingly hot sex. feels a little like i'm reaching for the stars here but i guess thats why i signed up for this site. i like all the typical things, beach, movies, gym, hanging with my friends, and some not so typical things, sitting home and reading, and doing lots of volunteer work. but wish me luck on this search of mine and feel free to hit me up.

start up again. didn't do very well and found i used my "hand" wedge quite a bit, but i'm still interested.  i love trying new adventures and can be spontaneous, as long as i can plan for it. i've been skydiving, parasailing and hot air ballooning and i'm actually afraid of heights. i know... it doesn't make sense...  my politics are on the liberal side and some of my favorite "political" shows are keith olbermann, rachel maddow, daily show and the colbert report.  i am spiritual and i believe there is a higher being, but i find i don't trust most organized religions. don't even get me started...  i don't smoke and i rarely drink alcohol.  i am very fortunate to have a wonderful family (we put the "fun" in dysfunctional) and wonderful friends and they are all very important to me. i hope to find someone who feels the same way about their family and friends.  i enjoy quiet evenings at home, but i also enjoy getting dressed up and going out on occasion.  and finally, i have 2 cats -- yes, i'm definitely a cat person so it would help if you're not allergic or are at least willing to take allergy medication. no worries, i'm not like "debbie" on youtube who did the, uh, "eharmony video bio." although, i couldn't resist and did my own variation/parody and it's also on youtube if you're interested. just be sure you you watch her video before mine, otherwise you might think i'm a total nut job. but i love all animals and would hope that the person i meet feel the same. as for careers, i've done everything from being a telex operator, accountant, hot tubs salesperson, event planner, dance instructor, and i was even in a movie directed by toby hooper when i was young and firm of flesh. by the way, it was a terrible movie, worse than b and they had to change the name of the movie 7 times to get anyone to watch it. oh, and i played a prostitute, although, you have no need to worry, it wasn't typecasting. oh wait, i might have better luck on this site if it was typecasting  i have since grown up (loosely defined) and i love my current job and find it very rewarding. we're making a difference by improving the lives of struggling learners. it's important for me to actually enjoy and believe in what i'm doing to earn a living, especially since it takes up so much of my time. laughing and making others laugh and finding humor in just about every situation.  listening.  my job.  singing. love to sing jazz standards & old torch songs and my friends tell me i'm good -- but then, what else are they going to say, they're my friends.  dancing. i used to be a ballroom dance instructor in my younger years, competing in the latin division, and just recently started teaching again. a friend of mine and i started a meetup group for ballroom dance lessons and i'm having a great time teaching. yup, i still got it! it would be wonderful to share my passion of dancing with someone and be able to dance on a regular basis. i love all dances, especially salsa. i've been told my smile, although, that's not usually where they're looking... i love reading mysteries, biographies, scifis, satires, and self-help.  for movies, i enjoy; action movies, mysteries, psychological thrillers, deep, thought-provoking movies of cartoon characters, animated films, documentaries, foreign films (with subtitles, not dubbing) and some comedies, but nothing too sappy and no romantic comedies.  for music, i love old jazz standards, latin, pop, r&b, golden oldies, blues, jazz, some rap, some country, and karaoke (that's a type of music, right?)  food -- peet's coffee for favorite beverage. as for food, probably my favorite is sushi, but then any type of food is really okay with me, except for liver, lima beans and raisins. 1. my family and friends 2. my cats 3. singing 4. dancing 5. decaf, non-fat, sugar-free vanilla, iced latte (especially if it's from peets) 6. my electric matress pad why am i here, how can i make the world a better place, are we alone in the universe, why can't democrats and republicans play nice together, how can i be on the liberal side politically when other members of my family are on the conservative side, will i ever be able to retire, how can i lose 10 pounds, did i get everything done at work today, does online dating really work, why do some men lie about their age on dating sites, why do i sometimes have trouble falling asleep at night... hmmm... i might need to stop thinking so much... about once a month i'm getting a massage and then relax the rest of the evening at home. other fridays i might be dancing or dining with friends, or just vegging in front of the tv or reading a good book (or even a bad book at times, but not intentionally). so i guess i don't really have a typical friday. well, if i admit it, then it wouldn't be private... you want to.

full_bio: i am sweet, silly, down to earth and have lots of energy and a hint of mischief. an sf native, i get there a couple times a week still...and i just realize i dislike talking about myself. so anyway, how are you? oh yeah this is all about me...  i like to check out live music and comedy and want to check new cirque totem show soon. i am up for anything fun. just did the come out and play festival which was a city wide game and super fun journeying to the end of the night. i am busy balancing city and country, alone and together, inward and outward. traveled all over the world for work and pleasure. well...work is also pleasurable.  i am single (as of a couple years ago) after being with one person for 15 years and yes i am too good to be true. er i mean no i am not. what? dating in the digital age, i sometimes wish it was as easy as it used to be...just meet a great guy by tripping him by "mistake"on my way to buy bananas. oh yeah and there are no coincidences by the way in case you were wondering. i am the full package and looking to meet people who want to have fun. not expecting to meet mr. right immediately but if that happens i won't shut the door and a window will open...or something like that... working doing what i love in film making and am busy being humble and extraordinary...challenging myself and others to be my/their best. living by the motto "it's hard to keep it simple". aiming for simple...like expanding my horizons about other cultures, health, environment, fitness, music and art. planning my next trip...suggestions, ideas, concerns, fanatical rants? everything (ha!)...you will have to find out...no really...i am athletic, love to read, and enjoy good company. i am a good friend. i work out daily and average biking a half marathon a day. all i hear is i look even better in person...so i guess i need some new pix? i have lots of energy. i am fun, thoughtful, sweet and down to earth...i don't like to toot my own horn ever...but i guess you are supposed to here...i feel uncomfortable all of a sudden. i dislike the limits if a list... latest: reading steve jobs bio and love his rebelious pranks mixed with insight and vision. i like that is adoptive parents never scolded him for getting in trouble at school instead they told the teachers they needed to keep him challenged. that lead to him skipping two grades...  recently read "just kids"and "bossy pants" "if the buddha married"...liked woody allen's "midnight in paris"...reading books on film editing and screenwriting. some i dig: haruki murakami, salinger, sam shepard..i like to read mulitple books at a time since i am a moody reader...i need a new fiction read. ideas?  i hit stern grove, outside lands, treasure island, and hardly strictly music festivals last summer broken social scene, ra ra riot, hey rosetta!, galactic, ladytron, david bowie, phantogram, gomez, naked and the famous, grouplove, mgmt, alo, florence and the machine, adele, modest mouse, trombone shorty, pepper, john legend, the xx, explosions in the sky, beach house, ladytron, lykke li, lcd soundsystem air, phoenix, santigold, feist, paramore, the shins, janes addition, massive attack, tricky, thievery corporation, matisyahu, prince, marvin gaye, sly & the family stone, maceo parker, james brown, miles davis, the pretenders, the police, fleetwood mac, curtis mayfield, stevie wonder, the roots, dr. dre, b.i.g., funkadelic...so much music so little time...  films etc..too many but here are some i dig...experimental narratives, surrealism and comedy are big winners. i appreciate anything that challenges the norm even if it fails at other aspects i will still give it kudos. i have been on a tv hiatus for the last year which was recently broken by breaking bad and louis ck. so worth it. i appreciate films that entertain and push the boundaries...even if certain films fall short of being excellent films i appreciate innovation...sideways, american splendor, about schmidt, momento, the royal tenenbaums, being john malkovitz, raising arizona, galaxy quest, maya deren, jean cocteau, all pixar films, election, she's gotta have it, do the right thing, curb your enthusiasm, 30 rock, tenacious d. npr,pri, and kcrw podcasts (among others) art and photography that push the limits. 1. friends and family 2. love and passion hand in hand 3. fun things to explore 4. music! 5. walking with the sun on my back 6. travel fun things to get into daydreaming sex friends and pen pals chocolate...raspberries, red zin  finding balance. inward and outward time and expression writing music what's typical about any night? i am usually relaxing at home reading and listening to music or going out with friends...or looking up the meaning of a typical friday night um....no ask me later you want to experience fun and maybe something deeper if the stars align. ready?

full_bio: well i'm a young 63 yr old --looking for someone to spend my golden years with--i'm a stage technician for iatse local#16-in sf but have been a professional bass player for 45 yrs--since i've lived in marin co.  i've played with the bands--fat chance(primarily at uncle charlies in corte madera)--maria muldaurs band for 5 yrs--roy roger and the delta rhythem kings for 3 years--commander cody and his lost planet airmen for 9 yrs--until cody moved east in '97-been a union stage tech eve since rising to the rank of journeyman in 2003--i like hdtv--movies --music--beaches--scotch--i smoke--and i love my cat chester-- working with my union at the same time putting together a band for my daughters wedding--with all my pro-musician friends playing bass--cracking jokes my wry eyes---while i look to find humor in everything! left wing political books, love good movies --dramas,documentaries, blues, r&b, good rock, no rap of hip hop--italian food, steaks-- mostly a meat and potatoes kinda guy my daughter's love, baseball, football, good movies, hd, my cat whether or not i'm going to spend all of my boundless energy in my older years only with my cat and grandchildren--which i don;t have yet--my daughter marries in 3 weeks... which one's friday?? just quitting smoking--it's hard you're looking for a hard working funny guy who's looking for a woman with whom i can laugh, love, and oh yeah again laugh--oh and i'm a great kisser!

full_bio: i'm shy but courageous, smart with a dash of sass, and sometimes i like to dance in my underpants.  (note: i'm a queer boi. *shakes fist at okcupid* boo to ye olde male/female only options! can i get a queer all up in here? and yes, that last sentence was meant to be read in ru paul's voice. actually, if ru could narrate my entire profile that would be great. i should revamp this thang anyhow. updates to come!) i'm a scientist, turned public health policy analyst, turned domestic violence legal advocate. you know, just your regular garden variety do-gooder.  i'm also starting a non-profit direct legal & medical services practice for all the l.g.b.trans*multicultural people in san francisco. it's a behemoth project, but it's the most invigorating and most joyful work i've ever done in my life. i hope we succeed. cheering people up, throwing slumber parties, and rockin the windmill whilst nailing every note in rock band. my adorable boi-ish charms?  that and that i remind them of someone...no i am not one of the new cast members of k-town. though, don't get me wrong, i did send them my audition reel. it was the same one i used when i auditioned for glee. j/k ;) ::books::  nina here nor there, running with scissors, the partly cloudy patriot, basically everything by sarah vowell, stone butch blues, me talk pretty one day, cunt, middlesex, open me carefully: intimate letters from emily dickenson to susan hunnigton, lesbian pulp novels by ann bannon, the case of the good for nothing girlfriend, and tons more!  ::movies:: i love you phillip morris, 500 days of summer, the fighter, blue valentine, black swan, the baxter, grind house, secretary, shortbus, but i'm a cheerleader, primer, strangers with candy, weeds, the minor accomplishments of jackie woodman, katy griffin: she'll cut a bitch, maragret cho  ::music:: tennis, cults, jens lekman, andrew bird, grizzly bear, passion pit, tegan and sara, hot chip, ra ra riot, sleater-kinney, mirah, lisa loeb, belle and sebastian (how great was the life pursuit and if you're feeling sinister )cat power, neko case, the microphones, camera obscura, voxtrot, phoenix, the blow, the pipettes, the decemberists, elliott smith, the gossip, pony up!,rufus wainwright, etc, etc cell phone, car keys, chuck taylors, and nun chucks moped adventures and of course winning it all on super market sweep someday. napping; so that i may gain the strength to dance again! i will sing "don't rain on my parade" anytime & anywhere in full volume. watch out queer karaoke night!!! :d  i can't help it, i've got this happy homo side of me that loves to sing and diva out at times, and girrrrl i do loves me some mariah carey circa 1990-2001! mmh hmm *snap*  i should also mention that if anyone plays old school madonna anywhere near me i will magically emerge with a hairbrush and sing along to every verse whilst vogue-ing. (naturally)  ********************************************  i'm also transmasculine identified and enjoy re/de-constructing gender in a whole new way. since i started testosterone, this has been the best year of my life--sans silly singing voice and all. i'm looking for queer identified folks who are interested in other queer guys. no rules, no boxes, no need to conform to any gender performances or expectations. you just be you, and let's see if we vibe well together.  * trans, queer, ftm knowledgeable = huge plus*

full_bio: still giving life some positive thoughts, still prometheus bound by all the wear and tear of gravity, still afloat in this tumbling tide. i like to think it's all for something good. thinking that life is what happens while one is making plans, spinning slowly in a big circle, looking for my double. reading menus and finding spelling mistakes, remembering songs i like the second they start, tasting apricots and peaches off the branch, getting lost and asking for directions while running on empty, holding onto books i plan to reread and not letting them go nor rereading them, avoiding tv like chewing bad gum and tripping, recovering from deadly fashion mistakes, repairing bike flats, looking at maps as possibilities. i will have to research this and get back. sampling : ovid's metamorphoses, portrait of an artist as a young dog, portrait of an artist as a young man, speak memory, the saragossa manuscript, the conformist, les miserables, amor, the new yorker, vanity fair, la cucina italiana, books on vintage posters, the wine enthusiast, blonde on blonde, music from big pink, ry cooder, exile on main street, martha and the vandellas, marvin gaye, sam cooke, nina simone, django and stephane, mumford and sons, foster the people, amy winehouse, pachobel, french bistro, roman, emilia romagna, thai, spanish, hungarian, louisiana, picnic, grill six senses five friends four corners of the globe three wishes two kids one love how it is here, how much nicer it can be, how work is going, what is coming into season so i can taste and share, fitting everything into what seemed like an hour and turned into a decade. torturing myself with relaxation techniques such as good wines and good songs, good movies and good bed linens. usually do not go out on fridays since it is bridge-tunnel crowds around here - north beach. but i'm up for a little dinner or music or a hike around the hilly streets. here's the great one for the imagination...but it wouldn't be private if it shows up here. you're into a man who walks and talks at the same time, peripetetic but no longer overly philosophical, a man who appreciates what it takes to be a lady in this world ("these worlds" could be more accurate), and a man who's into doing things that are fun. whatever that means could take a little explanation and a few messages.

full_bio: you do not have to be good. you do not have to walk on your knees for a hundred miles through the desert, repenting. you only have to let the soft animal of your body love what it loves  my work is loving the worldlet me keep my mind on what matters, which is my work, which is mostly standing still and learning to be astonished. (both by mary oliver)  we are good, beautiful, strong and holy. i have stood in circles of women across the country and recited this mantra. i hope that i am all of these things, as well as intelligent, i am a student!, pretty darn funny and deeply spiritual. spirit has always been a part of my path. i used to worship god. i now feel embraced with a fierce, tender passion by the divine feminine, my mother, and bow down in awe in the presence of nature.  what else does the soft animal of my body love? walking with those who have known much hardship has always been a part of my journey. i developed an empowerment program for women based on a book that i wrote which i took into shelters, safe houses and prison. i founded a house of hospitality for homeless women and children, worked as a community organizer and anti-nuclear activist and lived with central american refugees as part of the sanctuary movement. i have been a student, a teacher and a student, again. i have had brown hair, gray hair and, now, bottled brown hair. the soft animal of my body has walked, ran, limped and soared in her desire to become more whole, to live a full life. and, all the while, i have danced and played, filled with joy and ecstasy when i participate in interplay, a practice that allows me to move my body, tell my story and connect with amazing people. i am currently studying somatic psychology because i appreciate the importance of connecting with one's body in order to healand taking out student loans in order to do so :-). therefore, i am reading, writing papers, ok, you get the idea. i am doing restorative yoga, meditating, swimming, dabbling in improv, taking walks around my neighborhood and nearby parks, laughing a lot. i am creating my life one step at a time. being a loyal friend, being grateful, making people laugh, writing, facilitating groups, trying new things--like this :-) my smile, my eyes, my sense of humor. i love any book by isabel allende, jean shinoda bolen, sue monk kidd, elizabeth gilbert, clarissa pinkola estes or amy tan. lots of what i'm reading in school interests me, as well, especially reading about somatic approaches to healing trauma and ways that i can become a more culturally competent therapist. i also enjoy reading novels--most recently racing in the rain. i listen to jennifer berezan "praises for the world," celtic music, the indigo girls, anything from the 60's, 70's and 80's and broadway tunes. i enjoy watching a wide variety of movies except for science fiction or horror flicks. i'm somewhat of a gleek since i watch glee on tv, as well as modern family and once upon a time. i eat oatmeal in the am, lots of soup, salad and sandwiches, burritos and breakfast food. love those omelets and pancakes! decaf irish breakfast tea in the morning, laughter, exercise, connection, something sweet to eat, beauty what's on my to do list. yes, i am a compulsive list maker. i admit it! and then there are those too rare, sweet moments, especially when i'm on retreat or moving my body, when i just stop thinking and planning. i simply am. spending time with friends--sharing a meal, going to a movie, playing cards or sometimes going to a concert or moving at a dance jam. if i'm on my own, you can find me at a yoga studio doing restorative yoga, or getting an occasional massage :-). going through my divorce was the hardest thing i have ever done but it made me the strong, resilient woman that i am today. you are spiritual, have a fabulous sense of humor, are compassionate and passionate--about life, your work, your family and friends, learning and growing, accompanying those who are marginalized/making this world a little better place...and me? you are creative, playful and take care of your body, heart, soul and mind. you are open to exploring the possiblity of friendship and, perhaps, hopefully, more...i'm also open to surprises. we might be quite different from each other and get along great.  i look forward to meeting you.  ps if you suggest getting together, i'll respond to your mesage. i would appreciate the same courtesy if i contact you.

full_bio: okay, i'm long overdue to give this an honest shot. i'd done the normal routine and have found decent long term relationships, but have yet to find the right one.  i'm not looking for games or ridiculous drama. i'm a pretty straight forward guy that values honesty and good communication. when i find the right person, i'm very devoted and sincere -- and i hope to find the same in a partner.  as for who i am, i enjoy anything from various physical activities to zoning out in front of a good movie, to exploring some previously unexplored land -- or just an unexplored road. i like cooking or baking when i have someone to share that with, and really i'm up for trying most new things.  i value intelligence and insightful conversation. i love when i meet someone who is passionate about a subject i know little about. i'm happy debating almost anything even if it means playing devil's advocate. critical thought and logic are a big part of how i've grown up and defined myself and my beliefs.  and then, of course, i also need to be able to let my guard down and have fun making bad jokes or indulging in some offhand sarcasm. to be able to let the little kid out who is fascinated poking around in tide pools and also amused by the jelly bean dispenser that "poops" the candy ... that's what keeps life fun and beautiful.  last think i have to say is, along with not caring for games, i have no need for conventions and am very open minded. feel free to contact me if i simply haven't managed to notice you yet, if you're so inclined. it's flattering and a big plus in my book when someone can admit what they're intrigued by.  hope to hear from you, -alex i'm working on tech specialist within the legal sector. it's strange work that not many people know or even care exists. however, it's something i seem to have a knack for and my coworkers appreciate what i can do, so it keeps things rewarding. eventually i'd like to keep moving more toward software development, but all that in due time. i love trying to understand new problems and devise solutions. this is entirely why i ended up in my career. i also tend to remember weird random facts that i regurgitate sporadically. people think i must be really good at trivia games. i'm not. i couldn't say for sure, but i do get asked about the white streak of hair a lot. and to preempt that: it's a birthmark. i've had it all my life and i've never dyed or cut it off or anything. after that, people usually find me to be intelligent and insightful once they really get me talking. i have trouble typing that out though, because i'm also modest, arguably to a fault. (this advertising of one's self is rough!)

full_bio: i think i'm bit like a guy in a lot of respects as far as attitude and activities go (sci-fi, computers, longboarding, gaming, climbing). i like to laugh, and can see the humor in just about everything - people shouldn't take themselves too seriously. some people think it's cute that i'm kind of awkward. doing my undergraduate at stanford - architecture major and computer science minor. would like to do something cool with it - we'll see how it goes. i kind of fall into that 'jack of all trades, master of none' description. i play a bunch of nerdy musical instruments - particularly oboe, recorder, shawm (it's like a renaissance, unrefined, horrible-sounding oboe). my hair. it's usually some ridiculous color (blue, purple, pink). currently it's supposed to be white, but it ended up kind of blonde, and i guess blonde asians are a bit uncommon around here.  update: it's now purple.  update 2: green  update 3: pinkish purple books ender's game, dune, atlas shrugged, catch-22, hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy, snow crash, anything by isaac asimov, anything sci-fi  movies the matrix, scott pilgrim vs. the world, the sting, terminator, star wars, back to the future, minority report, pulp fiction, firefly series, battlestar galactica, sarah connor chronicles  music motion city soundtrack, a day to remember, - that flavor of alt/punk, mumford and sons, klaxons, foster the people, strokes also classical stuff, esp. if it's epic or pretty  food vegetarian -longboarding (wheels, not water) -musical instruments -dreams -laughing -stretching (the kind where you lean back in your chair and your senses completely shut off for a second) -my family all the cool things the future's going to bring  "'cause it's gonna be the future soon and i won't always be this way when the things that make me weak and strange get engineered away" -jonathan coulton longboarding down a parking garage, exploring campus, living in the architecture studio uh, i'm not you want to hang out or do something fun. i'm not really interested in a super serious relationship, or super sketchy guys but everyone else - it'd be fun to meet you!

religion: atheism and very serious about it
full_bio: the name's jeff. i work a lot. i get easily addicted to games and people (and apparently okc). i fall too fast. i have a music degree that i no longer use. i'm a really nice guy and usually put everyone else's needs before mine. that's why i've been working in customer support for 15 years. i travel a lot for work. i was on the road for over four months last year (mostly manila). oh, and i'm originally from wv (go 'eers) and lived in seattle for a few years before moving to the bay area "at the end of the millenium." never enough, right? life is just starting and i'm always looking forward to the next chapter while savoring the one i'm on. i lead customer care for a small company in silicon valley and i love my job. trying to keep millions of people happy every day is the best challenge of my career. and it's really weird to see ads for my employer on this page as i type. making people laugh. typing. security checkpoints. it's probably my smile. i use it a lot and i have the lines to prove it. i'm a really nice guy, too. making people happy is my job and i'm pretty good at it. i'm not much of a reader...maybe 2 books a year and they're all over the map (often work-related). i know people who read a lot are much cooler than i am but it's just never been my thing.  i like lots of movies. some examples are: 500 days of summer, amelie, shine, black swan, elizabeth, the pianist, goonies, hotel rwanda, milk, the bourne movies, porn, and just about anything that doesn't have adam sandler or jennifer aniston in it. and am i missing something about the profundity of eternal sunshine of the spotless thingamajig? every gay dude around here seems to list it. it was good and everything, but maybe i just missed something terribly profound. the french know how to make awesome gay movies.  i love premium channel series, specifically nurse jackie, true blood and united states of tara right now. 30 rock is awesome, as is parks and recreation. the office used to be. i dvr modern family and glee as well.  i stereotypically love musicals. i musically directed a few in summer stock when i was younger and i've been hooked since. i've seen rent nearly a dozen times. i like big and small productions, but i don't like mediocrity. sondheim is never appreciated enough. into the woods was one of the first obc cds i ever memorized. i think passion is underappreciated.  i have a bachelor's in piano, and i love playing bach, brahms, mozart, schubert, rachmaninov, and, when nobody else is around, i play and sing showtunes of all types. i also love playing for singers (classical and musicals). i'm a little rusty, and i require realized music, but i love it. when i play solo stuff, i like loud and fast.  i'm a sucker for pop/top 40, too...the usual suspects. i can't resist a catchy tune. i think some dance music djs are pure genius and seeing a dj mix live transfixes me. i listen to radiodanz a lot (streaming radio). bluegrass makes me proud of wv. i don't usually have the patience for country, though.  i'm not a vegetarian, but i would be if i had to watch something die to be on my plate (cowardly, i know). i don't do meat with skin or bones but i love a good burger and fries once in a while. i've had some pretty euphoric experiences stuffing my face, but eating sometimes feels like a waste of time. i love pizza, but only pepperoni for me. i'm an oatmeal raisin cookie connoisseur. my favorite of the moment is from caf epi in palo alto. family & friends, raisin bran, coke zero, iphone, internet access, & passport. honesty my weight (i was a fat kid and will always be a fat kid inside) trying not to think about my weight fiber everything. i wish i could stop thinking long enough to sleep more than 5 hours. with friends or wishing i were with friends. um...i've never had fish. my motto: if it's from the sea, let it be. and i don't like any flesh that looks like it used to be an animal. no bones, skin or organs. i'm a freak. i know.  i live with my ex. some people think it's weird, but it works. we broke up about 2 years ago. ...you've been fooled by all these lies, lies, lies!  jk...go on...message me and make my day. i'm horrific at making the first move.

full_bio: i recently moved to california. i am interested in making new friends who, hopefully, share some of my interests. i am a classical pianist who loves music and all things artistic and beautiful. i am interested in living the most fulfilling life i possibly can, in terms of financial success, artistic success, health and happiness. i'm highly intelligent, very rational, and well spoken, but in the end i'd prefer to share laughs over intellectual banter. i don't like to take myself or anything else too seriously. i play and teach piano. i'm a classical pianist. i'm good at other things, but i can't discuss that here. ;) people all agree that i am very easy to talk to. and i can get people i hardly know to open up and disclose things that they rarely tell anyone, all in a matter of minutes. i don't know how i do it. books: kurt vonnegut novels such as sirens of titan and god bless you mr. rosewater, ken kesey's one flew over the cuckoo's nest, dostoevsky novels... especially the brothers karamazov, frank herbert's dune, huxley's a brave new world, the collected stories of jorge luis borges, various nonfiction, etc.  movies: quentin tarantino films such as pulp fiction or kill bill, stanley kubrick films such as a clockwork orange or 2001, guy ritchie films such as lock, stock and two smoking barrels and snatch, terry gilliam's brazil and 12 monkeys, cohen brothers films... especially the big lebowski, i heart huckabees, amelie, cidade de deus, etc.  shows: i still haven't gotten into tv...  music: my favorite composers are alexander scriabin, beethoven, bach, dmitri shostakovich, alfred schnittke, franz schubert, maurice ravel, nikolai medtner, rachmaninoff, luciano berio, gyorgy ligeti, and frederick rzewski. i have a whole slew of favorite performers.  food: good italian food is hard to pass up. vietnamese cuisine is good for the soul. i love the richness of indian cuisine. i can't ever seem to eat enough sushi. as far as chinese goes, i'll do dim sum. i recently discovered that i love burmese food. oh, and if it's the right place, mexican food. a piano. friends. love. a phone. the internet. music. i think a lot about how to improve myself and my general well being. and according to the latest research, i think about sex roughly every 52 seconds. staying up way too late drinking dry martinis and listening to great music. well, i certainly don't have much to hide. message me if you're interested in meeting and having an outing. it could be as simple as getting coffee. or we could, say, go to a concert.
