offspring: doesn't have kids, but wants them
full_bio: if you just want the cut and dry version, i recently described myself like this: i wear boat shoes and flip flops, expensive jeans, i don't eat meat (but plenty of fish), i like organic food and exercising, random road trips, the sun and the snow, animals, and i firmly believe in equal human rights.  if any of that appeals to you, read on...  i grew up in the bay area, lived in la for school, but was ready to come back. i love going on adventures, including impromptu ski trips or weekend getaways. i want to go rock climbing more.  in terms of personality, i'm pretty goofy and appreciate spontaneity. if something fun comes up at the last minute and i'm all ready for bed, chances are i'll hop up and change and we'll go. i can be lazy though and just enjoy being around the house. intelligence is very important to me, as well as being with someone whom enjoys playing and watching sports, values relationships, and loves dogs. i have an old beagle and a silly lab mix puppy. both of them make coming home fun.  i love to spoil the person i'm with so i hope you're okay with that. i'm at a point in life where i'm not looking to casually date, although not willing to force a situation that isn't there. if that scares you, i'd rather you know now.  be who you are and communicate that. it's that simple.  ps - i think there should be an option that says "likes dogs and is allergic to cats". i'm seriously allergic to cats so it is probably a deal breaker if you plan to have cats forever, or now. starting grad school in the fall. i want to work with kids in hospitals, but it's not that simple so i'll spare you the explanation for now. before applying to grad schools i was teaching 1st grade, have coached basketball to little kids and worked in an icu as an emt. i also used to volunteer with dogs at the animal shelter but got a little too busy with dog stuff when i rescued my puppy peyton. i'd love to do it again someday though. making people laugh, being goofy and having fun, getting injured. being a good friend. being loyal, probably to a fault. sports. finding parking spots. people always tell me i have an interesting accent. i'm from ca so i have no idea what they are talking about. lately someone guessed canada but most often it's the midwest. you can decide for yourself, eh? books: mostly non-fiction - there is something about crazy stories being real that gets to me. love augusten burroughs and david sedaris. also the nytimes everyday (except now that they're charging for it i'm at a major news loss. suggestions are welcome.) i read the economist when i'm on air planes.  movies: garden state, rom coms. i can't handle anything scary and i'm ok with that.  shows: the wire, arrested development (r.i.p.), dexter, weeds, glee, parks and rec, modern family, some bad reality shows like the bachelor (i know, i know, it's pretty bad...). it's apparently not the cool thing to like tv these days. i'm thankful for netflix and my dvr so that i can play outside when i want and catch up on shows when it's time to relax.  music: i love almost all music. no really i do. minus maybe jazz. currently listening to eric hutchinson, james morrison, joshua radin, amos lee, andy grammer, matt nathanson, joe purdy, brett dennen, the weepies, fun....  food: sushi, cheese, mexican, guacamole, quinoa, ethiopian. lots of fruits and vegetables. fresh, organic food is really important to me.  living healthy is very important to me but life is too short to say no to french fries. 1) love and support 2) my sonicare 3) my beagle nigel (we've been together 14 years and counting...) and my new pup peyton 4) music 5) sports/exercise/sunshine 6) flip flops (rainbows) what i want to be when i grow up, the way things work, what to eat next, where i can escape to next, what life will be like in a few years, how many dogs i can have one day before i'm officially the "dog lady", where have the genuine guys gone...  i shower in the morning before i ski. apparently that's not normal? i had no idea that was weird until this past year. in case you were wondering, i also shower after i ski, and hopefully hot tub.  also, i'm a sucker for guys that play the guitar. it's really just not fair. i also have a thing for blondes, but it's not a must by any means and i dare you to convince me otherwise. if you are blonde and play the guitar, i'm pretty helpless.  i'm allergic to gluten. it's a recent discovery and it's made me a bummer to date. i'm not trying to be a part of the fad, i'm actually seriously allergic to it and it can be a hassle. guess you should know now. *you also went to usc (or you can handle me going a little crazy on football saturdays) and/or * say what you feel, call when you want and not when you "should", and always go to sleep on a good note. *you are not afraid if this turns into something serious and long-term. i have great friends, and if i was looking for more, i wouldn't look here. *you want to take sailing lessons with me. *you aren't too bummed out that i can't drink beer anymore (gluten). trust me, i'm bummed enough for the both of us. but don't worry, you can still drink it and i can still drink other alcoholic things. phew, now that we're done with that... *you floss.  *also, if you think the toilet paper should roll to the outside. it's important!

full_bio: motivated but laid back, optimistic and active. i absolutely love doing anything outside - hiking, backpacking, rafting, hopefully someday learning to climb - usually with my camera in tow (though i'm far from an expert!). though i love exploring the city, i also tend to get a little claustrophobic if i don't get out every once in a while. i played sports in college so i enjoy staying active, and i love a good game of pickup soccer, basketball, sand volleyball... though a round of hallway bowling, don't-touch-the-ground tag, or nerf wars w/ the roommies are equally great forms of activity in our apartment. i've also discovered that there are very few things i won't do for a friend (and often people i hardly know) - whether it's a ride to the airport, a long conversation at 1am when someone's had a rough day, or staying an extra hour to chat or play cards with a patient - and i'm lucky to have friends who i trust would do the same for me. working on medical school right now and loving it - i am constantly inspired by the people i work with, and it's pretty amazing to have found something that i can easily see myself doing happily for the rest of my life. aside from that i also do some research and have done a small bit of medical work abroad in the past, and hope to find a way to someday incorporate both into my career. handstands in deserts. not letting bad things get to me. 80s/90s sing-alongs. roadtrips/random adventures. cooking? (well, i enjoy it!)  books: the power of one, into the wild/into thin air/anything by jon krakauer, the poisonwood bible.  movies: into the wild (theme here?), garden state, rain man, hoosiers, bourne series  shows: 30 rock, modern family, daily show, the occasional cheesy crime/spy show...  music: incubus, o.a.r., muse, eddie vedder, third eye blind, alexi murdoch, any and all classic rock! 1. family & friends 2. nature 3. music 4. campfires & bbqs 5. science 6. a comfy bed work (but fun work thoughts! i.e. the best things to do for my patients), how to be a better friend/sister/daughter/physician, how cheesy some of this sounds when written, knees and giant magnets, where to go on my next adventure, what to have for dinner. ideally spending the afternoon playing soccer in the park and then heading off on some weekend adventure. but when that's not possible, anything from grabbing a drink with friends, hosting a potluck or game night, or just relaxing at home with a movie. as a kid i used to hate loud noises so much that anytime i was given a balloon (at a restaurant, fair, etc), once home i would hide in the upstairs closet, bury my head in a pillow, and demand my mom pop the balloon downstairs to avoid the risk of said balloon popping unexpectedly in my presence. fireworks also needed to be watched from indoors.  thankfully, i have since gotten over this fear :) you've got some good hiking suggestions or photography tips, you know what lacrosse is, or you just want to say hi! bonus points if you can start a campfire or can cook (or at least enjoy trying), or if you aren't bothered by a girl who likes watching football!  i'm also getting ready for a big cross-country roadtrip from pa to sf and welcome any suggestions of places to check out!

sign: gemini and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: a constant theme in my life is learning and seeking out new experiences that will lead to personal growth and richer relationships.  i take joy in simple acts like listening to bluegrass music, riding my bike along the bay trail, and taking walks up verdant hills and local park paths.  i used to not believe in astrology (i studied the sciences, so if something hasn't been proven, it's not true) but i have to say, the description of a gemini fits me to a t... duality should be my middle name! oh, and i'm an infp (who can be very extroverted at times : ) learning, adventuring, reconnecting w/ friends, making new friends, course-correction with respect to work. striking up conversation with random strangers and sometimes making new friends that way, finding adventure in my own backyard, enjoying the company and laughter of friends, exploring the beauty of the bay area and beyond. my name... it's unusual and somewhat difficult to pronounce (there is a story behind it)... and no, it's not a hippie-sounding name. a blue-ish colored freckle (a very odd word in print, i must say!) on my right cheek. recently i've been learning more about meditation and spirituality. currently reading "just one thing: developing a buddha brain one simple practice at a time" as well as "the spirituality of aging." recently read my stroke of insight about a neuroscientist who suffers a stroke and tells the tale from the unusual perspective of stroke sufferer _and_ neuroscientist. best books i've read include dune; eat, pray, love; kiterunner; cider house rules; the prophet; nonviolent communication; gaia's garden. i have eclectic music tastes, from jack johnson to john craigie (naked skype is hilarious!) to lilly . movies... hmmm, i haven't been watching movies recently, i guess b/c there are so many things to do that involve live entertainment here in the bay area. i admit that having been raised by a dad who loves musicals, my all-time favorite movies include my fair lady and sound of music. i love food from all different cultures: turkish, korean, thai, chinese (north and south), japanese, fancy-california, tex-mex, etc.! friends. adventure. access to nature. my bike... i ride vs drive whenever possible! music... makes everything more enjoyable! good physical health. where i want to travel to next! on a walk if it's nice weather out, relaxing at home, seeing friends, checking out a local music group, or taking in the oakland art murmur scene... about half the time i'm in an energetic mood and the other half of the time i'm into doing something quiet/restorative. i was 19 when i first kissed a guy! ok, that's as much as i'll say online : ) ... your idea of a great first date includes a walk through golden gate park or other regional park. ... you're into backpacking, camping, or cycle-touring. ... te gusta el idea de viajar a sudamrica o amrica central.

full_bio: i seek balance between the creative, open-minded, spontaneous, generous, artistic lifestyle i experienced at burning man and the thorough, driven, profound, positively impactful life which academia and industry has set ahead for me.  i am an outgoing, spontaneous, passionate person who loves to experience all that life has to offer. i like to create an adventure out of life and take on large life projects as well as enjoy the here and now as much as possible. i have run 3 marathons, 1 super sprint triathon, and am looking to train for more triathlons. i also have a pilot's license. there is almost nothing that i won't be interested in or into. some of my highlights are music (classical and other), delicious food, great conversation, and whatever crazy adventure you can think of i am most definitely in!  i am guided by the following philosophy:  "it is better to regret what you did, instead of what you did not do."  this decision-making criteria has resulted in a truly adventurous life which includes regular travel for olympics and world cups as well as serious undertaking of hobbies including music, scuba, and airplane piloting. in the same spirit, i am now an entrepreneur, and am in anxious anticipation of my newest adventure that is currently revealing itself. i run a startup biotech company located in berkeley. we are trying to improve the lives for people suffering from chronic diseases. ask if you're interested in learning more...  i also play piano in a trio that occasionally performs classical music for friends in both the sf and la areas.  and finally, to round out my life, i have a burning desire to explore all that life has to offer - this quest brought me to burning man for the first time this past summer which definitely affected my perspective on how one can live life to the fullest. being modest and concise.   piano, violin, delicious food, schubert, good scotch, and amazing friends how each action i make today will impact the future both for me and for others.

religion: atheism and somewhat serious about it
full_bio: doesn't "summary" sort of imply that it's over and i'm dead? or does hyphenating it to "self" somehow keep it in the present? ...anyway, here's a short self-ennutshellation:  i guess you could say my lifestyle is a bit "alternative" - my living space is more of a recording studio than a proper apartment. my only car is a cityshare. though i could afford a grown-up apartment and a car, those types of things have simply never been a priority for me. i'm in no way opposed to them - it just hasn't come up. i am, however, among the most stable people i know - i tend to not move very much (10 years+ in my current living situation, such as it is), and tend to stay with projects and relationships for a long, long time. creating and helping to create music. i am a composer, and i've actually made a living at it for quite a while. i do music for film and television - mostly indie films and tv commercials. i also record and produce local artists in the bay area. recently i've started composing for modern dance, which is having an ever bigger footprint on my time. when i get busy i get very busy, but the last few years i've done mostly music for tv ads and those tend not to take over my life like film score composing does. ...ironically, i watch almost no tv! can't stand it. i've been told by many people that i'm good at listening - at least i think that's what they said. [update]...since "listening" seems to be such a popular response on okc to this question, i'll add: i'm good at finding humor in any situation. if it's there i'll find it. i'm good at giving tech-support to my friends with macs - i do it like once a day. i'm good at making simple websites. the goiter. since i think people skip down to this bit right after they look at the pics (i do) here's a bunch:  books: a lot of art lit. "on photography" by susan sontag. (...sounds boring, but it's an amazing book) and "accidental masterpiece" by michael kimmelman to name two and then occasionally old non-fiction and poetry type stuff. "frankenstein" by mary shelley. "the complete poems of emily dickinson" by guess-who! "night and day" by virginia woolf.  movies: elephant (the weird ambient film by gus van sant. not the elephant man though i like that film too), soy cuba, el patrullero, italian westerns such as the great silence, the good the bad and the ugly, once upon a time in the west etc., 2001: a space odyssey, the exterminating angel, his girl friday or any of those howard hawks movies with overlapping dialogue, orpheus, rr (it's a 111 minute experimental film made up entirely of 43 static shots of trains going by. that's it. if you think this sounds interesting, there's something very wrong with you and you should definitely send me a message!)  music: music from italian westerns and other films. the last few years i've gotten into a lot of early and mid 20th century music - pre-jazz, ragtime, gypsy jazz and then classical music from that period - satie, copland, prokovief, ligeti (he's later i know) plus, the usual stuff: yeah, yeah, yeahs, arcade fire, pj harvey, pogues, the notwist, the books, pixies, clash, iggy, patti smith, joy division & all the new bands that sound like them interpol, the editors, etc. bjork, x and more of course. the latter, indie/punk/rock/pitchforky, artists are in my comfort zone musically (i was in bands for years), the former, soundtrack and period artists i'm finding more challenging of late.  art (random few): gerhard richter, paul klee, david park, joseph cornell, andy warhol, chuck close, pierre bonnard, mile bernard, henry darger, felix gonzales-torres, jean-michel basquiat, frida kahlo, georges braque, ray johnson, mark rothko, barnett newman, bill viola, alfred stieglitz, walker evans, luc tuyman.  food: i like to eat things i find on the ground. - iphone (boringly) - fastpass (now clippercard) - tacos (frm la taqueria) - elliptical bike - glasses - pores (i read you'd suffocate if you didn't have pores) creativity and art. visual art mostly these days. although i'm a musician, visual art really gets me going the last few years. i spend a stupid amount of time at sfmoma and art galleries and talking to artists when i can corner them. why? i don't really know why. i just like it. it seems to be filling a very big hole. it feels something like religion to me. since i've been working freelance for a long time. weekends have become an obsolete concept, but i guess i know what the question is looking for:  because i work at home, i like to try and get out of the house most evenings - so i guess, in this sense, every night is friday night for me. there's a lot of good music in the mission that i often end up seeing at smaller places. red poppy, amnesia, cafe revolution etc. i also check visual-art listings regularly (squid list, fecal face etc.) and go to art openings when i see something that looks like it won't be too annoying. i'll usually go to any art event that features, or includes sound-design and/or music-to-picture type exhibits. i hate this ipad. you should message me because i feel a little weird about sending messages.  perhaps because of a sense of propriety i am, so far, reluctant to initiate contact. i realize this is why we're all here, but there's something about approaching women uninvited that makes me uncomfortable. it's not that i'm shy. it's more to do with how i was raised. i never do this in the real world so it seems strange to do it here.  so please do message if:  if you want to hang out and talk about and/or go see art and music.  i'm open to whatever may happen, but this site seems more geared than i am to those looking for life-partners and/or sex-partners. i want those things too, but what got me *here*, to this site, is just wanting someone to do things with. somehow, expecting more seems like it would diminish the possibilities of what could come out of the experience.   please don't bother messaging me if you're a big sports fan - i'm allergic to the stuff. or if one of the things you can't do without includes the tv show "lost". ...or any tv show really. the okc matching robot really thinks i should hang out with jocks and people that care deeply about their "shows", but i shall not bend to the will of this robot.  and if you really thought i had a goiter when you read my "what people notice first about me" and you kept reading (!), our senses of humor are not in sync, but you have a good heart and i'd love to hear from you.

full_bio: born and raised in the bay area. i spend most of the year in the east coast though for graduate school. i specialize in a foreign language, and i'm really looking forward for a year abroad in europe in the fall.  people characterize me as a noble and lovely person. others have also noted me for my crazy-ass sense of humor regardless of any situation. overall, i'm really just an intuitive and considerate person. i'm a graduate student in the humanities. i spend most of my free time people-watching and taking shots of the city with my dslr. i also enjoy travelling and using my camera to take city life shots. i usually like to first explore the city, and look for places where great photographs can be taken. in a sense, i 'contemplate' the city first, and i then walk through the city the next day with the camera in hand. urban photography writing facebooking my smile. my kindness. my awkward facial expressions. 2666, borges, 100 years of solitude, the trial, the brothers karamazov, peter stamm, clarice lispector, cortazar  bourne trilogy, rounders, being flynn, shame, adrift in manhatten  indie, new wave, chillwave  mexican food, italian  the meaning of our existence. seriously, it was the subject of my thesis. okay, only marginally. :p on fb/reading/procrastinating/listening to music/movie theater/viewing city life photographs i don't live in sf, but close to it :p you want a new friend.

full_bio: just moved here to sf from ny. originally from va beach, virginia and now living for the first time on the wc. i've been doing investment banking for around 5 years and am enjoying the change of pace here in sf from manhattan. it's refreshing for sure. i'm not your stereotypical banker; i'm working on comedy in my spare time and looking to give standup a chance. i love people and think everyone has something unique and interesting about them. i'm quite friendly and just like to have a good time, enjoy life, and make people laugh. and i'm caring and sweet as shit in case you couldn't tell. looking for the next adventure and someone to help me spend this insane trust fund. making chicken and waffles, freestyling, knowing when to use there, they're, and their, and picking up the check at dinner. my awesomeness. or the fact that i'm in their bushes (their- nailed it). books: patriot games, the art of fielding, ballad of birmingham (poem), gonzo: life of hunter s. thompson movies: braveheart, shawshank redemption, kickboxer/bloodsport (was pretty much obsessed with van damme as a kid), snatch, legends of the fall, die hard, beverly hills cop, lethal weapon, road house, point break. music: like everything, sublime, bush, offspring, nas, biggie, lil wayne, akon, jt (not ashamed), lotta others food: italian, thai, ethnic in general.. corn dogs and chicken pot pie are pretty money too. my throwback jordan's, thomas pink shirts, dexter, and game of thrones!!!! also my tempurpedic bed, seriously i think it was custom crafted by a chinese person who happened to have my exact build. my purpose, staring into puddles asking myself, "who am i". and why they don't call missiles, hitiles. out with friends or under the covers with a flashlight reading goosebumps. i'm a gay-virgin.. if you want to take me hiking, or anywhere outside the city for that matter, ride a beach cruiser with me, go surfing, laugh a lot, watch legends of the fall with me, go to a comedy show, and basically just have an effing good time. u also have to accept the fact that i forget things from time to time, haha.

full_bio: life is full of adventures and i'm always trying to ride the waves as they come with a little beauty and grace if i can. currently i live nomadically between multiple worlds and realities. it suits me for now, but i'm always seeking a deeper sense of home. i help run a non-profit dedicated to celebrating and sustaining ancient mbira music of the shona people in zimbabwe.  i do a lot of "rewilding" work mostly in the great basin restoring the biodiversity of wild foods through ancient traditional methods of cultivation.  i spend a lot of time learning and practicing permaculture, folklore, history, botany, plant medicine, and ancient cultures. i also spend a lot of time crafting and creating, dancing and making music. hunting music and books. going with the flow. learning songs. understanding patterns. usually people comment on my hair. books: the man who fell in love with the moon by tom spanbauer daughters of copperwoman by anne cameron growing up in occupied america by finisia medrano edible forest gardens by dave jacke stealing benefacio's roses by martin prechtel the nart sagas and the kalevala  movies: the dark crystal, satyricon, federico fellini, david lynch, lars von trier  music: coil, diamanda galas, buffy sainte-marie, rasputina, judy trejo, kanopic descent, alio die, i listen to a lot of old field recordings of archaic music from all over the world...  food: my favorite foods are wild and gathered by hand in an old good way. yampah, bread root, wild rabbit, salmon, black cap raspberries, blueberries, currants, pinenuts, nettles, miner's lettuce. these are just a few of my favorite wild foods. when i'm in the civilized world, if i can't get wild foods, i tend to go for a more simple, veggie-rich, grain-less diet. plants water wild open lands songs and stories community movement other worlds, history, cultural paradigms, gender and sexuality, botany, wilderness, satyrs, monsters, faeries, old songs and stories either stargazing in the wilderness by a fire telling stories or crafting with friends and making a feast in the city. i believe in faeries ...you're interested in sharing love and intimacy with people but you have other interests that are just as important to you ...you're interested in making beauty more than making money ...you have an active creative life ...you love the biosphere ...you aren't shallow and aren't looking for someone with perfect skin or a perfect body ...you aren't concerned with fitting into the status-quo ...you are an autonomous individual

full_bio: hi! not looking for dates right now!  graduated 2009 with a master's in sexuality studies. yes, it was fun and yes we talked about sex a lot and yes it means i'm good in bed. i now have a job. groan! however, it's a perfect challenge and i love the way it's allowing me to grow and learn. i work in mental health with homeless adults and young adults on the peninsula. the system doesn't consider me a youth anymore so i'm a youth ally and activist. i approach my work and life from a framework of anti-oppression, intersecting isms etc...  some stuff i like: exploring other countries by plane, boat, foot and wheel; hiking; riding my bike; stretching and dancing my ass off; exploring complexities; movement; radical politics; harm reduction; sex; bdsm/kink; visiting the public library; reading; drawing birds; spending time with my family and friends; going to the park; enjoying san francisco in all its queer glory. i've been here for 8 years and i still am discovering new places to get excited about.  i am candid, elated, and restless i'm very connected with my family, all of whom live in the area. i'm the oldest of many many cousins (that's my mexican-irish catholic background), consider my aunts and uncles my confidantes, my grandparents my mentors, my parents my teachers. i have a wide circle of friends from many different parts of my life; some intersect, many do not. from april-october i'm a die hard giants baseball fan and like going to games when i can. i am a bit of a foodie- i don't mind spending my money on it and like to eat well in an overarching sustainable way now that i can afford it. i enjoy a good debate that gets feisty even though i like to be right. this city was made for exploring and loving and i am constantly thrilled by how satisfied, stimulated and happy i am here.  lately i've been using this membership card to get into a bunch of museums for free. free art and music are nice.  i like listening to people, and sharing myself too. i enjoy one on one interactions more than big groups. that said, i like dance parties and community activities. fitting a lot of stuff into a week and still getting 8 hours of sleep. having endless amounts of energy and staying calm. budgeting. keeping my fragile plants alive. showing enthusiasm only when i mean it. making out. taking it. no clue. you tell me.  ok i was told, "your curls, your hella intense eyes, your ass if they are behind you, and your piercing." there. bookshelf says: queer stuff, sex stuff, sf queer sex history, ol' standbys from undergrad, bukowski, david wojnarowicz, dorothy allison, audrey lorde, rumi, zora. these are my devoted, prized companions. trying to read more fiction right now.  i've watched one movie in the last several months. it was called revolutionary road. i did enjoy it. (i watched two more movies! the fall and the fountain. both very good.)  i like dancing music, bad pop 'n hip hop, cumbia, rock en espa~ol, late 80s/early 90s punk rock, ska/rocksteady, dancehall, bluegrass, bright eyes, sea wolf, coco rosie, julieta venegas, decemberists, hot chip, sidestepper, mstrkrft, orchestra baobab etc etc. i recently saw silvio rodriguez in concert after he got his visa for the first time in 40 years. i cried, it was so beautiful. i also recently saw mstrkrft and i took my shirt off at the club.  home cooked greens and macaroni and cheese, fresh tortillas, enchiladas, sashimi, mandarins, grass-fed beef, kale, cheeses, smoothies. truthfully, i'll eat anything, but i usually eat with the seasons. i found some mangos in a dumpster recently and made some delicious agua de mango. that said, i'll definitely eat it if it's free. the next week i found flowers. i didn't eat those. movement, beer, dare i say coffee, lovin'...that's sufficient. work and personal boundaries, communication, when the next time i can dance is, how i feel in the moment, my favorite kpfa radio shows, the great outdoors, food, the system, ageism and other intersecting isms, the state of the world, budget cuts, how all the remodeled houses in palo alto and lofts in the city should house people for free while they're vacant, kinky sex, and if i have time to meet new lovers.  oh, and champagne. grinding somewhere. tonight, i will love you forever. i like radical queers and unchecked passion. i'm looking to connect with folks on an individual level involving intimacy (i.e. hot sex), friendship, growth and independence.  you'd like to have some city adventures involving art, coffee, beer, snacks, gardens, and dancing. bonus if we can do it all in one date.  you're able to communicate your needs. big ups if youre interested in open relationships/poly/non-monogamy 'cause that's how i do things.  i do have a soft spot for ftm, butch, genderqueer and andro types, but am also very aware of the way masculinity is valued and femininity squashed in our society, so pretty happy with the hot femmes i've been making out/sexing with recently. would love to meet more of ya!

full_bio: welcome to my profile, your search is now over.  i love mountain biking, sketching in nature, urban hikes, camping, climbing, yoga classes, getting really hungry and then eating, cyclocross, bike touring and spending time with friends doing those things (some of those things are not nearly as fun to do alone) what am i doing with my life? designing software, going to yoga a lot, riding bikes with friends, learning to cook new things, and planning some tropical travel with a bike ...maybe a surfboard. being corny. perseverance. i'm laughing at my own joke (or yours). reading: the onion, mcsweeney's.net cracks me up pretty regularly, unaccustomed earth, hunger games, yoga journal, bike magazine, alan o'bannons guide to backpacking, ...  music: iron and wine, band of horses, mgmt, the sounds, little dragon, the new pornographers, beach house, feist, nouvelle vauge, sean hayes, the rapture, the music they play in yoga classes, bon iver, gayngs, otis redding, chris isaak, dick dale, ..., ..., ...., and i'm a sucker for cover songs (i was at an iron & wine concert, they covered wild horses and i kinda melted.)  movies: the princess bride, run lola run, the departed, best in show, ... room with a view---yep, i'm a chick. i also enjoyed watching jack ass, watched it with my friend and it was hillarious, but then i saw some of it by myself and i hated it (just goes to show that who you're with changes everything).  tv: i don't have a tv or cable, but occasionally i'll get hooked on a tv show via netflix like friday night lights downton abbey the muppets made a big impression on me as a kid, i love those guys in the balcony, i love animal!!!, but my favorite is the swedish chef --> smrg di brg!!! i think i would watch more sports if i had a tv. i would probably watch a lot of pbs and probably rot my brain with reality tv (i'm glad i can't) i have these luxuries and i really appreciate them:  - my city bike --- it's so liberating to be able to get anywhere in san francisco on my bike in less time than muni could get me there and for free ... and it keeps me fit and energized to ride everywhere i go.  - pencil paper and watercolors --- i can draw anything i set my mind to.  - bike racing --- this has become a corner stone of my social life and i love it.  - the vast expanse of wild places around the bay area to hike and mountain bike (we are so lucky!). the only bad thing about these is if you sit home on a weekend you really have to feel guilty for not going someplace amazing.  - earthworms---we can't live without them.  - i'm also pretty happy about hot springs  and flowers, especially wildflowers  (i'm going to add to this)  my mountain bike hydration love activities, planning mountain bike rides.  food---i like to eat well, i enjoy cooking healthy food and i appreciate artfully prepared food very much.  travel  work (i get absorbed in work in a good way)  adopting a greyhound ..even though i enjoy not having the responsibility right now.  art and things i want to paint packing for an adventure, out with friends or having friends over for a feast. religion: christianity but not too serious about it
full_bio: let's be honest, you're really not looking for a painful explanation on how "deep" or "interesting" i am; you're really only reading this to see if i am a complete nut job (which i am) or to find some minutiae upon which to start a conversation. you can get all the serious conversation over coffee or a beer. to satisfy your curiosity, here's a convenient list of my finer points.  there is nothing i despise more in this world than shaving. facial hair is definitely one of my favorite things in the world.  strawberry andre will always hold a special place in my heart.  celery and grapefruit makes my mouth go numb.  sunlight makes me sneeze (photic sneeze reflex).  i was born and raised on video games. very few things make me quite as happy as an rainy afternoon playing video game with someone who i can at least tolerate. the snes is and will always be the best gaming console in existence.  i'll kick your butt in any smash brothers game. guaranteed. conversely, you'll probably kick mine in mario kart. either way, i claim peach.  i spend a lot of time trying to discover latent super powers that i know i must have. like, a lot of time.  algunas veces, trato de hablar espanol. no gano, nunca. (y odm, creo k chatspeak espanol s mas loco k... ps... todo.)  i'm a big fan of beards, beer (ipas are my personal favorite), crappy covers of good oldies, dunkaroos, embedded systems, flannel, peanut butter and jelly sandwiches, piercings, and tattoos.  my dream job would be a lumberjack, ghost hunter, or a tornado chaser.  i am bearded, quixotic, and (sometimes) awesome just graduated from the university of michigan with dual degrees in computer engineering and math (yes, i'm probably a bigger nerd than you). landed a great job in san francisco and am now working at becoming the world's youngest sugar daddy. taking naps. cuddling. my sincerity and my general "like" for everyone and everything. my rule of thumb... everyone and everything is inherently good until they give me a reason to regard them otherwise.  to put it into perspective, i've only met two people in my entire life that i don't care for much. i love everyone else... so your odds are good. books: ender's game, the hobbit, the great gatsby, odd thomas, fahrenheit 451, the republic, the art of racing in the rain, catcher in the rye, slaughterhouse-5, a brave new world, dune, foundation, and on and on... i'm a voracious reader.  food: any and all? i can honestly count, on one hand, the number of foods that i do not enjoy. everything else is fair game. fair warning, spicy things are my personal favorite... and when i say "spicy," i mean, "as spicy as you can possibly make it."  music: maylene and the sons of disaster, he is legend, emery, atreyu, foxy shazam!, thrice, funeral for a friend, senses fail, finch, boysetsfire, boys night out, everytime i die, a static lullaby, rise against, my hero is me, say anything, the used, the bled, killswitch engage, haste the day, the devil wears prada, gwen stacey... basically lots of bands that go rawrawrawr. 1. naps 2. caffeine (i am completely addicted to it.) 3. facial hair (i look ridiculous without any. trust me.) 4. video games 5. hoodies 6. good people unique holes that i can put into my head/ears. the (tattoo) sleeve that i hope to get started in the near future. wherever i have the opportunity to be- i have no set location that i "need" to be. no matter if it is a bar, club, couch, or any other number of places, as long as i am with good people, i will be enjoying myself. you can sneak up on me from the left side reeeeeally easily. you're looking for a friend. i am looking to start making/recruiting/soliciting some new friends to fill the role of accomplice... i mean...  ideally, i'd love to meet people who just enjoy hanging out (i have video games, hint hint) and/or enjoy going to grab coffee or going out for the night. i really would love a bar buddy or two.  if you've made it this far in my profile, it probably means that you should message me... just saying. ;)

full_bio: as you can see, my name is cristian. i currently reside in oakland, but have spent time migrating between my hometown la and the bay area. i feel firmly grounded in the bay and am seeking to establish relationships with others who have similar interests as me.  i have a cat, and live in a house in dt oakland, that was built before the 1900's and was a speakeasy in the '30's.  i ride a scooter and bike to work and around town. both means of transport are more then utilitarian and are fundamental aspects of my life-style.  when i am not working, i tend to write, with my interest pertaining mostly to film. however, as the weather has been increasingly warm i have found myself outside hiking, chilling by lake merritt making mimosas with friends, getting drinks at heinolds and kayaking in the estuary by jack london square.  i enjoy life and consider myself to be zealous, however, i am extremely realistic and at times sardonic. these days my focus tends towards becoming a more ideal version of myself; which means, pursuing a healthy and active lifestyle as well as applying my ideology to my day to day. apart from self improvement, i enjoy cooking and attempting alternative recipes and making my own juices. i am always looking for take up a new project ranging from painting my backyard fence to playing and occasionally winning dart tournaments. urban living at its best: utilizing craigslist and ebay as a resource for trading and selling. also thrift store shopping.  making cocktails and hosting friends. been told by many i have great hair. books/what i read: beat generation, kurt vonnegut,friedrich nietzsche, jd salinger, f. scott fitzgerald, john steinbeck, edgar allan poe, sigmund freud, a people's history of the united states, i ching, erich von daniken, mark twain, crime and punishment, the bible, witchcraft, buddhism, philosophy, biographies, screen plays, wikipedia  film: anything kubrick, pt anderson, woody allen, scorsese, alfonso cuaron, vicent gallo, gus van sant, orsen wells....  music: i like just about everything, indie, hip hop, classic rock, classical, jazz, blues and whateva's goood.  food: pizza, mexican, seafood, a nice bloody steak, soups of all sorts, italian and late night burritos from the truck. bicycle  films  friends/family  a lover  iphone  passion to live the current world situation and how fucked we are. the potential of humanity and how we just don't realize it. film ideas. out and about. enjoying drinks with coworkers after after work. catching shows in downtown oakland or in the mission. i have a medical prescription for pot;) ...i seem like someone you could have fun with, and you're up for adventure, good conversation, drinks, shows, burrito trucks, poker...

religion: atheism and very serious about it
full_bio: anything's fair game with me. i'm a real nice guy, and i'm sure there's not a whole lot of them left. lived up and down the west coast, did grad school in san diego, came out to dc, worked a few non-profit jobs, and starting over with law school. i'm brainy but i love working with my hands. i'm outspoken and curious. 80's and 90's nostalgia are most enjoyable. law school's important to me in building a future that i can be proud of. i'm interested in employment and immigration law. ultimately, i want to serve the public interest. anything analytical, visual, film, writing, legal or technology related. my mac is over six years old, and i take care of it like it were my child. hasn't failed me yet.  some have called me "mr. wikipedia," so if you're looking for a trivia master, i may be able to help out. some have looked at my glasses calling me a hipster, to which i responded that i don't join trends - apparently the necessary response of a closeted hipster. i'll just take this to mean "entertainment." i had a lot of all these things, but i've less interest in them. sure, i'm open to expanding my tastes. i prefer the internet over tv, however.  books: non-fiction, history, policy or science-related movies: anything that would get lampooned on mystery science theatre 3000 shows: 30 rock, the office, parks and rec, breaking bad, the walking dead, hell on wheels, real time with bill maher, the daily show, ugly americans the colbert report. been loving key and peele on comedy central lately. music: rock food: brussel sprouts (seriously, it's brain food) humor the difference between the use of "fewer" and "less." catching up on bill moyers on pbs. i've been to four high schools, three middle schools, and two elementary schools. no, i wasn't an army brat or a "problem kid." just moved a lot. if you like brainy guys with a touch of humor.

full_bio: i'm a music lover and all around audio/visual media guru. i'm not really the bar type, more like bike rides, cameras, hammocks, and friendly weirdos.  looking for good music, good food, and good conversations. i'm in the bay for 2 months putting together a curriculum for a non-profit in sf (but i'm staying in oakland). looking for someone to explore both cities with, who's down with fun spontaneous adventures. rapping. filming. riding my bike through traffic really fast. talking. listening. cooking. my looooooong ass dreads. they're down to my back now. music outkas, little dragon, blu & exile, pharcyde, peter bjorn and john, k-os, fela kuti, sergio mendes, louis armstrong, ella fitzgerald, cab calloway, three 6 mafia, weird al yankovic, ida maria, souls of mischeif....this list could go on and on but i don't have enough patience.  movies the big lebowski, oldboy (really anything from chan woo park), the shawshank redemption.  books 100 years of solitude. malcolm x. ....and anything about globalization or social entreprenuership. a microphone. a camera. a computer. a bike. a hammock. & summer. taking over the world. at a concert, art gallery opening or riding my bike around looking for something new to see. sometimes i listen fergie's album. i don't care if i'm a grown ass heterosexual black man, that album is fire! you're pretty chill.

offspring: doesn't have kids, but might want them
full_bio: somewhat-un-serious-guy-seeks-deadly-serious-relationship...was that gross hyperbole? i'll try again...very easy-going guy here...try to keep my life as drama-free as possible...love finding that undefinable chemistry with other humans (it doesn't happen very often and it's awesome when it does)...honest, genuine, loyal, no b.s. and very monogamously oriented...  ridiculous opening line aside, i am indeed looking for a serious/stellar/awesome/amazing/epic/spectacular/incredible relationship with just one fella...you know, the "life-partner" thing, or whatever the kids are calling it these days...maybe a house...maybe some dogs (from the shelter, of course)...maybe some kids (also from the shelter)...we'll figure it out...the universe has been a bit slow in presenting this guy to me, so here i am at the mercy of cupid...**important adjectives ahead** loving, kind, passionate, considerate, communicative, affectionate, romantic, compassionate, funny, intelligent, honest, fit, masculine, respectful...if you see your picture when you look those words up in the dictionary, then consider throwing something at my head to get my attention...or just message me... let's see...when i'm not at the day job being a cubicle chimp for ucsf, i try to tap into my reserve energy to stay creative and sane...have been involved with a theater co. the past few years as a writer/director/actor, so that can keep me busy; have an awesome '67 epiphone riviera guitar that i neglect way too much and it sits in the corner of my living room mocking me; have lived in sf for 22 years, but never tire of urban hiking & checking out the sites; have an amazing little brother (in the big brother program) that i try to spend as much time with as possible; love my bike and biking around the city and into marin when time/weather permits; try to see as many movies as possible, but never seem to make everything on the list; wish i went to more concerts...working on that (same with theater); was taking krav maga this past year and loving it, but that's been temporarily put on hold after an injury...started playing soccer in april for the first time since high school...that's been, um, interesting...write as much as i can, but tend to write more when deadlines are looming... ...procrastinating, which makes me want to come back to this question later (i guess it's actually a statement) but...let's see...being loyal, honest, considerate, respectful, affectionate, genuine, and best of all, can make food so spicy it has been known to cause internal 3rd degree burns... hmmm...i haven't a clue...probably that i'm carrying a bike on my shoulder because i pretty much get around everywhere on my trusty jamis; second...probably that i'm a smart-ass (hopefully a funny one); third...probably that i'm laughing at something (usually myself) in order to keep my sanity... in no particular order...just my brain uncorked: mad men, it's always sunny in philadelphia, the alienist, wings of desire, the awful truth, the science of enlightenment, anything kubrick, coen brothers, the dick van dyke show, m.i.a., the dead weather, vertigo, alien, howard hawks, flirting with disaster, firefly, my man godfrey, double indemnity, i wake up screaming, anything noir, james m. cain, lover come back, pre-code flicks, burn notice, arrested development, rear window, diamanda galas, blossom dearie, scout niblett, breaking bad, carol lombard, busby berkeley, fight club, the bridge on the river kwai, le tigre, nick cave, babes in toyland, before the devil knows you're dead, throwing muses, christmas in connecticut, his girl friday, showgirls, the cramps, pj harvey, anything spicy, curb your enthusiasm, ella fitzgerald, bing crosby, ball of fire, ratatouille, an american in paris, mr. deeds goes to town, memento, triplets of belleville, hugo, detour, borat, down with love, super troopers, a face in the crowd, the grifters, harold and kumar, the big lebowski, yeah yeah yeahs, white stripes, screamin' jay hawkins, the office, sons of anarchy, archer, jazz 20's-50's, 30 rock, peaches, the kills, julie london, peggy lee, cibo matto, chicks on speed, cat power, beastie boys, elia kazan, late 70's/early 80's punk rock, adam's rib, 40-year old virgin, superbad, psycho, my favorite wife, the conversation, exterminating angel, playtime, sweet smell of success... my health friends music absurdity movies maple syrup i suppose love should be on the list somewhere, but i really don't want to bump maple syrup... ...the state of the world and what i can and can not do, and then trying not to get depressed about it; how one person (me) can dirty so many dishes in such a short amount of time; why it's so difficult to get decent bike lanes in the city; that i'm not spending enough time in my creative outlets because i spend so much time at the day job... ...possibly exhausted from the week and curled up in bed with netflix...yes, that exciting...also quite possibly out at a restaurant, movie, play, improv, concert, critical mass or fill-in-the-blank social event... ...that i want to come back in my next life as either robert mitchum or cary grant... ...you are not in prison (paroled soon? eh, sure, why not) ...you are able to detach yourself from your iphone for more than 3 minutes without breaking into a cold sweat ...you don't think the universe revolves around the castro ...you don't begin every other sentence with the phrase, "as a gay man..." ...you are not looking for a hook-up ...you think gays are rather selfish for co-opting rainbows; refracted light belongs to everybody ...you are not turned off by my somewhat obsessive use of 3-dot writing... ...you actually read all the above, quite possibly smiled or laughed, and said to yourself, "i'll be damned...this joe character seems like a helluva dude...i'm kind of interested"...but then you don't message me because you're distracted by something on qvc and you forget and then i never got the message...

full_bio: let's see. not really sure where to start. so i will just list random things as they pop into my head. i like film. i used to have a dog name petey. i'm a singer in a band. ich spreche deutsch. i lived in iowa before i lived here and i raised sheep on a hobby farm. i rarely read books. that doesn't mean that i am stupid, just prefer a more engaged form of entertainment, like film or conversation. i like the color yellow. i enjoy playing sports more than watching them. i do not believe in god. pizza is my favorite food. video games are fun. computers are even better.  i am smart, charming, and self diluted i work as a software engineer at a company called slide. i make facebook games. its not as fun as it sounds, but it's pretty fun. being a nerd...  i don't read a lot of books but my favorite series would have to be hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy.  movies: inglorious bastards, rejected, pan's labyrinth, donnie darko, jerry maguire, house of flying daggers, walk the line, fox and the hound, v for vendetta, american history x, the matrix, sideways, hero  tv: modern family, community, 30 rock, lost, house, heroes, always sunny in philadelphia 1. my computer / phone / the internet 2. friends and family (corny i know) 3. public transit 4. television 5. beer 6. food i am a guy so... usually drinking happy hour specials, and wishing i could be doing something more exciting.  i'm up for anything... so try me.

religion: atheism and somewhat serious about it

full_bio: ah, the self-summary... what a splendor it is. mine was due for a revamp i suppose. i'm tall and smart. i occasionally get called good looking. i'm into dorky things like music and science and long philosophical rants. in ways, i'm almost as uptight as my lily-white mormon farm boy upbringing would lead you to believe, and in other ways i'm a stark raving shameless freak. i go to school full-time for architecture and urban planning so i'm always running around working on some project involving colored pencils, foam core, and hot glue. (i think i've got discount cards for all the arts stores in sf at this point.) my wonderful family is in the bay area, so i'm always loved/annoyed/delighted/entertained. i have 3 cats which are pretty much the most noble creatures on the planet. (just ask them, they will undoubtedly agree.) i'm also in a relationship with one of the world's most amazing guys (hale_) at the moment, but we like inviting boys over to cuddle, bake cookies, and watch movies (either separately or together) so say hello.  i am conscientious, charming, and mercurial attending berkeley so i can save the world. meeting the amazing denizens of our amazing city. cuddling. making jokes that no one else gets. making jokes that only my friends get. changing the subject. making myself feel uncomfortable around new people. deep tissue massage. knowing obscure song lyrics. kissing. is the pitchfork i carry. the doors of perception, the perks of being a wallflower, the halloween tree, to kill a mockingbird, frannie and zooey, oryx and crake, naked, the poisonwood bible, where the wild things are, to the lighthouse, ham on rye...  up, the last supper, mysterious skin, clash of the titans, the fountain, eternal sunshine of the spotless mind, dangerous liasons, impromptu, shaun of the dead, adaptation, what the bleep do we know, i heart huckabees, sinbad and the eye of the tiger, time bandits, lotr, the cook, the thief, his wife, and her lover, something wicked this way comes, the dark crystal, american beauty, like water for chocolate, cinema paradiso, dark city, clue...  sufjan stevens, rufus wainwright, sigur ros, stars, jens lekman, underworld, thievery corp, iron & wine, dead can dance, erlend oye, frou frou, howie day, kitchens of distinction, magnetic fields, peter murphy, stereolab, the cure, the notwist, unkle, the decemberists, legendary pink dots, sisters of mercy, mazzy star, portishead, fila brazilia, kings of convenience, air, alphaville, barry adamson, xtc, bjork, bright eyes, fischerspooner, grace jones, kate bush, low, pink floyd, radiohead, zero 7, the the, etc...  i'm vegetarian, so indian, thai, mexican, my mom's house, freezer aisle at tj's. the people i love, moments of transcendent clarity, hope, beautiful men, biological needs, unicorns! whether or not the universe really is trying to tell me something. how much our own focus and expectations influence the future. how so many people have become brainwashed into brave-new-world-style intellectual submission. the cumulative weight of our everyday choices as humans. amused in some fashion. i'm a shop-aholic who rarely buys anything. you enjoy clap-tracks and cowbells, are a celebrity in your own mind, or you have a non-derogatory nickname that you got as an adult. or you're bored. whatever.

religion: judaism but not too serious about it
full_bio: i'm inclined towards dry humor and all kinds of dance and lake swimming and good books. when possible i travel by bicycle. i teach (all ages, but mostly adults) and make art. i'm often immersed in projects and then head for mountains or water or desert for some out of city perspective.  winging it exceptionally labor intensive and often absurd projects spending time on the details over thinking things not sure, but apparently i haven't changed much... i was recognized on the street by a classmate from preschool. i wouldn't have believed it, except that they got the school right and remembered i only ever wore cowboy boots or sandals. i kind of wish i still had those cowboy boots... this american life on the radio (my primary form of entertainment when i lived on a farm in upstate ny) the little prince, writers borges, raymond carver, oscar wilde, joan didion lately it's been lots of textbooks, so i'm open to suggestions for some good page turners or book swapping (i have more than just textbooks to swap). get mortified readings lots of music (blues, folk, rock, blue grass, old country, deep raspy voices, jazz, lots of stuff i know by sound but not by name) movies: delicatesen (post-apocalyptic surrealist black comedy), most of almodvar's work i don't mind subtitles but also enjoy some hollywood escape food: fresh and local and all kinds dark chocolate endorphins making stuff music desirable destinations and friends in walking/biking distance family non-chlorinated swimmable water avocados lots of dark leafy greens wall assisted handstands  dancing to music in my kitchen or someone's living room or searching for an actual dance venue that also has live music and serves alcohol so that more of my friends will join me recovering from the morning lecture on art from the andes or how korean potters ended up making japanese tea bowls or the function of footnotes going to a friend's art opening renting a movie (yes, i actually still frequent the neighborhood video store) going to another friend's art opening happily diverted from yet another art opening i prefer making pots to cooking in them (though i'm a sucker for home cooked meals... i'm full_bio: first of all, let me say, this self-summary is a work-in-progress. turns out, it's more challenging to write about one's self without sounding cocky, than i thought it would be...  anyway, like everyone else in the bay area, i love the outdoors (especially when the weather warm). hiking, kayaking, swimming and surfing are my favorite. but i must say, i do not like surfing here, it's waaaay too cold.  in general, i'm laid back, very social, optimistic, yet, realistic. i work hard and play hard.  i'm from nyc and therefore have that famous east coast sarcasm we're known for. i think fast, talk fast and act fast. life is too short to sit around. gotta make it happen! i'm still trying to figure out what i want to be when i grow up. but for now, i work in event marketing and am studying for the gre in the first step toward my pursuit of a masters in urban planning. figuring things out. my blond hair, blue eyes and big boobs (although they're not huge, they're real and that always seem to be amazing to everyone). book: the story of stuff is my favorite but in general i like nonfiction books.  music: i like everything, even country. but my favorite music is jamband music and seeing live shows at the indy or fillmore or bbr is one of my favorite things to do in sf.  band: widespread panic is my favorite but i like i a wide variety of bands and music. friends and family a way to communicate the sun the beach pizza music i'm obsessed with all things environmental. i was into reducing, reusing and recycling since way before it was cool to be into it. i am determined to find a way to halt production of plastic (especially plastic bags) and just reuse what we already have clogged the earth with.  i also think a lot about why there's no reliable, easily-accessible, public transportation system connecting the bay area, like nyc does. at a show at a venue such as the independent, fillmore, gamh or bbr. or, i'm home on my couch. i can put my foot behind my head. you're interested

full_bio: i'm pretty laid back, and i definitely don't take myself very seriously when i'm not on the grind. i would describe myself as being ambitious, goofy, and loyal. i have an unconditional love for my family and friends.  ideally, i'd like to meet someone with a strong head on their shoulders that i can get to know and share things with. i'm looking for a woman who can be my partner in crime, not my sidekick.  i'm on okc because although i like the bar/lounge scenes at times, they tend to set a pretty seedy context to conversation. plus, between my day job and my side job, i spend a little over 70 hours a week on the grind. given the time constraint, it makes meeting people a lot harder. i'm currently working as a project manager at a construction firm in san francisco. i'm either imprisoned in the office, or on a job site somewhere in the city. at nights, i get on my mouse wheel, and then i hit the books. i've just been counting the days until my next trip/adventure since i don't really have weekends anymore.  update: just got recruited by one of the best development firms in the city. i'm damn excited. if you live in the city, you will definitely see my handiwork in a few months!  apparently my hair is something noteworthy. books: brave new world, crime and punishment, catch-22, 1984, ender's game series, the northern lights trilogy, [more to come]  movies: the gladiator, equilibrium, memento, requiem for a dream, lotr's, drive, snatch, fight club...  music: i'm into a little bit of everything. r&b has always been my fall back though. if you're an artist, i'd love to hear your stuff.  update: usher - looking 4 myself (check it out!)  food: i'll make the leviathan look like an aspiring anorexic. i will eat practically anything and love it too. please don't message me if you don't like thai or mexican - it wouldn't work between us :)  tv shows: it's always sunny in philadelphia, the big bang theory, how i met your mother, fringe, house, lie to me, the office, family guy, arrested development, firefly, breaking bad, revenge, jeopardy, the daily show, spaced, the booth at the end, x-files... are: family and friends internet toothbrush my droid phone my neon-green tape measure calculator... well, i could do without a calculator, but i'd lose my mind with an abacus. when i'm going to buckle down and write you a message... working in some manner or another. i'm usually out on saturdays, checking out a new bar or lounge in the city with good company. is... contrary to my pictures, i don't only wear suits. actually, i'm usually in beaten up sneakers, jeans, and a t-shirt. you found something of interest in my profile or in my questions.  if you don't know where to start or what to say, don't sweat it. just send me a simple greeting. the better conversations can follow.

full_bio: just got started here so i am pasting in something from another listing so there is at least something here to read (maybe too much?!)  who am i?... that is a lifelong inquiry. might you be interested in exploring the depths of this question for each of us together?  maybe a better place to start is what brought me here. i have spent much of my time exploring life through my education, careers, participating in active and athletic activities, creating a home, and involving myself in self-realization and spiritual pursuits. as a part of this, i am blessed with a network of good close friends, and have enjoyed and grown in the intimate relationships i have had. however, up to this point, i have not put a lot of energy into pursuing a primary intimate relationship. i now feel ready to put in the effort needed to create this kind of relationship in my life.  i would like to have an intimate spiritual relationship where there is mutual attraction and mutual support for our highest intentions. i believe it takes some chemistry to overcome initial obstacles or resistances and that same chemistry may also present the personal growth needed to make a relationship and intimacy work. i am interested in finding someone to take this journey with me and who also has the intention of fully engaging the mind, heart, and body, in persevering through the thrills and challenges of relationship. i realize that all of this takes time and i am looking forward to seeing what comes of my efforts in the dating process. i figure at least i will meet a number of great people that will expand my network of friends.  some qualities of mine that i would also like to have in a partner are: honesty, loyalty, healthy living, curiosity, caring, affection, integrity, open communication, compassion, creativity, sensuous, a lover of nature, appreciative, and joyous.  i definitely have a serious side which is balanced with a quirky, silly, and playful one. i think that is why i appreciate the children in my network of friends and why they appreciate me. it also manifests through my love to dance (especially partner dancing) and my stints of doing synchronized swimming. i have a very romantic streak which i would like to have the opportunity to act upon. i like to be a participant in life by being active and trying a variety of activities and would look forward to expanding my experiences through the interests of a companion. some of my active pursuits include or have included: hiking, swimming (competitive and synchronized), dancing (mostly partner lately), kayaking, biking, volleyball, tennis, camping, t'ai chi, and exercising. i also spend time each day investigating my inner world through meditation and study of the teachings of the buddha.  for a long time period of time now i have used the lens of the teachings of the buddha to view and live in the world.      unconditional happiness, its causes, and the path leading to unconditional full_bio: hmm, what to say. i like engaging in conversations that make me think and hopefully will change my mind about how i see myself, others, and the world. i have a masters in consciousness and another one in dreams so i spend a lot of time thinking about such things and how we can make the world a better place using both. i try to balance this seriousness out with lots of laughter and fun. after all, you can't take life too seriously.  i enjoy meeting new people and forever expanding my community. i am super active and spend more time out and about than i do at home. this makes me cherish any time i get in my pj's reading a good book or watching movies. don't have cable, tho, so i rely on my netflix. no cable means i'm not addicted to any current tv shows which is probably a good thing, but definitely puts me out of touch with pop culture sometimes.  i like being outdoors, and have recently taken to seeking out new hiking trails around the bay area. i enjoy seeing live music and like to get my groove on. i love spending time with friends and family but also make it a point to have quality alone time too. i'm way into going to the movies, tho i seem to not make it there as often as i'd like. sadly, i end up seeing most of the blockbusters on netflix. would love to hit up the big screen more often.  i am from the bay area and although i have lived elsewhere i seem to find myself back home in the end. i quite possibly have the best family one could wish for (despite their humanly flaws, of course) so i spend a lot of time with them. i love to travel and try to make it a point to get out of the country as often as possible. one of my ongoing life goals is to have visited as many countries as i am old. i'm close, but i'm not there yet. haven't decided where to next. any suggestions?  at my day job, i am a program coordinator for an education research agency (it is not as geeky as it sounds - okay, maybe it is) and love my job most of the time. i like giving back to the community and it is always cool to see our work making a tangible difference in the lives of the over 8 million students in our region.  what am i looking for? someone to laugh with. someone to share some (but not all) of my time with - be it out to dinner, hiking, hitting up the museum, or spending the day in the park or a coffee shop playing backgammon. i don't have a type, really, save for my desire to be with someone who is honest, open minded, and fun. hopefully something worthwhile.    family & friends, good food, travel, a good book, sunshine. where in the world i am headed next and what is for dinner.   you wanna hang out.

full_bio: i am a fun loving, witty, confident and kind woman. i am not stuck up or egotistical. i am ready to share my life with someone special. i have learned to rely on myself but i am a better person when i am in a partnership. i am attractive both inside and out. visually- i have big brown eyes, long thick brown hair, and have an overall awesome presentation. in conversation you will learn that i am smart and passionate about the things that matter in my life.  i am looking for a great man who is intelligent and who is not intimidated by an intelligent woman. you have a great sense of humor and get my new york sense of humor. you are someone who sees life as an adventure and are open to the adventure. you are spontaneous, creative, enthusiastic about something (your job, the environment, kids - something!) politically aware, caring, likes animals, likes to have a good time, can laugh at yourself, likes to try new foods (both at home and out), and respects women. but most of all you are a man who is confident in himmself, working toward happiness and open to where ever this may take us...... i am a therapeutic & educational consultant. i work with adolescents, young adults and their families who are in crisis. i get to be part of the solution. its good work. helping people reframe their experience. i am a "glass half full" kind of woman. i am good at helping people rethink their negativity and help them move to a more positive place. i am kinda shy and most people laugh when i tell them that. i do a good job of "faking it till you make it" especially when in new social situations. it takes me a minute or so to warm up but then i'm good. food is the ultimate. i'm a foodie. i love trying new foods, new restaurants. feed me!!!  top 3 movies - godfather 1 & 2, good fellas & magnolia. top 3 music - pink floyd, the beatles, & rolling stones 1) friends 2) family 3) good food 4) good conversations 5) sunshine 6) being outdoors  i am recuperating from the week but usually get a second wind and will get a drink or a meal.  you are for real.

full_bio: let's see . . . - made many friends with common interests over the last eleven years in the bay area, - passionate about environmental justice issues, and - generally enjoying life as things have really come together in a coherent and affirmative way.  others might describe me as thoughtful, committed (in a good way 8^) ), intelligent, and several other relatively-positive adjectives.  most recent exploring has been near home in the rich wilds of the bay area, although biking through france and ireland, seeing a tiger in the wild in india, installing a rainwater catchment and planting trees in el salvador, and searching for pyramids in the mayan yucatan outback are among the more memorable experiences. current aspirations include kayaking and rock climbing (and did include "to complete a triathlon", but that happened last year!) self-employed in business consulting, and an incorrigible networker. often out at community events, and not out hiking as much as i'd like. maybe you can help fix the latter? throw at least a few workouts a week + daily biking into the mix, some non-profit volunteering, a little too much time on computers, and some space for alone time, and you've pretty much got it.  lean, intellectual, good listener, and "you look younger than your age" are some of the more popular observations. i'm happy to report that psychotic has not made the typical first observation list ;^) small is beautiful, emotional intelligence, the post corporate world, biomimicry, spirit matters, the earth path, beyond the limits, the fifth discipline, and you can heal your life were all influential books.  music: eclectic, heavy on the folk, acoustic, and 80's metal tendencies, with hip-hop/rap and world making a push in recent years  movies that stayed with me for a long time include motorcycle diaries, pan's labyrinth, fearless, la mission, and fear of a black hat. and i refer to the minority report far too often, almost always regarding the politics of pre-crimes. :)  shows and food. yes. - great community - a lifestyle that treads lightly on the earth - gratitude - integrity - solar radiation - six additional things that i could never do without . . . sustainable food systems, going to/volunteering at any number of great events, ecological footprints, cycling and the nature of existence. not necessarily in that order.  also often on my mind: why people do the things they do; that as evolved as we pretend to be, so many of the things we do are based in primal instincts. y'know, i tend to be out more on t-th nights during the week, and out on the weekends, than friday night. go figure. not that i'm against a friday night dance outing, mind you. it's not really private, as there was a photographer there who uploaded the picture to facebook, _tagged_ me before fb had upgraded their controls, and so everyone in my network saw. cryptic enough? ;^)

full_bio: i'm a designer and artist and i've been happily going at it for over 10 years. i'm looking for ways to leave my desk more...  i enjoy biking around the city, exploring, traveling, cooking, eating, exercising, learning, speaking italian, listening to and playing music, tattoos, knitting scarves in winter, sailing, telling my friends and family how important they are to me, hugs, and much more.  i'm a fun and passionate person, shy and outgoing, kind, sane but never borring. being inspired is very important to me. i'm pretty social and enjoy having conversations about almost anything. i also appreciate moments of silence. i'm extremely curious. i get my energy from the city and nature. i see myself living in the country and owning a goat one day. i admire driven, open-minded, independent, and well adjusted individuals - i consider myself to be one of them. i like myself. working for a living and enjoying my life. i've just moved to the city and i'm finally living closer to family. i'm making new friends;) parallel parking. untying knots. communicating. dressing myself. roller skating. cooking healthy. learning to dance. i think my eyes - either because i can make eye contact during a conversation or because they're blue and green and pretty. books: the alchemist, choke, the road, travel and typography books. tv: i don't watch much tv or involve myself in pop-culture/movie star drama. i do netflix and have enjoyed mad men, breaking bad, the office, deadwood. movies: i enjoy movies except for most romantic comedies or hollywood blockbusters. i'm into the cohen brothers, hitchcock, mobster, western, comedy. i get really into old twilight zone episodes. music: i have an appreciation for all good music regardless of the genre. some bands in my rotation are: thee oh sees, cramps, brmc, murder city devils, black keys, heartless bastards, beirut, bill callahan, pixies, loretta lynne, wayne hancock, cash, springsteen, hall and oats, dylan, holiday, etc. food: breakfast burritos, rice and veggies, homemade everything, hot sauce. after 7 years of vegetarianism, i have converted. so, i'm learning how to eat and cook meat again. there are a few food items, my friends and family, music, vacations. humanity having an amazing time at whatever it is i'm doing! seeing a show. making a mixed tape. resting up for the weekend with a good movie. not working. i have this online profile. #1: we have similar interests and you think we'd get a kick out of each other. #2: you are tall(er than me). if you're not tall or if you're tall and 500lbs., we can still be friends. #3: you are a man and don't need to get on the beard wagon to prove it. if you have a beard that's fine. if you have a beard and you've been coddled by a female all your life, we can still be friends. #4: you value friends and community. #5: you want to meet a down to earth awesome woman who may open your eyes to new things...  * side note: do not message me if burning man was the best thing to ever happened to you or you need drugs to have a good time.

full_bio: born and raised in the bay area, studied music and dance at a very early age. i'm creative and open minded and get along well with others who are also creative, spontaneous and witty. i read as much as i can and write even more. if i had more money, i would travel the world and back. i love sf for its diversity and good weather. i don't take online dating too seriously and i'm only here looking for friends. i just want to see what's out there and paint the town red. although i'm very comfortable in my sexuality and am not prude, please don't message me if you're looking for a threesome. i'm looking to meet people with substance and depth. personality and chemistry are huge for me. i love to dance, workout and volunteer as much as i can. i'm a true leo in every way. if you're looking for a travel buddy, i might just be your gal. i love road trips and i've been to burning man and back. i used to model too and still do when i have free time. living in the now arts n crafts, baking, drawing, painting, being a domestic goddess my height i guess especially when i wear heels. deepak chopra, oprah, tony robbins sleep silence sun music friends family burning man somewhere new exploring the city, typically not the girl who likes to go to the same place where everyone knows my name. i'm really not that private in person lol you're emotionally stable and have a sense of humor too. i'm an awesome wing girl.
