sign: scorpio and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: in 1959 i was a tin can on the side of a dust choked colorado road. now i am a rodeo clown. i'm going to school to be a clocktower repairwoman. my calling, however, is ostrich racing. 2/2/12 oakland! exploring the bay, hoping to let some creative juices flow, ya know.// what's up ya'll? befriend me and show me the town!! ;)  1/9/12 west or bust:)  11/9/11 slow-walkin' :) in western nc for awhile longer, plotting my next moves and chillin'! enjoying fall in the mtns for the first time in 7 years!!! hanging out in asheville a good bit, having fun and making friends:) so, gimme a shout if you are an awesome ashevillain!  7/26/11 yo. so i figure since i find myself on the move and tooling back around this site, i will be updating my profile. the below was written years ago. life has given richly of experiences since, but much of what i was doing while around gainesville kept to the same heartbeat. i graduated, adventured to the west coast, came back, left again last summer, went back again, and left again two months ago. perhaps third time's the charm for truly leaving that beautiful vortex... for those who are not familiar with gville, it is as swell a town and community as i could have hoped to land in... more than i hoped actually. i will always have a home there, as i do here in the nc mountains. yeah, i'm lucky;) now for the journey... i like to think that i share the helm with the universe, but sometimes the wind leaves no choice on which way to tack. there is always a reason. i have learned so much in the past couple years. namely because i looked for the lessons and kept my heart open as best i could. i live, love, learn... always... anywho, westward ho! taking it slow. catching the scenery, some grand times, new skills, and great new friends:) looking to keep working on different types of farms, gaining more homesteading and natural building skills. light being. create. share. i don't care how many chigger bites i have or how long it has been since i had a shower or clean clothes (though if i am on the internet it likely means i'm fairly fresh;), i love the woods. it is something i don't have words for. not in this language. if you understand that other language i imply, perhaps we will do well with communication. which i prize highly, by the way. i like to be direct, but can only do that when i know what i'm trying to say. of course, there are plenty of ways to convey truth and to share of one's soul. i like variety. sometimes choices can be a bit paralyzing, but i live for the majestic infinity that is the universe. hmmm. i digress, what am i doing with my life? there are the things mentioned above. there are the dreams which i follow. there is a desire to share with the mysterious you, who's very possibility of existence i cherish. thus here i am, on the internets, trying to convey things which really require the presence of hearts, eyes, grins, and laugh lines. in the forest or the field. dancing.  'years ago'/heartbeat creating symphonies in my head and biking in circles on beautiful days. i'm doing my time at uf. should graduate in spring with an anthropology major and minors in international agriculture and latin american studies. the student ghetto is my realm. i bum from one friend's company to another, sleep a lot, prepare/eat good food, do a bit of schoolish stuff, paint when i feel the urge, read, and listen to music -- i love going to shows or hearing my many very talented friends play. also, i like to take off on adventures of exploration into the area around gville (and elsewhere when i can), often alone. i get lost on purpose. i have a good sense of direction. this sometimes causes me problems when i am trying to get lost. i hope to be working on giving myself an outlet in strings soon - mandolin. i sing to myself a lot, often things i make up and then proceed to forget. i love communities of many sorts, forming them, participating in them, perpetuating them... one day maybe i'll live in a more communal fashion. for now, i love the folks i associate with who are down for dumpster diving, potlucks, activism, free markets, interpretive dancing, drum circles and general good cheer...  ;) sleeping. laughing. colors. moonlight.  i also have skills in the areas of growing things, piloting small aircraft, riding horses, scuba diving, cooking, general handicraft, sailing, climbing, positive energy, learning, loving, being... i'm wearing something colorful? glasses? oh oh i know... the hat! or maybe my shoelaces. folks seem to like my smile. i'm told i have a swagger. books (a few): kon-tiki, keep the river on your right, the count of monte cristo, two years before the mast, brave new world, 1984, regarding wave, love in a time of cholera, skinny legs and all, atlas shrugged, graceland, middlesex, the secret life of plants, nichts neues im westen, venus on the half-shell, still life with woodpecker, the moon is a harsh mistress, heart of darkness, the sun also rises, etc...  poets: t.s elliot, andrea gibson, rumi, gary snyder, loren eiseley, allen ginsberg, w.h. auden, h.d thoreau, pablo neruda...  movies (not a movie person): harold and maude, snow walker, cidade de dios, everything is illuminated, shortbus, lola rent, forest gump, the hours, old star wars, waste land, boondock saints, robin hood men in tights, christmas on mars, fear and loathing, tombstone, james bond films, gatica, cry freetown, man with a movie camera, koyaanisquatsi, baraka, brazil, apocalypse now, etc...  music: the doors, be good tanyas, gillian welch, van morrison, black moth super rainbow, bon iver, ratatat,dirty fist!, mike dorsey, gilberto gil, cessaria evore, fela, rem, rhcp, talking heads, pinback, the flaming lips, little dragon, jefferson airplane, jethro tull, the shins, of montreal, the avett bros, mason jennings, rogue wave, neutral milk hotel, mccartney, blind pilot, bright eyes, dire straits, dylan, tv on the radio, devendra, puccini, clapton, tool, cat power, beirut, modest mouse, ugly casanova, pearl jam, bob marley, billy idol, the beatles, hayden, iron and wine, steve earle, the allman brothers, rage agianst the machine, neil young, the cure, bach, edvard grieg, the grateful dead, nine inch nails, cake, warren zevon, john prine, pink floyd, the b-52s, the who, led zeppelin, hendrix, philip glass, fischerspooner, death cab, blind melon, james taylor, john mellencamp, tracy chapman, ziggy marley, the presidents of the united states, grizzly bear, talk demonic, godspeed you black emperor, the gb3, chris smither, jann tierson, mogwai, beck, ccr, q and not u, peter mawanga, built to spill, radiohead, queen, bela fleck, doc watson, etta baker, regina spektor, joanna newsom, 3eb, the wallflowers, andrew bird, the fleet foxes...  food: vietnamese, thai, mexican, japanese, german, brazilian, ethiopian, jamaican... i love my fried egg, munster, onion, and tomato sandwiches. i'm a big fan of dressing (the turkey kind and the krishna kind). tempeh = yum!! whole milk :-) citrus!! acai!!! venison! aww hell, i love 98% of food. me  earth  water  air  fire  duct tape possibilities. trees. food. friends. family. patterns. war. peace. politics. greed. grass. death. capitalism. music. history. sex. boxes. waves. colors. love. agriculture. existence. flying. babylon. carousing with friends. talking. laughing. singing. dancing. loving. i have measured out my life with coffee spoons you want to  would love to meet as many fantastic folks as care to come out of the wood works!! cheers to the wandering wonderers;)

full_bio: wow...these things always seem so contrived but here goes...i just turned 40, i'm queer, i like to create things like art and organizations, i work a lot and have a lot of commitments in the community.  since i turned 40 i've been thinking a lot about what my priorities are and have recently changed my profile to reflect those thoughts so please keep reading :)  i'm polyamorous and in a long-term poly relationship so i'm looking to date and get involved with other folks. i'm not looking for an exclusive boyfriend and i'm *not* looking for a booty call. rather i seek something somewhat to very substantive but not exclusive. i'm open to other poly folks & single folks looking to explore but i'm definitively not interested in cheaters. i like sex and am very open-minded, kinky and pretty shame free but i have zero interest in having sex with people with whom i can't have a conversation or cuddle with. fyi for me queer means that i'm open to folks of all genders from male to female and all the variations in between.  i'm politically progressive, a great cook and i love having debates with folks who pay attention to the news (especially those who can tell a credible source from one that is so biased as to be laughable).  i am clever, compassionate, and sexy working? fomenting revolution in my spare time? shifting the paradigm as much as i can? creating community, caring for others, cooking, playing, sex are that i am expressive, friendly and straight forward, oh, and fat, people do tend to notice the round bits right away :) there are so many books...right now i'm reading bad science, the life and times of mexico, all the books by david d. burns, the discworld series (pratchett makes me happy) and the elegant universe by brian greene. i don't watch a lot of movies but did recently see winter's bone which was lovely and poignant...i love annie lennox, me'shell nedeggeocello, erykah badu, patty smith, dead prez, india arie, adele, charmparticles, zap mama, the andrews sisters, lucille bogan, billy holiday, paul simon, 9 inch nails, billy bragg, tribe 8, the cure, siouxsie sioux, the dead kennedys...to name a few... weird question; by default air food and water then friends, intellectual stimulation and sex moral and ethical ambiguities and their implications at some damn meeting or other fomenting revolution! wow, really? once i get to know you i'll tell you pretty much anything... you'd like to know more about me or have an interest in getting to know me. bonus points if you're kinky, queer or poly :)

full_bio: i'll update everything soon. figured i'd put a quick descriptionin here for the time being:  fairly laid back, curious individual. deep conversations on the meaning of life are awesome, as long as it's understood that jokes will be made periodically to lighten the mood. i can't help it.  also, i tend to think my jokes are funny. others tend to just close their eyes, sigh, and shake their heads. what can i say, i like puns.  if you're sarcastic, i am intrigued. i'm a mechanical engineer working for a green tech startup.  just moved to the west coast looking for an adventure. trying new things. not sure if this is something one can be good at, but i rarely fail to try new things, i generally learn something from trying new things (even if what i learn is that i don't want to do it again), and people tend to be surprised (usually in a good way, sometimes in a weirded-out kind of way) by the new things i try. my laugh; i do it often, and i'm told it's fairly distinctive. books: portrait of dorian gray, good omens, the count of monte cristo  movies: pulp fiction, lucky number sleven, lock stock and two smoking barrels  music: alkaline trio, blink 182, louie armstrong, ella fitzgerald  food: many things come close, but nothing can ever beat a good cheeseburger. korean bbq is a close second though. not sure i have six things i need, but let's see...  my music (my soundtrack to life) my friends (to have someone to share it all with) my journal (to remember where i have been) a challenge (because otherwise it would be boring) my favorite t-shirt (comfort is key) ...and i'll have a coke. life, the universe, and everything. i'll get to the bottom of it one day.  that and what i will be eating for dinner, and if i locked my car door. celebrating the end of a good week with dinner and drinks, or falling asleep on caltrain home and hopefully not sleeping through my stop. i'm willing to admit just about anything i've done. feel free to ask anything...

full_bio: there are two different ways to play super mario brothers. one can hold down b and run and jump across the screen as quickly as possible, getting to each successive level faster than a gliding turtle shell. or, one can explore each level, seeking total completion, jumping at an empty sky to reveal a hidden coin block, kneeling on top of the white platform to find the secret warp to world 4, pursuing that blue koopa troopa just to see what yoshi will turn into when he noms on shells of that hue.  i employ the latter strategy. i could claim that it wins the game more effectively by way of warps and special powers, but really i do it for the love of exploration itself. dropping bowser into the lava is an intellectual fait accompli, but finding that hidden coin block that nobody else has ever found, or a glitch that lets you bypass the water level, that moment is like scratching your initials into the universe. i got my green card now, so the rest is gravy, yo. go on the welfares, sprout off some babies (hydra-style), then demand that canadian history be taught in the public schools. i'm your worst nightmare, arizona! inventing. here's one right off the top of my head: scaredolphins. exactly like scarecrows, except wearing a snorkel and mask. boom! million dollar idea, just like that.  cornfields free of the dolphin scourge at last, and best of all: made in the usa. did you hear that obama? how about some grant money? the stigmata. hello, awkward handshakes. why make it that easy? how about you see if you can recognize any of the following quotes.  music: *"maybe there's a god above / but all i've ever learned from love / was how to shoot at someone who outdrew ya" *"and my popcorn squeaks a silent question / wonders why i'm not at home / where you wait beside a silent telephone"  tv: *"has anyone in this family ever even seen a chicken?" *"no resurrection ship. you understand? she's just as dead as your friend"  movies: *"i don't want to cram in sex or guns or car chases, you know... or characters, you know, learning profound life lessons or growing or coming to like each other or overcoming obstacles to succeed in the end, you know. i mean... the book isn't like that, and life isn't like that." *"o'doyle rules!"  books: *"none of you seem to understand. i'm not locked in here with you. you're locked in here with me." *"all i want is' - and he uttered the final words through clenched teeth and with a sort of shame - 'to retain my freedom.'" *"time is an illusion. lunchtime doubly so."  here are some hints: i like canadian indie music and existentialism.  how-can-we-save-the-world type shit. the citizen / corporation power struggle. can we redesign, kludge, or legislate away the inherent consume-spend-grow values of our little frankenstein's monster, the corporation? and most importantly, what i can do in my little sphere of influence to make this world more humane.  oh, and live music, board games, the books i'm reading, and sweet-ass elbow patches.   you can name any source of a quote in my "favourites" section.  yes, that is how i'm going to spell "favourites".  do girls actually message guys on this thing? i mean, statistically, you're only going to write me if i wrote you first, so "you should message me if" you liked what i said when i wrote you. it's called a conversation. it's what separates us from the animals. so we're doomed to keep on having conversations until we create animal-proof forcefields.

of software development. it's not that i decided not to be a writer or teacher or do-gooder, it's just that here i am doing what i do with most of the hours of my life. it's quite fun, mind you, i just feel odd describing it as if it's something that "i'm doing with my life." actually, i guess it's exactly what i'm doing with my life, but i don't want you to convey that it's "what i intended to full_bio: i am an upbeat person. i live with esprit, a strong sense of self and have a decent emotional vocabulary. i value people mostly based on their integrity and compassion and try to live simply and without want easier said than done but i work at it. humor fills my cup better than any 'thing'. people, books, live music and periodic meditation help me to continually revise, revitalize and improve myself. i wish to make a difference in the world while i'm here and i deeply value friends, art and the biosphere. i am single. i'm currently on a forrest yoga kick and can honestly say it has vastly improved my strength and vitality. but in between yoga sessions i'm an entrepreneur. commerce and its conventions fascinate me as does how it feeds the world (albeit poorly just now). clean energy, human nutrition/food production as well as it are my business pursuits. fyi - money does not dominate my thinking nor does want for material things. i desire a good life but in the right way. i'm pretty good at relating with people. i'm able to put politics aside and focus on the things that we have in common. i also have a green thumb and whip up meals from scratch all the time. i'm multi-talented =) i look younger than my age and i have brown eyes. right now i'm reading "reading lolita in tehran" and am impressed what a pleasure it is to read. i watch a lot of old foreign movies and american classics but can also be a sucker for big blockbusters and corny comedy when i want to take a break. i really do listen to all sorts of music, devour healthy food, exercise and even (dare i say it) fast -- as in willfully not eat for lengths of time that would make most people difficult to be around. dental floss & peroxide (they go together), my friends & family (so do they), animal crackers (unto themselves), my guitar (how would i keep myself company ?), crisp sweet apples & sharp cheddar (you know they go together) and finally my sense of self worth. how i feel and why, my close friends, what my next steps will be, object oriented programming, the future of the planet, the perfect vacation spot. out having fun with friends or in having fun with friends. i can be pretty absent minded with things in the oven. if you want to end up with love actually and not love virtually. sounding like a tool, i love to travel, eat, drink, read, discover new music, watch documentaries and debate (which is just a fancy way of saying "argue"). if wit and sarcasm accompany any of these interests, i am in heaven!  i have a passion for languages and would love to live in as many countries as i can and learn as many languages as i can.  i am independent and not afraid to put myself into unknown situations. i can also be everything from care free and laid back to very focused and stubborn (in a charming way, of course). i speak my mind and will argue what i believe until the cows come home. with that said, i am also not afraid to let loose and make a complete fool of myself once i trust someone.  watching bad reality tv and singing as loud as i can in the car with the music turned up so loud i can't hear myself, trying new restaurants, taking day trips our of the city and going to festivals of any kind are some of my favorite things to do. i have worked (cashiered, served, managed) in busy s.f. restaurants for the past 8 years and briefly taught english in spain last year. i am passionate about human rights and international development and plan on working in international nonprofits based throughout latin america before applying to graduate school in order to pursue a masters degree in development anthropology.  i intern at an amazing nonprofit right now and also support myself by working in two restaurants. banter. singing horribly. organizing. finding new music. eating. learning languages. sarcasm. darts and kickball. most people don't know what ethnicity i am. it turns out it isn't that exciting. books: the phantom tollbooth, on the road, the poisonwood bible, naked, lamb, a heartbreaking work of staggering genious, the midwife trilogies, catch 22; david sedaris, zizek, christopher moore, haruki murakami.  music: sam cooke, the avett brothers, bon iver, etta james, 2pac, too short, old crow medicine show, mgmt, rihanna, adele, crystal fighters, the shins, black keys, modest mouse, tv on the radio, the mars volta, estelle, jimmy cliff, groundation, the national, local natives, mumford and sons, arcade fire, neil diamond, bob dylan, the four tops, hector lavoe, ojos de brujo, the civil wars, the head and the heart, florence and the machine, wye oak, the pixies, the vaccines, hot chip, biggy smalls,, etc...  movies: nightmare before christmas, memento, the usual suspects, forgetting sarah marshall, the fugitive, pretty woman, a muppet christmas carol, anchor man, crazy heart, the professionalwhen harry met sally, 3 men and a little lady.  food: everything and anything that is good! in no particular order:  1. laughter 2. love 3. sex 4. burritos 5. dogs 6. friends and family  working, at a dive bar, dancing or drinking wine and watching bad reality tv, family guy, the office or friends.  you can make me laugh.

full_bio: i moved to cali ~2 years ago to live the good life... surfing, mountain biking, snowboarding, motorcycling, backpacking, sailing - i love it all! i work at an environmental certification body doing international business development and auditing. when i'm not in the cage (aka my office), i'm usually traveling, camping, checking out live music, or exploring the bay.. snowboarding, cooking, pretending to surf, making people laugh i'm really tan for a polish-irish-swiss-german mutt.. favorite authors: kurt vonnegutt, george orwell, antoine de saint-exupry, erich fromm, lawrence gonzales, lois lowry  music: lots of ska, reggae, surf, jazz, hip hop, blue grass, alternative, blues... mostly everything except christian music - i fuckin' hate that shit.  food: everything, anything friends, family, music, nature, adventure, gyros the present dancing at john colins a couple weeks ago, sitting by a bonfire on the russian river last friday, going on a backpacking trip in the desolation wilderness this friday, drankin' in wisconsin with old friends the following friday... ok, ok - i admit it: my junk is huge. you are happy, funny/sarcastic, adventurous, spontaneous and love livin' life

full_bio: i'd probably always say i'm a weirdo. and i love that. i enjoy finding humor in the odd and random. most days i act pretty silly, borderline child-like. heavy doses of dark sarcasm, too.  i'm a hardcore scientist. it makes me very analytical, both a blessing and a curse. along with that i am very introspective, and my mind is constantly thinking about something. i tend to live more in my head than the outside world.  i made this video and now i shall shamelessly promote it http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vl2suvew6hc you can catch my sense of humor...slightly. please forgive my shorts.  finally, i have a good heart and believe in being a good person. this is something very important to me. i spend a lot of time working. grad school kinda requires that. but time can be found for the right person, much as i still am able to maintain my close friendships.  my life lately is a continued quest for self improvement and being proactive. it's pretty empowering to take some control. whining should be reserved for those really horrible things.  i do have some hobbies. i like crafty stuff like sewing and redoing furniture and whipping up some crappy artwork. i loooove rock climbing but also never make it as often as i would like. my biggest favorite social hobby is going out dancing to some awesome electronic music. i like to do some social drinking, too. being me! i'm good at making awkward comments or silly jokes that not everyone gets. i'm good at making things. i'm good at being a friend and supportive person. i'm really good at science. it used to be piercings, but sadly some of them had to go. i wish it still was. now i'm probably just a gal with boobs. hmm...maybe my deep, dark soulless eyes. i have a pretty nice smile, and i'm not afraid to use it. i mumble a lot, too, apparently. i like music, am not all snobby about anything, and am usually listening to something electronic-flavored. i have some real feel good songs - flim by aphex twin, sweet by 311, unicorn by apoptygma berzerk (or maybe kathy's song, they're both beautiful!), oh yoko by john lennon, this must be the place (live version) by talking heads. i really love ladytron and how they have evolved over the years.  i love the comedy of strangers with candy and wet hot american summer.  (one more, the secret to my heart is peewee's big adventure.)  for those who want the book-loving type of gal, you best search elsewhere. i confess that i really just do not read for pleasure. it doesn't make me a dummy, though. city life music while working those oh so special people in my life my bike/car/bus pass. i probably get the most pleasure from the bike. p.s. it totally has gears. positive thinking! (actually i have done without it, but it's really not fun at all) mental stimulation thinking. if you can teach me how to turn my brain off occasionally without drug use, i'd be grateful.  i am very analytical about myself and the world around me.  sometimes i'll think a lot about science, too. and i should. some friday nights i fall asleep earlier than i do during the rest of the week. sad! but if i stay awake, i like to go do some rock climbing and then go out with my friends for some drinks. maybe even going out dancing. sometimes i am cuckoo for cocoa puffs! not much lately, but once i was eating them heavily. yes, that was just a euphemism.  i like to check out my rock climbing muscles in the mirror.  i know way too much about celebrity gossip. that's a huge guilty pleasure. okay, for the record i am not interested in people who live far away. like outside of any of the cities that border oakland (exceptions for san francisco since there's just a bridge!)  you feel like you're a little off kilter with the rest of the general population. but not a lot off kilter! i feel like we can compare the personalities of people to waves. i want someone with large amplitudes, not a line. i am guaranteed to become bored and bitchy without a stimulating partner.  along with that, i want someone who is honest. ( lying is a great way for me to get angry.) relationships based on respect tend to go well...  i'd appreciate if you appreciate the art of piercing too, having collected 14 different body/facial piercings, though only 5 remain.  i'm thinking i'm more interested in meeting up with whoever right away. i don't want to talk too much in internet-land cause i think it makes the transition to people-land weird.

sign: taurus and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: i'm a pleasure seeker with a thirst for adventure and a strong work ethic. as much as i treasure my bohemian northern californian lifestyle and values, i cannot escape the influence of my breeding as a chicago born, east coast educated, jewish american princess (friends here say my upbringing gives me an edge). my family is super important to me and we're really close, despite the 1,000's of miles between us. and with my family of birth being so far away, i feel really fortunate that i've created a really solid chosen family out here with amazing and inspiring friends; supportive, collaborative colleagues and most importantly, my dog.  i love to surf, paddleboard, hike, camp, backpack, skinny dip, soak in hot springs, travel to warm places, dance, go on road trips, make frowning people smile, cause a little trouble, take photographs and discuss art, politics and the teachings of the buddha. i love to get messy, and still clean up so well that no one would ever guess that we were just rolling in the dirt.  a lot of people call me bossy, which i totally admit to, but i think of it more as being clear about what i want and need. i've been called intense in ways that were both hot and difficult for men.  i am sweet, salty, and a little bit spicy... just like the food i enjoy. i'm self employed in a variety of ways, but mostly, my job is to help people relax... i teach yoga, i help babies be born and keep their parents present while it happens. i take photographs of this momentous event and do my best to help the parents make conscious choices. i get to say the word "vagina" at work a lot, which i really enjoy!  i feel so blessed that i've had the chutzpah to create a meaningful, creative, sustainable and challenging career for myself. i can honestly and humbly say that when i go to work, i am helping to make the world a happier, healthier place. work often feels like play and it changes all the time, so i'm almost always feeling inspired, excited and fulfilled.  other than getting paid to be the mellowest person in the room, i love to travel (locally, domestically and internationally), be outside, ride my bike around the mission, hang with friends and family and follow my dog around the bay area. to balance out all the running around i do, i attend a weekly meditation group where i practice sitting still. i surf or paddleboard 1-2x a week and always look for more ocean time whenever possible. i spend more time outside than any other city dweller i know. my pleasure principle is very high, in case you haven't noticed.  i've traveled all over the world and leave the country at least once a year. some of my favorite places have been tanzania (climbed kilimanjaro), florence, tiny islands in greece, thailand, india (to study yoga with the iyengars), mexico and costa rica. closer to home, i love tassajara, big sur, the sierras, the sonoma coast, brc and mid-week hikes in montara.  i'm also really curious... i love to learn... about so many things, but especially about the body, health and healing. there's an anatomy book on my kitchen table now, along with a braising cookbook.  i'm working on a book and aspire to be interviewed by teri gross on fresh air after it's published.  i've also just started to take spanish lessons. it's about time i learn to talk to the guys in the line up in costa rica. getting excited and experiencing ecstasy (without having to take the drug) balancing work and play making people feel at home in my home dancing like a maniac riding horses really fast with or without a saddle having fun and inspiring those around me to do so helping people relax seeing the bright side doing yoga in the airport fermenting dairy, veggies and kombucha taking pictures getting very intimate holding up my end of the conversation keeping my mind open walking long distances (sometimes with a heavy pack) cuddling, kissing, body rubbing and so on (enjoying) getting lost and finding my way back making healthy food taste good packing all the right stuff for every trip twirling a giant illuminated jellyfish in dark, wide open spaces sitting quietly in the presence of 1 or 100 others both being serious and being a total dork taking care of myself and others laying naked on warm river rocks writing online dating profiles (what do you think?) on the physical level, my wild mane.  beyond the physical, people often say that they find me to be empathetic, warm, straightforward, opinionated, positive, calm, grounded, and easy going.  sometimes it takes me a while to come out of my shell, so occasionally people find me to be cool, standoffish or quiet. i guess it depends on my company and context. books: anything by david sedaris, susie bright & anne lamott, lots of yoga, buddhism, photography, womens' health and birth related titles. right now i'm reading inside of a dog. love the new yorker.  music: neko case, gillian welch, stevie wonder, joni mitchell, 70's and 80's rock, pop, funk, r&b and soul. when i'm alone in the car, or getting ready to go dancing i'm totally into lady gaga and my glee pandora station. whatever the opel dj's are playing.  it's not really music, but i love npr, especially this american life, car talk, fresh air and marketplace. i like to stay well informed about politics and current events, but sometimes max out, so i go on regular media/ news fasts. (how much coverage of the gop nomination circus and the proposed obliteration of planned parenthood can one liberal lady tolerate?)  food: whatever's in season at the farmer's market, the fatted calf, nopalito, lahore karahi, mission chinese food, kabuto, club waziema. i cook a lot: stews, slow cooked meat, roasted veggies, tons of kale and random labor intensive, nutrient dense goodies.  movies: rushmore (and most wes anderson flix) and annie hall. lately i've just been making my way through the whole 30 rock and glee series. (i have a tv but it's never played tv before.)  dancing: love the boom boom room and most burning man-ish dance parties. my dog the ocean organic produce love, human touch and connection sunshine yoga ...how to find someone i want to go on more than 1 date with ;-) ...how to replace more car rides with bike rides ...how to save money and work less but still have all this fun ...how to make the world a better place, especially in the realms of the environment and health care ...what my dog is dreaming about ...my next meal ...the trip i just took to costa rica ...which tropical beach i want to go to next ...why pot makes it impossible for me to type or use my iphone teaching yoga, then... eating out with or cooking for friends or watching movies or taking a bath or going out dancing or preparing to leave town for the weekend/ week/ month or all of the above no thanks, i am done embarrassing myself in public. you are into strong, direct, independent women that like to have fun and say what they mean and want. a lot of men have told me that i intimidate them, so i'm really looking forward to meeting someone that likes/ is attracted to how powerful i am.  you're a goofball, dork and / or silly sweetheart that knows when it's appropriate to be serious.  if you're looking for marriage and kids, that's awesome, since that's ultimately what i want. if not, that could be awesome too, if we have a hot/ sweet/ fun connection.  you're really committed to or moving towards living a healthy life, in body, mind and spirit. i don't do well with bulls**t and call it like i see it. party boys beware, i have zero patience for drunks or those that get wasted regularly.

full_bio: independent and content with who i am, and where i am. i love people and life, try to see good in any person or situation, and put my heart in everything i do. i smile easily, but genuinely. affectionate, loyal, a bit stubborn, and a bit goofy. somewhat well-read and opinionated, but humble. i am a whole bunch of other adjectives, but there is really no point in listing them all here. =)  i have a healthy social life and friends from all over the world, so i am here, probably/hopefully just like you, trying to meet people i otherwise wouldn't run into. i am currently a science student in sfsu, studying to work on making this world a better place (at least that's the idea). i have about a year and a half left before i complete my first bs degree in environmental chemistry. ..cooking ..telling things how they are (with hopefully a healthy balance between tact and truth). ..researching and figuring out how stuff works ..making others look good on camera - (i help one of my friends shoot weddings once in a while). ..some other artsy stuff. ..btw, i am yet to understand how the "personality" is determined on this site, because mine is all sideways. among other oddities - i went to art school for 5 years, and they are telling me i am not artsy at all.. quiet laughable actually.. =) i don't usually ask, maybe i should start... =) *books*: "sea wolf" by jack london (<=#1 favorite by far); "the idiot" by fyodor dostoyevsky; "the little prince" by antoine de saint-exupry; "night watch" series by sergei lukyanenko (yes, it's not just a movie); "africa: biography of a continent" by john reader; "the custom of the sea" by neil hanson; almost anything by robert sheckley (especially his short stories) *movies*: the age of stupid, princess and the warrior, seven pounds, 500 days of summer, transformers, angel-a, edward scissorhands, hard candy, pan's labyrinth, religulous, zombieland, saun of the dead, etc.. *shows*: bones, battlestar galactica, the big bang theory, farscape, stargate, jon stewart, bully beatdown, etc.. *music*: i love psy trance, electro swing, cello, guitar. in moderation, i can listen to pretty much anything, but i am not big on country music.. some of the current favorite "main-streamers" that are in my mix on spotify: rihanna, chrisette michele, madcon, nneka, ne-yo, janelle monae, leona lewis, mary j blige, julie london, paula abdul, amy whinehouse, ashanti, macy gray, floetry, adele, etc, etc..  *food*: japanese cuisine is my favorite, but i am adventurous when it comes to food, and will try anything once (well probably not monkey brains though =)) ). i rarely eat red meat, and prefer seafood, poultry and tofu, with lots of vegetables, but can cook anything from tofu to ribs (the kind that melts in your mouth) - all depends on whether i cook for myself or for others. ..opposable thumbs, gravity, fire, water/food, sanity/logic, good company ... ... after the "blackout" against sopa, i realized that wikipedia should be on this list also... =) ..homework and other school stuff. ..would i be able to survive in a zombie apocalypse, or if transported to a certain time in the past (midieval england, egypt 3000 years ago, jurasic, etc.) ..what would it feel like to be in someone else's shoes.. to be a homeless beggar, to live in tibet, taiga, kibera, santorini before the volcano eruption 3600 years ago, to be a horse, or an octopus, there are too many possibilities to mention... just trying to understand how people and other living things must feel and how things work.. ..what the landscape i am looking at looked like before humans got there, or what geological process made it look the way it does. ..a whole bunch of other magical/sci-fyish "what ifs".. =) ..music ...either out with friends, or in a library doing my homework, or vegetating on my bed and watching netflix. on a rare occasion, i'd play a video game. =) ..that stuff in "i spend a lot of time thinking" was private enough for a profile. =) ..you want to. =) i am looking for someone "balanced", someone i can respect and be a friend to, someone i can learn a few things from. strong character and intelligence in the eyes are always a plus. if i stumble upon some of that chemistry everyone is talking about - great, if not - no biggy - new friends are always an enrichment. i think that's pretty reasonable... =)

full_bio: i'm taken. sorry boys, just here to take quizzes and leer.  found my partner in crime. he has a bicycle.  i study humans and figure out what makes them do the things they do. drinking wine or whiskey and reading books [on my back porch, weather permitting]  not smoking  http://thedailyinkfish.tumblr.com/ http://graphitemind.tumblr.com/ talking to strangers. apparently i missed that lesson from my parents. is my teeth. i mean what the hell? but for some reason everyone comments on what nice teeth i have.  i've told myself before they actually mean that i have a great smile, but you'll have to be the judge. after carting a library of books up 3flights of stairs, i am considering an ipad... but i would miss the books on the walls. here are some... - neal stephenson 'diamond age' - matt ridley 'genome' - margaret atwood 'oryx and crake' - orson scott card 'lovelock' - carolyn parkhurst 'dogs of babel' - jhonen vasquez 'jthm'  i need to remind myself to watch movies. they're a great excuse for knitting, but i forget how excellent many indies are. yes, i love action flicks and anime and will go to the theater to see 3d and explosions. - x-men - the city of the lost children - princess bride - howl's moving castle - the illusionist [tati's animated one]  tv is for knitting and cooking - house - fringe - top gear bbc - battlestar galactica - black books - castle - qi  music i am terrible at finding new music i love. sxsw and pandora are my salvation. - mumford & sons - tapes 'n tapes - mozart - x-ray specs - matt & kim  food. i love broccoli, spinach and beets. veggies really are dandy. i can't deal with someone who eats nothing but junk food every day or no other food groups than meat and potatoes. - thai - indian - ethiopian - spicy things make me cry. i love them but please bring me a handkerchief? people to talk to a bike to ride fresh books to read sunshine your music to listen to unfamiliar places - puzzles. any of them. all of them. - where i can travel to - my friends and family and how they are doing - making things - what i need to learn next none of my fridays are typical. what i don't do really is more apt: - i will not be "watching the big game man!" - i avoid rampant inebriation and have no need to get 'tanked' - clubs can be visited in small does and with heavy levels of irony and dancing i fantasize about running away to china . . . and not telling anyone. you have an adventure for us to go on together.  . . or just want to grab coffee or a drink.  [extra points for dancing]

full_bio: i'm going to rewrite this. in the meantime, feel free to project your ideal characteristics of a mate on to me. odds are i'll meet one or two of them.  . . . . . . . . .  here is a new, limited write up. more like bullet points. it will change after i come back and reread it.  + i like to eat and drink and travel and go to fairs and festivals and concerts and meet friendly, interesting people and play with babies and animals.  - i don't like waiting in line, heavy winds, loudness for no reason, and i think dessert is a dumb idea because, hey, i just finished eating. i may never see a movie in a theater again.  + i live downtown in san francisco and i love having so much of everything accessible with nothing more than my feet. i love that the streets are filled with people and life, some beautiful, some not. i love that i can wear indiscreet sweat pants and old slippers to the store and no one cares.  - i've been working in compliance and marketing for a large non-profit for almost 6 years now. i'm long overdue for a change.  + i've become increasingly health conscious in recent years and i have a lot of fun buying my foods carefully and deliberately. i'm almost a passable cook. and though i tend to eat somewhat selectivity, i'll eat any food on occasion, especially if i've never tried it.  - i am very independent. i've done well in relationships, but it's a shift away from my default mode and it has taken some time in the past to adjust.  + i'm a smart guy, generally speaking. i read the news, vote, and have lots of opinions. i follow politics closely. my ba is in international politics. i like to argue and debate. i like to date smart people.  - i have several female friends who i will sometimes hangout with one on one, which includes an ex-girlfriend.  + i can't make any promises, but i will probably get along well with your friends.  - i smoke cigarettes, which is terrible. i don't necessarily have to smoke them around you, at least not often. i had quit for nearly 6 months, which i allowed myself to spoil on a trip to europe. i will quit again.  + i like walking. i do it all the time. bicycles, cars, etc all seem unnecessary when i can just walk. i really like to walk.  - i have a broad range of what i find attractive in women, but i won't date someone if i don't have a fairly strong initial attraction. i'd expect the same from you.  that's plenty for now. if you want to message me, please do. doesn't need to be long or fancy or clever. if you have some interest in meeting me, and i have the same towards you, then we should go out on a date and meet each other. i don't get much out of sending messages back and forth. i apologize if i don't respond but i don't want to engage with someone if i'm not interested. no way to say that less bluntly.  phil

full_bio: i'm a midwestern gal who has wandered around quite a bit, from new york to spain to cuba to peru to austin to new york to austin to mexico to nebraska to puerto rico to san francisco to ... i'm an adventurous spirit who doesn't take life too seriously. oh, and i like hiking and biking, yoga, picnics, cooking, being on the water or looking at the water, dancing. i wish i was more musically-inclined. i own a classical guitar, but never learned more than that opening part of stairway to heaven and mexican corridos. maybe i should pick up the recorder. my brother still has his. i like all these things, but my life right now is consumed with studying, and that's boring. working over the summer at a legal aid organization in san francisco, hopefully moving here full time after i graduate- one more year to go! i'm average at most things. i'm not sure what other people notice about me, but i always notice other people's hands. i listen to soul, funk and r&b a lot these days. i just melt when i hear otis redding or etta james, rip.  tacos al pastor!  treme! 1. laughing 2. 3. 4. 5. i feel pressure to fill this out. let me get back to you on that.    you have a record player and like adventures.

full_bio: smart. snarky. a little bit naughty. currently wishing i had someone with a british accent to read his profile out loud to prospective dates, because the word "naughty" just isn't the same without it.  i'm a voracious learner, and i'm all about thoughtful intellectual conversation. i'm a nerd, and i'm proud of it. i love having real conversation about politics, science, philosophy, or literature as much as i love talking recipes and kitchen tricks or just flinging witticisms back and forth with reckless abandon.  i may have added that last part just so i could slip "flinging witticisms" and "reckless abandon" into a sentence.  i'm a firm believer in only taking life as seriously as it deserves, and i'm fluent in snark. generally speaking, i'm looking for someone i can be myself with, someone i can go out and enjoy life with who can stay in and get cozy, too. i get a kick out of just...taking it in when i'm somewhere new and beautiful (not hard to find in the bay area) and i'd love to have a partner to do it with. getting my phd at stanford, studying neuroscience. i'm interested in what makes people tick, how they learn, how they change...and so i decided to study it in more detail, and get into the wet, goopy stuff that does the thinking. i'm a fourth year now, which means i'm mostly in the lab, my schedule is more flexible, and my mind is on the science rather than the homework.  i also try to keep myself acquainted with the finer things in life, going wine or beer tasting with friends, trying new restaurants and recipes to feed my body, and hanging around bookstores to feed my brain. i'm also slowly making progress on teaching myself to cook, which in my opinion amounts to more of a synthesis/improvisation skill than simply following recipes. telling stories that make people laugh, cracking jokes, translating science into plain english. ..is probably a joke; a pun, a sarcastic wisecrack, some aspect of my semi-humorous commentary on life. food: i love ethnic food of all kinds. thai and indian are up there, for sure, not to mention italian, japanese, middle eastern.... i'm slowly branching out into cooking some of these foods, though i'm currently best at vaguely mediterranean food (does it use olive oil, garlic, and basil or oregano? then i can cook it!), a few traditional jewish recipes, and italian. i can also cook vaguely asian food, though i'm less inclined to say that i'm any good at it.  books: i'm a voracious reader/learner across a pretty broad spectrum. charles dickens classics, shakespeare, salman rushdie, but also offbeat nonfiction, like the immortal life of henrietta lacks, moonwalking with einstein, or david sedaris. my comfort reading is usually the scifi and fantasy i grew up with (david eddings, tolkien, orson scott card) or a with a slightly more literary, adult vibe (china mieville, lev grossman, and so on), though i've been reading it long enough that i like it with a touch of self-parody and lampshade hanging (think terry pratchett or kurt vonnegut). anyone who writes with a unique outlook on life appeals to me, whether it's because of a unique supernatural world or a slightly off-kilter personality. recently been reading stephen hunt for that little added touch of steampunk.  movies: the princess bride, pulp fiction, garden state, the star wars trilogy, stranger than fiction, anchorman, the dark knight, the frisco kid (anyone else heard of this one?), iron man, scott pilgrim versus the world, superbad, the king's speech, cedar rapids. generally speaking, i like anything with solid characters, but have a higher tolerance/lower standards when it comes to goofy comedy or scifi. i'm also more than happy to dip into the "so bad it's good" category for a laugh now and then.  tv: i keep up with the daily show, and how i met your mother as they come out. when i get some free time, i'm also capable of binging on, among other things, dexter, scrubs, arrested development, firefly, doctor who, and coupling. developed a newfound love for misfits, community, and parks & recreation lately!  music: my musical tastes are widespread, from classical spanish guitar to classic rock to jazz to modern alt-rock. i rock out to led zeppelin, the rolling stones, and the who, i love john coltrane and miles davis, but i also have coldplay, common, and some obscure mashup on my ipod. not to mention nerdier stuff--i'm a huge jonathan coulton fan, and i still have some vintage barenaked ladies and they might be giants i keep in playlists for special occasions. my computer a good book (okay, a probably a whole bookshelf. i could use it to float to safety if/when i'm marooned on a desert island!) fresh fruit music in my life time to sit and think a sense of humor why human beings act the way they do fun words, why they're fun, where they came from in the history of language the way life fits together into interesting narratives, with irony, foreshadowing, and the occasional bit of theme music. either out to a movie or a show with some friends, or out grabbing dinner and drinks with the aforementioned friends. when i'm in the mood for a quiet night, sometimes i'll just curl up with a good book or pull a dvd off the shelf. my bike commute to work every morning passes through lovely residential menlo park, and on occasion i've been fortunate enough to pass by a particularly fascinating elderly lady on her little motorized scooter. what makes her unique is that this lady's scooter is *tricked out* with large, swooping, chopper-motorcycle-style handlebars. i've never spoken to her, i've smiled at her once or twice, but i sadly know basically nothing about her.  in my head, her name is chopper grandma, and she fights crime. you think you'd enjoy talking to me based on reading my profile. the rest is kinda...details, right?

full_bio: i'm an intuitive, genuine, laid-back guy looking to meet someone interesting, fun and smart. i also.. - like the outdoors and fitness - i mostly run and climb - but also yoga, hike, bike, snowboard ... you get the idea... - do pottery and play piano / guitar in my spare time - enjoy concerts - particularly indie / folk / classical music - am a novice but enthusiastic cook - lots of good options for restaurants though - love nuanced indie movies and well-written, series-arched tv - enjoy traveling a few times a year, lately enjoying exploring some great outdoor spots (south africa, banff), good food (new orleans) and some relaxing beaches (st. martin)  my anecdote? in college, despite my general infp tendencies i was a prospective student tour guide (recognize me?) and i loved it! there was one day though when i had a particularly large tour that i tried to get into an elevator - ~20 people - and when we hit our floor - the elevator slowed...but didn't stop. a few folks started freaking out - but i called maintenance and started telling more folklore / stand-up comedy until they arrived. to make up for the experience - i treated folks to free ice cream in the student center. to this day - when an elevator starts making that annoying beeping sound - i just get out and wait for the next one! professionally - i'm in tech working on analytics - if you haven't yet, go read blog.okcupid.com - and realize that straight brown yuppies are totally underrated! i feel fortunate after a few different careers (including rocket scientist) i've found an interesting job i love and i do well!  personally, i think creativity and personal expression are an important part of a well-lived life. i also like to hang out with friends, eat, drink and generally partake in the various avocations sf has to offer.  - my hair - no, i'm not the 'fonz, but it's a bit on the longer side - my 3-7 day stubble - i'm fortunate not to have to do the clean cut corporate look! - my eyelashes - it's an indian thing - my non-indian-ness - i grew up in nj - and pretty much lost all touch with the old world a tv junkie - recently mad men, friday night lights, in treatment, how i met your mother - although the wire, six feet under & bsg will always set the standard  movie tastes can be more refined - most things on ifc, the lives of others, eternal sunshine of a spotless mind - as well as the great sunday afternoon b movie - especially thrillers, comedies, and the occasional cheesy romcom...  music is mainly folk inspired indie rock - think iron & wine, bright eyes, mumford & sons, calexico, bon iver - and some epic instrumental stuff - explosions in the sky, sigur ros, radiohead. also some jazz standards and once in a while classical.  food - recently did trip to new orleans almost exclusively for food - love chocolate, spicy things, earthy full red wines, ipas - i have a penchant for all foods / drinks rich & complex - perhaps this is true for everything in my life?  books - i barely find time to keep up with my new yorker subscription - although i'm a loyal npr listener (and occasional member) - does that count?   drinks at a local pub or other excuse to hang out prepping for a weekend trip to tahoe, yosemite, marin, points beyond... dining in or out - definitely not a full-blown foodie, but i appreciate a great meal and tasty wine concerts / talks / anything cultural - i like broadening my horizons whenever i can chillaxing (yes - i actually use that word frequently) - usually with a movie and a beer  ...you realize that life's too short to spend time just looking!

full_bio: when did proper use of grammar become an attractive trait rather than an assumed skill?  on that note, i've heard that dissecting a joke and dissecting a frog are much the same: most people lose interest and both die in the process. here goes my personality.. ;)  i've been described as being able to talk at length about nearly any topic, and as long as it isn't popular culture, they might be right. this is typically because i'm always trying to understand people and how things work, which was bolstered by degrees in physics and philosophy. somehow that gets balanced with a healthy appetite for silliness, and i rarely miss a chance to see the absurdity in a situation.  i've been travelling for the last few years, spending 6 months in nyc, germany, and london respectively with intermittent semesters in boston. now i'm settling down in california for grad school; so far so good.  i enjoy doing stuff like snowboarding, hockey, muay thai, juggling, driving, biking, walking, reading...and okcupid? following einstein's advice and banking on some correlation: try not to become a man of success, but a man of value being really intense about whatever i think is worthwhile. i'm doing something they want to be doing.  ;) appreciation is a wonderful thing: it makes what is excellent in others belong to us as well. - voltaire friends: obviously. computers: what an amazing tool. sports: adrenaline is my drug of choice. free time: necessary for perspective.. jokes: nuff said. time outdoors: try it; you'll like it. math: see, i have six things! everything... it's a good approximation.  i have a strong tendency for the abstract, but all my grad school friends say i'm too practical for the ph.d. figure that out. if it's deemed an adventure, you must venture. most people can't keep up with me. you want to defy the trend that most interactions on here are started by guys.  you typically say yes to the question: "hey, want to go do x?"  you are a wholehearted, vulnerable person, and want to meet someone who embodies those qualities: http://www.ted.com/talks/brene_brown_on_vulnerability.html  why not?

full_bio: i am a transplant from the east coast. lived in sf for the better part of lat year, traveled abroad for a bit and came right back 'cause i just can't get enough of this place. i grew up in maine, went to school in boston, and haven't stayed put since. i'm easy-going, love to laugh more than anything else, appreciate a sharp wit and great sense of humor. i'm most content when i'm exploring and experiencing new cultures, people, food, etc. in love with the outdoors, particularly hiking and kayaking. but i do relish the occasional rainy day for the excuse to go a whole day without changing out of your sweats. i generally prefer dive bars to dance clubs, but i'll bust a move any chance i get. i'm big on food and quoting funny movies. basically, i'm looking to have as much fun as possible anywhere i go.. "i'm doin' what i do, the way i've always done it, and the way i'll always do it."  if you're familiar with the quote, i already like you. i'm currently working in the bay area as a traveling physical therapist. loving san fran for now but will eventually move on since there are way too many places i need to experience! unfortunately, my constant relocation provides for a small social circle; so, i'm looking to meet some cool people to experience this great city with! baking. crossword puzzles and word games. relaxing. laughing at myself (and others, honestly). gift giving. recounting obscure details. whistling. people watching. toasting marshmallows. making lists. my laugh. both quality and quantity of it. more so quantity, haha. my all time favorite show is seinfeld. in any given day i will find myself saying, "it's like that episode of seinfeld.." that's probably why it's so damn funny. i also really enjoy 30 rock, arrested development, archer aaand glee. guilty pleasure. i'm a big movie watcher; love myself a funny indie flick or cult film, but open to all genres. among my favorites are "the princess bride," "across the universe," "dazed and confused, " and wes anderson anything. as for music, it's mostly rock, but i like a pretty mixed bag. the beatles are my faaavorite. currently also at the top are the black keys, phoenix, kings of leon, death cab, pete yorn. i can't even start in on books..too many. historical fictions are good..shogun, for example. and food...anything and everything! food, in general, is my favorite. family&friends. passport. camera. vacations. laughter. sunshine. snuck an extra in there, but friends are basically family. the ones that count, anyway. my next meal. my next destination. either out on the town with friends, or keeping my roommate (who has to work saturdays) company on the couch watching movies.  you'd like to.

full_bio: good humored, well traveled, and for better or worse, very much employed. non-profit attorney, environmental-style. like erin brokovich, but on a bike.  i believe in lefty causes and social justice and try to return the favor at least a little, helping old ladies use the card reader on muni and doing the compost in the community garden.  i do not begrudge the day-to-day when we live in one of the best places on earth. but i am also a fan of independent travel. last was colombia and a mix of planes, buses, hostels on distant beaches, second cities, random towns and deep-fried street food. but after umpteen solo adventures, and how many solo waterfalls, i could use some good company. asking questions. seeing forest and trees. cultivating a healthy sense of the absurd. fixing my 1973 brown british 10 speed bike into barely working condition, dreaming of the day when i will have a big, shiny, rusty, probably identical brown 10 speed british bike that works a tiny bit better, gardening/compost, indie shows on a budget, getting out and about in this amazing city, leaving this amazing city for hikes/trips, progressive politics, history, pie.  i am so grateful for this awesome life in sf and love my neighborhood. it feels like i'm eating my cake and having it too. i possess an amazing ability to mix metaphors.  i play tennis but it's hard to find anyone cool to hit with who's not terrible but not a 5.0 either. any takers?  favorite book ever is confederacy of dunces, followed by blood meridian by cormac mccarthy and master and margarita by mikhail bulgakov. three nearly-finished rushdie books sit forlornly next to my mother's frayed clothbound copy of the sound and the fury that i must have read four times. i re-read favorite books. just finished 1493, a book about spanish trade and bolivian silver and african malaria and peruvian potates in china and ireland and us. it was fascinating.  fav films: the shining, zombie, dolemite, the party, miller's crossing, and almost everything herzog and haneke. i loved the last episode of breaking bad and that the title was "face off."  this will get me no dates, but lately it's been old modest mouse b-sides. guilty. also: atlas sound, the antlers, die antwoort, one old kate bush album that i am probably the only straight guy that likes.  classics: modest mouse, lonesome crowded west. wire, pink flag. television, marquee moon.  food: i cook well and i make damn good coffee. and pies. and pie crust.  if you are a coffee lover but have not been to henry's house of coffee, then you are missing out on some fine beans. 1 lemon, 1 clove garlic, 1 egg (or substitute olive oil), dash worcestershire sauce, 1 mashed anchovy, 1 tsp dijon mustard, 1/2 c. parmesan cheese, salt and pepper to taste. what am i?   i think keyboard cat is brilliant. you're wildly curious and awesome.

full_bio: quote: life isn't about how many breaths you take; it's about the times you have your breath taken away. that kind of sums it up.  just a little more context about myself:  1) born and raised in singapore 2) picked up tennis when i was in my late teens; i've been in love with the sport since. 3) ain't actively looking for anything too serious to start things with; one step at a time and we'll see where the friendship take us both. 4) lived in asia, europe and the us; summer of 2009 in europe was amazing! 5) enjoys a good read or a movie anytime i get paid to travel to client sites on weekdays; a la consultant. the fancy hotels and restaurants aside, i'd much rather pay to travel for leisure. parallel parking stock picking (i like to think so) driving. p.s: i don't like to be a passenger. a contagious laugh asian (hard to miss that) food lover food - laksa, nasi lemak, chicken curry, pho, shabu shabu, hainanese chicken rice, escargot, caramel machiato from starbucks and so much more....  books: how to win friends and influence people, the big short, when genius failed. freakonmics, angels and demons, the bible.  movies: christopher nolan films come to mind. spielberg's pretty good too. no strings attached, friends with benefits, love actually, a beautiful mind, i am legend.  shows: notable mentions include: grey's anatomy, entourage, the walking dead.  up's probably my favorite animation movie (i get teary while watching it). what's yours?  music - bob marley (three little birds), corrinne may, taylor swift, lady gaga, jay chou, leo ku, wang lee hom, jj lin, stefanie sun in no particular order -  foooood family friends sleep music phone (soon to be lumia)  1) out with close friends enjoying a lovely meal in the city. 2) winding down while watching a feel good movie. horror movies? no thanks. :) 3) winding down the car windows and soaking up the fresh air at the likes of sausilito beach. trying a space cake when i was in amsterdam. woot~ you want to....  1) explore the city of san francisco with someone who can share a good laugh together.  2) loves food and the sun (you get brownie points if you're into asian food).  3) appreciate the abundance of nature around the city and the willingness to explore them  4) most important one - create happy memories and experience moments that will take your breath away.

full_bio: bike rider, book reader, san franciscan. sailor by trade. i'm not as serious as i may come off at times, i'm might just be observing before i speak my mind. while here...the okcupid forum(click here). riding my bicycle for hours on end. i don't know what people notice, but they sure do stare a lot. i don't dress to stand out. i just don't get it. sometimes it's the bicycle i'm riding, that's for sure. bukowski is alright. palahniuk is an okay read, burroughs is complex at times. books i've thoroughly enjoyed: tropic of cancer this perfect day 1984 the first three anne rice vampire novels(all of 'em really, no matter how shitty the story was told the premise held my attention) lost souls romo my life on the edge: living dreams and slaying dragons the black hand: the bloody rise and redemption of "boxer" enriquez, a mexican mob killer and many more i've forgotten.  movies: yeah, once in a while. shows: south park, news radio, daria, sledge hammer, married with children  music: motorhead, hope sandoval, los tigres del norte, slayer, vicente fernandez, murder city devils, cocteau twins, the drive-by truckers, ugly kid joe, megadeth, some goth, southern rock, country and everything in between.  favorite foods: tamales, spumoni ice cream, chow fun, cheeseburgers, pizza, tacos, sushi, pinto beans, borshch and folgers coffee.  lemme insert something about sports here. the only professional sport i've kept up with over the past 18 years is mountain biking. this has waned ever since tara llanes broke her back and became a paraplegic. bicycle sweater reading material laptop and two other things. everything, whatever it may be that piques my interest will be studied thoroughly. more often than not these things will in no way be a part of my life. what is to be said of this; when i have an opinion about something it isn't uninformed. listening to lemmy:  apparently people don't like the truth, but i do like it; i like it because it upsets a lot of people. if you show them enough times that their arguments are bullshit, then maybe just once, one of them will say, 'oh! wait a minute - i was wrong.' i live for that happening. rare, i assure you.  "it really bothers me when i hear myself being called the one who started all this thrash shit. i think they're trying to make it a compliment, but considering some of the music i'm hearing, i don't know if it is. motorhead never set out to start a trend - we were there to go against all the trends. the fact that we might have started something like that makes me feel a little scared."  people don't read any more. it's a sad state of affairs. reading's the only thing that allows you to use your imagination. when you watch films it's someone else's vision, isn't it?  you want a new friend, i know i could go for one. i'm pretty easy to talk to. people confess and confide many things to which i... feel privileged that i'm trusted so much. you don't fall in love too easily.

full_bio: bjrn seeks wood nymph in the melodrama by the finnish composer jean sibelius, the handsome young bjrn is enticed into the forest by the black elves. he goes willingly, bravely, but with trepidation as the elves spin a web of moon beams about him. deep in the forest he encounters the wood nymph. he is enchanted by her beauty and she steels his heart. but the elves have played a trick on bjrn. she belongs to the forest and cannot leave and the wood nymph, once she steels your heart, she does not give it back. bjrn longs for the wood nymph, cannot love a wife and as the years look in through the door he lives out his days in loneliness.  i'm a writer, photographer and design professional. writing is somewhat new for me and as a former c student in english, not something i ever expected to take an interest in. but my first book is almost finished and a second is in the works. i am also an accomplished fine art photographer, my work is in galleries and has been exhibited and collected.  i'm a straight forward, no nonsense, but relaxed and down-to-earth kind of guy. i'm currently single and unattached and don't bring any unwanted baggage. i'm a very creative, visual person, who is in touch with the world around him. photography is the great passion in my life, but i am also interested in other visual arts and almost anything else that moves or that involves making or fixing things. i work out regularly, not because i enjoy it, it is something of a chore, but because it keeps me feeling young and vital. and, yes, the pictures are current, well now a couple of years old.  with the right person, i can be spontaneous, funny, witty and full of interesting stories, it just depends. i can be laid-back or outspoken, talkative or quiet, depending on who i'm around and how they inspire me, but on my own i tend to be serious and intense as i'm passionate about the things i do. i have been told by people with an appreciation for such things, that i am someone who knows who he is. recently i was told that i am stable, sensitive and observant and a couple of recent dates told me that my eyes are very expressive. i'm not above putting a little spin on some of what i say, or of playing my cards close to my chest, i am a scorpio, but what i do say you can count on, i just don't know how to do otherwise. i've also been accused of being someone who knows everything, but i know that that isn't true.  i have a spiritual side, i've always pursued learning and personal growth although not through any particular school or philosophy. i understand something of the architecture of the mind. i'm an estj, but in recent years have learned to live as an extraverted intuitive and become much more outgoing and social and with that live is lived more fully. but as an extroverted thinker, my intellect is my real strength.  i'm most comfortable in levis, birkenstocks and an old flannel shirt, but can be just as at home in a brooks brothers suit. what i really want in a women is someone that i can share my passions with and someone whom i can be passionate about. i like dining out, trips to the country, a day at the beach, a secluded camp site, an evening at the symphony, all those wonderful things but best of all time spent with that certain someone special wether at home or . . .  i'm not hard to get along with. although i don't have any of my own, i have a way kids, perhaps because in some ways i've never grown up. or perhaps its because i tend to put myself in someone else's shoes and understand where they are coming from. i also have a soft spot for animals that purr and those that wag their tails. i used to have a big black lab who was just the greatest.  i am fairly tall and of scandianian extraction. i have been working out regularly for over 15 years and am quite strong. i have been told that i look much younger than my years, that i don't have that slow heavy look common with guys my age. some gals have even told me that i'm handsome.  up close and personal, women have told me that i'm a lot sexier than they expected or that you're very sexual. i've heard this more than once, so perhaps there's something to it. it's not something i try to project, my personal identity is based on other things, but beneath the surface . . . there's only one way for you to find out. i see from reading profiles that some of you gals really like to kiss, well the ladies volunteer to me that i'm a great kisser, deep wet kisses being a specialty. (i don't know how they are comparing me to.) why don't we see more people kissing in public? let's set an example. see above see above how do i know i'm always working on a book, usually history or biography, some of my favorites have been boyd, the wise men, target tokyo, the killer angels, the day books of edward weston, requiem for steam. does burning man count as a show? music, i'm a dead head and a sibelius junky. food, most all, but i love sea food, asian fusion and a really good tender steak is hard to beat. i have to think about this a bit, my tools, my cameras, my computer, those things are all extensions of myself. i've been known to live out of the truck of my car on the road, so i could do without a lot of things. but who wants to live with just the things one can not do without. projects i'm working on, the words i'm planning to write, not photography or design they're non-verbal.  recent projects have included the complete remodeling of my bathroom, with skylights, and my front yard. i did the room with a combination of victorian, arts & crafts and contemporary materials with all the metal fixtures in polished brass. colors have been sunny tans & other earth tones plus glass greens, perfect with the skylights and brass fittings. to that i've added some house plants and art works.  the front yard has brick & tile paving, and almost all new plants, the garden parts getting me started on gardening as a new and very active hobby.  currently i'm tuning the attic into a storage space, next the hallway, then the master bedroom and finally the big project, the kitchen. sometimes taking dancing lessons, sometimes at the gym, sometimes at singles events, sometimes on a date, sometimes attending a lecture, sometimes at home watching the news hour, washington week, and charlie rose. by definition, if i admitted it, it wouldn't be private now would it. you are the complete package. someone i can talk to as well as ... as well as someone that i would want to watch walking around the house, or gaze at across the breakfast table. someone who inspires me to pay them complements. someone who inspires me to be affectionate. could that be you? i'm very physically affectionate, i love to kiss and make out for hours and you?  wit, charm and wisdom are much appreciated. i tend to be down to earth and like that in a women. over breakfast, i'd love to be able to discuss art, history, politics, even psychology, whatever we have in common.  i am looking for that special person, the one who always has something fresh and original to say, who is full of surprises, who can expand my life and i hers. for that, my partner needs to be someone who has a brain, is well-educated and intelligent, can hold a conversation, and has culture and class, but is also well grounded and down to earth. having a creative side would be a definite plus. a women who knows how to laugh and smile.  so if you would like some stimulating and informed conversation, some admiring glances, some long wet passionate kisses . . . if you'd like to hear that sibelius melodrama (it has piano trills that send chills up my spine), well if you read this after an email from me, then there is a little button down below that says reply.  i'm really looking for that soul mate/long term relationship, but in the meantime, some fun and adventure is nice too.  please, no photo, no response and canned messages don't mean much.  p.s. in my book, you get extra points if you like to drive a car with a stick shift. if you do, i just know we'll have a lot in common.

full_bio: i grew up on the east coast, but now that i've made it to the bay area i'm never leaving. i'm really into food (cooking and eating), exploring sf and the area, finding adventure, and doing anything that's a challenge. travel has been a huge part of my life and i plan to keep it that way.  i spend a lot of time with friends, fluctuating between low-key hanging and instigating trouble. i worked for a few years after college both in the us and in developing countries, and have since started a masters program. i'm in the process of figuring out my next steps before i finish school in another year. making sandwiches i don't take anything too seriously. reading: dave eggers, kurt vonnegut, anthony bourdain, lucky peach, ft.com, sometimes fast company movies & shows: caddyshack, arrested development music: karaoke and live shows food: everything, particularly asian - chinese delivery - a good sandwich - live music - time to space out once in a while - the great outdoors - cooking and baking my next meal there's nothing i love more than a good dinner party, but my weeks usually vary a lot.

full_bio: i am a mid-western kid who fell in love with northern california (just like all the rest). i'm a reformed vegetarian and i root for the underdog. i like to try new things, but also love walking into a neighborhood joint and being served "the usual" without asking. i'm into talking politics, taking photographs, drinking beers with friends, seeing movies, reading books, going to events, and spending time outdoors.  i am witty, charming, and political. ok, i got "witty" from my friends, but i once when i had to have a second opinion, the doctor referred to me in his report as "extremely pleasant." i work at a community clinic in san francisco for the uninsured. it's one of those jobs where no matter how crappy my day, i come home knowing that i help make the world one (at times infinitesimal) step better.  i also spend my time swimming (i'm on a swim and a synchronized swimming team), hanging with my family & friends, and checking out flea markets. finding humor in the situation taking photos remembering people's names spontaneous toasts appreciating puns organizing things telling stories finding cool things at flea markets nerding out over politics, books, and weird art projects navigation remembering random facts mishearing song lyrics trouble shooting problems my curly hair. although... a fair number of homeless people have commented on my smile. books: "my year of meats" by ruth ozeki; "sacred hunger" by barry unsworth; "the bone people" by keri hulme; "simila's sense of snow" by peter hoeg; "the white bone" by barbara gowdy; the dark matter trilogy by phillip pullman; "imagings of sand" by andre brink;
