full_bio: i like:  to wake up to the sunrise  make it back home in time for the same in reverse  to walk in beauty  to laugh, including at myself  watching others laugh  a good purpose in life  to live in harmony  to cultivate loyalty, belief, and faith  to stay calm, see it through the storm  to say 'thank you' with something in hand  to trust my intuition  to observe without judgement  to give my word only when i can live up to it  gratitude for all the difficult situations in life: 'out of suffering have emerged the strongest souls; the most massive characters are seared with scars.' khalil gibran  knowing that lovers, friends, adversaries - each one teaches me, each one reveals my shadow to the light  being financially independent... because i am  to not give up, it's better to fall flat on face fail finishing up a doctorate in clinical psychology as the next step on a lovely career trajectory.  i am happy at my current placement as a therapist at a non-profit, so happy in fact that i asked to stay a second year and they said yes.  i hope that my dissertation will eventually help to better fund culturally relevant treatment. -curiosity! i like to learn something new about myself and others every day. -through practice and good teaching i am a competent clinician. -i have a farming background, so fixing or building things makes me happy. thanks dad! -and i'm really bad at... statistics. top of my bad list... bad, bad, bad. my rambunctious laughter that gets carried away into snorting.  after aboot three sentences into a conversation that i have a canadian accent, well, because i'm a crazy winnipeger (just 3 hours north of fargo nd, where they can get creative with their wood-chippers). hmm, plenty to say here, ready to bust a move?  -nana mouskouri - quand tu chantes (and she's right, it does feel good to sing!) full_bio: i'm a long time bay area resident, happy to be in this liberal, foodie mecca. i've lived abroad, in central ca and in oregon and alaska, and this is a good spot to land. most of my friends and family are on the west coast, so i get to see them often. i enjoy hiking and kayaking pt reyes, bodega bay and moss landing, and sampling any number of the local restaurants. just tried the boiling crab for the first time, and it was well worth the trip to san jose. i work in corporate finance, responding to market calamities and such, and while it provides a living, can't say it's my passion. traveling to and exploring new cities and foreign countries is a lot more fun, and i like hobbies that give those trips a bit more meaning. i've volunteered in new orleans, tangoed into the early am in argentina and scuba'd in thailand. i'm planning a scuba trip to the bay islands for this summer, and another to the red sea over the winter holidays, and i'm hoping to complete my dive master training by february. exploring new areas, keeping myself busy, making new friends, being loyal, avoiding lousy restaurant meals, having adventures far from home. i've been told my big smile can blonde hair can be spotted from far off, and quick wit from up close. (any way to not come off as a complete narcissist with this question?) i majored in us history and french lit, so i love me some howard zinn and moliere. (anyone else see the similarities between la tartuffe and palin's influence on the gop??) i've been immersed in various studies lately to obtain financial licenses and accrediations, so my recent readings have focused on insurance, taxation and investments. otherwise i'm online catching up on current events and pop culture via dan savage, andrew sullivan and nick denton's sites. for tv shows, the office, 30 rock, modern family, always sunny, and american horror story. haven't seen too many movies lately, but i remember laughing embarassingly loudly at 21 jump st and bridesmaids. i listen to npr and this american life at work when possible. friends and loved ones my iphone/laptop internet access amazon delivery my kits (a brother-sister matched set) trader joe's (at this point he's my personal chef) what more i can do with my life , and how i can become involved in something bigger than myself. catching up with friends at dinner, over drinks or at whatever goldstar event landed in my inbox that week. i'm beginning to think my csa is ripping me off... you'd like to know more! i did while away a good number of commute hours answering a sh!t ton of questions, so browse those to get a sense of who i am.

full_bio: im a man deeply intimate with spirit. my heart is, more often than not, in the right place. i walk a strong, clear and grateful path that is more and more in tune with the natural world. and, im always up for a solid adventure.  my ideal life, that is a'comin, is living with my partner and kid, and a ton of other good friends in a high desert forest somewhere, with some adorable doggies, a teepee, a nightly fire drum circle with tons of art and music, love, laughter, innovation, good simple soups and the smell of sweet pine sap. but if not, let it be even better than im imagining.  my intention is to meet a sexy, solid, strong and kind man who radiates love and who sees me, gets me and wants me. he realizes that this life is an opportunity to play wildly, to soar valiantly, to sit quietly and surrender to this beautiful existence.  you feelin' me? lots............... z) screenwriting an upcoming blockbuster movie musical a) director a kickass school for teenagers aa) caring for my little scruffy dog b) practicing classical guitar c) horseback riding d) yoga yoga yoga e) supporting indigenous rights f) biking my thighs off around this hilly city g) eating for health: green juice, quinoa, kale! h) art!:: painting, drawing, typewriter poetry, art installations, ritual theatre, clothes making i) tarot reading j) plants! i have them and love them. k) rock climbing, tennis, running and swimming. l) cuddddddddling! m) meditating (this is moving on up the list) facilitating ritual.  oh, and making tea. as my flight attendant said on my recent trip to the airport:  "sir, excuse me, sir, i just had to tell you - you have an incredibly bright spirit."  she then handed me 10 bags of peanuts. books:  nature and the human soul the tao of healing return of the bird tribes wraeththu by storm constantine a new earth and the power of now by eckhart tolle the fionavar tapestry by guy gavriel kay talking with angels hua hu ching anything by terrance mckenna  movies:  game of thrones earth lord of the rings the dark crystal the talented mr ripley the never ending story baraka fantastic mr. fox do love me some fantasy.  food:  veg juice sauerkraut kambucha raw cacao mole burrito kale salad with almond butter (damn so good!) curry sushi  music:  ambient electronic (moskitoo, cepia, pasadilla, mum, proem, rj valeo) indigenous chants old old jazz (like 20s and 30s - ma rainey, benny goodman) crunchy electronica (to dance) sounds true podcasts 6. sweet touch  5. simple honesty  4. a huge renovated barn on land with friends with a fireplace surrounded by an epic forest and bright warm sun.  3. listening to old jazz on my bike  2. inspiration and art  1. silence and trees  0. laughing at myself gratitude. adventuring. i strongly prefer to sleep naked. if you can feel my vibe.  if you're solid in your life.  if you have integrity with your word.  if you cherish nature.  if you are playyyyyyyful.  if you are communicative.  if you are a gentle-man.  if you are sexy as fuck with a sense of style.

full_bio: i am always up for adventure. new experiences always hold the promise of a new perspective. generally, i will try almost anything once or twice. however, i have given up trying to like fish (although i have tried a lot of different kinds). i wish i liked fish....  i love music; writing, playing of listening. i enjoy musical collaboration. musical harmonies are exciting. i see musical talent every where, i just have to encourage people to believe that they have it. sometimes i get bored with myself and have to convince myself that i have talent as well. i have been teaching people how to write songs lately.  i have done a lot of traveling. i was a peace corps volunteer. i am fascinated by how people adapt to their environments and how people's experiences shape their perspectives. i love to see examples of ingenuity and creativity wherever i go (they are not hard to find). i also love trying local food wherever i go. i challenge anyone to find someone who has gone on more road trips than me.  i do tend to ask a lot of personal questions as i am genuinely interested in people's stories. i can learn a lot about myself through other people's stories.  for fun, i love home projects, fixing things, walking around and eating cheese. i am a dodger fan too.  i would love to have a dog twice a week. i always have a lot of projects going. i do take on all sorts hair-brained projects; starting companies, building stuff, and playing music. i have been exploring the balance between all these projects and personal fun time. how can i shut the logical side of my brain and just feel.  don't get me wrong, i love my projects, but sometimes i just need to stare at the stars knowing they are going to be here, long after i leave.  one of my jobs is working in the tech industry. although, i can be very technical, my interests are more centered around building teams, creating a positive working culture, and making sure people are communicating.  i also am involved in a non-profit which raises money and awareness for various charities and ideas that i feel are important.  i continue to pound away at various musical instruments, i have played in bands for years. i have been writing and recording mostly, these days. believing in the impossible. helping people feel comfortable and safe, while challenging them to take on new things. seeing the little things that make people extraordinary and trying to bring them to their attention. fixing things listening trying new things adventures making pancakes having random pieces of obtuse information. i generally ask a lot of questions - and love to hear the answers. i am a big music fan. some of my favorites include: the beatles, tom waits, wilco, uncle tupelo, voxtrot, the magnetic fields, elvis costello, billy bragg, the boss, ron sexsmith, nick lowe.  i am currently reading the george r.r. martin trilogy "game of thrones". i am a big vonnegut fan, as i like how he pokes fun at our contradictory belief structure. i have enjoyed a lot of tim o'brien stuff. i enjoyed reading "omnivores dilemma". i seem to go back and forth between fiction and non-fiction; books just seem to find me and create a story of their own. i love reading historical stuff too. i travel a lot so i often read novels that take place where i will be traveling. i am currently reading a lot about explorers.  i love movies, although i haven't seen very many. i consider myself to have about a 3 grade outlook on them. everything seems to be new, as i am not very well versed in cinematic cliches.  i often fall asleep when i stay in one place too long. this is great for cuddling, but not very good for quoting lines from movies.  i love cheese!!!!! i love most international cuisines. cheese stringed instruments my family laughter adventure sun block finding balance.....  my life's path has taken me around the world many times, i have been on countless adventures, i am very spontaneous and i wouldn't trade any of my experiences.  how to reduce the amount of fear in the world. how do to create forums of discussion. what types of communities will be created to face the challenges of the 21st century.  how my life would have been different if i would have been a night owl.  my next adventure.....  the next song to write... having beers with friends, playing music, playing games, reading, sleeping, cooking, eating or traveling. i do enjoy star trek and buffy the vampire slayer (although i wouldn't have believed this 4 years ago). if you think kindness is something to be admired rather than a weakness. if the beatles make you smile. if you were in the peace corps. if you have traveled around the world. if you find yourself randomly singing for no reason of all. if you are able to see when you are taking yourself too seriously, and then start to laugh. if you have unique perspective. if you have a creative passion.

full_bio: i moved from boston to the south bay about two years ago, barely escaped with my soul, and now live happily in the mission. i'm into biking, live music, good food, the occasional blunder into the psuedo-wilderness, and anything else that keeps me sane despite my somewhat insane work schedule. in a former life i was a painter, world traveller, dj, musician, potter, dancer and day-dreamer. i'm trying to keep those parts of me alive, but mostly right now i'm focused on the hustle. i'm starting my own company, like any self-respecting geek in the current tech bubble. i'm in the widget business. i make widgets that other people can use to make bigger widgets. it would be boring if i didn't think widgets were so fascinating.  other than that, plotting my next trip to lyon, france. being conspicuously out of place. like that clueless guy in a studio full of professional jazz dancers, that's me. or the only white boy on a highschool step team, me. i was the lone nerd in an office full of navy seals. the only man in a knitting class.  i guess i like being different, it means there's a good chance i'll learn something. i guess the voice. i think people expect me to be older and bigger after talking to me on the phone. i also look young for my age. the overall effect is rather like that scene in spaceballs when rick moranis first takes off his helmet. i really enjoy classic scifi books like dune, arthur c clarke's work and the foundation series.  when it comes to movies, i like to get engrossed in-the-moment. i enjoy clever plot twists more than character studies. (although i loved the royal tenenbaums) i really enjoyed the brain-fight at the end of dark city.  music - justice, aphex twin, femi kuti, dre, trombone shorty and pretty much every live act i saw from '02-'03 on frenchmen street. my friends and fam-- even though i don't spend enough time with them. when they're not around everything just stops. after having moved about 12 times in my life, i know what that's like.  my macbook and iphone 3gs (video camera, very important).  my bike.  um, that's it. ice cream.  i used to spend a lot of time figuring out what to do with my life. i think i'm over that now. hopefully trekking around the city for some simple but well seasoned fare, good dark beer, live music or dancing, and a bit of the unexpected. i've got a thing for chickens. i think they're cute. i try not to eat them. you're ambitious but also curious. you notice the little things and have a sense of adventure. you have a cashmere sweater. you would enjoy getting caught in a snowball fight, but wouldn't escalate it into a serious competition. you're not a big tv person, but love curling up for a good movie at home.  you're happy, trusting and you believe that humans are generally good.

full_bio: i am a transplant from a super-liberal, tiny town of 8,000 where the whole town knows their chavasana from their uttapanasana. being in san francisco, the high number of yoga classes per capita has not changed, but i now have the excitement of being a small town girl in a crazy skyscraper-filled city. saving the world....from unnecessary interventional cardiology procedures. i am a product manager for a startup company that helps doctors better diagnose coronary artery disease.  i really want to retire early somewhere cheap abroad, not necessarily be super-rich. just live in a hut on the beach in uruguay, or a little flat in a chill town in panama or argentina. paint, do photography, play guitar, maybe do some part time english teaching and get out of the corporate grind. - getting jiggy wit it, salsa, ballet, tango, lindy or random freeform goofiness. -cooking vegetarian food -scaring away perfectly suitable men with weird dance moves and steak-less food i'm a minimalist. i like electricity, bathe regularly and am not writing this by candlelight from my grass hut (just because i couldn't find any to rent in soma), but... i purposely don't own a tv or have netflix i try not to accumulate "stuff" books: 1984, animal farm, zen and the art of motorcycle maintenance, the art of happiness, how to practice (the dalai lama), a brave new world, fahrenheit 451, the scarlet letter, the omnivore's dilemma, einstein's dreams, wuthering heights, how to make friends and influence people movies & tv: a night at the roxbury, zoolander, inception, avatar, better off dead, kick-ass, arrested development, the dollhouse music: blues!, far east movement, kanye west, the cure, billie holiday, flight of the conchords, jack johnson, barbara morrison, bajofondo tango project food: yes please! healthy and vegetarian :) - dslr camera - i love photography and want to capture all the moments of life - a warm bed with lotsa pillows to sleep in - fruits & veggies - dancing shoes - acoustic guitar for jamming and bad karaoke - a passport -my next adventure -how to take over the world -happiness and why i should be content not taking over the world ...trying not to settle into the routine of having a typical friday. any suggestions? i'm a board game nerd. -you are interested in meeting in person at some point (not pen pals) -for practicality reasons, you live in san francisco (i live in soma) or in the peninsula somewhere (i work in redwood city).

full_bio: i'm a kind, intelligent, down-to-earth person who enjoys making people laugh, eating weird food, reading pretty much anything, hiking, traveling, dancing badly to 80s music, and learning new things. i have a wide range of interests and i'm especially attracted to people who can teach or expose me to new stuff or who can captivate my intellectual interest (and this doesn't mean that they have to be formally educated). the size of your heart will impress me infinitely more than the size of your wallet. i'm pretty laid back and enjoy things like hanging out in dive bars or sharing stories of my (mis-)adventures. i'll often laugh with you, and will always laugh at myself.  i grew up in northern california and love the bay area. i'm on this site because i have a hard time meeting guys outside my normal range of existence who i would want to date...sort of a been-there-done-that situation and they all seem the same at this point. i don't think there is anything "wrong" with being on this site and i think there are upsides and downsides to most of the typical venues for meeting people. i enjoy dating and hope for something longer-term, but i'm not in any rush. job-wise, i work for a federal court in downtown san francisco, and what i actually do is hard to explain and you probably still won't understand, so please please don't ask me to explain over email. i'm not a lawyer and i can't help you with your traffic tickets (wrong court system honey). talking too loudly about inappropriate* things in public and being way too open and honest about myself. and my job. and making people laugh.  *as defined by other people ;) my eyes...hey, my eyes are up here! books: isaac asimov (the robot series and the foundation trilogy); jane austen; angela carter (especially wise children and the bloody chamber); irvine welsh (especially trainspotting, marabou stork nightmares and the acid house); stephen hawking's a brief history of time; beowulf (seamus heaney translation); atom.  movies: lock, stock, and two smoking barrels; the thirteenth warrior; pulp fiction; jackie brown; fight club; alien; trainspotting; pride and prejudice (1996 bbc version); donnie darko; spaceballs; silence of the lambs; leon (aka the professional); history of the world part 1; pitch black; contact; sense and sensibility (ang lee's beauty); transformers; hebrew hammer; the producers (and pretty much anything by mel brooks); quills; shaun of the dead (and hot fuzz is pretty great too); night watch (are they ever going to make the 3rd one??). this list is not nearly complete, but you get a general idea.  television: i don't actually watch the idiot box (i'm a netflix kind of girl), but i like arrested development, x-files, firefly, family guy, it's always sunny in philadelphia, modern family, parks and recreation, the tudors.  music: lately i've been into indie pop. i like rock in general and punk/ska will always remain close to my heart. i enjoy vampire weekend, death cab for cutie, the blow, yeah yeah yeahs, streetlight manifesto, operation ivy, link 80, leftover crack, misfits, star fucking hipsters, fratellis, mad caddies, rancid, arctic monkeys, and the list goes on.  food: i'm pretty easy to please but my favorites are probably sushi, lamb, duck, melted cheese in general, especially grilled cheese sandwiches, sweet potato fries. i try to eat healthy when i cook for myself. my least favorite is thai. in no particular order: bad action movies (especially sci-fi), good action movies, books, showers, sex, parentheses.  grabbing dinner and beers at a pub with my friends. i have no shame about eating foods way beyond their expiration dates. you're the kind of person who reads my profile before sending me a message. bonus points if you are interesting and funny and even more bonus points if you laugh at my jokes.  if you want the kind of woman who: -will laugh at your that's-what-she-said jokes, and top them -can give you your space when you need it -isn't afraid to get her hands dirty (as long as she can wash them afterwards) -has her own life but will make a place in it for you -won't sweat the little stuff  fyi, i don't have a lot of time for emailing, especially during the work week, so i might take a while to reply.

full_bio: people say that i'm a great friend - compassate and loving. i'm a proud mother of a 33 year old daughter who lives in roseville.  i've been retired for 4 years after 45 years in the personal insurance industry.  i love being in nature, however roughing it not for me. some of my favorite places are: waters: - half moon bay area where i can take the dogs to the beach. carribean, crusing on that magnificant blue water. being next to a river or stream... fresh air and the soothing sound of the water. lake tahoe, monterey/carmel/mendocino, marin coast line to name a few mountains: yosemite, lake tahoe, california coastal redwoods.. not much into the desert, however i would love to see it during the spring wildflower shows.  i was born and raised in the bay area and consider myself blessed.... it's such a wonderous place to be... in a few hours we can be to some of the most beautiful country in the world... (at least that i know of).  for me, things don't always need to be planned to "a gnat's eyebrow" as they say.. in fact i do prefer for their to be some basic structure but then the rest is up to what's in store - i'm free to decide to turn right instead of left to see where the road goes... adventurous to a point... but not a real risk taker.  i'm looking for someone who would be a great friend amd companion but who would still have some of their own interests...  animals are an important part of my life. i currently have two dogs and see an animal or two in my life forever...  i am spiritual by nature and attend the unity church. taking classes - watercolor and at church being a good friend. my blue eyes and great smile favorite show - les meserible... the music is just beautiful music - love john denver, james taylor, enya, yo yo ma, garth brooks... a very eclectic appitite movies - romance, documentary, animals, pixar - almost any kind except for violent or very noisy ones. food - favorites: chinese, mexican, fish and chips, macroni & cheese (especially mine).. don't like mushrooms. books - all kinds, however i haven't been doing much reading lately.. just been to busy. my grown daughter. my dogs nature my spiritual beliefs my friends a good time how things change and evolve... you never know what's around the next corner... i say "you learn something new every day... some days you just don't know what that is". doing nothing special.... since i'm retired i can do what i want at any time. meeting friends, watercoloring, driving up to see my daughter.  you find this interesting and waht to learn more.

full_bio: hmmmm.......how does one summarize ones self? ok, so all these questions are going to take some time. who has all this damn time to fill out all these damn online thingies anyways? i wish i knew... well, i feel like i am trying to keep moving forward...because the only way out is through. the real challenge is making the time, mustering the self discipline, to work on those things that call to your deeper self. but in the meantime, i'm busy as hell trying to raise 2 boys, run a small business by myself, and take care of my mind, body, and spirit. almost every thing i try. not finishing things i've started. collecting junk doing drugs and not dying not doing drugs and living sleeping lifting heavy objects avoiding jury duty i have never asked anyone what the first thing they noticed about me was..... i have never liked picking favorites. sure, there are songs that i love more than others, but i just can't be bothered trying to list them all. i will say i have been amazed and delighted at the number of folks on here who have taken the time to list every last fucking song/book/movie/whathaveyou that they have ever liked! i mean, did they already have all that written down somewhere or what? basically i like a lot of everything, so long as its good. i don't care for crappy novels, shitty movies, crappy pop music, processed, frozen, fake food crap, etc.. jeez, all these lists...i hate these lists. um...motorcycles, i really like music, .....i am so bad at lists. i guess i'm feeling like i should be able to live without anything because i have too much stuff....am i just taking these questions way too seriously??? the nature of the universe and the origin of all life. why throughout history, the forces of "good", freedom, liberty, etc., have always had to fight to get out from under the boot of oppression? in other words, why are the people with all the power always assholes? is it true then that power always corrupts? if so, why? how and why societies/cultures/nations start/grow/peak/wither/die, and where/when are we in that cycle. for we are certainly in one of those stages.... and, how to make more money while actually working less, and, how to have better sex more often. not necessarily in that order.  i don't wear underwear....unless they are womens. you want to see me dance in high heels.

full_bio: i moved to sf bay area from sweden after i got a great job here.  don't know a lot of people here but i'd love to get to know more, and if i happen to find something more, the better. =)  i love watching movies, drink beer and laugh. laughter is pure essence of life and if you won't laugh with me a butterfly will get his/her/its feelings hurt so you'd better get at it. those sad eyes isn't my doing.  i can get pretty enthusiastic about my hobbies so i sure hope you are open-minded. i'm a software architect and gadget lover.  i have periods where i read a lot. i'm a fast and enthusiastic reader and is always dying to tell anyone who will listen about the cool new books i've found.  i am funny, laid back, and intelligent. i usually say that i don't work that much, but i guess its just a lie i tell myself, hoping that it will become true. at least i've been very successful in what i'm doing. i want to spend more time hiking, climbing and hanging out in bars with friends.  when i lived in northern sweden, i used to go on camping/fishing trips all summers and thats something thing i'd love to get back into. getting people to laugh with me and my silly jokes about life, the universe and everything. anything technical speaks to me. my smile, eyes and calm. sometimes i get bubbly, and that will overshadow everything! authors: douglas adams, terry pratchett, neil gaiman, george r r martin, steven erikson, dean koontz and robert zubrin among many i've forgotten to mention.  when it comes to music i listen to almost any music, but rock/alternative is probably a good middle ground. i used to be into metal and iron maiden & metallica is the coolest bands i've seen live to date. my books (its really my kindle now), funny action movies, friends, beer, coffee and amazon. i think a lot. in sweden we call it grubblare, but i'm not sure exactly how to translate it.  one of my favorite cafes in sweden overlooks a train station so you can see all kinds of people there. i love to watch people and then try to figure out what they are doing.  sometimes i try to figure out how things could become as broken as they are. i.e. economy, politics and so on.  enough brain dumping for now, it might start to smell. at a bar, teaching people how to pronounce skl, the swedish version of cheers, or just at home watching some funny movie. hopefully, wherever i am, i'll have plenty of cool people around me =)  .. you are interesting to talk to

full_bio: i have: neat handwriting. good posture. lots of books. i tend to: over-think things. spill on myself. talk a lot when i'm nervous. i almost never: get truly angry. break a promise. forget a face. i nearly always: laugh at bad puns. want to hike longer. will have another beer!  i'm a happy and thoughtful person who has a pretty low threshold for bullshit. this means i am pretty upfront with people, and it's easy to tell how i'm feeling. besides that, i like making inappropriate jokes at generally inopportune times, but then am charming so everyone feels at ease with my occasional awkwardness.  my friends know me as funny, honest and supportive. i have a few really really close people in my life, but i also like cultivating a wide group of friends.  i was born and raised in san francisco and am still deeply in love with the bay area. other places are super great too, and i like going to visit them, but this place.... it's my home. work hard, play hard. the food industry has a way of pushing you to extremes: if you're meant for it, you run with it... i work in a small kitchen, sweating and swearing. nothing feels better than knowing i help make really great food that people love.  besides that: yoga, bike rides, cooking at home, and laughing too loud at bars. saying how it is. i believe in honesty above most other things.  crossword puzzles, fried egg sandwiches, high fives. i'm a sassy motherfucker. my bookshelf looks like: murakami/kerouac/mccarthy/kesey/robbins and then bauman, berman & other nerdy sociology books.  my playlist of the moment looks like: fleet foxes/blonde redhead/arcade fire/coltrane/brian jonestown massacre/local natives & animal collective  movies: my favorite movie is rushmore.  food: salty > sweet. - eggs - something to read - a pen/moleskine - sleep - beer - sex    you make people laugh without trying. jokes both good and pretty terrible count. you read books. regularly. and like to talk about them. you're creative. this is open to interpretation. you have a green thumb. you like beer. the good stuff.

full_bio: i'm a california girl through and through. i love the sunshine and being outdoors. though i chase the shade like a madman; if i get too much uva/uvb i'll burst into flames. i'm always down to snowboard, wind surf, wake board, hike, swim, climb trees...whatever. i really like music but don't go to enough shows. i like a variety of music, everything from underground hip hop to heavy metal (but no country). i love animals...killing spiders makes me feel guilty...but i really don't like them and sometimes that fear reflex of smashing them to bits takes over. eep! going to school. i'm working on my masters in nursing, emphasis in pediatrics...once i get this degree i'll be a pediatric clinical nurse specialist. i spend a lot of time studying...but i suppose that is what you have to do if you want to be the best of the best. when i'm not reading i like to hike, windsurf, snowboard, listen to music, go to concerts or take some time to just chill. though it seems like my time is pretty well split between books and fun. snowboarding knitting writing talking to people taking care of animals probably my hair or that i smile a lot. as far as books go i really like the power of one and anything by chuck palahniuk. on the less specific side i love vampire novels and autobiographies (i really like human nature). i really like comedies and horror films. the hangover is probably one of the funniest movies to be released in a long time. rob zombie is my favorite horror director at the moment (he has an eye for gore, and white zombie is one of my favorite bands). i am all about music...i used to work for roadrunner records, which is a heavy metal label touting names like slipknot and ill nino. so, i am obviously into metal, but i like pretty much everything (minus country). my top two favorite bands are tool and nine inch nails. but outside of the metal world i really like amy winehouse, d12, the doors, billy idol...a wide variety, really. i'm willing to try (almost) anything at least once, but i really like mexican and italian. 1. music 2. books 3. yarn 4. animals 5. education 6. friends/family everything and nothing. i like to daydream. i have an imagination that could rival a five year old's. probably with friends or hanging out at home. i don't go "out" much...i would rather go to a concert than to a club. i like to dance, but i always seem to have something more outdoorsy to do the next day that interests me much more. i like cheese. if you are interesting, can hold an intelligent conversation, and like cheese.

full_bio: to quote high fidelity "it's not what you are like, it's what you like".  i stand out in a crowd, even in this city. i like my metal combined with marachi and i wear my berets with spats. i felt like lindsay lohan when my fake fur jacket was stolen. my hair is probably longer than yours. i love obscure records, and have all of mr rogers recordings, and when he says "won't you be my neighbor, he really means to be my friend." when the zombie apocalypse happens i am ready.  after 9 years in santa monica, i'm relatively new to the bay area, so it's all a process of discovery. as a very social person i thrive in urban areas, and love to explore the best this city has to offer- the more obscure, unique, and bizarre the better. looking for someone to have adventures with, romance, and lots of kisses. have existed as an self-employed independent manufacturers representative specializing in flexible packaging for longer than i care to admit, while its been good perhaps its time for something else, had to relocate to honestly re-evaluate my next move. the bay area is the largest concentration of people taking risks to create the new economy that is our only hope. i already am blessed to have a number of friends here, so the transition was not going to be totally starting over, but in a lot of ways it is. my favorite quote of the moment is, "if you don't run your life someone else will." executing plans talking to random strangers accessorizing quad skating mikey's hug deli burning man watching english premiere team arsenal, preferably in a pub and two sports... cocktails and firearms. disclaimer: if you don't think drinking is a sport then we should not be speaking. my deep voice, my cartoon like face, winning looks, charisma, and rapunzel-esque hair. unless i'm driving, then its my 1974 thing. for film, anything playing at the cinefamily in los angeles. it's not just about the films, but the experience at this theater. i had been a black card member there for years.  my music tastes are quite diverse. bands i like include: man or astro man?, the nutty jazz, metalachi, mariachi el bronx, janet klein and the parlor boys, nouvelle vague hollywood mon amour, fitz & the tantrums, mickey avalon, hypercrush, duran duran, james wilsey, jimbo matthus, love and rockets, pixies, sonic youth, the stone roses, satan's pilgrims, t-bone walker, professor longhair, lady smith black mambazo, noisettes, etc. genres include, all forms of house, jazz, blues, rockabilly, ska, punk hardcore, outlaw country, kitch and exotica, surf, and really interesting cover versions, such as bossa and stones, or the italian secret service lounge version of it smells like teen spirit. sunglasses and sunscreen cellular service caffeine good ethnic food live music pen and paper  going out voted "most likely to establish a religious cult in nebraska" by my high school graduating class, helped catch the btk killer, game show contestant, know huell houser personally, and i have been to band camp. you're up for something totally different

full_bio: i'm an east coast girl living the west coast dream and bring the sassy to your town. i live for good cocktails, great friends, good music, and occasionally travel where ever i want. i'm often found dancing where ever there is a beat and normally being forward and mouthy. i like this to be real. i dig a good adventure and trying new things and going to cool places (as in countries, hiking trails, casual cocktail establishment). i like to hang things from my ceiling. photography is my passion, i have a my ba in it and am currently rebuilding my portfolio. i recently participated in my first sf art show which i'm super proud of. i believe in meaningful relationships with everyone (friends, fam, who ever). i like people who aren't afraid to get real and be honest. i've been to 11 different countries and my next trip is to india. i'm looking to find the experience rather than reading about it in a book. i enjoy being silly and spontanious. i hate people who flake. i'm just a very unique person. weird me facts- i hate being called sher (that is not my full name) i want to live in a tree house that resembles the swiss family robinson house i can be caught drinking whiskey or champagne, occasionally bloody marys i went to private school for 7 years and hated it but appreciate the education i received long drives are a favorite past time and often i wish i could just jump in my car and take off i miss fireflys  i am sassy, classy, and trashy i have this fancy job as a waitress, no, no excuse me, a server at a brewery. but really i just want to dance. also i'm a contributer to an online magazine. and i'm having an art show! photography eating healthy finding fun things to do dancing being super weird laughing and being sassy being conscience smelling really good- and i say that because people tell me eyelashes and titties- im just being honest books- the alchemis, go ask alice, the raven: the untold story of jim jones and the peoples temple, 5 people you meet in heaven, the life of pi (i'm a super slow reader, i hate skimming, but i eventually finish) i like magazines and photo books  movies- anchorman, role models, the last dragon, legend, harry potter, lord of the rings, hook, house of flying daggers, hero, kung fu hustle, live at pompeii, pans laberinth, alice in wonderland, goonies, crybaby (any john waters movie), dejerring limited, fast times at ridgemont high, thrashin. any skate video, beetle juice, rob zombie flicks, polterguist, so horror, comedy, all sorts of stuff tv- the office, csi, family guy, 30 rock, lost, glee, burn notice, law and order  music- i like a lot of music, i can't count how many shows i've been to or the number of bands. rough idea: misfits, pink floyd (#1), bob dylan, the animals, janis joplin, fleetwood mac, billy idol, sam cooke, stevie wonder, supertramp, the decemberists, santigold, metric, simon and garfunkle, hall and oates, metalica, sound garden, alice and chains, the smiths, bloodhound gang, outcast, jimmy cliff, totes and the maytels, bob marley, fleetfoxes, blonde, sioux and the banchess, the shins, radiohead, rolling stones, and many many more my medium dog sized cat mr. woo my camera friends photobooths my music water don't ask questions you don't want answers to. lets just make out just kidding. if i were a hat who's head would i be on and why why is fro yo so addictive where in the world can i go why is awd so popular in san fran and i don't mean all wheel drive how did i get here or where is my car parked what's next how silly people can be (and i'm putting it nicely) and how crazy it must have been to cross the country in a covered wagon probably working (hopefully working) then out and about. normally ends with me in some weird convo. with a creepy old man at the bar or dancing either or maybe both whoa whoa whoa, lets not get so serious you are awesome or you are a jedi and isn't afraid to dance

full_bio: i wear my emotions on my sleeves literally. i am who i am, and that is how i life my life. i am an open honest person that loves great conversation and a good meal if possible! good company is so important to me. good eye contact can make the difference between somebody that i want to hang out with and somebody that i will ignore. either way i give everybody a chance because life is short and full of opportunities to meet great people. i hope to meet you soon! after two years of teaching 1st grade in the san francisco bay area (my hometown), i am now teaching english in seoul, south korea, loving every day of my new life and experience. i love the idea that i am meeting new people every day and enjoying a new culture that was made possibly by some friendly suggestions and a positive risk. here i am! so many people in seoul and looking forward to getting to know you.... and now back in the bay area and there is nothing like it! communicating. which may be the single most important part of a relationship. after the initial attraction there must be constant and consistent communication. first they notice my height (6'5"), then they notice that i have tremendous confidence. not cockiness, but confidence. very important to distinguish the difference between the two. i love sports, and i am currently writing my own book about values that i feel are extremely important to any relationship that can be encountered. i love comedies and feel good movies that have a purpose. i love sports once again, but do not want to make that too much of a priority at the beginning! as far as food goes, just try me and i will be game:) family, family, family, food, water, and shelter. i really believe that having a bond with your family somehow, someway is the most important aspect of life. how i can help others and make them feel as happy about living in this beautiful life as i do. i live to help children and anybody that needs help. i feel like i can make a difference. relaxing, reflecting on the week and getting ready for my saturday night, because i need to relax on friday! is that i am afraid of roller coasters!!! you are somebody that enjoys good solid conversation. i am a real person with real values. i am dedicated to getting to know somebody for who they are. i want honesty and passion. that is all i ask for. if you have those things, i would love to hear from you soon!

for social justice, and still very pleased with it. other things i love:  paint, the secret history and uses of letters, clear nights for star viewing, sleeping on the roof, snorkeling, wigs, strange drag, resistance and surrender to sugar, resistance and surrender in general, plantlife, artlife, transformative justice, the happy end of the binary gender system, scarlet runner beans, almost anything eaten directly out of the garden. making things, reading things, looking at things. adoring dear friends. drawing goats. making golem costumes. getting my hands dirty. growing plants. appreciating. empathy, drawing, sex, making hungarian plum soup, collecting seeds, scavenging art supplies, spotting and picking blackberries, endurance, laughing at myself and being silly. my full_bio: born and raised in the east bay. escaped to los angeles during my college days. somehow the bay area kept echoing my name to return. i'm currently teaching in the neighborhood i grew up in. has a sense of twilight zone to it knowing that i'm a hop skip away from my childhood home, but i can't complain. i love being a positive role model in a small town community. possibly the closest i'll get to being a super hero.  making people laugh and smile. i  have a tendency to entertain those around me. there is no tale to tall for me to tell. this talent (or oddity to a few)  has allowed me to be very sociable in various settings known to man.  adults will say my eyes or smile. my students tell me my grey hair which to them automatically makes me old. a little salt and pepper never hurt anybody.   the art of saving the world   you need a laugh.

full_bio: two things i strive for are: 1) have as much fun as possible 2) try to leave the world slightly better than how i found it. so i try to enjoy life, be good to other people, and avoid the drama when i can.  i stay active by sparring at a kickboxing gym, which is both a great work out and really fun. i try to travel when i can - going abroad never leaves me unchanged in some way. i have been to norway and peru in the past few years, and i'm thinking about either going to austria or turkey next. at some point, i would love to take a road trip out east (when i can get my hands on an electric car.)  the things i value most are good friends, good conversation, and laughing at dumb jokes =d. architect by day, crafter by night. transitioning from drawing instructions for other people back to making things with my own hands. =d i'm currently learning metal machining and will probably try to learn some welding in the near future. i'm also kind of re-learning how to sew, but let's just say i wouldn't wear anything i make right now. procrastinating. haha... i'm only partly joking. i'm pretty good at figuring out how things work, how things are put together. some people also think i'm also good at being a friend and listening. oh, and also epic karaoke. well, i guess i'll ask someone and get back to you. =d music: (current favorites) milk carton kids, blind pilot, blitzen trapper, jukebox the ghost, dr. dog. not really music but i love radiolab and the moth podcasts. movie: the rage in placid lake show: big bang theory food: vegetarian books: (lately) anything written by malcolm gladwell my bicycle my sketch book a black fine point sharpie wool socks peanut butter jelly what does it mean to be a good person in a sincere way - and not, you know, just because i want other people to like me. throwing together a peach cobbler for friends. i can do the bar scene occasionally (to be more precise, i don't mind not drinking while watching my friends drink their ass off), but i really prefer having a chill evening with good conversation and good friends. if i've had a long week, nothing like hitting a karaoke bar to start the weekend. i love epic 80's love ballads. the cheesier the better. you're generally a happy guy. i'm not catherine, and i'm not looking for heathcliff.

full_bio: i have a job, come from a good family and have nice teeth - what else could you ask for? :) i am a san francisco native; born and raised. lived in los angeles for 7 years and found myself longing for the bay area so i moved home. my entire family still lives close by and i spend a lot of time with them. a girlfriend (a "friend who is a girl" kind) told me that i'm "well rounded" - i speak enough italian to get us into and out of trouble, know how to turn eggs, flour and water into crepes, bread and pasta, play sports, watch foreign films, keep up with current events, travel to foreign countries, etc.. or do you think she was trying to tell me i'm fat? hmmmph... i work in corporate real estate and have been with the same company for ~18 yrs (how did that happen?). i also spend quite a bit of time running in gg park and biking in the presidio (a recent re-discovery after a 25 yr hiatus!). * being reliable; your "go to guy" * quickly analyzing situations and finding the path to resolution * finding deals * googling (which helps with "finding deals") i know a little about a lot of things. i was once told that i'd be a good person to take along in the "cash cab." ok, i'll admit it, i don't do a whole lot of recreational reading unless it's food related. speaking of food - love it all. i especially love seeking out and trying foodie items or places (think hole in the wall, hidden jewels rather than michelin star establishments). and music? all over the map but recently discovered dengue fever - good on cd but great in concert and my current favorite band has to be greenday - their 3 hr concert at shoreline was amazing. favorite movie? singles. great music and takes place in one of my favorite cities. also prefer foreign and independent movies over hollywood blockbusters. apart from the cliche' family and friends, my pavoni espresso machine (seriously, it has a tiny leak but i can't bear to leave it with the repair guy for a week. i'll wait until it's *really* leaking), a patagonia jacket (functional/durable/well designed/well made). i'll have to give other "never do withouts" some thought. * does online dating really work? * what does "% enemy" mean on ok cupid? i mean, how can we be enemies? we don't even know each other. * how is it that ok cupid says my highest possible match is 99% yet when i search for the highest % match who has been online the last decade, the highest match is 97%? * why does everyone's profile sound overly witty and smartalecky?? is that how they are in real life? where are all the normal/down to earth types? * what other ways are there to meet potential suitors (because online dating works sooo well; not) * most importantly, where are you? having dinner with my parents. no, really, i try to see them once a week. it's really low key and they're more than happy if i skip dinner with them to be on a date with you! i suck at online dating; it's so contrived. i'm much better at getting to know someone over time and having shared experiences be the basis for a great relationship. there, i said it. i'm a firm believer in sharing common interests and attitudes. so if you think we have lots to talk about or would enjoy doing stuff together with the possibility of it developing into something incredible; you know where to find me! but, please don't message me if you don't intend to return my reply. seriously, why reach out to begin with if you don't intend to continue the conversation?

full_bio: i'm an independent, happy and adventurous girl with an intellectual and empathetic streak. i love to spend time with my friends and family sharing funny/silly stories, laughter and encouragement. i like to try new things, travel, be outdoors in the hills, eat and cook a variety of cuisines, soak in a hot tub/onsen, snuggle on the couch with a good movie and ice cream.  i can laugh and be giggly for no reason, especially if i'm nervous or excited. i'm very open minded and tend to be thoughtful about new/unusual things and experiences before making any judgement calls. some other things that i like to do are hiking/biking/camping, spend time with friends going to plays/theater, concerts, cook together (not much into cooking for myself).  i think happiness is meant to be shared with someone special and am looking for someone who has found balance in his life and is ready for a fun, new friendship.  and on the ethnicity - i'm pretty sure there's some french and oriental (japanese/chinese) in my gene pool. living it....  glibs aside... my day job involves software architecture and development for a fortune 500 company. i have a pretty flexible schedule (by silicon valley standards) and i strive to find balance between work and personal life. i run a grassroots women's tech group in the south bay focused on coding, networking and sharing. outside of work, i stay in shape by hiking/biking and yoga. i am pondering which variety of tomatoes to plant in my backyard this summer. got any suggestions? last year i traveled to japan and costa rica. on my list are: africa, vietnam/cambodia/thailand, china, south america, new zealand, turkey and spain. i'm leaning towards new zealand this summer, but, we'll see. cooking various types of cuisines and experimenting with food/drink (i make a mean mojito, chicken curry and flan), being funny/silly and making people laugh/smile, being cuddly, cute and happy thinking about solutions to problems and discussing them, my ability to take on challenges (fixing plumbing issues in my house, managing contractors, juggling a variety of things everyday) with aplomb, relative ease and a positive attitude, being loyal, supportive and responsible, giving advice on life/career/direction when asked learning something from everyone i get to know my smile, my eyes and my straightforward, cheerful personality my great interest in all things food related i've been told i'm sweet, caring, insightful, trustworthy, courageous and honest i tend to have less time for reading books lately. i listen to npr a lot. i like to cook and experiment with different cuisines (as varied as you can think of) at home and love to eat out at different restaurants. i tend to order the unusual dishes and cool/weird drinks and have no problem with being vocal about my reactions to them. if you know what "sous vide" and "braising" mean and might have done either, please do contact me. i love meat and fish of all sorts while being good at maintaining a balance with veggies and whole grains. i do not like natto.  my music preferences are very varied and range from classical to rock to pop to rap, 70's, 80's, 90's hits. as long as it's not head banging heavy metal or very loud, i'm fine with it.  i love to go to shows with friends and like both fun-to-watch shows as well as somewhat serious themes. i like plays, theater, opera and shows like beach, blanket babylon (yes, i know it's touristy, but, i had fun). i, often, go to the stanford theater to watch the classics and hear the live organ. i've been to a few stand-up comedy shows and have yet to find one that was consistently intellectually stimulating/funny/appealing throughout. but, happy to try new things with someone.  i do like my "home" and "cuddle/couch with chocolate and wine" time very much. my friends and family, sunblock/hat, outdoor activities, enjoying different cuisines, being geeky, constantly learning something new, appreciating what i have and being excited about the future no matter what it brings my life so far and what i want to do/be in 5 years, 10 years etc. i, also, spend a lot of time thinking about the friends and family i care about. when/where to go para-sailing and if i can find a buddy to drag along planning for a new zealand trip and, maybe find a great travel buddy. no particular routine. hanging out with friends, watching tv while eating a home cooked meal, going to a concert or show. going up to the city to meet friends for a night out is high on my to-do list. planning the weekend and, perhaps, having weekend road trips to places just to get out and have a change of scenery. i've thought a lot about this and have come to the conclusion that i would rather share private matters in private :). you are looking for someone to hang out with, like intelligent, thoughtful and adventurous women, like to go out to eat at different restaurants and critique the food/wine/service, and we'll take it from there...

full_bio: okc enemies is chipping days off of my life. it's so bad and i can't stop looking. which reinforces the following:  basically since i've had a profile here, i've had an apology to start things off, for, you know. my gender. not in general (although a case could certainly be made for that), but because i know that a lot of guys here suck. i try my hardest not to be that guy. i've been led to believe that i succeed more often than i fail.  i'm an artist, and a writer, and some other things too.  shit. i write for a living. building something with people i respect, killing myself for my own dreams, instead of shareholders or ceos that i haven't met or will never meet.  i record fairly popular podcasts. i draw and paint, though not as much as i'd like. it's hard to find models when you're not in school, or at least, i haven't figured out a good way to find them. i'm good with my hands. i'm good with words. i'm good at reading people, and ok at knowing when to tell them i'm doing so. i'm good at listening.  also banter and back and forth.  (okcenemies has made me realize that saying i'm good with my hands makes me sounds like a giant fucking creep. i'm sorry! i didn't know!) it's the shaved head and/or the tattoos.  lately, i probably look like the guy with the shaved head, dark jeans and t-shirt reading hi-fructose, my kindle, or some other book about low-brow art on the bart or muni. yes, my sunglasses sit on the collar of my shirt, and yes, i realize that can look super douche-y, but otherwise i will lose them and have! expensive!  and my eyes are pretty light sensitive. and now if i'm wearing a short-sleeved shirt, it would be the tattoo fingers on my right arm's pulse.  edit: just lost and had to replace another pair.  i guess i come off as kind of broody (brooding?). it's nothing against you, i promise. it's that other guy. you know the one. that guy. the one reading atlas shrugged on the bart in hundred dollar sweatpants who thinks he's a maverick and a titan among men. still crawling through infinite jest, and maybe that's to make up for giving up on pynchon in college. i'm noticing a very strong transhumanism bent in my recent reading tastes, even with stuff like the immortal life of henrietta lacks. i've read the same foreign book everyone else is reading right now, and i thought it could have used some pretty major edits/trimming (something the film adaptation pulled off reasonably well, actually). right now, reading griftopia by matt taibbi. you know, the angry, angry dude from rolling stone. morgan, king, vonnegut, books on painting, anything highly recommended by people i respect. i wish i had more time to read, but the writing gets in the way.  edit: i just spend way too much on a bunch of books on mucha. oops.  movies with well played moral ambiguity and endings that aren't stereotypical while managing to seem appropriate. because of this, grosse pointe blank is my favorite john cusack movie. also just one of my favorite movies. i sort of studied cronenberg academically off and on, so i hope that doesn't bother you. i see a fair amount of movies, and i stream on netflix constantly.  there are shows i enjoy very much - at the top of the list right is the wire, most likely. i've watched it all the way through twice, and i'm working my way through it again. it's the most intelligent, literary, heartbreakingly conscious show i've ever seen, and the ability to consistently add new ensemble casts to the show pretty much every year is just as impressive. i enjoy true blood as well, because it's relentlessly watchable trash that manages to find relatable ground for most of its characters. other favorites include the venture bros., louie (a fat bald white guy vehicle that discusses social issues from a working class perspective? holy shit.), californication, and others i'm either forgetting or which i am too ashamed to name publicly. i have acquired the first season of daria with the original licensed music, because yes, that was important.  i listen to a lot of music. end cliche. the most recent bands/groups/persons seem to be darker my love, gnarls barkley, ellie gould, la roux, nachtmystium, alice in chains, queens of the stone age, the do, and on and on. it's eclectic. the last few days have been massive attack sorts of days. oh, and diving way into the flat duo jets this week. and nin is creeping back into rotation.  this week it's blood red shoes and the kills.  foodwise, i like to cook. i seem to have a knack for italian. i don't eat a lot of meat (which is partly resultant from having a vegetarian room mate). i am, however, terrible at grocery shopping. i am apparently awesome at making tortillas from scratch. i'm noticing a strong "my cooking will make my/your ass bigger" bent here. sketchbooks, a not even combination of isolation and human contact, something to read, my conscience, my macbook (which makes me feel like a gigantic douchebag), and a means to be aware of world and domestic events and happenings. how writer's block dies a quick death at 1am.  causality, why things and people are the way they are.  how if i were going to expatriate, i think i'd want to live in vancouver for a while.  what does it mean to be an ally? can i assign that to myself? should i assign that to myself? does it matter?  also! i see people around here that seem so bright and so kind and altruistic list ayn rand as a favorite author, and my head almost literally shoots off of my shoulders in confusion and frustration. objectivism on the whole goes against my moral foundation. also, she was an awful writer. i just bought a fucking gigantic roll of canvas, so i should probably do something about that. i'm a very socially functional, engaged, diverse-interest blessed nerd. i'm paid for it. i am really super embarrassed by twitter.  when i see a sudden influx of visitors to my profile i wonder if i said something stupid to someone on here and then they posted it on their journal. i hope i didn't.  hm. i never thought i would actually need to say this, but: racists need not apply. the hair on my head has nothing to do with the views contained therein. (this wouldn't be here if i hadn't learned that it needed to be here.)  there are people on here that i think are amazing that i don't message or respond to. there are a variety of reasons for that, but a major one being that i don't think i'm what they're looking for, based on their profile. to whit, i am seeing people. seeing meaning naked, people meaning more than one. i'm in a period of complicated non-monogamy, and i know that's not for everyone. i respect that. so, if i see your profile and that seems like something that is outside of your comfort area, then i don't want to impose on that. i am interested in dates, and friendship, and generally casual-but-meaningful engagement with people.  i am also incredibly busy with a great, stressful job and fantastic people in my life, and only have so much unspoken for time.  make sense? cool. i now expect i'll get zero messages, but i'd rather be honest up front. and i do still like messages. all that said...  how about... message me. be active. assume agency. i message people. you should too. if you're interested, if you're repulsed (god, i hope you're not actually repulsed, i have feelings). i might not answer, but i can guarantee you the reason i didn't answer has nothing to do with you being the one to say hi first. what's the worst that can happen?  i don't know. message me if you want to have tea or coffee or food, have a conversation. see a movie, go to a bookstore, etc. i'm cool with friends and dates. i'd like to have fun with some new people.  have you ever stumbled across a complete stranger and wanted so badly for them to find everything they're looking for that your heart hurt? not in a romantic way, but as a fellow human being? does that make sense to you? it would be nice if maybe it did. i wish we weren't all so cold so often.

full_bio: i should first state that i just moved to san francisco and i am looking forward to these next 32 years of my life.  i am a secure, confident, nerdy, silly, sincere guy. i live and thirst for knowledge of current tech. movies that combine romance and time travel really get to me in a happy/emotional way. i do not care too much for the superficial world and i am rarely bored.  i work for myself doing what i love, truly blessed and grateful. i never was big into school. what i learn i teach myself.  if there was a 24 hour museum i would probably be married.  when i am into something my passion for it glows from me.  i am easy going. i love to share (yet not allow for people to be dependent of me in an unhealthy way), and i will give everything and anything to a person i love.  if i am not interested in something i walk away pretty quickly. i am not a time waster.  i am leaving out a ton of stuff, but i really do not know what is too lil to write or too much so i will keep it simple.  my ideal match is a girl that i share interest and/or views with. these interest can change. i grow a lot and wish for my ideal girl to be a grower too.  a girl that can inspire me. a girl that is not satisfied with the normal pop of society. a girl that loves classic rock and 80's monster ballads is a plus.  a girl that is caring, yet confident. a girl that is beautiful is a unique way. i am not much of a fan of the "what the world thinks is beauty" thing. good taste in what is funny is a must. if you think billy madison is funny then you are for me.  if you cheat, steal, lie, are cheap when it comes to buying quality stuff please do not contact me.  i have a lot of experience and have had many time that i have learned what i do not want in a girl if the line above doesn't prove that. lol  last but not least - a girl that is not scared to be herself even if it is silly and take risks. making pretty things on the computer. looking for the last piece of the puzzle. i am a visual designer/graphic artist, and i have been doing photography professionally since i can remember. i love it! don't know if its a good thing, but my tattoos. computers, software, my iphone, collectors stuff, cartoons, stone crabs from joes, my bed, cuddling, laying on the sofa, visiting new places, harry potter, inspiring tv and people. so much more, feel free to ask. 1. my doggie (gunnar) 2. my mac 3. my "simple" shoes 4. my iphone 5. my ipad 6. my inspiration/ambitions about pixels, love and gadgets. at home creating things. =) i used to think it was cool to wear a dress in public. lol you are ready to live creatively and have an open mind to the things that make life worth living. if you think we have similar interests. a good knowledge of pop culture is a huge plus.

full_bio: well. let's see. i'm a calm person. takes a lot to get me pissed off. i don't take life too serious. life is fun, i like enjoying it. drama fffffffree. don't do drugs. those are bad. don't like judging people for the most part though, that's not cool. friends tell me i'm also very blunt with stuff.. supposedly it gets me in trouble sometimes.. =]  when in a relationship: i'm not a jealous guy. i'm not an over-possessive guy. i don't sneak around or anything dumb like that. i'm a faithful guy. i've never cheated in my life. i won't lie, i love cupcaking, snuggling, whatever you want to call it. =]  i like: music, football, clubbing/barhoppin, chillen, enjoying life, video games  i am easy-going, respectful, and entertaining! work for a family owned restaurant, eventually want to open my own bar. still a few years away from attempting that though. -learning. i hate school, but it's mostly because i get everything so fast. like, have you ever been at school, and it take some people one time to learn something while it takes others like 50 times? yeah. i'm a first-timer.  -making people feel better. when my friends are upset, i'm usually the first person they call. :d  -video games.  -enjoying life. i'm an honest, calm, and down to earth person. also, i love having a good time. :) books: the last book i've read was "alive", the story about the argentine rugby team who got stuck in the mountains and had to eat each other to survive. i'm not going to lie, only reason i started reading it was because i had to read something for detention in high school. however, i enjoy reading all of the sports new articles on websites like espn, profootballtalk, and every once in a while i'll mix in news stories not related to the sporting world. :)  movies: don't really have fav movies, more or less have favorite actors/actresses. will smith, ryan reynolds, dwayne johnson (the rock!), matthew mcconaughey, kate beckinsale, ice cube, trey crews, johnny depp, milla jovavich, denzel washington, ali larter, josh harnett, seth rogan. to name a few, of course.  music: mostly hiphop, but my favorites are dev, the cataracs, bobby brackins, fareast movement, lmfao, dmx, joey galaxy (formerly known as young cash), cassidy, the pack (as a group and individually), pitbull, lost generation, b.o.b., soulja boy, fabolous, usher, and hyper crush.  food: i must admit, since my dad has worked in the italian cuisine industry since i was born, that is my favorite type of food. :) 1. friends/family, where would i be without em? 2. my st. louis rams. 3. roof over my head, it would suck to sleep in my car. 4. variation, i love doing new things all the time. i hate routines. 5. my job, i need it! 6. a computer, so i can be doing things like this. football. sadly, i heart it more than a fat boy hearts cake. hm.. also think of new ways to be silly because life is waaaaaaaay to short to be hecka serious all the time :d usually working then any of the following (sometimes all of the following) video games, bars, or clubbing. usually. i use a lot of smiley emoticons. oh.. i also have huge man crushes on dwayne johnson, ryan reynolds, will smith, and usher. no homo, of course.  i didn't think that "the hangover" was as funny as people made it seem and thought it just raaaaaaaan on for days.  ..and, i like ke$ha. :x you want someone to be honest, real, and who doesn't beat around the bush. :)

full_bio: i'm curious. i like to read, learn, travel, explore, and overthink things. i like words: witty conversation, an intelligent argument. i am sometimes goofy, i laugh easily, and i am wary of people who takes themselves too seriously. i get really enthusiastic about things i love, like solar eclipses and fireworks and other beautiful things. i hate the gym, but can dance, or walk/hike with my ipod, for hours. i get antsy if i don't get outdoors regularly. i want to try something new, to have an adventure, big or small. i am trying not to sit still - a day of doing nothing makes me stir crazy. i go hiking; i am learning to cook better; i took a class on coursera.com and want to take more; i go to or volunteer at events (most recently, maker faire) that help me learn something new.  i want to generally say "yes" to more things. making people laugh, dancing, knowing big words and random astronomy facts, powering through books, taking photos, not being a couch potato, being genuinely enthusiastic, being completely ignorant of all things sports-related. my oblivious dancing or oblivious reading. my "expressive face" which is basically my inability to hide my emotions, positive or negative. i read a ton, mostly fiction... some favorites: the blind assassin, gilead, empire falls, st. lucy's home for girls raised by wolves. the da vinci code doesn't count as a book.  i seem to have stopped watching tv... i will read pretty much any book recommended to me, but i can't sit through a movie. it's a mystery.  i enjoy eating. i will generally take spicy ethnic food over pasta. i like music, too, from joss stone to johnny cash to thievery corporation. i would not put great store in either of those to summarize my personality though :) my friends; a good book; nature; my camera; dancing; something to look forward to. how to possibly absorb all the info i want to read, or make time to learn the things i want to learn. what cool new event is happening in the city. the next great book i should pick up. how mind-bendingly far apart everything in the universe is. why people act the way they do. how i can get away with quitting my job to travel instead. out dancing. or seeing a ballet, or a play, or a gallery. or trying a new restaurant/bar with close friends. or at one of those random quirky events that seem to pop up in sf so often (island-wide odyssey performance on angel island, exploratorium after dark:modernist cuisine, fire spinning jam on the embarcadero) - i'm open-minded when it comes to activities! i have 5 hours' worth of queen on my ipod. and i sing along with it, too. ...if you have personality, if you are funny, if you tell good stories, and hold up your end of conversations on topics other than your job. if you have an idea for an outing or activity - dinner dates are awkward! (also, i am much more likely to respond to messages than to ims. especially if your opening im is "hey". just sayin'.)

full_bio: i've been living in san francisco for almost four years now, but it's with the terrible threat of my uncertain future that i'm looking at this place with a set of new eyes. trying to make new friends, get rid of toxic relationships, find new hobbies, create more beauty, discover and appreciate things that go unnoticed. fighting off post grad ennui playing devil's advocate, doodling on important paperwork, watering plants. pale, pale, skin been reading the girl with the dragon tattoo series as of late, and i'm a fan of anything by jonathan safran foer. guilty pleasure=the most terrible television series' that get aired. my taste in music is super eclectic but lately i've been all about al green, the pixies, most 90s rock. i'm a snack food addict. it's weird and a little gross at times. my bike sarcasm gummy bears hard cider a pen living close to nature i'm a chronic day dreamer, almost to an embarrassing degree. i've got a lot of things going on in my head, so a lot of times i can be spotted writing lists or drawing in a journal, just to have a physical representation of everything swimming around in there. probably working, being tired, letting my friends convince me to have a drink or two i've been super lazy about answering messages lately, apologies you're down for anything at anytime, including midnight adventures and night hikes in the park, bike rides to the beach, exploring secret san franciscan spots, eating great food, trying something new.

full_bio: when i was sick w/ the flu a few months ago, i watched every episode of star trek from 64. now when i'm on the phone, instead of saying 'goodbye' i say 'kirk out.' it's more fun when people don't get it. i also try to trick the old dutch guy in operations into doing the roger rabbit. these are good things.  i work dispatching tug boats for oil tankers and container vessels in san francisco. i work strange hours, three days on, then three days off, seventy two hours straight no breaks.  i have two canaries. they chirp a bunch. they are named scoriovante and cannatella. they just built a nest, it's sort of amazing. im trying to breed them for some reason. they have lights that are timed so they get fifteen hour days and some vitamins which make them want to mate. they are still pretty young so we'll see if any eggs come of all this. at any rate, they are happy and quite entertaining. i recently finished a three year recording project. i recorded a full length psychedelic rock record at different fur studios. i play guitar, bass, keys and try to sing.  i'm gonna go to space one day, not sure how or when but i'm going to. space rules. virgin galactic looks cool but i want to look into what russia has to offer. i really want to go. i build ukuleles and other instruments. i eat a lot of candy. i make music whenever i can. i have many tattoos. giraffe. ducks. catfish. i'm thinking about getting a dalek tat on my leg. doctor who blows my mind. books: gravities rainbow. infinite jest. tristessa. rum diary. movies: dark crystal. donnie darko. life of birds series. blue planet. science documentaries. music. candy. moog synths. the sun. birds. my car. i think about outer space way too much. quantum physics sometimes keeps me up at night as well. i'm probably poof reading my portbowlio or playing one of my moogs. most likely both. i've never driven a stick shift before. i can also blow up pigeons with my mind, i'm not proud of that. it just sort of happens. really? you should message me either way, i'm so damn bored some nights. it's hard to make friends with this busy schedule i've got. i'll even accept the super weirdos out there... send me messages and photos, any interaction is welcome.
