sign: capricorn and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: sometimes geeky, often sarcastic, i've got relatively disparate interests and a penchant for learning new things.  likes: dogs, cars (lotus, formula 1), dungeons & dragons and some other rpgs, teh intarwebs, egyptian hieroglyphs, ancient history/archaeology in general, legos, dinosaurs, lighting design, random bouts of silliness, hiking, sleep, coffee, music  dislikes: fall out boy & my chemical romance, most emo bands, thai food. i tend to have other dislikes, but i generally don't have long lasting ones. or at least, i can't remember them.  i have opinions on nearly anything, but depending on the day, they often change. i tend to prefer red to white, amber ales to hefeweizen, vodka to rum, coffee to tea, and pepsi to coke. i have a cynical new england exterior, but am a romantic at heart.  i am curious, snarky, and quirky working for an internet advertising company in san francisco. kind of odd, given my background in classical studies (history of ancient egyptians, romans and occasionally greeks), but i like my job and love the people i work with, so it ends up turning out all right. also, our office has this gorgeous view of the bay bridge and sushi every day, so there's really nothing to dislike.  i recently moved up to the city from the peninsula, where i'd been for nearly 8 years. i miss the cafe and bookstore down there i used to frequent, and have yet to find entirely suitable replacements up in the city. although, to be fair, there are some local places that are okay.  my family (and dog) is on the east coast, so i spend much of my time bouncing between coasts. i also adore traveling, so i try and get away from the states as often as i can.  i had a fish. then i had 10 fish. now i have none, and i feel kind of bad about it. some part of me wants more, but aquariums in the city seem like a poor choice.  i have recently acquired an xbox360 and have beaten fable 2 to death. now moving on to mass effect, which will be more of a challenge, since i'm terrible at shooters. even rpg-hybrid ones. editing things that i didn't write. making foam for lattes/cappuccinos. focusing lighting instruments. figuring out games/programs without the manual (although this sometimes gets me into trouble). putting together mix cds (yes, it requires talent!). quest for glory and other old sierra adventure games. it used to be my height, but then i moved to california where people are taller, so that became less noticeable. oftentimes, it's now the sarcasm. i tend to talk in a somewhat wry tone of voice, so even when i'm not being sarcastic, it often gets misinterpreted as such. also, i'm almost always cold. books: i'm a biblioholic, so this is a hard one for me. ender's game, scout's progress, city of thieves, axiomatic, elric, song of ice and fire, neil gaiman, some other stuff. trying to expand my library beyond scifi/fantasy, so any recommendations you've got would be welcome.  movies: high fidelity, 10 things i hate about you, risky business, doom, fight club, six-string samurai, stardust, heavy metal (the one from the 80s), serendipity, requiem for a dream  tv: pushing daisies, firefly, battlestar galactica, buffy, angel, rome, entourage, star trek : tng  music: bon jovi, guns 'n roses, nin, atb, amr diab, schandmaul, zero 7, smile.dk, alabama 3, aubrey hodges, frank sinatra, tom petty, vast, various club hiphop (timbaland), paul van dyk, sisters of mercy, neil young, glitchmob/edit/ooah, bassnectar, some country  food: lebanese, moroccan, other mediterranean, ethiopian, italian, sushi, coffee... i eat pretty much anything that doesn't contain peanuts or concentrated soy. and i love lobster. my weighted companion cube. friends & family. my collection of books. my music collection. glow in the dark stars. sunshine. that might be six, it might be more than that. where i want to go for my next vacation. what i want to go to graduate school in. what makes people tick. the deliberate similarities of battlestar galactica and deep space 9. whether python really is the best language out there. proper grammar usage. what the heck i should be doing with my life. it varies. gaming (rpg campaign, board games, xbox), concerts, things involving books (either reading or browsing a bookstore), drinking (coffee/hot chocolate/chai or less salubrious things), or movie watching are all viable options. sometimes sleeping. laundry if all else fails.  this friday i'm going to a wine bar, last friday i stayed up way the hell too late playing xbox, the friday prior to that i went to the hrc for a showcase of local bands... my navel is pierced. i got it during a scavenger hunt where we got extra points if we actually got something pierced. it was a whim, and its presence says very little about me. you feel like it. i'm generally willing to talk to whomever about whatever. however, bear in mind: i don't respond to woos, and i don't respond to casual encounter requests.

full_bio: i am very close to my family and love spending time with my nieces and nephew, enjoy dinner and time out with friends and like nothing more than exploring a new city with my nikon d40 in hand.  i am comfortable whether i'm in a nice/fancy restaurant, dive bar, cocktail party or house party, have a deep love of art, have a bottomless appetite for great books and can hold my own discussing music-art-movies-travels-books-history-politics-food-running-yoga-soccer-technology-finance-etc.  my ideal partner is able to teach me new things, lives life with a passion and shares my desire to see and experience anything new. i am attracted to confidence and intellectual curiosity. work: i work in finance. play: i am very active and enjoy running, cycling and doing bikram. i spend a lot of time with my family, love checking out new restaurants and art shows.... i also love traveling and escaping the city on weekends. eating, learning and living  favorite recent books: the lotus eaters, matterhorn, the book thief, the nine, stieg larsson trilogy, shantaram, long walk to freedom, kite runner, standing at the scratch line, etc.  favorite restaurants: slow club, delfina, bar jules, the house, sushi groove, and catagna.  favorite musica: snow patrol, xx, metric, spiritualized, radiohead, broken bells, mgmt, broken bells, johnny cash, yeah yeah yeahs, flaming lips, elbow, serge gainsbourg, beasties, dj am, air, daft punk, prince, mia etc. family, books, soccer, music/art, chocolate and mangos. coffee and pizza get honorable mention on the list. the merits of crunchy vs. creamy peanut butter, blended vs. on the rocks margaritas, and what fruit i should eat today. happy hour followed by dinner somewhere yummy... a concert... keeping it mellow at home... hanging out with family....  intellectually curious and/or active.

offspring: doesn't have kids, but might want them
full_bio: i've recently been told that my whole profile is too long. it probably is. in that case, feel free to just read those highlighted bits. those are the buzzwords to my brain/soul/dangly bits anyway.  i'm a pretty understated person who doesn't get much sleep. i like blasting tinny, low bitrate indie and bedroom radio stations all night quite a bit, but peace and quiet is even better.  there was some text here, but now it's gone! schoolwise, i'm busy learning anything social work-related so i can better understand and help the people i interact with. once this is all said and done, i'd like to work mostly with children and teenagers, since that seems to be where i could do the most good. but it's all good, really. of course it means i'll probably be in school for a good long while yet.  i also have a day job! sometimes i get called out on it by the crimethinc folks, workshys and freeloaders in my life, but it pays the bills.  most of all, i love composing and creating electronic music. i've been into it since i was a tiny babby and now that i'm an adult i still haven't gotten over the fact that you can actually make the stuff. i sometimes collaborate with buddies up humboldt way, but most of those projects seem to be as awesome as they are short-lived.  at the moment, i'm trying to put together a surf rock band. for living on the coast, it's a lot harder than i thought it would be. ...listening because most of the time i actually do give a shit about you and your sick cat, job you hate, and secret dreams.  i'm also pretty good at recommending music, and i can sure as hell beat you at mario kart. beard? you tell me, i've never bothered to ask. here's where i really get to stretch it out for that 5,000 word mark! i don't know if anyone ever reads these things too closely, but it sure feels good to write them so here goes:  authors: everyone aboard the bizarro gravy train especially mykle hansen and carlton mellick iii; hunter thompson, douglas coupland, irvine welsh, ray bradbury, g. k. chesterton, chuck palahniuck, amy hempel, bret e. ellis, warren ellis, tolkien (more similarion, less the hobbit), douglas adams and kurt vonnegut.  movies: moody movies with small sets and tiny casts that kind of linger on. after hours is a good example. the first half of fight club, too. carlito's way, downtown 81, oldboy, etc.  shows: anything i can catch up on 2 years too late on netflix. breaking bad, walking dead, mad men, nip/tuck, king of the hill, spaced, house. the first couple of seasons of the office. still in love with the original star trek and the next generation, trying my hardest to get into the other spin-offs.  music: first wave and proto-punk, la death rock, 60's french pop, minimal wave, old school industrial, italo disco/hi-nrg (the queenlier, the better), silly rap but not goofy rap (clouddead not mc chris), no wave, post-punk, surf instrumentals, old goth bands, breaks-oriented idm, lo-fi electronics, musique concret, krautrock. honestly, i'll listen to anything once.  food: chinese food. that's all. ok, i guess italian, too. internet radio stations delicious beverages a horde of pets big warm jackets not quite as delicious, but still very effective beverages sturdy bookcases i spend most of my time not thinking, lately. thinking is a chump's game, folks. staying up til dawn, sample-hunting and knob-twiddling. but, on off nights, perusing some old kung-fu movies, reading or enjoying the small town homies discount at the local bar. to be honest, i only started coming here to spy on people i know. you can learn a lot about the people you think you know by seeing how they put themselves out there on the market. i don't creep as much as i used to though; most of the folks i know share their profiles loud n' proud these days.  also, some sites have regular okc criticism threads and it's much easier to have a profile of my own so i can skip the muss and fuss of registering fake ones every time. i'm not entirely sold on the idea of online dating, but i'm willing to give anything a shot once. or twice or three times.  i definitely have a thing for easy-going but passionate ladies. short hair is definitely a plus. artsy farts, quiet types, musicians, tattoo enthusiasts, homebodies, feverish gardeners, gamers and leather-jacket-and-dress-wearers are all great too.  the funnier/more interesting/more infuriating you are, the more likely we are to get along!

full_bio: i'm a software developer and a semi-reluctant california transplant. most likely i shouldn't be reluctant at this point, as i've been out here since i graduated from high school. one saving grace is the fact that i grew up as a niners fan, so at least i can catch them every sunday. full_bio: i grew up in the bay area, went to college in western massachusetts, got my masters in paris, then moved to sf. i miss traveling and living abroad when everyday is an adventure, but i'm loving life back in the states with friends and family nearby.  some random points: - i've been to 38 states and 5 continents. - played volleyball in college. - i have a mean golf drive. - love discovering new things (about people and places). - i am reputed to be the best guacamole maker in all of paris. - i frequently go out of my way to say hi to passing puppies on the street. - i'm pretty competitive and love winning, but lose with humility. - i'm the person my friends call in times of need. - i'm 5th generation californian, but could probably be happy living anywhere...at least for a little while. - while i can't convey it too well in this profile, i'm a huge goofball. balancing a busy schedule of work, friends, family and travel - sometimes well and sometimes not.  trying to make room for more art/culture and volunteering in my day-to-day life in sf. giving gifts; finding my way around foreign countries; sports requiring hand eye coordination; recognizing faces and images. my height and possibly my green eyes. or my renowned "wine face." books: orientalism; pride and prejudice (and yes, i am named after mr. darcy); the help; new york times; wuthering heights; harry potter; le ventre de l'atlantique; nuit des origines; shrazade.  art: pissarro; delaunay; kandinsky; jean-lon grome.  music: i am no music snob and listen to just about anything. favorites are girl talk, lil' wayne, yelle, ellie goulding, martin solveig, david guetta, bebe, mia, broken bells. give me a good beat that i can sing, dance, drive or work out to it and i'll be happy.  food: everything but especially mexican, italian, french, spanish, persian, and sushi. looooove to cook but am too busy to do it often.  movies. not a fan of scary movies. if it's funny, exciting, thought provoking, aesthetically pleasing, educational, or ground breaking, i'll probably watch it.  tv: modern family, the office, glee, 30 rock, always sunny my friends and family, art, challenge, travel, laughter, physical activity. where i'm traveling to next or my weekend plans. usually with friends or coworkers at a new restaurant, movie, bar or giants game. or on the couch for movie night with the roomies after a long week. i can't do a cartwheel. you're fun, intelligent, social and don't have a problem laughing at yourself every once and a while.

full_bio: when i was looking over how my answers to the various questions came across, i realized that it makes me look very religious. i'm not, really. i just have my own way of interpreting god/goddess/the divine/the all - what have you. so when i answered questions about believing in god, or how important it is to have god in ones life, it made me look more like (traditionally) devout person than i am. i believe that everything that's mundane is part of everything that's divine, so that's why i answered those questions the way i did. i have no problem with however you see the infinite. we're all wrong anyway. any definition has to be wrong, because definite is the opposite of infinite.  as my user name states, i'm polyamorous. that basically means that i'm in a loving, committed, non-monagamous relationship that allows both myself and my wife to enter into other love relationships at the same time.  if that sounds weird, maybe it is, and that's fine by me. there's very little about me or the way i see the world that's typical. the way i think often makes people's heads spin.  i'm an artist, actor & musician. i play mandolin, make clothes & jewelry sculptures that glow in the dark, and perform primarily comedies on stage, film or elsewhere when i can.  i'm an easy going guy, but at the same time i'm a passionate person. i try to live my life in a way that feeds my passions. i also don't take myself too seriously.  i tend to collect experiences rather than things.  i'm pretty well educated, but i believe imagination is more important than education.  i believe honesty is more important still. everything on my profile is accurate. i don't lie about my age, weight, my marital status (i'm married) or anything else.  i believe kindness is the most important thing of all.  i'm always busy with a zillion projects. if i've marked you as a favorite, and didn't message you, that's typical for me. i haven't lost interest, i just got suddenly busy. don't take it personally if i never get in touch. if i marked you as a favorite, i am interested in you. to be honest, i've rarely messaged anyone on here. if you find me interesting, i'm afraid you're going to have to make the first move.  when i find myself with a rare afternoon or evening free, it generally takes me by surprise. i love people who are up for doing things at the spur of the moment.  maybe because my life can be busy & complicated, i often like my social time to be simple. i like to hang out & enjoy a conversation, a sunset or a movie on the couch. i like to go for a walk or a bike ride on a beautiful day.  i'm very affectionate - big into both public displays of affection & private displays of affection. i love hugging, kissing, cuddle talks & sex.  i really like making out - a lot - probably more than i like sex. all i know is that i can't have sex without it. does that make me a kissing fetishist? i guess it does. that's my one & only fetish, though i have many kinks. ;)  i like using emotocons. ;p  anything else you want to know about me, you'll just have to find out for yourself.  yes, that's an invitation. ;) going where the muse takes me. accepting people for who they are - laughing - making you laugh - getting distracted - being open minded - thinking outside of the box - being silly - hugging - kissing - cuddling - disorganization. being both passionate & pragmatic at the same time. my smile - my laugh - that i smell good. my favorite books, movies shows & music are typically the works of my friends.  i love all kinds of exotic dining experiences. unfortunately, i can't eat any starches any more. my favorite food is ethiopian. don't worry, i won't order the kitfo if it freaks you out. there are plenty of other yummy things on the menu.  i don't need to eat meat, but i do enjoy it from time to time. my wife is a vegetarian, so i rarely eat meat when we're eating together.  i respect vegetarians, but i personally believe that life feeds on life, and just because a plant doesn't cry out when you cut its stem, that doesn't mean that it feels no pain. love  friends  performing  sleep  sound  air my next project. either performing, seeing other friends perform or chilling at home.  going out with you? i'm shy - no really!  my friends don't believe me when i say it. even though i'm very outgoing and a shameless flirt, and i live to be on stage, when i see someone i'm attracted to, i clam up.  once i know someone likes me, i'm very flirtatious, but i'm always concerned a stranger might not like being approached.  i figured ok cupid would be a good place to break the ice & get to know people before meeting them in person, circumventing my shyness with strangers issue, but i have to admit, even here, i've been pretty shy. if i like someone here, i put her on my favorites list, but i wait to see if she contacts me.  if i put you on my favorites list, rest assured, i like you. you're 21-39. you're not a bbw, (more power to you, beautiful - just not my thing) you're okay with the fact that i'm married, (yes, she knows i'm dating you). you're nice, you think i'm interesting & you can type "hello" on your keyboard.

religion: atheism and very serious about it
full_bio: i'm tom duncan and i'm young and privilaged! i love indulgence, intoxication, and sleeping in past noon. after spending a year cooking in a fine dining restaurant i learned that i do not want to cook at any restaurant ever again. so i switched teams and now i'm serving at the same place. i'm back in school after a two year hiatus studying hospitality/management because i think that my infatuation with the restaurant industry could be a fruitful career. if you don't believe me let's have a dinner party, and i can guarantee you the best service of your life. i'm good at whistling, a lot of people find it rather annoying, but if you don't like the sound of whistling, chances are you have no soul. i'm also quite good at doing dishes and appeasing people. i'm really not sure what people notice first about me. i'd like to think that people are taken aback by my dashing good looks, and witty, charming diction but, who can really say? you'll have to ask someone who has just met me or meet me yourself. 1984, farenheit 451, extremely loud and incredibly close, the botany of desire, one flew over the cuckoo's nest, the upside of irrationality, i drink for a reason, on food and cooking, war dances  animal collective, beirut, fleet foxes, mice parade, mister heavenly, david bowie, ratatat, lcd soundsystem, diplo, the tallest man alive, washed out, metronomy, toro y moi, the fresh and the onlys, maps & atlases, john coltrane, man man, james blake, flying lotus, the black keys, the books, gold panda, sleigh bells, major lazer, a tribe called quest, black lips, bon iver, big boi, das racist.  hard eight, breaking away, 12 angry men, royal tennebaums, jesus christ superstar, casablanca, the graduate, fargo, pulp fiction, the big lebowski. you know, all your basic good movies made in the last few decades.  tim and eric awesome show great job, always sunny in philadelphia, portlandia, arrested development, trailer park boys, breaking bad.  food is an interesting category. there aren't many kinds of food i dislike but, my favorite food textures are warm, fluffy, and dry. it's weird i know. just think of the best bowl of rice you've ever had. water dogs shoes showers sunshine & my car often times i contemplate my own existential philosophy. the aesthetics of the world around me. how i can best live a moral life, and most importantly what it would be like to play mariachi for a living. working at the restaurant. sundays are the new fridays, and i'm usually rolling spliffs, and taking shots. i'm terrible with names and money. you want to talk about all the fantastic things that could happen in the future, or if you just like drinking copious amounts of booze into the wee hours of the morning. also if your free on mondays and tuesdays. those are the only days i don't work 10 or so hours so let's do something.

religion: catholicism and somewhat serious about it
full_bio: i'm originally from sacramento, moved to the bay area for college, and decided to stay and work. i'm a third grade teacher and love every moment of it! i'm very family oriented and love hanging out with my family as much as going out with friends. i love traveling and exploring new places. besides work, i enjoy going out for happy hour or watching tv at home. i'm on a bowling league and, although i'm not that great, i have tons of fun with my teammates. we basically bowl for the good conversation and french fries! i also spend a good amount of time scrapbooking and crafting. i love taking pictures, especially picturesque-postcard looking ones. i decorate my apartment with the beautiful sights i've visited throughout the world. i recently started taking bachata dance lessons. i wish i had more opportunties to go out dancing! between bachata and zumba, i usually get my workouts through dance! i just got into yoga as well. listening, helping people out, and giving out hugs. my smile and how i rarely stop smiling. =) when i decide to sit down and read a book, it's usually something with a character in which i can relate. i wish i had more time to read, but lately i've been reading the chelsea handler books, shopaholic series, and sushi for beginners (novel, not a cookbook) my favorite movies are romantic comedies or dramas: my best friend's wedding, save the last dance, bridesmaids, lilo & stitch, the lion king (of course disney movies make the list!). tv shows: hart of dixie, grey's anatomy, modern family, happy endings, ugly betty, saved by the bell, full house, almost anything on e! i am also addicted to the news. i listen to all types of music and love anything that i can sing along and dance to. growing up with a mixed background, i have an appreciation for many types of foods. i enjoy cooking and usually stick to my mom's recipes. when eating out, i'm not much of an adventure girl, but i will try everything at least once! my family - staying at home or laughing with my cousins gives me the greatest feeling in the world! camera - i love taking pictures and capturing the moment. education - although i don't remember everything i've learned, i owe a lot to my past teachers. culture - the mixture of 3 cultures creates a unique blend that i'm proud to represent! passport - my passport provides access to traveling opportunities all over the world. laughter - what better way to enjoy life than through a giggle? where will my next travel destination be? should i return back to someplace i really liked? should i go visit family? should i explore someplace new? waking up from a nap! =) i usually take a quick nap in between work and whatever fun friday evening activities i have planned. usually i'm just cooking dinner and enjoying being a couch potato after an exhausting week at work!

full_bio: i'm on never-ending quest to explore how things work or how i can make them work. there is so much fascinating stuff out there to discover - technology, science, interesting people and different cultures. talking about different cultures - i am german, but i lived, studied and worked in a number of places around the world. i went to university in germany and australia and now i'll be in the bay area for a few years - at least! i love cali - and particularly sf. i recently started a fun new job at a local web company, where i'm working on letting you travel our wonderful planet virtually. however, i must admit there is nothing that could ever replace the great experience of exploring a new place yourself. i just love to travel. just got back from a backpacking trip to japan and korea. i also do some robotics projects with friends (i used to work in ai research). it's fun, but no matter how hard i try the robots are still pretty stupid :) apart from that, i go hiking, biking, and skiing, cook or explore the city. not bragging about the multitude of awesome skills i have. my german accent. - books: hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy, goethe's faust, life of pi - movies: kill bill, whatever works, life is beautiful, a beautiful mind, the meaning of life - tv: the daily show, big bang theory - food: i'm a sucker for all kinds of good food - which to me is not necessarily fancy food. bringing very few ingredients into perfect harmony is the real art of cooking. an art that i'm trying to master myself. fresh air, good food, sparkling water, adidas samba sneakers, friends and a some kind of computing device with an internet connection life, the universe and everything. how the human mind works. and how machines could fake it. clubbing with friends, watching a movie or getting ready for a weekend trip. i like girly/fruity cocktails (but i'm european, so it's ok). and i don't really like beer. i drink it occasionally, but i'll take a glass of wine or a cocktail over it anytime. you: - like what you just read - are an interesting person (that is you should have something that you are passionate about and so have something to tell) - want explore the city

full_bio: i am moving to san francisco in a few days and i am looking to meet new people to share adventures with around the bay. i am adventurous and playful but also driven and passionate. i love live music, playoff hockey, art, avocados, college football, redwood trees, book exchanges and travel (to name a few). i just returned from 8 months backpacking in central and south america where i was learning spanish, doing some non-profit work and having a blast. i'm creative, thoughtful, funny, independent and laid-back. i love being around people who can teach me new things and make me laugh. i just became the program director for a non-profit in the bay area. i have been working with non-profits and public schools for the past 5 years and i love it. i am also a certified yoga instructor and excited to start teaching around the bay.   some of my favorite books in the last year have been: 100 years of solitude, the alchemist, into thin air, confessions of an economic hit man (and probably many more that i can't think of right now).  i love live music, funny video clips, spicy food, and guacamole with just about everything.     you think you can make me laugh :)

full_bio: i love experiencing the planet (the outdoors) and protecting its future (sustainability); building meaningful things; having conversations that are deep, but don't take themselves too seriously; and anything involving maps laughing. building things that make a difference using technology. dancing. seeing live music! camping. cooking. reading. biking around this amazing city... life: maps, technology, brainstorming, and making people laugh. getting lost, preferably while traveling in random places. cooking vegetarian food! my finely tuned sense of style  and blue eyes, if you're into that kinda thing. i also have a red beard that people tend to think is incredible. musica: al green, simon and garfunkel, morgan heritage, the four seasons, random hip-hop/reggaeton (dancing!), 70's funk/soul/r&b. iron maiden, cynic, opeth, eluveitie and soo many more...  books: infinite jest, 1984, hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy  movies: jodhaa akbar, city of god, young frankenstein, little miss sunshine, lawrence of arabia, beverly hills cop  tv: the wire!, superjail, mad men, breaking bad  food: vegetarian, because i love animals and the earth (aww). love to cook, mostly indian, chinese and mexican.  other things that are amazing: pizza, chocolate chip cookies, extra-super-sharp vermont farmstead cheddar, anything homemade and delicious family & friends irony & sarcasm music seasons bicycle cilantro the future of our silly little species. environmental jusice. where to travel next... at a show, out with friends, or home with a good book i don't really like avocados (i try!). you want to explore this awesome city, go for a bike ride, or see some live music  or just get a beer and make fun of hipsters

full_bio: i'm not such a fan of talking about myself and less of a fan of making lists that start with "i" ...but, as it turns out, since i tend to add a little each time i log on, "i" have now come off looking proficient at it :p  my self-summary? well, i'm me.  i spent my first twenty years in massachusetts. i have now spent more time on the left coast than the right. i have never had trouble pronouncing words ending with the letter r, but, still find things wicked-cool. i'm kind. i'm loyal. i'm direct if i can't find room to be subtle. i'm my own best friend and my own worst enemy. i am not the missing piece nor am i seeking one. i am looking for someone to roll with. i am average looking, but, i have been known to talk people into thinking i'm better looking than that. i really know where my towel is. i am more than just-a-bit of a dork. i tend to only be picky about the important things...and then, in proportion. i often see altruism as selfishness with a long-view...and don't think that's a bad thing. it's hedonism with a conscience. i don't have any bugs or hangups that makes being close more complicated than it has to be.  i like my work: "i get paid to help people." it pays the rent, keeps the fridge full and allows me enough time to live most of my life outside of work.  i like to play. i like people who like to play. i am not a play-a. i play hockey two or three times a week and it is my favorite vice. i love music in all forms. i mess around with guitar, mandolin, harmonica, ukulele, and if i'm truly-inspired i'll play piano -terribly. i probably like your music as much as i like mine. i have a dry sense of humor that is often misunderstood...if understood at all. i'm good with directions and suggestions. i'm terrible with orders. i ride my bicycle as often as i can. sometimes i ride as an excuse not to drive, often just for the fun of it. i read more than just about everyone i know, but, that is never nearly enough. i roller blade, hike, camp, ski, and have been known to kick a hacky sac around for a few hours if the company is good. (even if it is my own good-company:) i move a lot and would like more friends who do too. i'm a big fan of sitting in the sun and hot water. i am still a lot and would like more friends who can be too. i like to get out at night. i like to stay in. i truly enjoy the experience of having a good beer. i like to make mirth. i love sun sets, sun rises and every phase of the moon.  i like to eat. even if we didn't need to eat...i'd probably still want to eat. read into that any way you wish :p i am a huge fan of my own cooking but still prefer to eat out more often than not. :::see above::: i've been vegan since '93 and it makes me happier than not being vegan would. i like being happy. i like people who like me being happy. it's my thing tho, not a deal breaker ....but certainly extra points awarded when someone "gets" it.  learn from yesterday, live for today, hope for tomorrow. the important thing is to not stop questioning ~albert einstein.  al was pretty cool sometimes. livin' it. lovin' it. finding quirky ways to describe it online... avoiding prosecution for the most part, people notice i'm just dead-sexy and everything i do and say is pure art and poetry blended seamlessly into a sublime operatic-ballet choreographed to the song-of-life ...or my bald spot.  usually the bald thing favorite books: how about favorite authors? ...howard zinn, gene wolfe, kurt vonnegut, kim stanley robinson, john steinbeck, douglas adams, david brin, neil stephenson, philip k dick, frank hebert  movies: anything that gets a good laugh or makes me think is fine in my book...err...movie i know that's a bit of a cop out...but, there it is.  fave music:  ...anything good really rock, blues, folk, reggae, oldies, newies, dance, classical, and the kind of country wayland would listen to...  food: anything vegan and yummy. ethiopian and thai struggling to stay on top of the list...just behind home-cookin'. i'm vegetarian, but not judgmental. ...well, at least not about that;)  to sum it up i'll quote myself: "follow your heart, just try not to be a dick about it". air, water, food, kindness, humor, and the antidote a mote in a hurricane. where to find more peril. the way women smell. making thursday jealous and saturday nervous  ...and playing hockey to me, unicorns taste a lot like chicken. you are: kind fun smart sexy awesome and would like to hang out  or you have cash and prizes you'd like to give me

religion: agnosticism but not too serious about it
sign: pisces and it’s fun to think about
full_bio: "two thumbs up"  "unexpected complexity and imagination." "deeply thought-provoking, yet highly entertaining."  "an idealistic expat returns from a charmed paris life to write her own recipe for success amid political upheaval and the challenges of dreaming big. weaving together romance and altruism reminiscent of "amelie" and spiced with culinary alchemy in the tradition of "like water for chocolate." superb, eclectic soundtrack accompanies our heroine as she tries to save the world one organic chicken at a time. in search of a soul mate, she places a personal ad on the internet. flash forward forty years, a sweet old couple, holding hands, laughing, still in love, best friends, reminisce about their life together and dote on the grandchildren. set on two continents, from east bay herb gardens to left-bank apartment with ubiquitous geraniums, the scenery is as richly hued as the characters are engaging." life is not a dress rehearsal. so, if the camera is rolling, so to speak, on my life adventure, then every minute counts (production costs being what they are). take one: laugh often and with real mirth; love friends, their children, family, pets; find sanctuary in domesticity, in cooking, gardening and, yes, even laundry; explore the world (even just outside my door), be captivated by its art and music; indulge in voracious daydreaming; re-learn tennis; revel in the magic of gardens, nature hikes and picnics at point reyes, reading on a blanket in the uc botanical garden, the smell of sugar pines at tahoe, hiking in olive groves in the val d'orcia, losing count of the blues and teals of a majorcan cove. quacking like a duck (highly useful skill ... though, strangely, not listed as a language in the "my details" section), believing the world can be a better place, jigsaw puzzles, remembering the latin names of plants, caring deeply, crying over the entire first part of "up" (every time i see it), dedication i really don't know. when i lived in france, sometimes people commented about my blond hair because it was unusual. the abridged lists ...  the sixteen pleasures, robert hellenga; good poems for hard times, selected by garrison keillor; a town like alice, nevel shute; treasure island, robert louis stevenson; the poisonwood bible, barbara kingsolver; persuasion, jane austen; the complete book of herbs, lesley bremness; potager, georgeanne brennan; hallelujah! the welcome table, maya angelou  truly, madly, deeply; enchanted april; le fabuleux destin d'amelie poulain; like water for chocolate; les choristes; the road home; cold comfort farm; up; bolt; moonstruck; when harry met sally; a room with a view; best in show; a mighty wind  van morrison; squirrel nut zippers; django reinhardt; yo yo ma; les pecheurs des perles; nora jones; gavin degraw; aza; gosford park soundtrack; gypsy kings; bob dylan; the oboe ... especially wafting up the stairwell of the metro loving friends, wilderness, gardens, music, fresh air, poetry being, as john donne wrote, "involved in mankind." what did people eat at various moments in history? where did my ladybugs go? recent friday night: return book to library, run box office for innovative local theater company run by former employee, miss bus, wait and wait for next bus, walk, walk, walk, cook and eat healthy meal alone (boo hoo) - wait - not alone - eat healthy meal while exchanging facebook messages with former employee stationed in afghanistan who's homesick and misses my cooking (ahhh), count blessings. i actually wanted to be a cowboy, when i was four ... and my best friend darrell and i were going to save all the wild animals, but he moved to saudi arabia after kindergarten. i haven't heard from him since (so, he may have saved wild animals, but i did not become a cowboy). compatible (not identical) goals and lifestyles are imperative. beyond compatibility, when you look at someone who loves you, their eyes reflect the goodness, the lovable-ness they see in you. you know the bumper sticker, "god give me the strength to be the person my dog thinks i am." perhaps this seems a strange reference in a personal ad, but i want to share with my partner a belief in our innate benevolence. plus, of course, friendship, humor, passion, devotion, "someone to spend national holidays with" (extra points if you name the movie that line is from). good luck in your search!

full_bio: i try to live by one of my favorite quotes:  ring the bells that you can ring forget your perfect offering there's a crack in everything, that's how the light gets in. i am a single mom with two spry lassies, 8 and 6. always hitting red lights and traffic when i'm running late. real red hair. i eat mostly organic, local and whole food. i'm a gluten free gal. my daughters my family my best gal pals physical touch faith in god why i should not be eating that. not typically doing the same thing. i am a hopeless romantic. if you like pina coladas, and getting caught in the rain... and the feel of the ocean and the taste of champagne. if you are into health food, and you have half a brain.

full_bio: gaaah, too much pressure! i can't do it.  basically, i like to have fun what ever that ends up meaning at the time. however, while i tend to be quirky, wacky and goofy, i've also got a pretty strong sense of dedication to my friends, work and relationships with others.  oh, and i *really* like pizza. i teach science at a local middle school and most days i have an absolute blast. in addition, i'm trying my hand at coaching our school's softball team but it's a lot of work even as an assistant coach.  to be honest, school takes up most of my time. even when i'm not there it feels like the grading and planning never let up. even with school taking up so much of my time, i know it will get easier as i keep going and i can see myself doing this for a long time to come. in the mean time, i'm trying to get back into yoga and maybe take up painting, we'll see. i enjoy making a mess in the kitchen, especially when tasty things result. i make an excellent backpacking/camping buddy too! i've got a whole list of references. believe it or not, i even know what to do with that awkward arm as a big spoon so yeah, i got the cuddling thing down as well.  on a more serious note, i also think i'm pretty good at my job and as a teacher of low-income and underprivileged students i feel like that's something i'm pretty proud of. i feel like this is a particularly strange question. first impressions are based on context. if you meet me a a board game night, i'm probably the loudest dude there but if you run into me at a random party, i'm more just as likely to be that awkward guy in the corner checking his phone all the time cause i don't really know anyone there.  i'm also quite bald. the pics don't really show that very well (this was a recent "choice" so i figure it's only fair to give you a heads up). books: i love to read when i can find the time. david sedaris, pill peet, stephen king, robert jordan, douglas adams, tom clancy, george r.r. martin, neil gaiman. never read bill bryson but it's sitting on my nightstand staring at me. i'll get there one day bill...  movies: despite what netflix suggests i would be interested in, i like good movies. doesn't need to be any particular genera although i do enjoy action thrillers with interesting plots (bourne trilogy!). funny though, i rarely have seen the movies that are usually up for oscars. maybe netflix was right about me...  shows: not sure how to describe this other than through examples. scrubs, house, modern family, the league, dexter, battlestar galactica, lost (but only seasons 1-4), the daily show. just started watching how i met your mother (verdict is good so far)  music: all depends on my mood. rock and classic rock are usually a pretty good bet but i can pretty much get down with anyone from "classical" to hip-hop. sorry, leave the country on the shelf. i'm also like listening to several podcasts, especially during long road trips. just saw lupe fiasco a few weeks back and it was probably the coolest show i've ever been to. (<- ended sentence w/ preposition, for shame)  food: i've yet to meet a vegetable i didn't like! while i prefer not to be tied down, i suppose i could say my life would be decidedly less satisfying without: 1) a place to go hiking 2) flannel sheets 3) friends to hang with (so clich it hurts!) 4) someone to laugh with 5) board game collection 6) my wristwatch summer vacation plans! so far this includes: 1) trip to italy/croatia, 2) backpacking in the cascades, 3) moving to a new apt(?) hanging out with friends and playing games. i've been known to both go to bed early and stay out ridiculously late. not so much into the club scene but it can be interesting from time to time. it feels narcissistic but i secretly enjoy the hell out of being an awesome friend. i've been a best man three times now and a groomsmen once (3/4 aint bad?). my friends joke that i belong in some sappy romcom opposite a katherine heigl. even got a title: "second best man;" wanna help me write the screen play?

full_bio: i am a retired firefighter and emt. i am also an avid horticulturalist, enjoying the beauty and serenity that a landscape can bring to the senses. i find intelligence an absolute turn on. i have a sometimes irreverent sense of humor and love to laugh. i express my thoughts and feelings articulately. my communication style can best be described as easy, but can be a bit direct at times. being scotch/irish, i am very passionate which can even be described as intense. i love animals. i have five "special needs" african grey parrots that i adore and take amazing care of, hence the name greywhisperer! i have a very gentle and compassionate nature; i enjoyed helping people in need when i was a firefighter. i have been active in parrot rescue and oiled bird rescue during the cosco busan spill a few years ago.  i am proud of my accomplishments, risking my life as a firefighter and being good at it. i am a good, loyal lover and friend, having never cheated on anyone in my life. i value integrity and honesty.  i'm looking for a beautiful, intelligent femme. someone to captivate me with her charms, wrapping me around her finger, and indulging me with her wit and and beauty. are you out there? what i really crave is a gentle soul; someone to share this often difficult human experience. someone who believes love is not just another four-letter word, not something to be played with or adulterated by selfishishness or self-indulgent ridiculous notions. i'm retired and finally enjoying the luxury of free time to do whatever i choose. working on my home, many projects, and enjoy spending time with my friends and parrots. the friends are loyal, and the parrots hilarious. they speak in my voice and actually speak in some sentences that they've heard from me. they even laugh like me! you'll love to meet them ...you have not lived until you've been laughed at by a parrot! being myself. being a good listener. being respectful to other people's wishes. i am pretty adept at whatever task i take on, always striving to improve myself or perform a better job. are my smile and expressive eyes and sense of humor. if i'm wearing a white tank top, it's my very dark, large areolas. i am adventuresome, sensual and am thrilled by risk. movies: the town, braveheart, to kill a mockingbird, princess bride, the wizard of oz.  i have not read enough books, nor treasured a lot of memories like i should have in my lifetime. i was always so busy working, and always believed there'd be another day to absorb knowledge and tell my friends and family that i love them. i don't do that anymore. every time i speak with or see family/friends i make the time to tell them how much i respect and acknowledge their many gifts of friendship and love, never putting off to tomorrow what should be said today.  books: silent spring and anam cara are the two books that changed my life, coming along at points in my life where i needed guidance, direction and focus to systain me. both books are beautifwittenbergitten and resonated something in me, sparking a transformative flame that was powerful enough to to change me for the better. rachel carson instilled a strong sense of stewardship for nature in me, and john o'donohue evoked a longing to belong that was absent before my coma. i love the lyrical poetry of their words; the appreciation for philosophy in john's words and the quiet desperation in rachel's.  other books: to kill a mockinbird, noah's garden, animals in translation, bay area wild, of men and fire, alex and me, sound truth and corporate myth, gone with the wind. anything by dr seuss...  tv: true blood, criminal minds, bones, dexter, 30rock, real time with bill mahr, john stewart, six feet under, the big bang theory, 30rock  music: cold play, matt nathanson, adele, the cure, bruce springsteen, cake, foster the people, bonnie rait, eagles, pink (too many to mention),judy garland , patsy cline (my parrots love their music!). i love local radio station kfog. my parrots listen to it all day! my parrots, my jeep, my iphone, friends, women, beauty in all forms (nature, art and the human form) women, sex, the condition and strife in oakland, farmer's markets, trying to eat nutritiously. my parrots. and of course exercising to stay fit and trim. there are no typical friday nights. each one is unique. you'll have to get to know me to find out! if anything in my profile resonates something in you that makes it irresistible to respond. if you're intelligent, passionate and like long slow wet kisses that go on for days, and love to laugh, i'd love to connect with you. life is too short to procrastinate in matters of the heart or friendship. grab the bull by the horns, swing yourself up and get ready for the ride of your life! rest not. life is sweeping by. go dare before you die. enjoy the roses, the notes, the dining and laughing while you still can appreciate the heart of a true romantic. fear less, hope more, whine less, breath more; talk less, say more; hate less, love more; and all good things are yours. ---swedish proverb

full_bio: i'm a 46 year old guy with twin daughters posing as a 27 year old guy for kicks. the 27 year old i was posing as, however, has taken over my body in some bizarre shamanic ritual. so, ladies, not only do i have a youthful body; i am also mature. since i am telling you all this i am also honest. on top of all that: i am the most modest man in the west.  ok. time to stop joking? okcupid is a very serious place.  i love poetry. i love meditation. i love climbing stuff. i love meeting new people. i love writing e-mails to my friends. i love biking around oakland. i love showing people my favorite spots around here and seeing theirs. i love honesty. i love dance parties. i love hugs. i love sex. i love absurd internet memes. i love absurd. i love dumpster diving. i love sharing. i love poetry readings. i love a good concert. i love quaker meetings. i love animals. there are other things i love too. i'm a poet. i use a lot of my time for reading and writing and going to poetry readings. i wrote a book called to carry you being and i want to write another one. for the cashmoney, i substitute teach, which is kind of fun a lot of the time. empathy. writing. e-mails. staying in touch. being a goofball. finding lots of free food. making people smile. climbing stuff. that i'm full of energy. that i am a goofball. uuummmmm....contemporary poetry...matvei yanelevich, dorothea laskey, james galvin, russell edson also older stuff (but i should really be looking into this more)...emily dickinson, danill kharms some spiritual classics...tao te ching, the profit, the book of mirdad...like this... fiction from all over the world. books about therapy. milton ericksson, virginia satir. books about communication.  movies...david lynch, heathers! help get me into more movies!  music...silver jews, pavement, destroyer, marnie stern, the jazz my friends who are into jazz play for me. stravinsky, zappa, john zorn, joni mitchell. help get me into more music!  food...i am veg and eat very little dairy. i'm not a food crazy person and just see it as being pretty practical. burritos are awesome... libraries, friends, poetry, love, only four now, sorry relationships, poetry, health sometimes a poetry reading. sometimes a dance party. sometimes hanging out alone. sometimes with just one friend. sometimes at a concert.  you think that people need each other to open each other up and see what is inside. you have little in the way of expectation. you don't need much to have a good time, not much more than imagination and friendship.

full_bio: not only am i lousy at self-summaries, i just don't like them... i'd far rather play "twenty questions" as i get to know someone. i'd have friends write a summary, but then it wouldn't be "self", would it... hrm. what a quandary.  lemme try a couple bullets:  - divorced, but have a *very* good relationship with my ex - father of the coolest daughter around... - moved to the bay area from the east coast in 2010... and loving it! - working in it (along with seemingly half the rest of silicon valley)  i'm a smartass who doesn't always know how not to be a smartass, but have gotten much better at knowing when to turn that off as i've aged... that's not to say that i'm always correct in that aspect, but i'm a work in progress, okay?  i am dichotomous, random, and loyal spending it wondering what i'm doing with my life... trying to raise a good kid... trying to remember how to *have* a life... sarcasm, sticking my foot in my mouth, insomnia, random conversation, and picking the slowest grocery checkout line... my lack of hair... the grey starting to creep into my beard... my laugh or lopsided grin, i suppose... books: the hobbit, hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy, ender's game, cryptonomicon, snow crash, neverwhere  movies: the princess bride, mirrormask, grosse pointe blank (love john cusack, and most movies involving his sister, joan), dug the new sherlock holmes movie... and i don't know that it's a favorite, but i have an uncanny ability to stumble across "the rock" (with sean connery and nicholas cage, not the wrestler guy) whenever it's on tv. oh, and i think i'm one of 12 people around who absolutely *loved* the wachowski brothers' take on speed racer  music: whatever happens to be on my ipod at any given time, coupled with whatever has come on my latest mix to be delivered... includes: lamb, ugly duckling, jump little children, roger clyne and the peacemakers, mighty mighty bosstones, they might be giants, r.e.m., eagles, jonathan coulton, tori amos... i'm all over the map  food: it's all over the map, really... i've come to believe that most people i really dig love sushi, i could eat (good) mexican most every day, indian (what i know of it) is wonderful, thai is great and the spice makes me happy, italian is hard to go wrong... really, i like to eat... i'm trying to be more active, though, so the evidence of that like is less, well, evident... heh. my phone my family (including the pets) music sarcasm (mine and others') internet sunlight random things (edited): -- whether i'll ever figure out the design for that tattoo i've been pondering since college (getting much closer) -- whether my brother will ever return to the states (i should really try to track his ass down and find that out) update: he did, but we still don't talk much. hrm. -- why certain drivers just suck -- what i have due at work tomorrow -- what goes into a job hunt -- whether the heels will be able to rebound from last year's crummy season with a bunch of freshmen in key roles -- sex -- sleep -- food -- (sadly) computers i'm not sure i have a "typical" friday night. some candidates involve hanging out with my daughter, seeing a movie, hanging out with friends... sometimes i can be a very private person. except when i'm not. any of this randomness sounded intriguing. or funny. or you feel like it. but not if you're actually hoping to get ahold of someone else. 'cause that wouldn't be me.

full_bio: i'm ridiculous. but in a positive way that makes me the life of a party. i like to enjoy myself where i go, and do my best to make others comfortable and happy where they are so they can enjoy themselves as well.  i have a habit of making terrible jokes. i also have a habit of laughing at my own jokes. you'll see, it's such good fun.  lived in the bay area for 3 years now, and i actually like it now.  i rock a 'hawk, cause it gives me the edge that i don't have in real life. mostly it's because i prefer being someone who can get ready faster than you and still look excellent.  for those that care or know what it means, i'm enfp and it's pretty fitting. working at a bitchin' tech startup (doesn't everyone say that about their start up though? hmm.) i know it's bitchin' cause when i interviewed and said i got a 'lady hard-on for analytics', they not only still hired me but thought i was a perfect match for the team.  prior to that i went on a long trip for a month and a half to europe (sfo  txl  arn  lhr  cwl) and i really needed it.  for a while i built backcountry trails and wielded a chainsaw for a conservation corps in nevada. even more bitchin' than the startup.  currently getting my cello chops back, and working on the plans for going to grad school for music education in the nearest future possible. i want to teach young brats how to be even brattier musicians, it's gonna be killer. i'm actually stupendous at making up new words. i usually don't mean to, but suddenly i'll try and say something really interesting and important and there it will be  a word you've never heard before used in a way that actually makes sense, but shouldn't.  changing career paths entirely. i've worked across the gamut doing everything from desk work, to hard physical labor, to tech, to education, to incredibly expensive violin sales. interestingly enough, it's all because i know exactly what i want in life, but also want to experience a bunch of other things first. i smile. a lot. i laugh. a lot. in general, i'm a pretty happy person.  the 'hawk. it's fake. it's more like a flopped over bit of hair with shaved sides. but people mention it often eough, even so far as to say it suits me.  on a serious note, i've been told my skin is like a "baby's bottom". i'm choosing to take that as a compliment. you can stroke my face to determine for yourself. try not to be a weirdo about it, ok? i read a lot. i forget books really easily, which just makes it easier to re-read them later. currently reading the ender's game series. just finished reading "lord of the flies" and jodi picoult's "19 minutes". still trying to get through "guns, germs, and steel" by jared diamond. one of my favorite authors is chuck palahniuk. i think this paragraph is pretty indicative of my reading schizophrenia.  if i have to look sorta-kinda cool for a minute, i would probably say: radiohead, death cab for cutie, phoenix, margot and the nuclear so & so's, the format and subsequently fun., nickel creek and subsequently the punch brothers, the unicorns and subsequently islands, matt costa, the weepies, guster, discovery, neutral milk hotel, the shins, white rabbit, beach house, andrew bird, bombay bicycle club, the dimes, good old war, grizzly bear, memoryhouse, miike snow, of monsters and men, now,now, two door cinema, young the giant, graffiti6. i also use rdio pretty heavily, in fact this list basically came from there. not entirely related, but not so far off either, i break into air drums with the electronic drum solo from phil collin's "in the air tonight".  i like cooking my own food. making my own stock, and kale. i really like kale. if you don't like kale, we can still be friends, but i will make fun of you. i like trying to make new things with or without recipes, but my next big attempt will be african cuisine. every morning before i leave my house i do my mental inventory of the things i need to have before i can leave. that list is probably all i really need in life:  - keys - phone - wallet - face  it may only be 4 things, and it will change in the future, but as of right now, when i walk out that door and if i came back to nothing in the evening, i know i could go to work the next day and as long as i had those 4 things, i'd be all good... i daydream a lot, about beautiful things, slightly awkward things, musical things...  my favorite moment in the day is when you're just coming back into consciousness from a beautiful dream and you're just asleep enough to fall back into it for one more snooze session. cooking with/for friends. hanging quietly with hulu for some good tv mind-sucking. perhaps some karaoke with the right crowd, or a good ole' jam session with some jazzy tunes.  or maybe i'm traveling for the weekend, i'm a big fan of short weekend trips to new places. or, if the time is right, longer, more extended trips to even newer places! i have two ultimate dreams in life. one is absolutely attainable, the other is my top choice, no questions asked.  i will share the likely unattainable goal, which involves thom yorke, jack black, and kevin cadogan (original lead guitarist from third eye blind) and myself all being in a band that does soulful jazz music.  please don't tell me it's not possible, it might crush my soul... i'm holding out for the possibility still! also if you have a way to make even part of that a reality you will literally be my hero for an extended period of time. ... you realize that while this profile may seem like a joke, it's not. the above is pretty indicative of my thought process and the fact that i'm rarely serious. don't really have time for it. far more fun to be fun. go figure.  however, while i'm mostly good times all around, i do know the edge. i toe it, but i recognize what falling over it looks like and i do my best to stay on top of it.  so if you're down for fun, fancy, and flamingoes, let's hit it up!

full_bio: i'm a catch. i enjoy crumbs - both from bread and conversation. i laugh with my whole body. new to sf, but not to life. i've been around, and really excited to find some new treasures here. i believe i eat and drink professionally. just moved to san francisco. taking a real shine to this place. eating and drinking well. i work in hospitality and have always had an affinity for sumptuous food and well-concocted cocktails. i'm always really observant of small details. my laugh. i've been told it's contagious. my polka-dots/stripes/print shirts. and maybe my blue eyes. i've always loved music. several artists of late that i've been incredibly into include: lake street dive, miss tess & the bon ton parade, crooked still, sea monsters, jesse dee, tom waits, and anything spanish. authors: zora neale hurston, dorothy parker, david sedaris, and anything food-related. food: the stranger the better. simple is great, too. i love summer tomatoes, silver queen corn, great olive oil, salt, and cheese. i love sharing meals/bites/drinks/laughs with friends more than anything. i usually like to sit at the bar if it's available over sitting at a table. my family, my friends, butter, live music, my memory, and my humor. what truly makes people happy. moreover, how humans choose to present themselves. language use and how it deals with perception. oh, and salt. getting out of work and trying to find the slowest bar imaginable with tom waits looking characters lined up in the bar stools. or taking my shirt off and dancing all sweaty like. take your pick. i love to ride city buses and listen to all of the great material being bandied about by bus denizens. they're my favorite. you've played this game too many times, and would simply like to meet an exceptional person. i am a pretty deft conversationalist, so you're guaranteed a good talk at the very least. simple requirements generally include two things: show up, and don't suck. if you can accomplish that, we'll probably do just fine.

full_bio: i am an amazing, attractive, funny, charming, interesting, sincere, independent, grounded person. i identify as queer. i don't discriminate, i'm just attracted to who i'm attracted to. right now i think i'm more interested in men than i am in women, but its not carved in stone. i'm pretty flexible, but i'm not a doormat. i have been in two long term relationships, and other than that i haven't really "dated" all that much. i'd really like to date for a while. and honestly, i'd really like to be in a really positive, sustaining long-term relationship. but i'm also at a point in my life where i'm not going to settle for something that doesn't really work. i'm looking to meet cool friends and poly people of all genders and denominations. i have dated people who identify as queer, genderqueer, trans, gay, bi, and in-between. i like amazing people and i have my own ideas about what makes someone "amazing". i like nerds of all stripes.  i put a lot of thought into my opinions, and i try not to confuse them with my beliefs. i think communication is key, in all relationship, so that's what i'm working on most in my life. i work too much, and i keep musician's hours (although i'm not actually a musician- but that would be awesome), which interferes with my social life. like i can date at 3 pm on a tuesday, or after 1am on a friday- crazyness! i like lunch dates.  i'm trying to practice meditation in everyday life. touching. kissing. fucking. taking care of people. taking care of myself. procrastination. getting things done. showing up on time. listening. planing. painting walls. starting fires. cooking. driving stick. growing things. my pretty eyes. my smile. my energy. lists are boring. i own lots and lots of movies, and many books. i ate chicken feet in china, kangaroo in australia. i'm omnivorous, but mostly vegetarian. i like tea. and coffee- i need coffee to start my boot process in the morning understanding. compassion. kindness. music. period. love...for myself and others people...specifically my friends, but also the strangers who make my day sometimes. showers- clean/ fresh/ hot water is the pinnacle of luxury. cell phones- i'm just never home anymore. coffee. people. where i am in time and space. possibilities  the weird things that humans think/say/do. growth. we could all use a lot of it. possibilities: this small box we live in day to day is infinitely bigger than what we see. hope. because without it, we fail. history, because it tells us how we got here. truth, because everyone has their own version of it. love. because it helps us grow. i am working. i work every friday night and alternate weekends and it sucks. when i'm not working i catch up with friends, catch something new on netflicks, or do something totally unexpected. i'm an introvert. that being said, after i get over the initial hump i love to talk and engage with people. i often prefer quiet conversation over wine or curling up under blankets to watch a movie, or reading a book to crowds and bars, but i'm pushing myself to move in new directions.  i really enjoy the slow exchange of energy between two people. soft caresses, breathing in and out, holding hands. you're interested

full_bio: ok, here's me in a big nutshell~ i'm an easy going, well grounded person with a good sense of humor. i appreciate the company of others who are curious about things, ready for adventure, don't judge too harshly and recognize they are creators of their own life experience in any given moment. i can really get into the simple things in life like making dinner going to farmers markets, or going for a walk. and i live for the excitement of going on new adventures, finding new places, doing something new and daring. i regularly practice yoga, meditate, surf (or something that sorta resembles surfing), bike, run and hike and currently i'm training for a mini triathlon. i'm hoping to find someone who values integrity and authenticity, but not necessarily an angel, because i'm sure not. good luck on your search! my life has been a journey of learning how to be grateful for what i have and feeling worthy of love, success, and joy for no reason other than the fact that i exist. i come from a rough childhood situation which has given me the opportunity to feel empathy for others. i crafted a nice career for myself as an artist and now i'm helping other artists by sharing projects and teaching them what i've learned. im dedicated to helping people find their own personal power, especially my nieces and nephews and other younger people. enjoying life and sharing that joy with others. i'm a great listener and pretty good at talking too. i like to find the humor in situations and lighten things up. i'm good at being a friend people can talk to and confide in. i'm thinking they're noticing my boobs first but really it's probably my eyes and my new koolaburra boots. my favorite author is barbara kingsolver. i appreciate indie movies that are a bit quirky with major character growth. i love outdoor music festivals and dancing. for food i enjoy fine dining and fresh organic food especially avocadoes  my next art project and how i can make the day the best it can be. with friends doing something fun like dancing or concerts and a great dining experience. i need deep connections with others. you resonate with my profile, value honesty and integrity and like to be active. good wit and some belly laughter also works.

full_bio: i love music. my favorite number is 8. i try to always see things clearly. i don't say no to a new experience. i crave stimulating conversation. i can shred a tasty guitar solo.  i'm generally curious about most things; this attitude is a recent acquisition.  until now, my life has felt like a string of bizarre events, fortuitous and unfortunate - yet the only plausible way of describing how i've become who i am today. one connection, interest, or decision leading unpredictably into another, but when i try and reflect back on what's happened, it all makes perfect sense. and after living in sf for a little while, i'm finally starting to see that i'm not just thrown into the world - but instead i can actually do something here.  i realize this all sounds a vague philosophy, but maybe it resonates with you to. working in tech. playing in a band. producing my own electronic music. basically hacking life. living the minimalists dream. listening. reading faces. i make eye-contact. always. i think its important. isn't this all stuff for a first date?  suffice to say, i like a good dystopian novel, albums that have no gaps between tracks, and good food, especially when it's made with love. laptop fancy headphones asian food (of all varieties) my bike an acoustic guitar my lil sis. sound waves. listening to live music, playing live music, or wondering why i decided to go a crowded bar without music. when i walk up stairs, i feel like there's someone behind me. i have felt this way for as long as i can remember. you're intrigued by anything that you've read and if you want to meet me. i'm not looking for a pen-pal, i'm more interested in a date.

full_bio: i'm certainly something. recent library school grad looking for a saj (single academic job) for cataloging and long walks through the young adult section. procrastinating.  what, you thought i was fllling this thing out for my health?  when it comes to books, movies and shows i almost never enjoy non-genre works. that is to say, the one abiding thread of enjoyment is that they are almost always sci-fi/fantasy/mystery/thriller or otherwise genre novels. if it doesn't have magic, spaceships, superheroes or spies, i'm probably not interested in any meaningful way. i can't list anything in particular because i have an attention span the length of a mayfly's life, so if i write down my favorite book series at the moment it's very likely to change in the next month or so. (that said, i'm currently-- july 2011-- split between ben aaronovitch's peter grant books and aliette de bodard's obsidian and blood series.)  i also like non-fiction about pop culture, religion, race and gender. not always at the same time, but almost.  my music tastes are eclectic, and i like pretty much anything that isn't overly twangy country or performed by someone under the age of 18. i'll listen to hebrew rap as easily as traditional celtic music and disney songs besides. sadly, i rarely go to concerts (only for little-known alt-rock musicians, more the hipster), but if someone got me tickets to see jessie j or clannad, i'd be there.  i will eat anything except for vegetables. 1. laptop (and charger) 2. ipod (and headphones) 3. library of books 4. vanilla beans 5. cocoa powder race, gender, class, sexuality, pop culture.  comic books, how to build a rocket pack, who would win in a fight between jesus and wolverine, how to sew a dress for a teddy bear, recipes for chocolate cake and bread. out to see a movie, at home watching terrible television, or trying to get writing done.

full_bio: i'm originally from omaha, ne, went to school in nashville, tn, and am now out here in sf for more school.  i'm interested in just about everything and love learning about new things. indeed, i'm the one who annoyingly reads the informational placards at points of interest when everyone else is trying to move on. 17th century you say? go on. . .  i also enjoy combining semi-educational activities with alcohol. to that end i really like going out for pub trivia.  i brew my own beer.  i like red wine. and cocktails. and whisky. if we get along i'll share my pappy van winkle with you. is that a euphamism? only time will tell.  also, i like coffee.  i guess i'm sort of a beverage snob.  i like to travel, but don't get the chance to as often as i'd like given my work schedule. i was lucky enough to spend six months in germany in college during the 2006 world cup, which was a great time. i've been back a few times since.  i'm easy going and tend to make jokes about just about everything. don't be offended.  i took the myers-briggs test on this site and when it stated "you are a decisive person" i quickly chose "i neither agree nor disagree," then i realized what just happened and started laughing out loud. you win this round, internet test. . .  i like cooking. and baking. when you get a dough going and its really supple but not sticky. so good. so good!  ... bad news, guys ... http://bit.ly/xdpuaf i am currently getting a phd in developmental biology at ucsf. once that's done (a few years?) i'd like to stay in science and do a post-doc somewhere. i could see myself staying in the bay area or moving somewhere else, so long as it's a cool city. candidates include new york, boston, seattle. we'll see. brewing beer, genotyping, trivia, drinking just enough so that i'm not wasted but start saying inappropriate things, wasting time reading my twitter feed. . . i guess my beard? it is pretty great. books: cat's cradle, slaughterhouse 5, the time machine, snow crash, dune, the selfish gene, hitchhiker's guide to the galaxy, american prometheus, the areas of my expertise  movies: pulp fiction, rushmore, the royal tenenbaums (wes anderson and bill murray are great - put them together so much the better), blazing saddles (any mel brooks, really), princess mononoke, oldboy, the big lebowski, goodbye lenin, die fetten jahre sind vorbei, airplane!  shows: antiques roadshow, poirot, archer, 30 rock, venture brothers, mad men, no reservations, party down, the guild, good eats, battlestar galactica, community, arrested development, prometheus and bob  music: i was raised on a healthy mixture of the beastie boys and weird al. pretty great.  these days i listen to all sorts of things. recent highlights include: clams casino, the weeknd, childish gambino, m83, das racist, lil b, beirut, broken social scene, cults, the glitch mob, thao with the get down stay down, the decemberists, robyn, lcd soundsystem, the national, sleigh bells, the flaming lips, animal collective, panda bear  podcasts: the history of rome, never not funny, jordan jesse go, stop podcasting yourself, the moth, this american life, studio 360, dance department, planet money, judge john hodgman, dan carlin's hardcore history, wtf  food: mission burritos. other food. 1. coffee 2. polymerase chain reaction 3. music and podcasts to listen to while setting up #2 4. beer 5. internet 6. my bicycle primary cilia. ask me about nature's greatest organelle! probably out in the mission w/ friends eating and/or drinking. at zeitgeist if the weather's nice.  occasionally fridays turn into beer and board game night w/ friends at school. (settlers, carcassonne, ticket to ride, betrayal at the house on the hill, others). if i had to date an animated character, it would probably be erin esurance (pink hair!). you want to hang out and your handle doesn't include any sort of baked good, baby animal, or royal title (e.g. princesskittenmuffins, stay back.)

full_bio: my life summed up in a paragraph: i was born in michigan, collected all of my childhood scars between north carolina and florida. grew up a little bit and went to university for biology in nc, worked in pathology for several years. decided to scrap the corporate climb and bought a one way ticket to europe. ended up traveling for two years stopping to work for brief stints in italy and then in australia. returned to the states and went to school for audio production here in san francisco. after i graduated i decided to travel again and now i've finally landed back in san francisco to start down a new path. i am currently in paramedic school and absolutely love being here in sf - feels like home... i'm all about change and keeping everyday as interesting as possible, i'm on okcupid to find someone to share it all with! sleepless student, daydreamer of overseas travel and music producer in my off hours.   guns germs and steel, fight club, zen and the brain, anything kerouac, bukowski, orwell, or palahniuk. i don't own a tv but i do stream hulu and netflix documentaries like it's my job :) food, i'll try anything twice, authentic mexican may be my favorite italian or indian a close second. espresso, passport, drums, toothbrush, backpack, bicycle the crazy things i may see when i become a paramedic  why people in the tenderloin always have conversations across the street from each other  why the wind always blows 50mph in the opposite direction when i'm on my bike  how much those old ladies collecting cans really make in a day  portion sizes in trendy sf restaurants, people that say "expresso"  my next travel destination.... writing, studying, reading, out for drinks, restaurant hopping or enjoying some live music i burned down three acres of forest in nc when i was 13. you have a great sense of humor, you're open-minded, goal oriented and have a willingness to take a risk every now and then.
