full_bio: this is work in progress...  i have lived in sf for quite a few years and love it here. i grew up on the east coast and went to college in philadelphia. after college, i moved to san francisco and have been here since except for two years in madison, wi where i attended business school.  i love to travel and love hearing about other people's travel adventures. some of my favorite cities are buenos aires and paris. what is not to love about these places, right?  i love to eat new and interesting foods. i can enjoy a meal at a hole-in-the-wall restaurant as much as one at a fancy restaurant. recently, i've been enjoying visiting some of the great food trucks that have popped up in san francisco. i am always up for a food adventure.  i would say that i am a good listener and a very loyal friend. it may take a while for me to warm up to you but once i do, i am a pretty dependable and loyal friend.  i am hoping to be pleasantly surprised by people that i meet on this site. it would be great to find a new friend to explore the city with or someone to go on a great (or even awkward) first date or two or three.  romantic at heart so small gestures and thoughtful actions go a long way with me. i work for a commercial real estate company. i like to stay active by playing volleyball, soccer, tennis and working out at the gym. i enjoy spending time at cafes, reading and browsing books stores, working, daydreaming, traveling, finding food adventures and talking/hanging out with friends. ...lending an ear. ...eating yummy food. ...being sarcastic. ...providing another point of view. ...coming up with ideas. my big smile and my legs. reads: anything written by peter mayle. middlesex. new yorker.  movies: gran turino, the devil wears prada, 400 days of summer, away we go, billy elliot, beautiful thing, city of god, sweet home alabama, crazy, stupid love, pretty women.  music: sometimes i like top 40, jazz, gay dance music, and definitely foster the people!  tv: i like hgtv and the travel channel, the golden girls, watching certain sports--college basketball, tennis, volleyball, swimming, soccer, and gymnastics. and the fabulous beekman boys.  food: i enjoy a wide range of foods. the more unusual or exotic the better. japanese, vietnamese, tapas, burmese, indian, thai, and seafood are on top of my food list. my faves include limon, baretta and esperpento. additionally, i love the food truck frenzy that is currently going on in sf.  if you have any restaurant recommendations, i would love to hear about them! full_bio: i've been meaning to rewrite this profile for a while, it seems so overwhelming!  i am spunky and caring. i love to laugh and always have an open ear or shoulder for friends. i enjoy playful jabs with coworkers - sometimes the sarcastic new yorker in me is all too apparent. i'm definitely not a girly-girl - i clean up nice, but make-up, contacts and hair down are about a once-a-month occasions (though if any of you gents out there have big dates planned i'll happily make that more frequent). i'm a totally out-of-the-closet nerd; i haven't been to a comicon, but i would do so proudly.  i'm sure there's a lot more to say about myself, but going on about myself is really not too me either. working - a lot. i have one of those passion jobs that you get wrapped up in and neglect everything else. i work in theater production, i'm kinda the middle management of the backstage world.  when i'm not working, i'm often catching up on sleep and laundry. i try to schedule time for gaming with friends, either random board games or d&d style adventuring. i will sometimes go out on the town bar hopping, but that's only ever as entertaining as the company i keep. if i get a bigger chunk of time off i'll do some adventuring like hiking or skiing, or flying to nyc to visit friends and family.  since i moved out to sf for a job most of my friends are work friends. this happens a lot in theater since we chose to make our hobby/passion our career - but it's not really a good idea to look for dates around the office :-) mitigating. the joke about my job is that it's like herding cats, so i spend a lot of time negotiating between strong personalities. my eyes. that seems cliche - i should ask some people that just met me... books: kurt vonnegutt, terry pratchet, neal stephenson, margret atwood, douglas adams, tom robbins, chris moore, neil gaiman.  movie: (i really don't watch movies anymore, i don't know what gives) boondock saints, princess bride, usual suspects, shrek, beauty and the beast, reservoir dogs.  music: beatles, they might be giants, fountains of wayne, jimmy eat world, weezer, guster, avett brothers  food: various combinations of tomato, cheese and carb; comfort food and whatever that means for me at the time. chocolate. my phone - these things do everything now. particularly the ability to call my mom, google map everything, get work emails (too many) and take pictures.  something to read, now in form of my nook.  walking, i'm a city kid, i walk everywhere.  hysterical fits of laughter. i need a weekly dose.  naps  good friends/shoulder to cry on/someone to vent to when i'm way too stressed i have a very hard time shutting my brain off, so i spend too much time thinking about the stupidest things. bigger things - where am i going to be in 5 yrs? whether i've shot myself in the foot professionally or socially...generally doubting my big decisions even when i know they were the right ones. either working late, grabbing a beer with friends or coworkers, enjoying a laid back game night, or at home enjoying some me-time when i've spent way too much time at work. i may act all cool and aloof, but i want so badly to be really excited and passionate about someone - i want to be thrilled by potential. you're awesome and don't really know it. you have something interesting to say. you're a nerd but not socially awkward. you enjoy games, but not the emotional kind. you're driven and ambitious yet don't take yourself too seriously. you're cute and sexy, but would never take a picture of yourself in the mirror showing off your abs.

you have an overwhelming desire for bad puns, stale one-liners, and flirtatious movie quotations. you want to feel tall. you want someone who will charm the pants off of your parents. you want someone who owns a foot-tall afro wig and white patent-leather disco shoes with 4-inch heels.

full_bio: i'm easy-going and like to laugh, but i'm pretty shy at first in real life. once you get to know me i'm comfortable talking about pretty much anything.  i got my b.s. from uc berkeley in electrical engineering and computer science, and my m.s. from ucla in biomedical engineering, specializing in neuroengineering. my main interest within the field of neuroengineering was brain-machine interfaces and neural prosthetics (basically like when luke skywalker gets a fancy robot hand after his dad lops the old one off). unfortunately i didn't have the chance to work on any bmi research projects specifically, so my graduate education basically amounted to some graduate neuroscience classes (which was a little trippy considering i hadn't taken a biology class since high school).  but enough of that bullshit. i don't really think what you study in school defines you as a person, and i often felt somewhat out of place among my ee/cs brethren. i guess it just felt cooler being the nerd in high school, because at least you feel a little different from most of your peers. going from that to being surrounded by dudes (and yeah, they were mostly dudes) who get off on differential equations and customizing their programming ides just made my life feel a little out of balance. so i rebelled against them and now i'm dumb. just kidding. all i'm trying to say is that despite my fancy education, the things i enjoy most often have nothing to do with it. i started working as an electronics engineer at a medical device start-up recently, so my free time has almost completely vanished. now that i have a steady source of income i'm looking to move out in the very near future, and once i do that i guess i'll completely be an adult and my life will basically be over. maybe i'll go back to school in a few years. i don't know.  when i'm not working i'm just hanging out, watching movies, playing video games, and exercising. i joined a rock climbing gym in december, and bouldering is pretty fun even though it hurts my delicate programmer hands. sleeping! oh, and i guess most people are surprised at how well i handle chopsticks for a white guy. i can also tie a cherry stem into a knot with just my mouth (this is not meant to be a sexual remark).  coming up with clever things to say long after the moment has passed. probably my eyebrows. they're a little more on the epic side of the spectrum, i swear they're real though. books: stephen king's alright, and i read a book recently that was a compilation of interviews with comedy writers that was pretty entertaining.  movies: jurassic park is my all time favorite. i also like most tarantino movies, the shawshank redemption, the jerk, star wars (especially empire strikes back, but return of the jedi is a close second), the usual supsects, the thing, and willy wonka and the chocolate factory (which is the gene wilder version, not that johnny depp pile of garbage).  shows: chappelle's show was awesome while it lasted, and dave chappelle is also one of my favorite stand up comedians. i also really like seinfeld, 30 rock, parks and rec, breaking bad, upright citizen's brigade, the daily show, the colbert report (even though the interviews aren't as good as the daily show), house (even though every episode is pretty much the same), peep show, and early seasons of the office, scrubs, and south park.  i'm a big fan of comedy in general. if you want to talk about it or the creation of it, whether it's stand-up, tv, movies, or whatever, it might be fun.  music: queens of the stone age, the white stripes, the black keys, wu-tang clan, the legendary roots crew, some jay-z, the beatles, led zeppelin, and the red hot chili peppers.  food: pretty much everything, although i'm a big advocate for using cliantro in moderation, if at all. i've been eating more healthy food recently, pretty much abandoning regular soda and potato chips, but god damn can i go to town on some potato chips. i was turned on to thai food in college, and i'm always on the lookout for a cheap place that does pad prik king as well as thai basil in berkeley's asian ghetto. i'm not so particular that i can't see myself living with basically the necessities, but i do particularly enjoy:  a really comfortable place to sleep. the internet/a computer. my family and friends. a nice big television. bacon cheeseburgers. video games! (no mmos though) where i'm headed in life and whether i'm living to my full potential. i kind of figured i'd be a millionaire by now, so i don't know what went wrong. in general i feel a duty to use whatever (debatable) intelligence i have to serve others and the greater good, but sometimes i imagine life just enjoying the things i have and those close to me. i don't know if that's selfish or not. at a friend's house making fun of nicolas cage or watching muted cartoons with hip-hop playing in the background. i once performed jay-z's "99 problems" in some dive bar on karaoke night after losing a bet. it was mostly filled with people in their 40s and 50s, who were probably wondering what the hell was going on.

full_bio: i'm a mechanical engineering student. i like to play hockey over in oakland, and surf when a good swell hits. i play guitar and piano in my free time. i love dancing and would like to find someone to learn swing dancing with at some point. anyway, i'm really looking forward to summer and trying new things! i went back to school last year after a long hiatus, switching my major from acting to engineering. i want to get a masters in aerospace engineering at sjsu, specializing in propulsion...aka being a rocket scientist! flying upside down with my hair on fire. i got my private pilot's license in high school, and although i haven't flown in years, my head is still always in the clouds. when i start flying again, i'm going to train for aerobatics competitions...ever seen the red bull air race? being licensed for unlimited aerobatics, which means pulling 10g's 10 feet off the deck at 200 plus knots, takes thousands of hours of experience...and i can't wait to get started! i'm a little shy and introverted around most people, but there are some people that bring out my more outgoing side, which is often larger than life. when polite conversation happens to touch on something i'm passionate about...i transform. i like the wild extravagance of 80's rock. def leppard, van halen, scorpions...i play neoclassical shred guitar a la yngwie malmsteen. my current favorite band is muse, which has the technicality and brashness associated with the hard rock era, while also being quite introspective at times. if i start a band, it would be heavily influenced by muse and their precursor, pink floyd. current tv shows: game of thrones, mad men...battlestar galactica. i started watching battlestar after seeing that portlandia skit about how addicting it is. and although i find it devolving into 'lost' territory as the second season begins, the show is like lay's potato chips...you can't have just one. the gym. homer walks by the gym and wonders, "giiim? what's a giiim?" (nods head in recognition) "ohh, right...a giiim!" moral? simpsons rule! the disconnect between relativity and quantum mechanics...a unifying theory must be found! just kidding. i'm probably one of the least nerdy engineering majors. one time in the middle of a microcontroller lab, i got so frustrated trying to make an led blink, i blurted out, "i hate computers!" everyone looked at me like i was from mars. i like engineering because its the fundamental underpinning of everything i want to do...fly, race motorcycles, cars, and sailboats. ...wishing i knew a cool girl to take to dinner! i sometimes mishear things people say and then repeat what i thought i heard back to them, which often is completely ridiculous and nonsensical. for instance, i once was at a waterpark, and a friend pointed out in the distance, "there's the hurricane over there." now, unaware it was the name of a ride, i rushed over and cried out, incredulous, "there's a hurricane over there?!" keep in mind, this was concord, not kansas. and, yes, if you're a practical joker, i'm quite gullible...my friends once had me completely convinced that neil patrick harris was a real 16 year old doctor before he had his own show, doogie howser m.d. you're a dreamer

full_bio: still sorta new to the bay area. i moved here from ann arbor, mi back in august. i've recently spent my time exploring sf, working, and enjoying the city nightlife with friends. i work in san mateo for a small company/startup called mybuys. i have a b.s. in computer science from the university of michigan and le grande diplome de le cordon bleu from le cordon bleu london, paris, and sydney. i bike like everywhere. i'm a bit of a nerd (hence the comp sci degree). i like to travel, meet new people, and hear great stories of other people's travels. i live for new experiences. there's about a million restaurants i want to try in sf. i live in the mission, but i don't particularly spend time in any one specific neighborhood. i love how every neighborhood in sf has something cool and unique to offer. i'm pretty laid back and i get along with just about anyone i meet. message me up if you want to grab a drink or try a new restaurant! working, living, enjoying life! cycling, cooking, hackey sack, wandering around cities, getting lost, finding myself, taking spur of the moment trips, walking into a store with the intent of buying one item and coming out with a cart full of stuff, working with computers, oragami, drinking beer, losing my phone, doing yoga (well working on it), waking up early in the morning (haha, right, jk), having a ton of the most random jobs before graduating from college (just ask!) that i'm not quite white. it's fun to hear all the guesses people have though, care to give it a shot? :) books: the singularity is near, hp, catcher in the rye... movies: waking life, slc punk!, big fish, crash, gladiator... shows: south park, how i met your mother, always sunny... music: going to ultra in miami, killers, muse, atmosphere, beatles... food: anything from the water, salads, bruschetta, healthy stuff... in no particular order... good food, my 2010 casati vola (road bike), my iphone, my passport, my laptop, and my friends/family where i'll be in the next few years (life, country, career, etc) oh and ideas for an awesome app or website to start my own (or with friends) business happy hour with co workers, out with friends, dance clubs, shows, exploring, trying a new restaurant i still have the teddy bear from when i was born, though he's seen better days. you want to go for a bike ride, try a new restaurant, try a new recipe, grab a drink at a cool bar, go for a hike, go dancing, want to see what's on the other side of the hill, have heard of string theory, want to catch a movie, want to run around in dolores (or golden gate) park, or want to go on some random crazy adventure.

full_bio: i intellectualize everything, and have strong (but revisable) opinions. this can either lead to great conversations or complete frustration...or both. yes, i have accepted that i'm a terrible self promoter.  i like to spend time working outdoors and with my hands. unfortunately, i find myself in front of a computer than i would like. some assorted interests are fishing, cooking, gardening, bonsai, reading and home-brewing. i made the (questionable) decision last year to quit a cushy job at microsoft and start a business selling carnivorous plants online. not a joke. i spend most of my "working" time managing the website, importing plants from around the world, and cultivating them in my greenhouse. things have actually gone surprisingly well so far, and i'm in the process of creating a few more similar businesses.  the transition from a desk job to being self-employed has left me with no accountability to anyone else. i'm still in the process of figuring out how to fill my free time. travel is starting to sound pretty good, but in the mean time i've just been enjoying the relaxation. mostly useless things: introspection, delay of gratification, long-term thinking, pull-ups, standardized testing, public speaking, blackjack usually my eyes (green.) occasional comments on my freckles. food: just about anything smoked, pickled, salted, or otherwise preserved. lamb (see below,) good cheese, mushrooms, organ meats (so much better than they sound,) craft-brewed beer, scotch, bacon, fried eggs and hashbrowns for any meal, sushi, curry, and anything i haven't tried.  shows: archer, house, top gear (the real one,) always sunny, mad men, daily show, colbert, firefly, bsg, sherlock, most of adult swim  movies: fight club, supertroopers, kung fu hustle, lotr, snatch (most other ritchie,) army of darkness (all other raimi,) harry potter series, tombstone, the hangover, i'm sure there's more  books: doing this by author because i'm lazy - vonnegut, palahniuk, gladwell, c. mccarthy, klosterman, tolkien, bradbury, arthur c. doyle, jhonen vasquez, dawkins, seth grahame-smith  music: muse, gnarles barkley, neil young, radiohead, pink floyd, gorillaz, danger mouse, broken bells - water (i drink a lot of it) - flannel sheets - lamb - internet connection - lots of sleep - excessively long showers new businesses or projects to work on. often at the expense of my current projects that could really use the attention. usually not doing things that sound cool. i'm most productive in the evenings, so i normally get wrapped-up in a project and don't pull away until it's too late to start thinking about going out. if not, i'm hanging out with friends, and i do occasionally go out to low-key spots in sf, palo alto, or mountain view. i'm shamefully bad at spelling. i also frequently don't know what day/date/month it is. you can't stand cilantro. we can commiserate.

full_bio: i'm 27, born and raised in san francisco and would never leave. others might describe me as genuine and honest. i like to think of myself as a go getter. anything i participate in, i give it 110%. i'm mature in situations, but can easily act half my age if called upon for some crude humor. i work as a financial planner during the day, and turn into a food critic by night. i love the city and can't ever imagine living anywhere else. while i love the city, i do enjoy weekends outdoors.  i'm holding onto the hope that we will have a late winter, otherwise i won't even have to wax my rusty snowboard. as for summer activities i enjoy, cycling, hiking and shooting. -fixing things, anything is possible with duct tape and a little elbow grease!  -making people laugh  -probably should include financial planning? no? i'm a comedian, typically i'm making people laugh, except for the crowd of people nearby that i've offended. food: at the moment, i'm trying to get my fill of foie gras before they ban it. laughter - even if i'm laughing at my own jokes.  knife - more uses than you can ever imagine, i'll give you a hint, serial killing isn't one of them, not any more.  cell phone - connected at the hip.  frying pan - i love cooking  red meat - i love eating, especially a good medium rare steak  coffee - just ask me on monday http://www.tumblr.com/blog/swine-time probably enjoying a nice dinner before we hit the bar i love to make people smile. you're outgoing and fun to be around. you like to cook and you love swine. you like to ski, snowboard, cycle or rock climb. you aren't easily offended, and love to laugh even if you know its wrong. you have a weird fixation with zombies.

full_bio: i'm a midwestern boy with roots in the mideast. being tired of fancying the middle, recently i ventured from chicago to boston, to san francisco.  i consider myself energetic. eager and willing to learn, challenge, and explore.  i appreciate the familiar - i treasure the unknown.  i have many interests - to each, i am diligent and passionate.  i'm photographer, a scientist, a biker - among many other labels.  i don't like labels. :) when i was younger, my dream was to be a scientist. after many years of school - including graduate school - i am, finally, a scientist.  the next chapter in my life seems bright and uncertain, but i can't wait to write it. i'd prefer to write it with good company - my family, friends and a partner in crime. listening. exploring. meditating.  i can cook. my smile - i hope. books: non-fiction. movies: independent movies. horror movies - and romantic comedies. food: anything spicy or vegetarian. family, friends, food, bike, camera, and my journal. the future. how the miniscule details of today are magnified into the themes of tomorrow. there's no typical. i'm disgusted by people who eat a kiwi without peeling it. :( you want to know more about me.

full_bio: i am like don quixote with a heavy handed pour of art and a dash of scotch.  ok, i know that was kinda cheesy but i love adventures, art, and food....and so does everyone else. um, i'm kinda funny and i have good ideas every once and a while?  i started sculpture because i wanted to make a sword in middle school and found that i had a talent for working with jewelry and metalsmithing (i couldn't get into the foundations and big metal classes.) i pursued art through college on the east coast and i still love it.  i am a san francisco native with a terrible case of wanderlust. i moved back to sf a year ago. after 5 years of living in ithaca studying, and brooklyn, spending my savings, and whidbey island off seattle, working on a farm and being an artists assistant, i thought i should find a job where i wasn't quite so poor. i gotta feed my addiction to restaurants, travel and armloads of books.  i believe a book is often worth its weight in gold, and i spend a lot of time reading on buses or at restaurants if no one is with me. well after going into space as an astronaut. i invested in a little thing called twitter. i took what i earned and just purchased a couple of small places in san fran. i generally fly around the world these days writing for various food and travel magazines or designing clothing for rvka. i am also working on illustrating a children's book. i learned to cook from an italian chef in naples recently, but according to him my tendencies still live in my earlier training at some of the chinese restaurants here in san francisco. i can dance pretty well but i'm learning salsa and if i have had a few i may try to breakdance. i am good at anything visual but while my writing is funny i lack obsession with grammar, which is why i only have one published book. i also part time as a professional massage therapist. i have a small space in between my front teeth but in a charming way, not in a cletus from simpsons kinda way. that and i look great in clothes. (i look better without them) books: i read a lot, i am addicted to scifi and fantasy, but i do not understand why every girl on this site likes david sedaris or bukowski not to mention lolita. its like they are quoting the last thing their writing professor told them to read. i read lolita and it was ok but come on people. not to be offensive i just don't understand it.  movies: the city of lost children, the fall, city of darkness, never ending story, zombie land, hook, drunken master, kung fu panda.  shows: bored to death, wilfred, game of thrones, how i met your mother. i dont really watch tv, so its whatever i feel like watching on my computer.  music: black keys, beirut, gorillaz, otis redding, the shins, cults, glitch mob, bon iver, the heavy, lupe fiasco. consider each of these as one of like five similar bands that i listen to. i am a huge fan of turntable.fm  food: everything, anything new, spicy food, i am partial to chinese food (not bad chinese food). my horse, my lotus, my money, my fame, my pet monkey that steals from people i dont like, good food. how to fight a bull with just my cape and if running faster than a cheetah and being stronger than a bear has practical uses. teaching children to read and curing diseases when i was hiking in alaska i met a family of polar bears and convinced them i was their new king. to do so i had to big spoon their current king. he was fluffy and warm. if you have a sense of humor and can hold a conversation. or if making love to you is like riding lightning and thunder and you are a goddess amongst mere mortals.

full_bio: i'm a geek. i overthink things. i find programming sexy. i've lived in a lot of different places, including new zealand, canada, and england. i like animals, especially horses and cats. i feel most comfortable when i'm alone, though i somehow tend to get into interesting social situations. i've been working as a software engineer at an animation studio for a few years now. for a while i was writing iphone apps. i've been taking improv classes for a year or two, and just finished my first semester of japanese at a little school in san francisco. programming! and horse riding. like most of us, probably more than i realize. probably my height. possibly my eyes. books: china mieville, ursula k. le guin movies: fight club, memento, lotr, serenity tv: doctor who, blake's 7, dexter, firefly, sherlock (bbc) music: trance, for work. otherwise i'm not much into music. or rather i am, but i think music is a private thing. food: indian and thai. i try to eat mostly meat, which makes italian difficult, and i think i overdid chinese in my first job, but other than that i like almost everything. cats. i haven't been catless for more than a few months since i was 6. chocolate. lord of the rings and blake's 7 dvds. a computer of some sort. makeup. a nice bathroom. sometimes i have nightmares about living in a place with a yucky bathroom. social structure in corporations. how to get myself to do the things that i want to do. thomas jefferson (he's awesome) usually home relaxing. all last year i took business classes from a 40-year-old guy who does gymnastics dressed up as a 18-year-old girl. i think it's awesome that he can make himself do that.  i spent 3 years playing ffxi, and 6 months or so playing wow. sometimes i miss it terribly. you're a guy, preferably with geek tendencies, looking for something possibly long term. listening skills are very important. i'll be extra happy if you've read impro.
